7. The charger is built right into the power cord plug. There's no separate unit to clutter the sink top when recharging.

My only gripe is that Norelco does not provide a hard case for the shaver. The soft travel bag is OK for packing on trips but isn't convenient for use in the house. The shaver has a small flat spot on the back and can be placed on a shelf but if you have to put it into a sliding drawer it has a tendency to rock over on its side. Oh well, you can't have everything!

Bottom line: this shaver is the real deal. Get one at a discount store if you can ........ but even at $130 its a winner.

reviews.title: Pretty good, but not quite as close as a blade.
reviews.text:
I have had a Braun, then a Remington, neither of which were particularly impressive. The Remington provided a very close shave for nearly three years, but soon after the 1 year warranty on that thing was up, the battery no longer held a charge, and the trimmer quit working. I decided to give it up for the pricier Norelco as I have this vision of Norelco being the Cadillac of the bunch.

I have had the Norelco 7885 now for about 5 months. It works well and is reliable. I particularly like the digital display that indicates when you need to properly wash it. The fact that the shaver is water proof makes cleaning it a breeze. The battery charge is excellent offering me 35 minutes of shaving time on a full charge (which takes only about 2 hours). The only problem I have is that I wish the shave were a little closer - it still provides a good shave, but it does seem as though my 5 o'clock shaddow comes a little earlier since I switched to the Norelco. Despite the fact that my old Remington needed the aid of the charger at all times, it did offer a closer cut. Of course, I did have some razor burn issues at the very beginning of using this product, but I think you get that with any new electric razor

reviews.title: end gashes and razor bumps
reviews.text:
I abandoned my electric (a Braun) after a considerably difficult, protracted experience and returned to bald shaving. I tried it all--sensitive skin products, shaving in the shower, every day, every other day, after the shower, on and on ..... I was lucky if once a week I didn't have blood trickling down my face or patches of abrasion.
The 7885XL has changed my shaving life! I'm no connoisseur of electrics, but I can report that right out of the box it gave me a smooth beautiful shave--after four weeks I haven't had a single pinch or cut. And I can shave with casual disregard--twice a day, every other day, dry-and-on-the-fly, whenever. Yeah I may have to give patches--like my neck--special attention, but every time I'm done in 3-4 minutes, and it looks perfect & feels smooth. I could not be happier.

reviews.title: Good buy
reviews.text:
This doesn't take any longer to shave than older Norelcos, but if you take your time, your shave will be closer then you could ever get before.
Love this shaver and the exact time remaining display.

reviews.title: dependable shaver...
reviews.text:
this is my first time with electric shaver...its been 17

months now and i have nothing to complain...i use it 7 days a

week...initially the battery charge was for 50+mins but

now it is usually around 43, still sufficient...

the only thing that maybe of concern is the performance

if shaving after a gap of more than 2 days, it isnt easy,

but the same holds true for the regular razor blade shave too...

cleaning is very easy. also the trimmer is real handy...

overall i'm completely satisfied

reviews.title: Better than a Braun
reviews.text: I recently tried out the Braun Synchro System, thinking it would be great to wake up every morning with a clean, well-oiled shaver.  Although certain areas of my face were easy to cut, I just couldn't get the shaver to  get the short hairs on my chin and neck.  I would spend at least five minutes on those areas alone.  Thinking it was just a face thing and that, eventually my skin would adjust, I carried on past the trial period until well over a month of use.  Finally, I gave up, took the shaver back, and tried the Norelco Quadra.  What a difference!  The Quadra took to my skin immediately and within two days, I was getting almost every hair that the BSS wasn't.  So now I'm a faithful Norelco fan.  Sometimes we fall in love with technology at the expense of the quality of the shave.  I learned my lesson.

reviews.title: Recommend. Plan to continue to purchase
reviews.text: Have ordered this kit twice recently as well as other face products from this line.  Seems to work great. Have sensitive facial skin and doesn't cause irritation.

reviews.title: This is a great product. It leaves my dry delicate skin feeling ...
reviews.text: This is a great product. It leaves my dry delicate skin feeling a little dry but does not irritate. It does a great job of tightening and exfoliating. I highly recommend this product.

reviews.title: Nothing
reviews.text: I noticed no difference in my skin after using for 3 days.  I will not be purchasing this product again.

reviews.title: Great product! Detailed review and pics.
reviews.text:
This product basically asks you to take a three-day vacation from your normal skin-care routine and only use the contents of a total of nine pretty small (0.05 ounce each) foil packets. You follow the three step process each night for three nights then only use a wet cloth to "wash" your face in the morning.

Because this product makes great promises in a VERY short period of time, (and for $20 for a small amount of product, let's be honest!) and because I read reviews before I make purchases, and because I was able to purchase this product at a discount in exchange for evaluation and my HONEST review, I have swallowed my pride (uhm, ego??!!??) and am not only going to share the process for this product but also, several of my "naked face" photos - before, during and after. This way you, my fellow Amazon buyer, may not only read my opinion BUT also decide for yourself how much difference this product made in at least this instance for me.

BASICS- I am a woman, former Marine and (ugh!) a smoker. I will be turning 50 in June 2016 and although I have (so far) not found any actual wrinkles, other than a couple of minor horizontal lines on my forehead (thanks Dad & grandma for the genetics!), I DID have fairly prominent acne from about 13 until after 30 (& even a couple now!) leaving scars mostly on my cheeks and chin. I also was a serious sun worshipper, getting as brown as I possibly could, including tanning beds, outdoor activities, the Marine Corps and beach/pool lounging (anyone my age group remember baby oil and iodine? The worst thing you can do for your skin?? Yep! That was me!). It honestly is a miracle I don't look like a raisin. I do have discoloration & brown patches all over my face. I stopped actively tanning just after my 40th birthday and now always wear sunscreen at least in my facial moisturizer. I do have a pretty good routine now because I want to try to preserve what I have and fight Mother Nature and gravity as much as possible, but my face needs some work.
ALL PHOTOS WERE TAKEN AT APPROX THE SAME TIME, IN THE SAME PLACE WITH CAMERA AT APPROX THE SAME DISTANCE FROM MY FACE (too close!!) AND IN THE SAME LIGHTING. NO FILTERS OR PHOTOSHOP USED.
BEFORE PICTURE- The "before" photo,
(hopefully these will show up in the proper order but in it I am wearing a grey t-shirt with black "squares" and a pony tail) shows the brown spots, scarring and general lack of "glow". This was the night I began the process.

THE PROCESS- Three steps/Three days
STEP ONE "Gentle Prep Cleanser"- The cleanser is a pearly white color (see photograph) The directions say it removes makeup, but as you can tell from my "before" pic, I did remove my makeup with a makeup remover cloth. I did this for two reason: 1- Because I didn't trust that the small amount of cleanser would actually remove makeup and clean enough and 2- Because I wanted naked skin to evaluate the cleanser itself. I need not have worried. The cleanser cleans the skin and prepares it for the "peel" product at step two. The cleanser was very pleasant "clean" smelling and although it foamed very little (see photo of this step) it amazingly did a great job of further cleansing the skin and removing every trace of makeup (I know this because I used a white wash cloth to rinse my face - no I didn't photograph the dirty cloth!). My skin felt quite clean, but not "squeaky/stripped", which is good, IMO.
STEP TWO- "Non-Irritating Pro Peel". This is a clear gel (see pic) which had a slight astringent smell to it. This is applied to the now clean and towel dries skin using fingertips. The directions say to keep it on for 5 minutes, or 2-3 for sensitive skin rinse them pat dry. I felt no tingling or burning at all, just the alcohol smell which is no big deal. This product does not dry like a mask and it doesn't turn white like some chemical peels are supposed to do. In fact, on the second night, I got distracted and left this on for over 10 minutes and still had no discomfort at all. When rinsing, be careful to avoid the eyes. After this step, my skin did feel kind of tight but not in an uncomfortable way at all!
STEP THREE- "Radiance Renewing Deep Moisturizer"- OH MY GOODNESS!!! I absolutely LOVE LOVE LOVE this moisturizer!!! I am going to try finding this alone for use as a night cream. As you can see from the photos of the "dots" of moisturizer on my face, there was not a lot of the cream in the packet & I thought it would not be enough (I ALWAYS treat my neck all around and my chest area with the same process as my face!) but - again - I was wrong to be worried. This moisturizer feels so very good! I honestly wanted to use it all over. When I find the moisturizer alone, I will edit this review and add the name of the product! This cream was the perfect way to end the process.

All three steps took less than 15 minutes per night.

The "after" photo was taken this morning because I wanted to allow the third night's process to fully work, so the lighting is a bit different. If anything, the daylight is harsher than the night photos and I took this picture even closer so I could really see.

RESULTS- It is usually difficult to be objective about your own appearance. At least it is for me. I look at this face each day and see all my faults. However, I honestly can say that I have noticed smaller pore appearance and brighter, smoother skin. Putting on my makeup this morning was so much easier and to my great surprise, the makeup actually lasted well the entire day without the blotchy, tired appearance I usually find by the end of the day (the photo of me with makeup shows that my hair has definitely fallen and my eyes look tired but the foundation held up pretty well!). I did do an informal survey of a few friends, texting only the before and after photos for their candid opinions. The feedback was quite good! I am an "average looking" woman, and this product did not make me suddenly a Cover Girl, but in my honest opinion, this product is a clear winner! At the $19.99 price point, I am definitely going to be repeating this "Three-Day Vacation" again... Perhaps up to four times a year!

I do enjoy testing products and sharing my experience, and hope the information I share helps other Amazon customers decide if a product is right for them, so if you find my review at all helpful (either good or bad for the product) please let me know by selecting "yes" in response to the question "was this review helpful?" At the bottom of this review. Also, please feel free to ask me any additional questions you may have. I am happy to help as much as I can!

reviews.title: Great addition to any skincare routine!
reviews.text: As someone who fully understands the complexity of an individual's skin, I am a fan of introducing my clients and students to a variety of skincare brands in order to diversify the skincare routine. However, our skin is prone to "acting out" or reacting to internal changes (stress, diet, hormones) as well as the external environment (pollution, weather). Thus, I have been keen to find to a product that can get the skin back in order, can calm down redness and irritation, and will provide actual long term change. I tried this product out for 3 days, and have since recommended it to dozens of clients and students, as well as make up artists. I found that each pack of 3 steps lasted me more than a day, and so this is a very good value because you can make this last a while. Immediately before and after using these 3 steps for 3 days, I used a gentle skincare routine that focused on oils and gentle exfoliation. Try to abstain from retinols and other heavy duty chemicals for several days before and after using this, and always wear SPF. After 3 day my skin lost a lot of the dryness and gray-ness that it has been carrying for a while. I noticed no change in acne, and my skin looked supple and healthy, especially around the mouth. There was also a loss of unwanted texture, and a slight reduction in redness and post acne scarring. I highly recommend this treatment as a once-a-month type procedure if you are serious about taking care of your skin. I received this product at a discounted rate in exchange for an honest review and will continue to recommend it during my seminars and will purchase more.

reviews.title: So soft & smooth after one use!
reviews.text: After using it the first night I could feel the difference in how smooth my skin felt.  And it has remained smooth days after I finished the three-day pack.  Would certainly purchase other MD Complete products based on this result.

reviews.title: and loved the results the first day
reviews.text: I just bought the cleanser, wrinkle reducer, moisturizer and 3 day Booster Pack.  I started with the 3 Day Pack...and loved the results the first day...so much I couldn't wait to do it again the next night.  The second morning I noticed a dryness around my mouth that as the day went on developed into a very chapped, red and cracked skin fold ( it caused some swelling which was not attractive).  That evening I did the peel but avoided the corners of my mouth (which I should have avoided in the first place) and was very happy with the final outcome...although the corners of my mouth are still chapped so I will be avoiding that area when I begin the Retinol cream tonight.  I am very hopeful that the rest of the regime will be what my skin needs.  I am 58 and a full time student in a Nursing School Program...so I feel and look exhausted..I am hoping to recapture some healthy glow...I will review the rest of the regime after I give it 30 days.

reviews.title: I love this product
reviews.text: I love this product.  It is a 3 step face peel product.  The first time I used it- my tface burned a little but after that it was ok.  I like that it really deep cleans my pores.  I left it on for about 10 mins.  Of course it's not like getting a deep peel at the Dr.s  but it will keep your face very smooth and clean.  I think that they are discounting this product though.

reviews.title: Good little kit
reviews.text: Nice booster kit

reviews.title: My skin is so soft and it definitely helps make fine lines disappear
reviews.text: I have been using this product for over a year.  My skin is so soft and it definitely helps make fine lines disappear.

reviews.title: Tighten and smooth
reviews.text: I like this booster pack. its like the vitamin c serum and wrinkle remedy in concentration. My results were my skin felt firmer and pores smaller.

reviews.title: Love this product
reviews.text: Love this product. Easy to use. Skin really looks brighter after use. I usually get complements after the first or second day.

reviews.title: A great skin product line from a Doctor I personally trust!
reviews.text: Excellent product.  Made my skin so much smoother and fresh.  Gentle yet very effective, will buy again.

reviews.title: Outstanding product!
reviews.text: Absolutely met all my expectations and more using MD Complete Booster Pack Anti-Aging by Dr. Zelickson.  Many of my friends often compliment me on how my face looks.....making me look younger than I am.  I have been using this product for several years and plan on doing so for along long time!

reviews.title: Tamara Crane 03/07/2016
reviews.text:
Ok, this is a great simple peel, but very light (at least for my skin). It does exactly as it states and your skin is definitely replenished, with a wonderful glow. Just remember, this is just a booster.....so now that I've used it, it will definitely work a lot better for a boost in-between my more intense peels. Like I mentioned though it's a very subtle peeling agent, so don't expect dramatic effects after the 3days, expect a glow of replenishment. The instructions are easy to follow, and as for a tingling sensation, it will vary for everyone. I felt a slight tingling the 1st night, but the 2nd and 3rd, I honestly forgot it was on my face. I received this product at a discounted price for my honest review and opinion of the product. I hope this helps for some who have a higher tolerance, like me.
Thank you for letting me test your product.

reviews.title: I would have loved to get more for a little less.
reviews.text:
Okay, yes I really liked this product. The first day's treatment made my face glow. The second and third day I guess I was getting used to looking great so my enthusiasm waned a bit. Actually, I think I saw such a difference the first time I used this that the other two days just couldn't compare. So, my idea would be to offer the product as a one time use for a special occasion or weekly application. But it would need to have a little more product of each of the three components. I did not think the packets contained enough product to justify the expense or make it feel luxurious. The 3 day booster at $19.97 is expensive for such a small amount of product I think. Therefore I graded 4 stars rather than 5 as I would have otherwise.
I was supplied a discounted product to try in exchange for an honest review. I depend on the reviews and ratings of others to help me make purchasing decisions

reviews.title: It wasted my money to try this product
reviews.text: No such thing as described. It wasted my money to try this product.

reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.text:
Terrific list of ingredients, this stuff is the real deal. I could feel immediately on application that this product contained Retinol as advertised and not a watered down 'serum' variation. Remember to moisturize after. This is a terrific product to use as your Retinol go to.

I got a lot of info from Marie Claire article by Katie Thomas 'Retinol - the only ingredient that will reduce wrinkles'

Your skin’s collagen level starts to deplete at age 25.
Retinol helps with skin elasticity, wrinkles/laugh lines and pigmentation aka age spots.
Retinol is a derivative of vitamin A.
Start low ( 0.3%) increase to 0.5%. 1% is best but only available with a prescription.
When you first try your skin may get dry, red & flaky.
Only use twice a week, gradually increase to daily as desired.
Apply a pea-sized amount at first and increase amount and % as negative side effects decrease.
Use anytime day or night, just use sunscreen after if going outdoors.
Anywhere you see signs of aging is a good place to apply, face, arms, hands, legs, neck.
Avoid the skin around your eyes as it is too delicate.

Aqua/Water/Eau,
Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride (emollient/natural moisturizing factor/replenishment/texture),
Glycerin (hydration/natural moisturizing factor/replenishment),
Dimethicone (conditioner/silicone),
Glyceryl Stearate (emollient/emulsifier),
PEG-100 Stearate (emulsifier),
Polysorbate 20 (emulsifier),
***Retinol (age delay/brightener),
Phenoxyethanol (preservative),
Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer (hydration/replenishment/restoration),
Carbomer (thickener),
Cetearyl Alcohol (thickener),
Caprylyl Glycol (conditioner/emollient/preservative),
Ceteareth-20 (emollient/emulsifier),
Ethylhexylglycerin (preservative),
BHT (preservative), Allantoin (sooth),
Disodium EDTA (pH buffer/preservative),
Hexylene Glycol (preservative),
Sodium Hydroxide (pH adjuster),
Pentylene Glycol (solvent),
BHA (preservative),
1,2-Hexanediol (preservative),
Beta-Glucan (antioxidant/sooth),
Myristoyl Pentapeptide-8 (age delay)

reviews.title: I do really like this one, slightly more expensive than others in this class
reviews.text:
This is a nice Retinol cream that is a little expensive considering it contains only 0.5% retinol. I have others that contain 3 times as much and cost half of this one. One word of caution, please keep in mind when you use products containing retinol to use sun blocker and to apply them in the evening since these can make your skin sensitive to sun light.

This serum comes in a stunning little plastic squeeze tube looking like upside down pump dispenser. It has a pleasant and mild scent that I really like.

The serum is lightweight and absorbs quickly into your skin. There is some residue for a brief while but that goes away pretty quickly. It leaves you with nice soft skin that is smooth and feels great. All in all I really do like this even though it is a bit more on the expensive side for what you get. My skin does like it though and with the lower concentration of the retinol this may work better for people who are more sensitive to retinol.

reviews.title: This is a company that cares about their clients
reviews.text: When I opened up the box, the tube of MBA was right there. It is not a jar, but a tube that you pump the product out. The circular on the inside advises newbies to use one pump all over the face and avoiding the eyes, mouth and neck areas. You start out twice a week or every three days. After two or three weeks, the skin adjusts to the usage and you can start using more often. They also included a trial size of Double Major, which is great, since you will probably have dead skin cells start to flake off.

reviews.title: not that strong
reviews.text: This is a fairly weak retinol formula. I have several on my shelf and I can tell from using them which ones are stronger. This isn't the worst I have tried but I would place it in the middle range. It's rather expensive for what you get. It's also quite thin and runny.

reviews.title: Good for a gentle exfoliation or those with already good skin.
reviews.text:
If you need a mild chemical exfoliant and want to cut out products from your routine, this might be the serum for you. It's a weaker concentration of retinol but with that trade, you get a less harsh serum that still keeps your skin hydrated and has a lesser chance of causing irritation. You can most likely use this as a serum/cream combo. Likely a big bonus for those with sensitive skin.

I've taken a break from my usual Tretinoin cream due to the extreme sun in my area but I do still need to keep up with chemical exfoliants and retinol has the added bonus of fighting acne like Tret does. My skin is definitely smoothing out again and looks more even, but it's a very very subtle improvement so I'm not sure if those that have significant texture issues or need a lot of brightening or fading will be pleased with this as it most likely would take a while to see visible results.

It's not a bad product but with such a low retinol percentage it is quite expensive compared to other retinol serums/creams on the market and also comes in a small bottle. I do like it but I don't think it's something I'd purchase myself if only because of that.

reviews.title: Great Retinol Serum For Sensitive Skin
reviews.text: The Serum by Higher Education is very moisturizing and delicate on the skin. The white cream feels silky, unlike most serums and really helps brighten skins appearance. My skin is sensitive and this doesn’t irritate or hurt my skin at all, instead it makes my skin feel soft and moist. This serum is a great investment and worth the price if you want healthy, moisturized, soft, clear skin.

reviews.title: Has kept my skin clear
reviews.text: I've been using different brands of retinol serum and I really like how this one smooths over my skin, which it does better than others I've used. It's a tube and pump combination and about two pumps is plenty to cover my face and neck. The consistency is somewhere between a gel and a slight cream. I've had no adverse reactions and I find that it leaves my face feeling moisturized without any clogging feeling. Also, there's no scent that I can pick up.

reviews.title: expires in less than 2 months
reviews.text:
Received July 23, 2020.

I use this 2x a week in the evenings after applying hyaluronic serum. I've experienced no allergic reaction. I chose this because the retinol concentration (0.5%) isn't too high.

This is the second Higher Education skin care product I've purchased that has a very short expiration period - expires in less than 2 months (Sep 2020). The first was the "Grinding Away skin polishing scrub" that expires in less than 3 months (Oct 2020).

I'm really annoyed that Higher Education is stocking Amazon with old products. If I were paying full price for this, I would expect to receive "fresh" products - with at least a year before expiration. If I were purchasing this from a physical store, I'd give this back to the counter lady and just look for something else.

One star for sending me an old product. Amazon, stop selling old products! Customers who paid full price for this - you should be returning the product.

reviews.title: Expires too soon!!
reviews.text: I have never written a bad review but this is warranted. I am giving this ONE star simply because the product expires soon!! There’s no way you can use this nightly serum in 2 months from the date of purchase. I purchased this in July and it will expire already in September. Also just remember this is not medical grade retinol meaning there is no way they can actually verify the quantity or potency of retinol.

reviews.title: Very short expiration date, but it seems to work.  Weird tube / pump.
reviews.text:
The cream seems to work to brighten and even out skin tone without drying the skin. I actually like the results. However, the tube I received in July has a September expiration date. Seems too soon. I'd rate it higher if it had a longer expiration term.

I am also a bit puzzled by the packaging. It looks like a tube, but it has a pump top. Not sure why it is packaged this way.

reviews.title: MBA Night Renewing Serum Helps Reduce the Appearance of Wrinkles Around My Eyes!
reviews.text:
No, I'm not vain, but I don't won't to look old before my time. I'm a fifty-six-year-old Caucasian male who just retired after 34 years of government service. Fortunately, I still look as though I am in my forties. What is my secret? Simple. I don't smoke, I don't lay out in the sun, I drink plenty of water, and I moisturize, moisturize, moisturize. Lately, I've also learned the importance of using products, such as facial serums, that have retinol, which reduces wrinkles around the eyes. I don't believe in plastic surgery but I do strongly believe in using topical products that reduce the appearance of aging. Everyone I personally know who has had plastic surgery ended up looking like a clown with a lopsided mouth and huge, shocked eyes.

I've been using several Higher Education products as part of a facial cleansing and healing regimen. The products that I've been using are the NO BRAINER Gentle Skin Cleanser and the GOAL DIGGER Moisturizing Cucumber Crème. Both are excellent products; I am quite pleased with them. Thanks to them, my skin looks healthy and glowing. After I retired, I moved from Virginia to Tennessee and the change in environment caused my face to become badly dehydrated. I was waking up with dry, irritated patches of skin on my face that sometimes stung. Thankfully, with the help of the Higher Education products, I have been able to heal my skin and keep the dry patches away.

I also decided to use the MBA Night Renewing Serum from Higher Education as part of my nightly ritual. The MBA is to be used only at night. Also, the consumer is to use it only twice a week and gradually increase its usage as tolerated. Well, I've been using it every night for two weeks without any issues. However, I use it sparingly around my eyes. I've noticed a difference in the three weeks that I have been using it. Fewer wrinkles and smoother skin. The serum is light-green and doesn't have a distinguishable scent. It burns slightly when I put it on but the burning sensation quickly goes away. I use the MBA after applying my GOAL DIGGER, which moisturizes my skin, preparing it for the MBA. Like I stated earlier, I've been using the MBA for three weeks and I haven't experienced any ill side effects or allergic reactions such as acne, hives, blisters, or red, swollen skin.

The serum arrived in a 1.7 fluid-ounce pump bottle with a plastic, transparent cap. For some unknown reason, I keep forgetting that the tube has a pump. I want to squeeze the tube as though it were toothpaste. The cap prevents accidental leakage and spillage. I think it would be difficult to tamper with the tube. The tube arrived inside a sturdy, cardboard box, which contained company literature. The MBA is a good product. My only issue with it is the expiration date. An expiration date is provided on the back of the tube's crimp and on the bottom of the box. It reads as follows: EXP 09/20. This is not a good date, especially for a product that will probably be in usage for three to four months, if used sparingly. I expected it have about the same expiration date as the one I found for the NO BRAINER Gentle Skin Cleanser and the GOAL DIGGER Moisturizing Cucumber Crème, which is 09/21.

I wonder if Higher Education decided that giving products that would soon expire to reviewers from the Amazon Vine Program was a good means for disposing of aging products. Whenever I review a food or hygiene product, I always look for an expiration date. Not all hygiene products have expiration dates. I think they all should in order to let consumers know whether or not they are receiving safe products with fresh ingredients. I hope that customers who actually purchase the MBA Night Renewing Serum will obtain a product with a better expiration date. Nevertheless, I believe that MBA is good for lessening the appearance of wrinkles. MBA won't remove them completely. I don't think anything will. However, MBA will help to minimize them.

reviews.title: Retinol for Beginners
reviews.text:
I would have given this 4 stars if it was about $10-$15 cheaper. It's a good product, yes. but it's not great. it's a $25 serum, not a $40 serum. The product just doesn't justify the price point.

That being said, It's a great 'introduction' to retinol for beginners. I began at 5% and my face was peeling for days after my first use. LOL.

I would certainly recommend this as an entry level serum or for younger skin that doesn't need much help yet... if it were cheaper.

reviews.title: Very nice gentle product
reviews.text: I've tried a few products from this brand and I've really liked them. Unfortunately this one and one other product I received expire in less than two months from when I recieved them. I have pretty nice skin because I take very good care of it. So for me 0.5% retinol is just right. It doesn't irritate my skin or dry it out which is always a concern of mine when I use any type of retinol product. This is a nice serum consistency. My skin looks healthier and brighter. It didn't cause me any redness, which can be a problem for me, nor did it cause any irritation. I recommend giving this a try along with some of their other products. Just keep in mind when ordering that a couple of products I recieved to review were very close to expiration.

reviews.title: My skin feels good
reviews.text: This Shiny Leaf combo of skin care helps my skin feel softer and looking fresh.  I have very sensitive skin and have not experienced any irritation or breakouts.  The consistency is thicker, kind of gel or creamy depending on the item.  This helps transfer to your skin without it slipping through your fingers.  I use 1-2 pumps for my face and neck, moving down to my chest if any is left.  I think they add good ingredients to my skin.  I follow up with a moisturizing night cream.  I am 65 and think these serums help.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text: My skin feels moisturized, tight, and soft after using these. I love the pumps. These kind of products usually have droppers and these work so much better. I think the price for these is really great also. I like how easy the instructions are.

reviews.title: Great serum set with easy application
reviews.text: I like this set of facial serums. The packaging of the product makes all the difference. These are glass bottles but instead of the annoying dropper cap, these serums are a gel-like consistency that is pumped out instead. No dripping serums wasted. It does have a bland scent which I'm not too crazy about but that's just from smelling it from the bottle. I can't smell it when it's on my face which is good. The serums all absorb quickly into my skin and they don't feel greasy. I haven't experienced any adverse skin reaction. After application I can see my skin glowing, when I first used it my skin became a little pink but it went away quickly. It was probably just my skin adjusting to the retinol and it doesn't happen anymore since I've been using it for a few nights now. I'm pretty happy with this set and it's reasonably priced if you're just starting to venture into the anti-aging skin care regimen.

reviews.title: A Triple Threat
reviews.text:
I've used these 3 serums in combination for a few years now. They are great for helping repair skin damaged from the sun or other elements. This combination is good for oily or dry skin, and when used with a good toner and moisturizer, can be a great daily skin regiment.

I use the vitamin c immediately after washing and toning my face. I rub it all over my face, chin and neck. I usually wait a few minutes before using the retinol serum followed by the hyaluronic acid. After I'm done, I finish with a good moisturizer to mitigate any dryness that may occur. My skin is usually oily, so I use a moisturizer with a good sun screen and oil controller.

The results are great, and once you start noticing the improvements you won't stray from the process. It's awesome to find great products at a good price. The Shiny Leaf brand delivers.

reviews.title: Great value
reviews.text: I was mainly interested in using this because it was such a great value and seemed interesting and for the price it's definitely worth trying. You get three products with this set three full sized bottles of retinol, hyaluronic acid, and vitamin c each of these are essential for your skin and are a great addition to any skin acne routine so you can use everything included. The serums themselves are of a decent quality and seem comparable to other serums but at times they do feel somewhat cheaper this is especially apparent when you feel the plastic bottles and in my case when I need to apply more product than I used to in order to reach all the areas of my face I like to cover but even with these issues it's still worth it you can spend the entire cost of this set on just one of these serums if you go with certain brands so there's definitely a value here and if you're ok getting a cheaper feeling bottle and having to use a little more product this is a great way to save money in all honesty I've tried lots of serums and they all seem to be almost identical in terms of performance so as long as you don't get a adverse reaction from a specific brand you can use whichever brand you like.

reviews.title: Excellent product / 10/10
reviews.text:
Absolutely amazing tonight was my first time trying them and they’re great
The after feel of your face is amazing very smooth and moisturizing I will continue using these products and I will be purchasing again when I’m out

reviews.title: It irritated my skin
reviews.text:
Updated review: I wanted to come back and update this after more use. One of these or all three of them have really irritated my skin for some reason. I don't know why, but maybe I'm allergic to something in them? It has really dried out my face to a painful extent, and my skin is red and flaky. I don't even know how to fix my skin from this because it hurts to put any products on it. I wish I knew more about skincare for this exact reason, but hopefully my skin recovers after a few days of not using this product.

Original review: I am definitely a beginner when it comes to skincare, so I like this pack because it tells you exactly how to use the products (bottle 1 goes on in the morning, and you use bottles 2 and 3 at night). That makes it a really easy way for me to start getting acquainted with skincare. All three bottles have a pump top, and the texture of the liquid is kind of thicker than I thought it would be, like aloe or shampoo instead of oil or water consistency. That makes it easier to get it from the bottle to my face, at least for me. Now, the smells of these products are very strange/slightly on the unpleasant side. Like I would be a lot more happy putting a better-smelling or scent-free product on my face than these. I can't exactly describe what the smell is, but it's just something I don't like. Oh well. It takes about four pumps of each one to cover my face entirely, and it does leave my skin feeling refreshed and smooth. They all dry without any greasy or sticky feeling, which is a huge plus. I can't exactly be sure if they're working, since I'm not a skincare expert, but they definitely feel hydrating at the very least.

reviews.title: A great find!
reviews.text: Love it. This is my new regime. Scent free, yet moisturizing.

reviews.title: Generous size, not sure about concentration
reviews.text: These serums feel good on my skin and I have also been mixing them with my moisturizer. I’m not sure about the concentration of their active ingredients, and it does not say anything specific on the box or the bottle. I know that I have a different vitamin C serum that consistently creates a warming sensation on my skin, but this serum does not do that so it must be below 12%.

reviews.title: I can already see a difference in my skin!
reviews.text:
This is my first adventure with these kind of skincare products. I had heard of retinol and hyaluronic acid and always thought they would be extremely expensive. Theses products are at a great price. I like that they are made in the USA, are paraben, sulfate, and chemical free - and are never tested on animals.

Shiny Leaf Skin Pro Series:
1. Vitamin C Serum
2. Retinol Serum
3. Hyaluronic Acid Serum

I have very sensitive skin and these serums do not bother my skin at all. I really like the way they go on my skin and how they leave my skin looking. The scent is very mild and pleasant. My daughter told me she can already see the difference in my skin!

I use them the way my daughter told me to use them.

First I wash my face with a mild skin cleanser, then I apply the Retinol Serum first, then the Hyaluronic Acid Serum second, then the vitamin C Serum third. Layering them on top of each other. In the morning I wash them off and then apply the Hyaluronic Acid Serum first, and then the Vitamin C Serum second - I do not use the Retinol Serum in the morning. I only use a small amount of each and rub it into my face, neck, and upper chest. Then continue to do my morning routine of my moisturizer that has sunscreen in it, and my foundation makeup that has sun screen in it, and the rest of my make up. My daughter told me to be sure to wear sunscreen when I go outside because the retinol can make my skin sensitive to the sun. I did test the products on a small area of my skin one night and one morning to make sure I did not have any allergies to the serums.

When I first started using the serums it took me a few pumps to get the serum out of the bottle which is typical of a never used product. I suggest when you push the pump each time to hold it at an angle toward your finger tip so the product does not over squirt and get wasted.

I plan to continue using these serums and will purchase more.

I will update if there are any changes.

reviews.title: Quite happy with the whole set
reviews.text: I’m happy with this Shiny Leaf set of skin care serums. I’m familiar with hyaluronic acid as I use it regularly. This serum is a bit thicker than the one I used previously. I can’t say that I prefer either. It’s just different. I always apply hyaluronic acid when my face is a bit wet. Otherwise it can be drying. It does make sense to me that the directions indicate that the retinol should be used at the same time as the hyaluronic acid. Retinol can be drying on its own. Especially if you’re not used to it. I do not find this one to be drying for the most part. I am applying a moisturizer on top of both serums before bed and a moisturizer with spf on top of the vitamin c serum in the morning. Back to the retinol… I did try it on my eyelids once (being cautious not to get it in my eyes). I did get quite a dry patch on one eyelid. I do occasionally get eczema on that eyelid specifically, so there’s some sort of issue there anyway. I can’t blame the serum, but I would suggest being cautious and starting slowly if you have any skin sensitivities. The vitamin C serum goes on nicely, but this one irritates my skin for a bit after application. I get very small bumps on my face similar to what I get on my arms when I do yard work. It will itch and feel warm for a bit as well. Since it cleared up without issue the first time I tried it, I’ve continued to use it. This is my first experience with vitamin C on my skin, so I cannot speak to whether or not I have this reaction with all such serums. I suspect that I would, though. Compared to other serums I’ve purchased this set seems to be a good value for the money. I like the pump and the amount dispensed with each pump is great. The bottles are glass rather than plastic. I appreciate that as well.

reviews.title: Fabulous
reviews.text: I’ve used several brands of these, all of which were fine and do the job, making my face smoother, more youthful, more even skin tones.  This particular brand is the best I’ve used.  The reason is the viscosity.  All the other brands I’ve tried were watery and dripped down my face when I apply.  This brand is thicker, kind of an aloe Vera gel consistency which I really like.  Not only that but for the same price, I get twice the product.  I will def buy this brand again.

reviews.title: Pleased!
reviews.text: My face feels smooth and hydrated.  Dryness is gone!

reviews.title: I recommend this as an affordable option.
reviews.text:
All the serums are thicker, almost gel like, compared to what I'm used to. They all absorb quickly and smoothly and should work well under makeup.

The vitamin C is clear and has almost no scent except for maybe the faintest hint of sweet almond. I wouldn't call it "ultra-brightening", but it produces an average glow.

The hyaluronic is cloudy, has a slight "not fun" smell, and didn't feel like it moisturized.

The retinol is beige and has a slight alcohol smell in the bottle but isn't noticeable when applying. It didn't feel like it moisturized either. It made me itchy, which is surprising because I regularly use concentrated retinol. I

These don't stand out as the best serums I've used, but they're probably the least expensive for average results. Definitely make sure to adjust to the retinol.

reviews.title: just started using it
reviews.text: I received this product a while ago and just started to use it a few days ago. It is very nice to apply but not a lot of moisturizing effect. The scent is very subtle. I have used other products like this one with great results for my skin. I have hopes that it will be great

reviews.title: Great set
reviews.text: This serum set is a great product and has made my skin smooth, younger looking and overall, much healthier. I will note though, that if you're not used to using retinol, do your research and use it sparingly. It definitely can cause your skin to be dry and flaky at first. I only use it every 3 days or so with this regime and make sure to use moisturizer after I finish applying the serums. I use these serums at night and wash my face gently in the morning and I also use sunscreen if I go out after using retinol as it can cause sunburn. Overall, the benefits of retinol and all of these serums are extremely positive.

reviews.title: Very nice trio for taking care of my skin
reviews.text:
I'm using the Vitamin C Serum, in the morning, after cleansing my face and before I apply sun screen. In the evening I cleanse my face and then apply Retinol Serum and then the Hyaluronic Acid Serum. I've had no adverse effects. I'm always happy when I can do things to protect and repair my skin because I spend a lot of time outdoors and I'm not young anymore. I do wear hats and sun protection clothing but we have temps over 100 and high, hot winds. Along with making sure to drink fluids, this is another way I can help my skin.

I didn't even notice a scent until I made an effort to do so. It's not bad, it's not good, it's just there and I don't think it is very noticeable at all.

reviews.title: This so far is working.  I am 68 and getting compliments for the 1st time.
reviews.text: So easy to use.  My skin is brighter, clearer and softer.

reviews.title: Bakuchiol what!?? Say HELLO to hydrated  and supple skin…
reviews.text:
I guess I’m not on top of the latest skin treatments because I admit I never heard of Bakuchiol until I tried this cream. Bakuchiol is a natural plant-based extract that works similarly to topical retinol for anti-aging effects by reducing the appearance of wrinkles and improving the firmness in skin. It is a gentler alternative to retinol, which is perfect for someone like me because I have sensitive skin.

This Korean beauty product arrived nicely packaged. From the outside of the jar, I thought this cream was a pale yellow, but it’s actually white. I used a small dollop and it was enough to cover my entire face and neck area too. The texture is light and creamy. There is no scent and it goes on clear after gently rubbing it in. I have combination to oily, sensitive skin and this did not feel greasy or make my face breakout, which is a huge plus!

I’ve been using this Bakuchiol cream in combination with a hyaluronic acid serum for only one week and the results are just AMAZING! My skin looks brighter and hydrated, and feels so nice and supple! Although I haven’t seen my wrinkles improve yet, I will happily continue using this product because the results so far have been very positive and promising! You dear Bakuchiol cream have earned a coveted spot on my limited bathroom counter space!

reviews.title: I have a skin condition, I know this won't solve but feels good.
reviews.text: I have this skin condition that is like having dandruff on any skin surface above the neck, so when I got this I immediately put it on, it was cold, and felt good going on stung just a little because of my skin condition, but I am hopeful it will at least help a little it has for sure took away a bit of the pain when rubbing the skin slightly after applying it feels like most lotions do shortly after applying lol. So unless I get a weird rash or something I feel I will most likely use this daily after my shower/face washing. Scared me a little when half the box was Chinese other half English, yet the Chinese have known their medicinal herbs etc for hundreds or more years, so no reason to doubt.

reviews.title: Moisturizing
reviews.text: It is very moisturizing. I like that there's no fragrance. Great for winter.

reviews.title: Nice alternative to retinol👍⭐️
reviews.text:
Lightweight and has no fragrance. I have sensitive skin and had no irritation when using this product. It can be used in the AM and PM because there’s no sensitivity to the sun.

I cleaned my face, used toner, and then applied the cream. I only used a fingertip size to cover my face and neck.

Retinol products tend to irritate my skin, and having this as an alternative has done wonders for my skin. It keeps my skin moisturized.

EXPIRATION DATE: 10/25/2024
I always check for expiration dates before using, ESPECIALLY with any facial/body products. This expiration date tells me the product hasn’t been sitting somewhere but has been manufactured recently for distribution.

DISCLAIMER:
📝Yes, I am a VINE 🗣VOICE member. EVERY product that I review, I selected. The product or item is ALWAYS something that is needed and now is being used in my Home🏡 , Yard 🌱🍃, by my FAMILY 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦& Pets🦮🐾🐈‍⬛, or JUST BY ME 💁🏻‍♀️.

reviews.title: Superb Bakuchiol cream
reviews.text:
This is a lovely Bakuchiol cream. Bakuchiol is a fantastic natural alternative to retinol. It’s safe for sensitive skin and pregnant women! It’s not photosensitive (unlike retinol) so it can be used both am and pm. The packaging of this product feels simple yet luxurious. The glass jar packaging reminds me a lot of herbivore products.

This cream is lovely to use. It is a thicker heavier cream so, it does take a little longer to absorb. It’s non greasy and although it is a thick cream it does feel light weight once applied. As the cream absorbs it does give a nice radiant dewy glow to my skin tone. I can easily see how this cream would help with anti aging and fine lines with repeated daily use. I’ve been using this cream for about almost a week now and I love seeing my skin so well nourished and hydrated.

The ingredients in the cream are delightful. There is one ingredient that I always acknowledge. This cream does use a silicone ingredient polysilicone-34. I honestly don’t know much about this silicone ingredient and it might be the first product I have used that includes this particular silicone. This cream is gentle and effective even for sensitive skin but I do always acknowledge silicones in the ingredient list. In the week I have used this cream I have not noticed any close comedones, millia or any other adverse side effects from adding this cream to my skin care routines.

I love how deeply nourishing, refreshed and hydrating this cream is for my skin. Even though it takes a while to absorb when I use this cream in the mornings its still easy to apply my make up once this cream does absorb. I don’t wear foundation most days but occasionally I do wear a finishing powder to even out my skin tone if need or to help blend out my highlighter or blush. This cream doesn't sit heavy on my skin or interfere with my make up application or longevity.

I am very happy with this cream and would absolutely recommend it to those who have sensitive skin (as long as you tolerate silicone okay), or to those who prefer a retinol alternative that can still yield good results.

reviews.title: Great product
reviews.text: I love this product. It comes in a glass jar and there isn't any smell/scent to it (although I personally wouldn't mind if they added a bit fragrance). I first tried it on my hands and they felt so smooth and silky afterwards. I later used it on my feet and no skin irritation either. My face is pretty sensitive so I tried this cream on my face last and I was fine too.

reviews.title: Perfect for sensitive skin
reviews.text:
I have been using this cream every day for almost two weeks, and I have to say, that it’s been a while since a skin care product impressed me so much. I honestly love everything about this cream, including the environmentally conscious packaging.
My skin is mature, clear and combination ( on the sensitive side), and prone to dryness during colder months. I can’t tolerate prescription strength retinols, so this is a wonderful alternative.
When I put this cream on - the consistency is of a thicker lotion and fragrance free- I get that luxurious feeling of well being and comfort. My skin feels well moisturized, soft, but not greasy.
I have been using the product twice a day with zero side effects. No dryness or burning. During the day, I wear a serum foundation, and this cream makes my face feel very comfortable and fresh, my foundation not sliding off or pilling.
I truly look forward to using this cream on a daily basis.

reviews.title: Very lightweight but feels good
reviews.text: I keep coming back to this cream even though I have a few in my bathroom cabinet. I think I like it because it is a nongreasy formula, very lightweight, but it still makes you feel moisturized and refreshed. It adds a healthy glow to your skin. The ingredients seem very high quality and there are only 9 of them! The smell is very faint and lovely. Overall, a pleasure to use.

reviews.title: Beautiful, Gentle Cream - So Simple & So Nice - Great For Dry Skin & Sensitive Skin
reviews.text:
(Ingredients list is at the bottom of this review.)

I am really loving the TINAZANA Vegan Bakuchiol Comforting Cream (2.7oz / 80ml). Only 9 ingredients. It is so good. Truly comforting and non-irritating. Even when my skin is already in an irritated state.

This is a beautifully thick, rich, white moisturizing cream. Yet, somehow it feels decently lightweight and comfortable on the face and neck. Not lightweight like a gel cream, by any means. But comfortable. This cream glides onto the face and neck smoothly, and it soaks into the skin quickly; But it doesn't disappear. It leaves a lasting, soothing, comforting layer of moisture and hydration on the surface of the skin. It helps to lock-in the moisture and hydration from the previous steps in my skincare routine, and leave my skin feeling so lovely and comfortable.

The scent is extremely slight. It is herbal, but it's almost undetectable, unless you're really seeking after the scent.

The packaging is a beautiful yellow glass jar with a beige twist-off lid. It feels very nice in the hand, and the cream feels luxurious on the face. Much more expensive than it is. The 80ml size is excellent for the price.

The addition of gentle, anti-aging Bakuchiol is also a big win with zero irritation. Plus, Shea Butter for maximum moisture. I layer gentle, hydrating, soothing, calming toners and serums underneath this cream, and I get excellent glowy, dewy results.

I use this cream day and night, as I have dry, sensitive skin. I imagine many skin types might like this for day or night, especially during colder months.

If you have sensitive skin, and you cannot use most skincare products (like me), this is likely going to be for you.

Overall, I'm highly impressed. Why did it take me so long to find this? This cream is so beautifully simple. It's truly an amazing find for someone like me, who struggles with sensitive skin. I will definitely repurchase, and I'm looking forward to trying more simple, gentle products from Tinazana.

I highly recommend this cream to anyone and everyone.

INGREDIENTS:

Water, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter, Butylene Glycol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Polysilicone-34, Isononyl Isononanoate, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Ethyhexylglycerin, Bakuchiol.

reviews.text: This cream is very light and soothing. The active ingredient works in the same way as tretinoin but is not as harsh. This cream has no scent. I am using this on my forearms and hands to help fade sun spots.  It is a very good moisturizer and it feels light and soothing.

reviews.title: Nice!
reviews.text: The first thing I noticed when I opened this up was the cream is very light. It has no smell to speak of and the appearance & feel of cold cream. That’s where the similarity ends. When I applied this to my face at first it felt very thin and almost runny but as I rubbed it in it began to feel thick and almost sticky. It left my face looking shiny and greasy, and I was prepared to write a bad review. However in a few minutes time the cream was completely absorbed and left my face feeling moisturized and soooo soft! It has caused no irritation or redness to my skin at all. I’ve only been using it about a week so I can’t see any long term improvements yet but so far I’m really happy with this comforting cream.

reviews.text: This is a premium quality Korean skin care product. It gives my face a glow. Right after applying I can feel the cooling sensation when the molecules are going through my skin. I also like how Korean skin care products give very nice presentation with the glass jar.

reviews.title: Wonderful product
reviews.text:
This review is for TINAZANA Vegan Bakuchiol Comforting Cream Sensitive Dry Skin.

It's true, this product is wonderful for people with dry sensitive skin. I noticed a difference in the texture of my skin in just a few weeks, and it's non-irritating and completely moisturizing so it's been great for my dry sensitive skin. Also, it has a nice calming effect; great for those days when my skin is just a little overworked.

reviews.title: hydrating toner adds moisture and other nourishing ingredients.
reviews.text: Nice feeling moisture boost. Lightweight, nothing oily about it to me. My dry skin loves a good hydrating toner, and this fits that need. I pat some on all over face, neck and chest. Sometimes I do this a few times to saturate my skin. Then I follow up with other products (serum, moisturizer, and/or sunscreen). Ceramides help build and maintain a healthy skin barrier. Hyaluronic acid attracts moisture (but be sure to lock it in with a moisturizer or an occlusive ingredient). Sedum provides intense hydration & minerals, and vitamins to the skin. So lots of good stuff going on with this toner.

reviews.title: Very  nice essence, very toner like
reviews.text:
This is a very nice serum. It has sedum extract in it which is supposed to help adding hydration to the skin, which in turn adds firmness and elasticity. It has a low molecular hyaluronic acid which helps adding moisture to the skin and is meant for daily treatment of your face.

It has a mild fragrance only and is nice and fresh. I for one do like it quite a bit. This is more like a toner and you use it quite similarly to one. Pour some of it on a cotton pad and gently pad the essence onto your skin until it is fully absorbed.

I find it absorbs quickly into the skin. It feels a tad sticky at first but that quickly goes away. My skin does feel nicely moisturized when I use this so I do like this quite a bit. This is a lovely product.

reviews.title: I need the moisture
reviews.text: I use so many products that give me moisture. I think I am addicted to them. What I know, is that from using this Celimax product and another one that I use, my face keeps moisture in it and that helps keep wrinkles from showing (which is where the hyaluronic acid comes in)

reviews.title: Left my face shiny and greasy
reviews.text: This didn't absorb well at all. It sat on the surface of my skin forming a shiny barrier nothing else could get through. I tried it a few times using less and less, thinking that I was just putting too much on. Nothing helped. I had better luck using it overnight as a deep hydrator. But it didn't play nice with my face cream, and prevented it from soaking in. In the morning my skin felt heavy and it was oily enough that my hair ended up greasy in the front from touching my forehead while I slept.

reviews.title: Soothing and hydrating. 5 stars⭐
reviews.text: I love how this Essence has such soothing and hydrating ingredients. I've just recently started using K-beauty products, but the first one I tried left my skin very irritated. This one feels so light, but moisturizing. I'm just learning about a lot of the ingredients that are in most Korean beauty products, and I'm also Learning that some of them do not do well with my skin. This one works wonderfully for me. I use Retin-A, so anything really harsh or very exfoliating is too much. The first one made my skin very red, swollen, and angry looking. This one feels very nice on my skin and I love the way my skin is looking and feeling now. I would definitely recommend it! Especially, for those using Retin-A or those with very sensitive skin.

reviews.title: My skin went from dry/dehydrated to oily after using this toner 😔
reviews.text:
The sedum extract in this essence was quite intriguing to me. As someone with chronically dry, sometimes dehydrated skin I thought this toner could help me. "Sedum is a succulent leaf known for its intense hydration, vitamins & mineral level. It holds more hydration than watermelon and is rich in phytoestrogen that further boosts hydration and improves the skin condition." When I read that, I thought this sounded like the recipe for perfect skin, but every time I use this essence my face turns super oily. I have never experienced this kind of change from any of the hydrating essences I own.

The scent on this is light, clean, and fresh, very herbal. You should be aware that this product is fragranced with natural fruit oils & extracts. A few minutes after application though, the scent dissipates quickly. My skin is crazy reactive & very sensitive, but I did not experience any adverse reactions to the ingredients used in this essence.

There was also a generous amount of samples from Celimax included with this toner. 😀 They sent 2 Noni ampoules, 3 serum packettes, and 2 AHA/BHA exfoliating/brightening pads (labeled for underarms, but can be used elsewhere). Korean brands are so very gracious with samples! Thank you, Celimax! ❤

Both the package & bottle both come safety sealed. The bottle is of nice size (150ml) and features a shaker style spout for easy dispensing. I pour a small amount of the essence into my hands, then pat into the skin after cleansing. Then I apply my serums & moisturizer.

Though I don't find this product to be a particularly good fit for my skin right now (during the hot & humid Florida summer heat, when my oil production is already at it's peak), I think this essence may work better for me in the winter. I do think it's a nice product, so it still gets a 4 star rating from me.

reviews.title: Love this Hydration!
reviews.text:
I really love this hydrating and moisturizing product.

I wasn't sure I would at first. Although the box is in English, and has clear instructions and ingredients, the product itself lacks those things. Celimax comes in a streamlined bottle that looks like mineral water. Aside from the general disaster that could pose with small children, the FDA requires cosmetic products sold in the USA list their ingredients on the bottle in English.

The ingredients, by the way, which I wish Amazon would leave a spot for cosmetic vendors to include in their listings, so we would know before we order in case of allergies, are Sedum Sarmentosum, Butylene Glycol, Water, 1,2-Hexanediol, Glycerin, Glycereth-26, Polyglutamic Acid, Hydrolyzed Hyaluronic Acid, Ceramide NP, Xylityglucoside, Anhydroxylitol, Xylitol, Dipotassium Glycrrhizate, Citrus Tangerina Peel Oil, Pelargonium Graveolens Flower Oil, Lavandula Angustifolia Oil, Glucose, Phospholifpids, Diethoxyethyl Succinate, Caprylic/Capric Triclyceride, Ammonium Acryloylidimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer, Hydrogenated Lecithin, C12-13 Pareth-9, Disodium EDTA, Chlorphenesin.

Having said that, I poured a little bit of the product onto a cotton pad in the evening and dabbed it onto my face, and onto the back of my hands. It initially felt just a bit tacky, but almost immediately absorbed. I followed up on my face with my nightly cream. My face this morning feels AMAZING. The moisture level is through the roof.

I also want to mention fragrance. Like 95% of chronic migraine headache sufferers, I have a sensitive sense of smell. Anything cosmetic that has perfume (think lip smackers) or added scent has the potential to make me nauseous and bring on a migraine. This had just a faint authentic citrus smell, vaguely pleasant, which thankfully disappeared within a few seconds of hitting the cotton ball. That kind of fragrance is ideal.

And the effects are just amazing. I don't see my face all day, but I can sure see my hands on the keyboard. My hands are visibly more plumper, less dry, and more hydrated. I am loving this product. Hopefully they're in process with getting their bottle properly labelled and safe for distribution in the United States, because this is my new routine. Such results!

reviews.title: Lightweight, High Absorption, Moisturizing Essence for Foundation of Skin Care Routine
reviews.text:
The Celimax The Real Sedum Aqua Boosting Essence comes in a clear bottle of 150ml. It's a good size bottle that will probably will probably last me 9-10 months. The packaging is attractive and highly simple and modern. There's almost no scent to the essence, maybe a very faint scent of citrus if I were to pinpoint, which is ideal for people who have sensitive nose.

I am not a chemist so the ingredients described have no meaning to me. Though, the essence contains hyaluronic acid which is the main ingredient in the toner that I use for final stage of my daily facial cleaning process. The toner is a lot thicker in content and leaves my skin sticky whereas this essence is very water based and feels amazing after applying. My skin reacted and absorbed the essence right away and left my skin smooth and moisturized without feeling any stickiness. Even my hands got moisturized and I do not have to wipe or wash away after the application. I learned through reading the product description that this essence contains low molecular weight hyaluronic acid, which with its small molecular structure makes it easier for it to absorb into the skin. This speaks true to the application because I use the essence right after I wash my face and this sets the base for my skincare routine and helps maxmize the absorbtion of other products.

Without reading too much into it, this is a five-star product that is lightweight with almost no scent, keeps your skin moisturized and hydrated, and prepped for your daily skin care routine.

reviews.text:
If you have dry to super dry skin your skin would love it. I love toners so much and it’s very important for my skincare routine. I tried so many different toners including Celimax Noni Toner. But this one… it’s a top notch product for my super dry skin!!

reviews.title: Great for Korean 7-skin method!!
reviews.text:
This product is a great option if you want to do the Korean 7-skin method, which consists on cleansing your face and then applying 7 layers of a toner or a lightweight, watery essence to your face and after that moisturize your face. This will help your skin to keep moisturized throughout the day and be radiant and bouncy all day long. I do method once a week and every other day do 3 to 5 layer depending on how I feel/see my skin.

Full Ingredient List:
Sedum Sarmentosum Extract, Butylene Glycol, Water, 1,2-Hexanediol, Glycerin, Glycereth-26, Polyglutamic Acid, Hydrolyzed Hyaluronic Acid, Ceramide NP, Xylitylglucoside, Anhydroxylitol, Xylitol, Dipotassium Glycyrrhizate, Citrus Tangerina Peel Oill, Pelargonium Graveolens Flower Oil, Lavandula Angustifolia Oil, Glucose, Phospholipids, Diethoxyethyl Succinate, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer, Hydrogenated Lecithin, C12-13 Pareth-9, Disodium EDTA, Chlorphenesin

If my review helped please like my post

reviews.title: Straightens without flattening hair
reviews.text:
I had been wanting a straightening brush for quite a while. Most of them had really mixed reviews, so I was hesitant to buy one. I jumped at the opportunity to buy this one at a discount to review. I decided on this one because it was advertised as professional quality, actually looked like a salon quality product that would last, and because it is purple. When I received the product, I could tell I made a great choice.

The controls are on the side. I was skeptical about that because many other products had reviews which said that the buttons were on the side and it was easy to accidentally turn it off or change the temperature. I did not find this to be true with this brush. To change the temperature or turn off the brush, I had to press and hold the buttons in a way that would be difficult to do inadvertently. I initially set the temperature at 400, and then decided to increase to 450 when I got to a stubborn section of my hair. I kept checking the temperature reading as I used the Envy brush, and it did not budge until I purposely changed the setting.

This brush heated up quickly. It gets really hot, but I thought it was easy to use safely. It seems like it would be difficult to accidentally burn yourself, although not impossible.

My hair is long and somewhat fine in texture, but I have a lot of hair. It usually takes me 20-30 minutes to straighten my hair with a CHI professional flat iron. I initially just started to use the Envy brush right after blow drying my hair. After a few brushes, I realized that I would need to completely comb through my hair to smooth it out before using this brush. I started out by just running the Envy brush through my hair. I found quickly that I needed to section off my hair for the best results. I sectioned off the top 2/3 of my hair, twisted and secured it to the top of my head, and focused on the underside first. When I finished the under layer, I moved on to the middle section, and then the top. The whole process took me about 20-25 minutes. This is close to the same amount of time it takes me to use a flat iron. I think the Envy brush will go much faster the next time I use it. There is a bit of a learning curve, but I felt comfortable with the technique by the time I was done styling my hair.

One difference I noticed was the way my hair felt after styling with the Envy brush. When I use my flat iron, my hair is...flat. The flat iron leaves my hair very smooth but it doesn't seem to have much texture or body left after straightening. When I used the Envy brush, my hair was straight, but still had plenty of volume.

I'm really happy I bought the Envy styling brush. I really like the volume my hair has after using this brush. I highly recommend this styling brush!

reviews.title: I have coily curly African hair which I stretch using ...
reviews.text: I have coily curly African hair which I stretch using a roller set. I bought this brush to straighten my hair further without using a flat iron or blow dryer. It does the job! If you have curls and you want a bone straight look, this might not be the right tool for you. But, if you want to straighten it enough to maximize the length of your style without too much heat damage and effort, this is it!

reviews.title: LOVE LOVE LOVE!!
reviews.text:
LOVE LOVE LOVE!
I have always wanted to get one of these fancy hair straightening brushes but just never got around to it until I saw this one online and decided to finally just purchase it. I am so glad I did! This brush is amazing! It is not only very easy to use, but works wonders. I will say that I do still love my straighter a little more because it does not pull my thin blonde hair as much, but it is well worth the money!
I tired this brush on my sister who has very curly light brown hair and she was amazed at how well, and fast it straightened her hair. When it normally took her an hour to straighten her hair with my straighten only took her 15 minutes with this brush!
The one down side to the brush is that the heat protectors on the hot bristle head are somewhat jell like. If you do not let the brush fully heat up to the temperature you would like to use, I have noticed that the brush will pull on more of your hair, possibly ripping it out.
The color of this brush, a very pretty purple, and the black cord makes the brush look very high class. The brush looks and works like a brush that should cost much more than $50. Heck my straighter was was twice that much and is nothing compared to this hair straightening brush with my sisters hair.
All in all, if you have thin, blonde hair like myself, this brush is nice to have but huge need to upgrade your flat iron straightener. BUT if you have super curly, thick hair, you should really think about getting this brush! You will not regret it!
I received this product at a discounted rate or free in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

reviews.title: Not Great for Thick/Wavy Hair. Pretty & OK for Touch-Ups
reviews.text:
My whole life I've needed to use a hair straightener. I have very thick strands of hair and a lot of it. Plus, my hair is poofy crazy curly. My hair used to be down to my butt, however, this past year I had to cut it all off due to getting a horrid matted mess. (If you don't know what it means to have matted hair - your lucky). It was a whole bunch of tears, pain, tantrums, home remedies, a ton of hair salon visits and a ton of money spent until realizing that I was s*** outta luck and had to cry as they cut my hair to only an inch in the back, but luckily had enough hair left / saved on top and on sides for shoulder length hair. So now more than ever, having short hair that's very thick and curly, I'm always looking for amazing hair straighteners and products in general.

I have wanted one of these heat and style brushes and finally decided to test this one out. I loved the color of this heat brush and it looked like a nice, high quality hair straightening brush in the images.

This brush has less options then I thought it would. I cannot control the temperature and I would like for it to get a little bit hotter. Since I have curly, wavy hair this doesn't heat up enough to get it straight. Or maybe it's not doing a great job because it doesn't have two "plates" like regular straighteners. If I straighten my hair with a flat iron, this is a great touch up tool for the days following the flat iron before I wash my hair again. SO - It does work, but it is NOT a miracle product. This hair straightening brush (& all brush type of straighteners) MIGHT work better / work faster / have better results if you have thin hair? Just a assumption.

One difference I noticed was the way my hair felt after styling with the Envy brush. When I use my flat iron, my hair is...flat. The flat iron leaves my hair very smooth but it doesn't seem to have much texture or body left after straightening. When I used the Envy brush, my hair was straight, but still had plenty of volume. I'm really happy I bought the Envy styling brush. I really like the volume my hair has after using this brush.

I had to completely comb through my hair to smooth it out before using this brush (obviously). I started out by just running the Envy brush through my hair. Once I was done brushing my hair, I sectioned off my hair for the best results. I sectioned off the top 2/3 of my hair, twisted and secured it to the top of my head. As always, I focused on the underside first. When I finished the under layer, I moved on to the middle section, and then the top. The whole process took me about 20minutes. Pretty close to the same amount of time that it takes me to use my regular flat iron.

An ion is as an atom in which the number of electrons and protons are not the same. When a hair tool has ionic in its name, it means that it can generate negative ions. Hair has high water content, making it positively charged. When negative ions come in contact with hair, the positive charge is neutralized. This leaves your hair straight, sleek, shiny and smooth.

Here is one crucial thing to keep in mind – not all flat irons are pure ceramic, many of them are aluminum. They are just coated with ceramic on the top. Such models are less expensive, but you need to be extra careful when using them. This coating can chip or peel, revealing less quality metal below. These peeled areas can snag your hair! So always try to find out if an iron is coated or not.
You can even find costly professional models that are not 100% ceramic. Solid or pure ceramic are words that you are looking for.

I'm not sure if this brush is ceramic, titanium or what..?!?

Titanium vs. Ceramic
In this battle, it is obvious that titanium is a winner. But does this mean that you should use only this type of iron? No. Many experts say that consumers should use ceramic for one obvious reason. Since titanium can get very hot and transfers heat better, it’s very easy to damage your hair if you are not careful. Also, this high heat is only advantageous for women with thick, coarse and stubborn hair. If your have thin or fragile hair, there is no need for high temperatures. In the end, it is up to you to find out what will work best for your hair. Now you have all the facts to make the right decision. If you are still indecisive, there is a simple solution! Get a flat iron that combines ceramic and titanium, such as Croc 2 Infrared mentioned above. This way you get ceramic’s safety and protection and titanium’s speed and efficiency.

I received this product at a discount or free in exchange for an honest review. I strongly depend on product reviews when making purchases so it's very important for me to leave an honest review. I do NOT review products just because they are free or discounted. That said, I only accept promotional items that I would actually purchase for myself and my family. The discount in no way affects my opinion, rating, or review of a product in question.
  I have read the reviews and information about the product myself prior to ordering so I am usually confident I will like the product – thus the reason many of my reviews are positive. However, I have and will continue to leave appropriate negative reviews for products that are not good quality products.
  Please note I have no relationship with this seller and I’m not compensated in any way for my review.

reviews.title: Not too bad
reviews.text: I've used this twice and every time it's pulling my hair out. I have very fine hair and I feel it gets tangled easily. Other than that, I love the way it makes my hair look and feel.

reviews.title: Worst product I have EVER purchased
reviews.text: Worst product I have EVER purchased. It dried my hair terribly, I used it 2 weeks ago and my hair is still super dry, I’ve spent over $60 on hair products trying to bring it back to life and nothing will work, especially my ends.

reviews.title: This product is useless. when I bought it was very expensive
reviews.text:
This product is useless .when I bought it was very expensive....I wish I can return it back
let me know if there is any compensation for that useless product

reviews.title: I loved the color of this heat brush and it looked ...
reviews.text:
I loved the color of this heat brush and it looked like it was high quality in the images. I have wanted one of these heat and style brushes and finally decided to test this one out. This brush has less options then I thought it would. I cannot control the temperature and I would like for it to get a little bit hotter. I have fairly wavy hair and this doesn't heat up enough to get it straight. However, if I straighten my hair with a flat iron this is a great touch up tool for the days following the flat iron before I was my hair again. It does work, but it is not a miracle product.

I did receive this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased opinion. All comments and thoughts expressed are my own, based upon my experiences with the product.

reviews.title: Best electric I've had
reviews.text:
This Norelco (actually made by Philips) one was bought to replace an older shaver (I've used Norelco, Philips and Panasonics over the years.)
