This one is more efficient (faster, better shave) than my older one, and an amazingly amount quieter. What do you know- this time, "new and improved" really is accurate!
What I like: the shaver fits the hand well, the pivoting blade heads fit the face and as a result shave quite well. The battery strength indicator, consisting of bright LEDs showing the percentage of charge remaining in 20% increments, is very useful, and the flip-up hinged shaver head section is easy to clean with warm water (I still have to consciously force myself to do this!) They have also "shrunk" the half-egg shaped power transformer, and you can use 100 - 220 volts to plug / charge, a good thing for someone who travels globally as I do.
What I don't like: the pop-up trimmer is only fair - it works, but it's actually not as effective as the one on my old shaver! The deep well holding the head rotors shafts is not so easy to clean with a brush, particularly with the hinged head; they are still using a non-user replaceable NiCd battery; the gimmicky cutter head plate section with holes seems merely that - gimmicky, not useful for any real shaving.
Tips: After cleaning the head / blade assemblies, allow them to dry fully and spray-lubricate with something like "Shaver Saver." When power seems low, turn the shaver on and allow to discharge fully before reccharging - these are, after all, still NiCd batteries with the "memory" effect. These tips have allowed my previous electrics' battery - and cutter head assemblies - to last a long time (I have a heavy beard - even with a blade shaver, I have to shave again in the afternoon if I have a dinner appointment.)
Recommendation: This is a very good, easy to use, quick and quiet electric shaver - the best I have ever used. It packs easily for travel, and though it is a bit finnicky to clean, I like it because it does the job I need it to do. Given the several deficits, I'd never give it four stars if not for how it handles its ultimate function - shaving my beard.

reviews.title: 0 dollars spent since 1st facial hair growth
reviews.text:
My mom gave me this as a gift from about 10 years ago. It's an amazing device. I originally complained to my mom when I started growing facial hair and before I could start buying disposable razors - she gave me this. Thanks mom - I've never had to spend a dime for facial shaving products.

PROs
- good battery life
- durable, I've dropped it a few times and actually broke the swivel notch for the trimmer but used crazy glue to repair it - I dropped it again so the notch is loose but seems to be swiveling open/close fine
- make and finish is amazing, no signs of wear and tear, the plastic or material used is perfect for hair or water
- it's easy to clean even when hair gets inside the cracks, just need to tap it a few times and it comes out - unless you want to open the device with some torx screw driver
- easy to take apart for extensive cleaning
- you can take it for travelling easily
- you can shave while it's charging

CONs
- even after over 10 years, nothing comes to mind

So impressed with this brand that I'm looking into their new 3d models. I would recommend this shaver even now competing with the newer models. I think I'm going to rebuild mine and keep it as a back up. It's still available in certain sites even in 2011.

reviews.title: Great Product
reviews.text: I have been using Norelco for over 40 years. This product is so far the best one I have used. I like their electrics the best and prefer electrics over a blade.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: After using this attractive product for three weeks, I am returning it for refund to Norelco.  Problems are skin irritation and painful hair pulling.  Additionally, the shave is not close compared with even a disposable blade, despite using pre-shave conditioners.  I was very disappointed in the quality of shave, because this product is well-made and there were many postive reviews.

reviews.title: Recommended - Nice design
reviews.text:
I have been using Norelco rotary shavers for the last 20 years. The last model I purchased worked for 8 years before the batteries wore out. It was a economy unit, model number 815RX.
I decided to buy a more deluxe shaver this time, and bought the model 7865XL shown here. It was more expensive than the old one. I charged and used the shaver this morning and was pleasantly suprised with its performance. The heads are floating within their retaining assembly and they conform to the curves on the face nicely. The shave was excellent and my skin was left soft.
To those who don't regularly use an electric shaver - some advice. In order for it to work properly, you MUST wash and then dry your face. If I don't do this, I have found that my skin will become irritated and red. It works even better if you use soap with cream in it during the wash (acts a lubricant), but just washing with water and a wash rag works well too.
I would recommend this shaver to anyone looking for a quality unit. If you are already a Norelco rotary shaver user, then you will find this model to be a nice upgrade.
Bobzt
Engineer

reviews.title: Great shaver, easy to use, close shave
reviews.text:
I bought this shaver to replace my Mach III blade. I have used it for over a month, past the amount of time it takes your skin and face to get used to it. Nothing will shave as close as the Mach III; the Norelco gets close to it, but does it more quickly. I have never owned an electric razor before this one, so I have nothing to compare it to. However, here is my opinion:
PROS:
*Shaves very close
*Easy to clean
*Runs for a long time between charges
*Charges quickly
*Conforms to your face well
*Useful voltage converter
*Nice carrying case
*Contrary to a previous reviewer, it does have one flat edge where it won't roll of the table
CONS:
*Trimmer doesn't trim as well as I'd like it to
*Takes a few weeks for your skin to get used to it and get a close shave

reviews.title: FAIR TO MIDDELING
reviews.text: BLESSED WITH A LIGHT BEARD, THE UNIT DOES ME JUST FINE.  CLEANING THE UNIT IS A LITTLE CUMBERSOME.  FAST RECHARGE.

reviews.title: Wash & dry face first for best results.  4.5 stars.
reviews.text:
It's true what other reviewers have said: you'll get the best results out of the 7865XL (and the others in the 7800 series) if you wash and dry your face first. Then it glides smoothly and works in very few passes. I probably have to make sure I cross each area about three times in different directions (mostly going against the grain of the whiskers) to get everything. If I follow those two basic guidelines, my chin is smooth as can be and the shave lasts all day. If I don't wash first it's still a joy to use, but it takes longer (more passes in different directions to get every whisker) and won't last all day (more like 8 hours).

This item is worth buying, it's fun to use... I'd give it 4 and a half stars. To get 5 it would need to do everything in a single pass, and that's a tall order.

I also like the fact that you can rinse the head under running water to clean it.

reviews.title: Wonderful, 7865XL beats my razor any time.
reviews.text: To achive the maximum closeness, you must follow the instruction from the small hand book included in the package.  At first, you will not see a dramatic change but keep using it and your facial hair will grow to be shaved by 7865XL faster and comfortable every time.  My facial hair is thick and a common razor's blade last about 3 to 4 weeks average use.  The 7865XL does it in no time and close to my face.  My face ends uplike a baby's bottom.  The first use always take time, but as you progress shaving over and over on your face it get faster.  Depends on your skin and your hair growth too.  I can shave in less than 5 min and I have side burns that the combs can go around the edges; of course with some help from my hand.  As I hold the hair upward, the electric razor does it job.  What more can I say?  You gotta try it to prove yourself wrong.  And remember, follow the instructions from the small booklet.

reviews.title: Quality of the material and functional
reviews.text:
Had no problem shaving and I got thick hair.
problems...
1.)The problem after 5 year is the blue power supply flexible cord become bristles and flacks. (see photo)
2.) The gummy thumb rest pad starts to deteriorate. (see photo)
3.) I Noticed behind the power on switch a small spiral plastic goes side ways and does not turn on or off the shaver, I resolve this by taking it apart and super gluing the small white spiral plastic so that it would become stable and had no more problem turning it on or off. (no photo, it is internal and under the on/off button.)

reviews.title: This shaver is great
reviews.text: My husband is disabled, therefore must use an electric one.  This one gives him the closest shave and is just right for his sensitive skin.

reviews.title: Great Razor
reviews.text: I bought this razor for my boyfriend who was tired of shaving all the time.  I gave this to him a while ago and it is still kicking and he's still using it.  The built-in power indicator makes it easy for him to figure out when to recharge it.  It's portable so it's great for travel and not having to bring along the recharger on every trip "just in case" is a definite added bonus.  Of course, it's never going to shave as close as a traditional razor but you can't beat it for convenience.

reviews.title: Definite upgrade from old Norelco
reviews.text:
I got this razor for my husband for his birthday. He had a Norelco 4845 (I think) for the last 5 years so I know that the products were long lasting. I was looking to buy new blades but they were hard to find so I figured I'd just buy a new razor.
Reasons I went with this one--quick charge time (only an hour), the super quick charge in a hurry (enough to jump into a shower while charging, and then quick shave), the charge % indicator, and able to wash the heads under water. All of these were an upgrade from my husband's old razor. In the last 4 months, he has only needed to charge it twice (and he shaves about twice a day, before work and if we go out in the evening--he grows his hair fast, so please don't think it is because the razor isn't doing it's job...he's just like that). He can rinse the heads under water, eliminating the hair around the trashcan when he misses. The charge % indicator and the quick charge is great so he doesn't waken up in the morning and realize he can't shave because it isn't charged. I also think the razor is cutting the hair better (and I know a wife can tell) than his old one.
Reasons for not getting a higher up model--Well, the price was one issue. I spent around $99 at Target for this model. The next highest in the series has an LCD charge % indicator, and I didn't feel it was necessary (and just one more electronic component to deal with). The 8 series looked cool, but comparing the one that was the same price, this one had more features. And it looked like the only difference with the heads was that the spacer for short and longer hairs were alternating rather than two separate areas. I really didn't see the advantage to spending more money for that. So I chose this one.

reviews.title: DON'T BUY THIS SHAVER!!!
reviews.text:
I've owned Norelco's for over 20 years now and this is the worst!

The side trimmer doesn't cut anything, the charge only lasts one week and the shaver causes a rash on my neck. My old Norelco's side trimmers always cut right so I think they ought to fire the engineer that designed this one. All the previous Norelco's I've owned had a charge that lasted two weeks so I think they are skimping out on the batteries they use. The design of the head is lousy; the stupid holes actually cause a rash on my neck.

I know they say to apply less pressure but if you do then you don't get a close shave on the rest of your face. The other thing I hate is the external adaptor on the cord. The old Norelco had a slim cord with no stupid adaptor on it. The only good thing I can say about this shaver is that it is easy to clean under warm water, but what good is this if you are not getting a close shave and developing rashes on your neck!

reviews.title: Just Not Close Enough
reviews.text:
After 30+ years of blade shaving I decided to go electric. I really, really wanted this to work out, but alas I'm back to the blade.
I found the 7865 XL to be a very well built product and I think this would be a fine shaver for those with fine to medium beards. I think my beard is just too tough for an electric razor. I tried shaving with Lectric Shave and without, but by 3 o'clock every day I looked like I needed a shave.
Go ahead and give it a shot. With the 60 day money-back guarantee you've got nothing to lose.

reviews.title: Norelco
reviews.text: I find the product performs well and holds a charge well into 4 days.  It does take a break-in period for your face and the razor.  You have to stick with it through an "uncomfortable" period.  Overall a very good product.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: It works really well, even in the dry and frigid winter air. Non-greasy and moisture rich.

reviews.title: Korean serum that delivers gentle hydration with ceramides and squalane
reviews.text: This Skin & Lab  Milky Serum is lightweight and unscented. I like the texture and feel of this serum - not sticky nor greasy but offers immediate hydration. It leaves the skin feeling soft and well hydrated.  The serum contains squalane and ceramides, providing moisture below the surface.

reviews.title: Very Hydrating
reviews.text:
This serum is a milky looking, thin serum that goes on easily and absorbs quickly. After applying, my skin looks hydrated but a bit shiny, probably a result of the squalane.

The serum is ultra hydrating and after 5 days of use, a few dry patches of skin, that nothing had helped, are no longer visible, even after washing my face.

reviews.title: Soothing & Skin Feels Less Sensitive, Hydrates & Improves Skin Texture, Free Of Fragrance/Alcohol
reviews.text:
Skin & Lab Barrierderm Milky Serum Milk Ceramide Complex is a milky serum free of fragrance and alcohol that comes in an easy to use dropper bottle. Ceramides can make skin healthier by strengthening the skin barrier, preventing moisture loss and protecting against environmental damage. Also, ceramides work better combined with certain types of ingredients that are contained in this serum, such as cholesterol, glycerin, squalane (rich in fatty acids) and more.

This serum feels wonderful on my dry, sensitive and mature (55+) skin that needs a lot of hydration and is aggravated easily by chemicals fragrance, natural fragrancing essential oils and extracts, drying alcohol, aggressive products, the environment and so on. I’ve been using this ceramide serum after cleansing and follow with my face cream, but it also layers well with my favorite staples, such as a Vitamin C and hyaluronic acid serum (followed by my moisturizer).

I like that this ceramide serum doesn’t appear to have any harsh ingredients, and it feels soothing and gentle to my skin. I’ve used it about a week so far and it adds noticeable hydration that isn't heavy, greasy or sticky, and my normally sensitive skin is calm and comfortable. Also, my skin texture feels soft and seems more elastic. I can’t judge the full benefits yet as that can take many weeks or months, but I’m pleased so far and will keep using this serum. Love it. 5 stars.

reviews.title: Nice gentle serum
reviews.text: Very nice gentle, hydrating serum. I have dry sensitive skin and this serum is great! Very light scent and makes my skin very soft.

reviews.title: Wonderful Product!
reviews.text:
It's called 'milky serum' so I don't know why I was so surprised at the fact the texture is well... milky!

It's lightweight, but not so light as say, a toner. It came with a dropper applicator which really grew on me over a few days, and while it's tacky when you first put it on, it really absorbs extremely well after a minute or two.

This has become a steady part of my morning routine, and it definitely makes a difference. My skin looks and feels great and I'm definitely going to continue using it.

reviews.title: Light Serum
reviews.text: I love that it’s fragrance free and cruelty free. This serum is milky white in color. To me, it’s more of a moisturizer lotion than a serum. It spreads  over the skin very smoothly, almost slippery, so you don’t need a lot to cover your face. It’s light weight yet very hydrating. I didn’t have any bad reaction from it. Another good Korean skincare product. I recommend it.

reviews.title: I feel rejuvenated!
reviews.text:
I am now 44 and have to admit that my skin will never be like it was in my 20's! I have been using the same moisturizer routine for a very long time with the same products and I have not been seeing any difference over the years.

This milky serum has turned the clock back for me and I am very happy with the results!

I replaced my oil free night time moisturizer with the Milky Serum and in only a few days I am seeing my dark spots fading and my fine lines are disappearing! I am outside all summer and even with SPF I still suffer from skin damage on my temples. I am shocked that this lotion has made those spots disappear!
I apply a dropper full to my forehead, cheeks and neck and gently stroke upward and the serum absorbs quickly with no stickiness. It has virtually no odor which is great because I am sensitive to perfumes.

This is a great product for the price and if you are looking to renew your aging skin I highly recommend!

reviews.title: Nice, light moisturizer
reviews.text:
When I first tried this serum, I wasn't sure I liked it...but it has grown on me. The serum is well-described as being milky. It's thin and white...so much so that I found it hard to believe it could be much of a moisturizer. And when I put it on my face, I noticed it had a bit of a tack/stickiness to it. It wasn't overwhelming like glue, but if I put my hands to my face, I noticed that tacky feeling. Again, these were not things I was used to in a moisturizer.

The moisturizer is fairly quickly absorbed; however, and that tackiness goes away within a minute or so. What you're left with is a nice smooth feeling. I don’t know that my face feels overly moisturized, but it feels healthy. I also tested it on one of my elbows (which has just a bit of eczema), and I believe the eczema has improved beyond what it was looking like with shea butter applications alone.

My wife has been using this serum as well, and she would give it 3 or 4 stars...while my opinion is 4 or 5. Four stars then seems fair. I love that this serum has its origins in Korea, as I trust their beauty standards. And admittedly I would say my feeling that the serum was a bit unusual was tempered by the product's origins. Again, I trust their beauty standards and somewhat assume I'm filling a hole I didn't know was there. A couple weeks in, I'm not yet completely convinced that I will make this or a similar product a standard in my skin care regimen. I nevertheless love the idea of rotating products/varying what you apply to your skin (to again cover any holes the products you've been using may be missing), so I'm very glad I tried it. If I notice any outstanding results (one way or another), I will provide an update.

reviews.title: No breakout with this amazing serum!
reviews.text: First time trying out this serum and love it! It absorbs into your skin quickly and no breakout for my sensitive skin. Will definitely order again!

reviews.title: Nice quality moisturizer
reviews.text: I've tried a few different moisturizers in the last couple of months and this one is my favorite so far!  I just use the dropper and add a little to my fingertips then dot it all over my face.  It seems to absorb quickly and leaves no tackiness or smell behind.  Leaves my skin feeling smooth and hydrated!  I do have trouble with flare ups of seborrheic dermatitis on my face and this doesn't seem to aggravate it at all.  I'd say it's probably worth a try for most people with sensitive skin.

reviews.title: Nice light milky serum from Korea!
reviews.text:
If you are looking for a nice, light high-quality serum moisturizer, this likely fits the bill. I have recently learned that Korea has very high standards for beauty and skin products with strict government regulations as to safety and quality.

This product meets expectations. It is light and absorbs easily. It is not at all greasy -- very light. There is a very slight fragrance that is not noticeable at all on my face. I found it unusual that a serum would be a milky white rather than clear, but it absorbs like typical serums. I put the droplets into my palm, then dot on my face and smooth it in, especially in the areas where I have lines and wrinkles. I let it absorb and dry for a few minutes, and then apply my moisturizer over it. I have had no sensitivity issues with this product.

I am very pleased with this product so far!

reviews.title: Milky Moisturizing Serum
reviews.text: This milky serum really delivers on providing moisturization and healthy glow to my skin, especially in this cold weather season.  I have oily/combo skin, however, during the cold season, I have oily yet tight parched skin and need some time of serum or moisturizer that helps relieve the tightness without leaving any oily residue, and this does the job.

reviews.title: Has a really great smell
reviews.text:
Pretty thin, so I reckon this will last a good deal of time, the lotion does a pretty good job of moisturizing the skin.
Love the smell...its the best part, though admittedly its a good moisturizer as well and it didn't leave a greasy film
We liked that it had a nice dropper dispenser too, so you don't have to worry about accidentally pulling too much out of the bottle.

reviews.title: Hello, moisturizing serum, and good-bye, dry skin!
reviews.text:
I was already prone to dry skin before relocating to a desert climate where I was forced to really up my hydration game. I have gone through numerous products that claim to help but then end up w/ minimal (if any) improvement.

After day 1, I wasn't sold on this product either. However, I decided to continue its use for at least five days since the product description claimed noticeable improvement "after 5 days." I am glad that I stuck it out.

I initially thought the eye dropper weird but quickly grew to appreciate it. A small dose goes a long way and the eye dropper allows easier control to obtain only the bare minimum product necessary without wasting anything. I do wish that it was slightly larger as the bulb does not fill completely, so I require a refill (or two) to cover the entirety of my face, but this is a minor nuisance at most.

This product is unscented (a plus!) and smooths easily across the face. It is somewhat noticeable for a short timeframe after application. It is semi-shiny and not sticky but maybe slightly tacky (?not sure how best to describe this). If you follow the product description's instructions to "gently pat for better absorption," there is less of this feeling but it is still noticeably present. I do not wear make-up, but I imagine that those who do might need to ensure sufficient time has passed after use before dolling themselves up (if applying in the morning).

There seemed to be some mild improvement after a couple days, but there was no question about its effectiveness after the aforementioned five days. My skin was definitely smoother and improved! I look forward to continuing its use in my facial care regimen and hope to try out some of this other brand's products.

reviews.title: Light, moisturizing, unscented
reviews.text: This is a very lightweight (almost watery) serum.  I do not detect a scent, which is great since I find many fragrances overpowering in moisturizers. It absorbs quickly and feels nice when applied.  The applicator is a bit odd—it’s a syringe type applicator with a bulb end. I assume it is dispensed this way because the consistency is so watery.  I just apply the wand directly to my face and then massage the product in with my fingers.  So far, I’m pleased with the moisturizing results.  I assume any anti-aging benefit will take a while to discern.

reviews.title: A must have
reviews.text: There is no scent to this serum. The consistency is like a watery cream. It's very thin and once applied to my face, it's light and doesn't have any scent. This is my last step to my skin routine and it does wonders. It claims it acts like a barrier and I totally agree. Once it has absorbed into my face, it feels like a silky veil, leaving my skin soft and glowy. I'm very pleased with this serum.

reviews.title: Gentle, Soothing Moisture
reviews.text:
Being that my facial skin changes greatly during the winter months, I thought this serum might help to re-establish some moisture while battling my winter dryness. This review is for: [SKIN&LAB] Barrierderm Milky Serum | Infused with Milk Ceramide Complex, Squalane, and Panthenol to ...

I have used products from Skin & Lab before and liked most of them, and this serum is no exception. I was coming off of a different moisturizer that really irritated my skin, so I introduced this serum slowly. I started using it only once a day and once that previous irritation cleared up I moved up to twice a day.

This serum is somewhere in between watery and a cream. The glass bottle is opaque and the serum does dispense as milky-looking. It has no scent that I can detect. The dropper dispenses enough for about half my face, so I've been using two droppers per application. It has soothed my skin, clearing up my previous Rosacea break out and smoothing out my skin. I have an oily t-zone and a super dry chin this winter, and all of that seems to be gone now.

Due to these cold, breezy winter months, I have been using a moisturizer over this serum. I suspect that in the summer months, I might not even need to do that.

I'm looking forward to continued use of this product and when it's gone I will most likely buy more. The price is affordable and this works wonderfully on my dry, winter skin.

reviews.title: Wonderful Serum
reviews.text:
This review is for [SKIN&LAB] Barrierderm Milky Serum.

It's great for me. My dry sensitive skin stays moist throughout the day and looks healthy and well-nourished, and it never feels irritated. This serum is lightweight and comfortable to wear and so I use it night and day.

reviews.title: I've found the BEST BB CREAMS from Korean beauty brands & this is no exception!!
reviews.text:
So, I'm recently 50 (I'm wondering how long I'll keep adding recently to my age??) and during 2020 I fell in love with Korean skin care and Korean BB Creams. This one gets high marks, and it's a bit different than the others I've tried.

I ordered the lightest shade since I'm VERY fair and it worked perfectly. That's one of the things I love about BB Creams in general, they provide coverage but they also seem to work with your skin tone rather than masking it. This applied beautifully with a brush and my fingers (my preferred method). I'm assuming it would work with a damp sponge, as well. On me that tends to look cakey for some reason, so I don't generally go that route. It worked well over multiple moisturizers and both of my favorite sunscreens. I use a primer occasionally and had no problems with application when I did. It goes on smoothly and blends out easily. It's a very light formula and feels like I'm not even wearing makeup. It doesn't have a high amount of coverage. I would call it a buildable medium, as far as that goes. Now, here's where it differs from the other Korean BB Creams I've tried.....it dries down to a very satin, skin like finish. Most others end up with either a dewy or slightly luminous finish, which is fine. This is the first one I've found that doesn't. It's definitely not a matte finish, though.

It wears really well. I can get a full 10 hours of wear out of it, whether I set it or not. That's another thing about the way this drys down, if you tend toward dry skin (I do) then you can get away with not powdering this one if that's your preference. When I do use powder I go in pretty lightly, and usually just in the areas where my pores might be having a particularly "showy" day with a blurring powder. The bonus that seems to come with most Korean beauty facial products is the SPF. This is a PA+++/SPF 50+. Now that doesn't mean I skip my normal sunscreen, cause you're never gonna convince me that what you get in foundation or BB Cream is adequate protection.....but I will never say no to MORE protection. EVER.

This is a beautiful BB Cream that works really well on aging skin, so I imagine it would be a dream on younger skin, perfect or textured alike. One other thing I noticed is that at the end of the day I still hardly noticed I was wearing makeup. And when I washed it off my skin felt and looked pretty good. It wasn't dried out and didn't have that worn out look that I've gotten used to seeing after a full day in makeup. Definitely a keeper!!

reviews.title: Good coverage with SPF
reviews.text: I was so happy with this product the packing was good . The tube makes it easy to pump the product . As an added benefit it comes with SPF so it makes the job easy no need additional sun protection .I am actually using this with my daily morning to go look its nice and the coverage was good not caky  and too much  bright its giving the natural look .So I go to my party look only with this and a lipstick its absolutely good .The only thing it has only two shades that makes the selection a bit difficult but this suited me.

reviews.title: Good, Everyday BB Cream
reviews.text: The FromNature Perfect Cover BB Cream is a great every day cream, I purchased it for my teen worrying it may be a little dark but it was great. BB stands for beauty blender/ing and is basically a tinted moisturizer. It works as described and isn't too thick for everyday use. Its a great price point and works great!

reviews.title: Please make a paler shade!
reviews.text: Everything but the color is perfect. Admittedly I’m extremely pale, I can’t even find a MAC shade to match me; but this was SO yellow. The scent was fruity, almost reminded me of watermelon? And it applied beautifully with both hands and foundation brush. If there was some way to make this shade paler I would ditch my current foundation for this. It didn’t feel oily or cakey and felt like I was wearing a moisturizer.

reviews.title: Poor bottle
reviews.text: Drying and wears off pretty quickly. The bottle is also very poorly made- no matter how I close it, the product keeps on spilling out of the nozzle leaving a mess.

reviews.title: Transfer resistant formula 😷 with excellent coverage! Unlike any BB cream I've ever tried!
reviews.text:
The Fromnature BB cream by Cosmocos is unlike any beauty balm I've ever tried. It has much better coverage, and a thicker consistency than any other BB's I've used (which, until now, have all been Western brands). It almost covers like a foundation, but feels extremely lightweight like a BB. It does dry down really fast, and it was a bit darker than I expected. I am light neutral in complexion and chose the shade "light beige". I believe I was able to make the color work for me, but I'm not sure if it'll suit all light skin tones. 👻

This BB has a beautiful satin matte finish, but did have a tendency to cling to my dry patches, so be sure to start with an exfoliated, well moisturized, and primed face. I did not use any primer with this product, (since it's marketed as a BB cream), and without the aid of a pore filling primer this product did eventually settle into my fine lines & large pores. I am really impressed with the staying power of this product though, even without primer & setting spray! After a full day of masking against covid in the South Florida heat, there was no transfer to my face mask. 😃 That's a huge, HUGE plus! With that being said, I highly recommend using a really good cleanser to efficiently remove this product at the end of the day.

PROS:
+ Great coverage that can be built up
+ Very lightweight on the skin
+ Excellent staying power
+ SPF 50
+ Transfer resistant! 🙌
+ No photo flashback!
+ Fragrance free!
+ 1.67 oz of product (vs. the U.S. standard 1 fl. oz)
+ 2 sample hair care products included as a bonus! ❤
+ Convenient PRIME Shipping

CONS:
- Dries very fast and can be difficult to blend
- Clings to dry patches
- Settles into pores & wrinkles (without primer)

TO BE CONSIDERED:
* Warm yellow undertone may not suit everyone

Side Note: There was one other thing I wanted to mention... When I took the manufacturer safety seal off of the tube, I had a volcanic eruption of BB cream that I wasn't prepared for, which made a mess of my tube. If you're like me and don't want to waste precious product, just be aware that it might come spilling out like lava when you break the product seal. 🙃

reviews.title: Cakey but nice coverage...
reviews.text: The BB cream works just as well as a foundation. Really concentrated and thick/creamy, and you can definitely feel the sunscreen in it, cuz it makes it more thicker than most other BB creams with low levels of SPF. The color selection is SUPER limited, but I chose the lightest one and I am normally like a 120 Ivory in Maybelline (Matte version) drugstore foundation. It blended out very well. You need only but a small amount of it so it lasts a long time, and it has a pleasant smell to it (at least for me). The only and only thing I dislike is the cakiness of this foundation that forms in creases (around the mouth, eyes, forehead, chin area etc.). Primer no primer, lotion or no/lotion, setting spray or no setting spray - or just on bare cleansed face. Cakiness still forms for me in about 30-60 minutes. This happens even with super little application - which is annoying. But the quality of coverage and texture is there if it wasn't for the cakiness I experience every time I use it.

reviews.title: Great coverage for a BB Cream!
reviews.text: I am very important with this BB cream and will be buying it again in the future! It covers blemishes and marks easily and doesn’t smear off. It’s lightweight and doesn’t clog my pores either! It gives me a perfect natural look without much effort and blends quickly with my skin tone. I like the amount of product that comes in the tube and that it lasts a decent amount of time with daily use. It feels good on my skin and gives my eyelids a primer to stick to as well. Overall, I have no complaints and will definitely recommend this and purchase it again!

reviews.title: Provides Great Coverage. Feels Nice On Skin & Looks Smooth!
reviews.text:
This comes with an outer product box. It has the same appearance as the tube of foundation - black with white wording. There’s also a little rectangle in the shade, with the shade number and name. The product packaging looks nice. It comes with a product card saying they love to hear from you and 2 samples for hair products (which I thought was odd for hair products with foundation, but whatever 😂) - 1 for “RG II Hair Loss Clinic Premium Shampoo” and 1 for “Sohair Morocco Argan Oil Hair Essence.” I didn’t try either of the samples so can’t say anything for how those work.

The foundation comes in a squeeze tube. When you twist off the main top, there’s a protective seal. I usually like pumps with my foundations. But, I don’t mind this being a squeeze tube. It isn’t messy at all. I just squirt some out onto my brush or sponge, dab it in areas on my face, then blend it out.

They don’t have a large range of shade options - there’s only 2. With BB & CC creams, they’re usually so thin that a shade that’s a little off from you is usually still okay. I got the shade “#1 Light Beige.” I’m pretty pale. I can make the shade work for me, but I do wish it was a tad lighter. See my photos. I did a photo showing a drop of this Bb Cream, then a swatch doing 1 swipe with a small amount on my finger, then took what was left on my finger from the swatch and dabbed it and blended it out. I also show a photo of a swatch of this BB Blemish Cream, where I also show some blended out. Then I compared it to a swatch of L’Oréal Infallible Pro Glow in 201 Classic Ivory and Maybelline 24 Hr Foundation in 102 Fair Porcelain (I tried to use foundations most people might have tried). As you can see, both are lighter than this BB Blemish Cream. The Maybelline is MUCH lighter. I’m pretty pale, but even that one is a tad too pale for me, so I always have to warm it up with bronzer. But, this BB Blemish Cream is definitely on the darker side for me, but I’m able to make it work.

The formula feels nice. It isn’t too thin or runny. It feels nice and creamy. On the product info page it said, “BB Cream Tinted Moisturizer Blemish Balm lets you perfectly blend & build tones from sheer to medium coverage.” Most BB creams I’ve tried are usually sheer to light, not able to go up to medium coverage. But, this actually provides a lot more coverage than I expected with a BB cream! I usually don’t go for BB/CC creams that often because they’re such light coverage. But, there’s times when I want lighter coverage and was looking for a new BB/CC cream and figured I’d give this a try. I’m really glad I did! It evens out my skin tone like I’d expect with a BB cream, but also provides decent coverage too. It doesn’t feel dry on my skin. You can also build it up too if you want, and it doesn’t look dry or cakey. I’d say you can get anywhere from really light coverage (like just evening out your skin tone and maybe covering small, light spots with a thin layer) to good medium coverage (if you build it up some). It builds up nicely enough where it might even be considered full coverage to some, but for me I consider it medium coverage 😂 I can’t build it up too much on myself because the color isn’t a good enough match for me. The more I build it up, the more it doesn’t match my skin. So, when I use this since it’s not really light enough for me, I can only use a thin layer of it. But, I did build it up just to test it out for this review and it looks nice and smooth on the skin whether you’re doing a light layer or building it up.

Overall, I think this is a really great BB cream! It gives you more coverage than other BB creams I’ve tried. I like the squeeze tube. The product feels and looks nice on the skin. I definitely recommend giving this a try if you’re looking for something that will give you light to medium coverage while looking nice, smooth, and not dry or cakey.

reviews.title: Great coverage for a BB Cream!
reviews.text: I am very important with this BB cream and will be buying it again in the future! It covers blemishes and marks easily and doesn’t smear off. It’s lightweight and doesn’t clog my pores either! It gives me a perfect natural look without much effort and blends quickly with my skin tone. I like the amount of product that comes in the tube and that it lasts a decent amount of time with daily use. It feels good on my skin and gives my eyelids a primer to stick to as well. Overall, I have no complaints and will definitely recommend this and purchase it again!

reviews.title: Working Well
reviews.text:
We picked this up to use on dogs and cats. Most of them are short-haired, but one of the cats is a bit fluffy. They're all happy with the brush (one cat and one dog get freaked out with certain brushes and I'm not sure why).

The self-cleaning feature is pretty cool. You're not stuck plucking out the pet hair because it pushes it out for you. Have not had any issue with that either.

However, the bristles are quite bendable. One of my kiddos pushed on some and bent them out of place. It looks bad, but it didn't impact the usability of it and the self-cleaning feature still works. Not really an issue, but something to be mindful of.

reviews.title: Nice!
reviews.text: I like this brush for my small, short-hair dog. It helps remove his loose hair and he seems to enjoy it when I brush him. I like that I can push a button and it pushes the hair to the ends of the bristles so that I can easily remove the hair. It doesn't get all of the hair, but it is a lot more effective than a normal brush. Overall,  I'm really happy with this brush and would get one like it again.

reviews.title: Ejection system works well but times are actually too long for long haired cats.
reviews.text:
So I have a cat which is probably classified as a Norwegian forest cat breed with very long hair. And I really need to comb him to ensure I can get whatever hair he sheds to control hairballs. This brush does do a fairly good job but with my cat specifically the problem is that the tines are all bent in one direction. Because of this you have to draw the brush downward on the cat in a smooth motion where the handle is always oriented downward in a line with your stroke. By that I mean you can't hold the brush sideways or you can't pull the brush upside down and just use it any direction you want you must always draw the brush downward in direction consistent with the handle, because else the bent tips work against you.

On top of that the tines are actually a little overly long. I found that if I partially engage the ejection button but I can much more easily brush my cat because it artificially shortens the tines. When the tines are fully engaged I basically catch too much and the they end up trying to bend versus navigate their way through the fur.

It's possible that I'll be forced to switch to a normal bristle brush and try that but I have had high hopes for this because I'm used to using stiff brushes in order to remove excess fur from pets before.

So I guess if I have one complaint it would be basically that I wish the times weren't bent and we're about 30% shorter. Then I could move the brush and any orientation I want and for my pet it would work very well. The deeper tines tend to get just caught and don't drag through the fur as easily.

reviews.title: Great fur release button
reviews.text: This tiny, round brush is in festive colors. The bumblebee pattern is indeed adorable. I especially love the one button fur release design. Simply push down to get rid of the fur pad is definitely convenient. However it's a little small to use when I tried to brush my pyrenes puppy. It works nice for smaller area that maybe I want to strengthen in brushing. But as for whole body I wish it's somewhat bigger to cover more range.

reviews.title: I love this
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:81a04eff843f7893740591f0fd77ef12]] This is the best pet brush I have used. Easy to hold and the bumble bee detail is an added touch of cuteness. The fact that one push and all the hair comes out at once, is amazing. So easy and simple.

reviews.title: Works smoothly, Bonus it's cute!
reviews.text: One of my cats is chunky so she doesn't give herself a bath that often so this brush works so well! It's well made and the best part is the push button to push the hair out of the brush. I hated having to pull the hair out of the brush every time. I think it also brushes well and picks up most of the hair on my shorthaired cats. It's not a huge area with the brush part so I think this one is best for cats and small dogs.

reviews.title: No surprises!
reviews.text: This product looks, feels, and works as indicated in the description; no surprises, and very easy to use and clean.

reviews.title: Super cute AND functional!
reviews.text:
With 4 kitties who share our home- two of which are super fluffy and all four are super 'sheddy' - I'm always on the hunt for ways to manage the fur tubleweeds that roll through our home! I ordered this brush because....well....I had nothing to lose! And boy - I was happily surprised!

Pros:
* The brushing area is nice size - it allows me to brush a large area at one time, which is key when dealing with kitties who don't love to be brushed.

* The bristles have tiny rubber tips, which is a small detail that makes a huge difference! This little 'bumper' seems to be much more appealing to our older kitties - perhaps their skin is more sensitive or fragile. Also, for our younger more rambunctious crew, it's not unusual to find minor 'battle wounds' (don't worry, nothing serious and always in good fun). Having this little rubber tip protector prevents from tearing off any scabs as they heal.

* It works! The 'self cleaning' aspect of this brush DOES work! With a wide variety of fur lengths and textures in our 'pack', I can attest to the fact that it catches it all, making it so much easier to keep clean. No more digging into the spikey needley prongs, ruining your fingers as you try to fish out all the pieces of fur! Hooray!

Cons:
* This isn't TRULY a con, but rather a small detail that would have made this tool even better....a cushioned brush. Several brushes that we've liked have 'cushioned' brush heads (where the bristles attach). This allows the brush to contour better to the kitty's body. That's the only thing I can think of that would have made this brush better.

Overall, this cute brush earns 15 paws of approval from our Pack! (One is a tripawd....)

If you find this review helpful, please let us know!

reviews.title: Efficient
reviews.text:
I love this and so do my cats

Pros- Easy to clean

Cons- A little big but not by too much

reviews.title: The cute isn't a cover up...
reviews.text:
A good number of times when you see something as cute as this little bee-esque slicker brush, the manufacturer is covering up a shoddy product and hoping the "awwww, it's SO CUUUUUUTE!!!!!" factor will overwhelm the buyer so much they don't notice and/or care when the product gives up the ghost after only a few uses. So yes, choosing this slicker based on looks probably wasn't the smartest of choices, but in the end it seems to be a good one. This slicker brush is rather nice (and should be for the $20 price tag). It's comfortable to hold. There is weight to it so this doesn't feel flimsy to where the grooming head will break off if you try to comb out a snarl in your pet's fur, or like the body is the thinnest molded plastic they could get away with so it didn't break just removing it from the packaging. Another plus is the end of the tines are tipped/dipped in protective little drops of plastic so the sharp metal ends don't harm your pet. I've always avoided those slickers as they seem like some kind of torture device - how can bare metal ends be comfortable when raked over skin?

For taking care of loose hair, this did a bang up job on our two Maine Coons, including the one who absolutely loathes - and I mean to the depths of H.E. double hockey sticks with a side of utter disgust - being brushed. She was adopted from the shelter as an abused kitty from the home of an older cat hoarder, and whatever happened during her first six years of life there were traumatic enough that she will do her best to eviscerate anyone who tries to groom her long fur beyond the area of her neck. She actually lets us very gently use this on the rest of her back and legs and tail for around 45-60 seconds before flipping out, and that's a big improvement! Who knows, maybe she's as amused by the itty bitty bee antenna as we are, but this is the first slicker brush (even with the safety tipped teeth) she's let us use on her back, sides, and legs. The other Maine Coon (a big chonker of a guy) lays on his back with his legs stretched out and purrs like a race car when he gets groomed, so it's not a surprise that he loves this slicker as well. Cleaning the hair out is easy. Just press the button, the plastic disk pushes up, and the hair is forced off of the tines for easy removal. A few of the finer hairs may stick to the plastic or around the tines, but nothing major. When the slicker gets dirty between the base and the underside of the plastic disk, one of those super skinny reusable straw scrubby brushes and a bit of dish soap does the trick. We clip a wooden clothespin on either side of the plastic disk to keep it raised so the tines can air dry and not rust from trapped moisture. Since the tines all seem to be securely anchored to the base of the slicker head, we aren't too concerned about one being pulled out while trying to work through a tangle. It's still a good idea to check the tines every so often to make sure they are all there, none are loose, and that none of the little safety tips at the end of the tines have come off.

The only thing that could improve this slicker is a price reduction. A pair of googly eyes wouldn't hurt, either.

reviews.title: Easy to Clean
reviews.text: The brush itself works fairly well on my short-haired kitties. It's not nearly as good as my Furminator, but my cats freak out with that brush. This gets the loose hair off, but it doesn't pick up everything. I actually brushed my cats against the fur growth. This released more fur. I do have to say, cleaning it is so easy. You just push the little button, and the plate pushes up past the bristles with the fur clinging to it. I think this would work great on thicker-haired pets. It's not terrible on short-haired animals, but it's not a miracle brush either. Overall, it's a pretty good brush with some limitations.

reviews.title: Great brush
reviews.text: Does what you want gets hair and easy to remove and clean

reviews.title: A handy and fun brush for my long-haired dog
reviews.text:
Honestly, it doesn’t really matter to me what this brush looks like, but it is very cute. :)

I am a sheltie person, and they get a thick undercoat and shed a lot, mostly seasonally. Did my dog love the brush? Well, she’s pretty particular and doesn’t really like to get brushed so much. But she did cooperate and it wasn’t annoying to her. As the bristle ends are covered in little plastic tips, this is not sharp if it makes contact with the dog or cat’s skin.

For me, I love it, It’s very easy to use, fits great in my hand, and the button to clean the fur off it is AMAZING. Just press the button and the fur basically ejects. It also has a good-sized brush head, so it provides wide brushing coverage, and it is very through and goes through tangles well. (Though you should cut any intense tangles first.)

You may notice that my review says: Vine Customer Review of Free Product
I try not to let that influence my reviews, because I’ve bought a lot of products over the years and I’m very price conscious. I do wonder about the price point of this compared to other similar brushes. This one looks great, feels great, and works great so maybe it is better than those that cost less (I haven’t tested those). But—it’s always best to compare prices and read reviews.

For me, I love this; it makes brushing easy, and will replace all my previous dog brushes.

reviews.title: cute and works great!
reviews.text: really gentle brush that works great!

reviews.title: Super Easy To Clean!
reviews.text:
This is the perfect brush to add to your pet grooming arsenal.

I use this brush as an indoor brush, I have another that cuts and lifts loose hair for outdoors. This brush is nice because of the trigger that easily releases all the pet hair. It is so convenient! It's a real cure design too. I was initially worried that the antennae on top would easily break off but the are made of a flexible rubber. The bristles are made of a durable metal with a white coating on the tips so they're not so sharp.

Overall, this is a great first pet brush, or a great addition to the supplies you already have. You can never have too many brushes. I recommend this to all the pet lovers out there.

reviews.title: How cute is this brush
reviews.text: I ordered 3 similar brushes of the 3 this one is definitely the cutest.  It's also a higher quality that the other 3.  It works well and is easy to clean.  This would make a great gift for a new pet parent!

reviews.title: Love it!
reviews.text: My cats love it! It’s easy to clean and it catches all the hair!!

reviews.title: Looking younger!!!
reviews.text:
I have never used a Skederm product, but I am pleasantly surprised about how much I love this product line. It is lightly scented and very soothing for my under eye puffiness. It has great instructions and is very easy to apply. I find myself using more than I should because it feels just so darn good!! I believe my under eye issues have improved - making me look younger!! YAY..what a great feeling. Thank You Skederm for making such a quality product at a reasonable price.
I purchased this product at a reduced price for a FAIR and HONEST review.

reviews.title: I was given a discount to review this product. ...
reviews.text: I was given a discount to review this product. It is a very silky cream...and definitely reduced the appearance of wrinkles! Win!

reviews.title: Rich Cream
reviews.text:
First off I have to say how great the SKEDERM company is to deal with. I had a problem with the container of Ulitimate Eye Cream and advised them about it. They quickly apologized about the problem and handled it wonderfully. I would buy from this company just based on this experience.
I really like this eye cream, I press a small amount into the palm of my hand. There is no scent, and it is a light cream color that I apply around and under my eyes in a Tapping Motion. Have only been using a short time but I think that it is getting rid of some of my puffy black circles.

This is a rich cream that goes on easy and quickly absorb, and I like it very much

reviews.title: Not good for my eyes
reviews.text: I guess my eyes are real sensitive to this. My eyes kept watering and stung. Dissapointed because I was hoping this would work.

reviews.title: Love this Eye Cream
reviews.text: Skederm Ultimate Eye Cream ~ I don't know that I've ever even said this before but I noticed a difference the very first time I used this eye cream! The packaging is very pretty. It's a beautiful blue cannister with a great little airless pump  which dispenses the perfect amount. It's 0.5 ounces but using it only on the eye area should last quite some time.  You only need a very little amount of this cream. It has a very faint scent to it. It's so faint I can hardly smell it at all.  The cream goes on beautifully. I have lived in Florida most of my life  so unfortunately I'm starting to see signs of fine lines above my eyes which I can't believe and they just make me feel older! The first thing people notice are your eyes, right?  They looked terrible and I wanted to take care of this quickly, if possible.  So when I applied the cream,  they immediately faded. Of course I know they're not gone but they're not as noticeable. My eyes also get a little puffy underneath  but the cream  has helped out with that problem as well.  I do have to say that it didn't soak in to my skin as quickly as I had hoped for but after seeing the results I can deal with that. It's not sticky at all! I thought it was at first and realized it was from one one of my vitamin C serums. This morning I scared myself looking in the mirror! My eyes looked I had been rough! So puffy underneath! I did not believe anything would help this mess out. Honestly! I used the cream and once again my eyes bounced back to normal. I mean, they looked so bad I didn't want to leave the house. This is by far my Favorite eye cream ever! I just can't say enough about it!  I've been using it twice a day and will continue to use this to see if there are any  permanent  results. Because of the retinol (vitamin A) I have hopes that there will be!  I received this at a reduced cost for my  honest  and unbiassed review of this product. I really like it and will definitely purchase it  again once it's gone as long as I continue getting good results from it. I do hope my review has helped you in some way.

reviews.title: Fairly good eye cream
reviews.text: Fairly good eye cream.There are many products out there and this one I feel is in the middle. I use many many products and rarely wear make up so I am always on the lookout for good products! Soaks in fast so if you do want to wear make up your good to go fairly quick! I have been using this twice a day morning and night. I have seen some of my fine lines go away so far so I am going to keep using and update this review...

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: good price

reviews.title: Im beginning to see the results I have been looking for for a very long time....
reviews.text:
Now that I'm in my mid 30's I have started noticing unwelcome changes in my skin. I'm seeing fine lines creeping up and dark circles under my eyes are nearly an everyday occurrence and my skin doesn't have that same elasticity and glow that it did when I was in my 20's. I seen SKEDERM and really wanted to give it a whirl. I have tried numerous under eye serums and treatments, some of them giving me great results and others end up being a complete and utter waste of money.
When my bottle of SKEDERM Ultimate Eye Cream arrived the very first thing I noticed was just how expensive the bottle looked to me. It really did have the appearance of one of those very expensive bottles of eye serums and treatments that you would purchase at a high end department store. I thought that the bottle itself could draw you in and make you want to purchase it. Very pretty.
As for the ingredients, it contains retinol, hyaluronic acid, and something called antarcticine. I had never heard of antarcticine before so I did look it up and found that it is a glycoprotein that is extracted from the Antarctic Sea, and it has anti-wrinkle and restructuring properties. Plus it contains moisturizing ingredients from rare Antarctic creatures that allow you to control moisture balance. Very cool stuff!
It can be used on pretty much all skin types like: dry skin, normal skin, oily skin, combination skin, and sensitive skin. Its recommended for use on aged skin, wrinkled skin, non-elastic skin, stressed skin, and dull skin. And sadly, my skin falls in almost all of those categories. Also, it is formulated WITHOUT synthetic colors, fragrances, parabens, phthalates, and sulfates.
You can use SKEDERM Ultimate Eye Cream every morning under your makeup or use it alone. Then you should reapply it on clean skin every night. It says to apply small amounts around your eyes using your fingertips until it is completely absorbed. And I will add that I little bit goes a long way. Which is good because that means a bottle will last a long while!
I have been using SKEDERM for almost a month now and I can see an improvement in the dark circles under my eyes. I usually have the appearance of someone that's always tired because of how my under eye area always looks, but its really starting to look better. I'm very pleased with that. My eyes are not nearly as puffy. I like that the cream goes on very smoothly and absorbs very well. I also like that it doesn't leave an oily residue after it absorbs. I also like that its fragrance free and my skin is extremely sensitive to a lot of different things and this worked great for me. A lot of times if I use eye serums or creams my eyes will water and my eyelids will get puffy, I don't know what causes it but I didn't experience that since using SKEDERM. I'm very happy with my experience and hope that after continuing to use it I will start to see the fine lines under and around my eyes will start to fade. So given my experience with SKEDERM Ultimate Eye Cream I would definitely recommend it to anyone with the same issues I have.

reviews.title: made my skin feel and look better
reviews.text:
The Skederm Ultimate eye cream has been working really well for me.

MY EXPERIENCE: I have some wrinkles around my eyes and dark circles. I’ve noticed an improvement in the area. The moisture benefit alone has helped minimize the appearance of wrinkles. I’ve also noticed that the skin looks brighter and healthier, like it has more elasticity. This moisturizer really penetrates deep into the skin cells rather than sitting on top of the skin. I can tell that my skin absorbs the moisturizer because it doesn’t leave an oily feeling and the skin feels a lot better.

BENEFITS: This has retinol, which is one of the few active ingredients for treating wrinkles. I’ve reacted badly to some retinol treatments in the past, but this one has worked well. The ceramide helps to retain a moisture barrier on the skin to prevent it from drying out. Collagen is what gives us that youthful appearance, so I always look for it in my moisturizers.
I am really happy with this moisturizer!

reviews.title: love it
reviews.text: great product will buy again, it makes your face really nice and soft i use it daily.

reviews.title: Wonderful Eye Cream - Delivers Results - Soothing and Cooling
reviews.text:
've been heavily looking into and researching Korean skin care beauty routine for about 4 months and trying a myriad of products. For years I have suffered from dry patches as well as acne breakouts ... which has perplexed me because I had never suffered from this as an adolescent ... only since I got into my 30s -- annoying and bizarre! So, I researched and researched and decided to give myself a go at the Korean skin care routine.

Skederm Ultimate Eye Cream is manufactured in South Korea, which is widely known all over the world for their cutting edge products in skincare .. so I was really hoping that this eye cream would hold up to other brands that claim the same regarding Korean skincare. Well, this eye cream is top notch ... it's incredibly soothing and cooling ... it hydrates like crazy and it absorbs into the skin very easily ... the texture is smooth and silky without being sticky and goopy. The scent is pretty much non-existent unless you really stick your nose up into it so that is a huge plus as the serum counterpart to this does have a strong scent, and while it is not an unpleasant scent it is just ... strong. This eye cream absorbs into the skin at a fast rate and really helps to plump up any under eye bags or fine lines you might have. I do really love it.

The Skederm Eye Cream has the following ingredients:

1 - It contains antarcticine. Antarcticine is a glycoprotein extracted from the Antarctic Sea. It's highly sought after because it has anti-wrinkle and restructuring properties. A bit of background information on how antarcticine was discovered and where it got it's name goes back to the Antarctic summer of 1988 in which a Spanish expedition went to the Admiralty Bay inlet (located near King George Island in Antarctica). The scientists collected mud samples and wound up managing to isolate and characterize a new bacterial strain called Pseudoalteromonas Antarctica. During its growth, the bacteria created an exopolymer of glycoproteins; an extracellular material that aids the bacteria to retain water, adheres to surfaces and has the ability to withstand the extreme cold.I ts protective function in nature has been applied to cosmetics and skincare products by scientists to protect and regenerate the skin. And there you have it ... your fun fact of the day!

2 - This eye cream also contains pure retinol which is a potent form of synthetic Vitamin A that helps to remove dead skin cells and prevent wrinkles. It brightens the eye orbitals and helps in improving skin elasticity as well as brightening dull skin tones and reduces pore size. Its anti-aging properties make it is a critical component to the success of maintaining one's skin.

If you found my review to be helpful please indicate so below as it helps me to know which of my reviews have been helpful to others. I make a lot of my purchase decisions based on reviews so I try to leave helpful ones myself. If you have questions, feel free to ask as I am more than happy to answer them as best I can.

Thank you!

reviews.title: Does wonders for Tired Eyes.
reviews.text: I have been searching for a new eye cream because my usual go to cream didn't appear to be working. I get up for work at 5am and I always look tired. Even if I got a decent night's sleep, I look tired. I keep this tube in the fridge. I wake up...go downstairs and put this around my eyes (I just pat it around), then I make coffee. While I'm waiting for coffee, I let the cream sink in. By the time I go back upstairs to do my makeup, the skin under my eyes looks SOOO much better. No yellow. No bags. No sunken in look.

reviews.title: Three Stars
reviews.text: Did not see a difference . Maybe my skin type. Although it does work for moisturizer

reviews.title: Very gentle
reviews.text: Very smooth and no strong scent that many other brands have. Love the soft feeling it leaves behind. It's not greasy either and rubs in quickly

reviews.title: Easy to handle
reviews.text: Sooth ❤️

reviews.title: A great purchase
reviews.text: Got this for my husband for Christmas. He’s had it a lil over a month and uses it about 2-3 times a week. It hasn’t rusted and has been holding up! He really loves this style and other razors we’ve seen have been $50+ so it was very worth it and a great buy!

reviews.title: High Quality Adjustable Razor
reviews.text: Awesome quality razor very well made.  Adjustment is very smooth and it is a great shaver with a quality feel.  I ordered this and the Qshave at the same time and they are identical razors, other than the Qshave comes with a small cleaning brush.  This is advertised as a "long" handle version but it is the exact same size as the Qshave.  Its well worth the price and I will probably be buying some for gifts, given the low price and high quality.  I love it.

reviews.title: ... this razor for over a week now and get awesome results. Gets a little to get used to
reviews.text: I've used this razor for over a week now and get awesome results. Gets a little to get used to, but the shave is close. I would definitely buy another one if i need to replace this one.

reviews.title: Not going to use
reviews.text:
This razor seemed to have a lot of promise but failed to make it. First off there are a few good points. The material seems fine with only a few minor flaws in the base material that are not seen unless specifically looked for. The finish is fine with no bubbling or flaking. The numbering is clear and easy to read.
The rest;
The head is way too big, nearly impossible to get hairs in area around nostrils.
The head does not hold the blade edge properly and causes flex/chatter.
The blade edge reveal seems incorrect. The blade edge seems too even with the comb so that when you adjust the razor you just get a wider gap that lets skin in but not more edge reveal. Just ends up harder to dial in a comfortable shave.
The head moves around too much causing uneven gap that you constantly have to correct.
Balance of the razor is terrible.

The included blades are acceptable, on par with your average blade but nothing I would purchase.

reviews.title: Third time was the charm
reviews.text:
Well I had tried two other versions of this same product, Q-Shave and River Lake and, despite the fact that they all likely came from the same Chinese manufacturer, this Manion razor was the only one that had a balanced gap alignment and blade reveal. So I lucked out, on my third try, before completely giving up on these Merkur Futur clones altogether. The packaging on this one is very basic. As far as I know, it does not come in a set with stand included. This one comes with 5 Dorco stainless steel blades. There are no instructions and no operational warnings enclosed. Had I not ordered the other brands, I am not sure I would have understood the proper way to remove the cover and load the blade. Please, please, whatever you do, DO NOT change the blade or adjust the blade gap with wet hands (you need to hold the head while you turn the handle to adjust the blade gap). Just so happens I neglected to adequately dry my hands, I went to adjust the blade gap, my hand slipped and I sliced my finger. Lesson learned.

As on all other models, including the Futur, settings range from #1-6. I would consider the shave to be very aggressive at settings above #2. The finish is smooth and unmarred. As others have mentioned, the handle can get slippery when wet. So just be careful and grasp the handle at the indent below the head for better control.

reviews.title: Excellent finish and good balance.
reviews.text: This is very good quality considering the economical price. The finish is smooth and the weight is balanced.

reviews.title: Ideal first double edge razor for Men or Women with adjustable setting from novice to expert
reviews.text:
I always wanted to try the Futura and my daily shaver is the Merkur HD which is an ideal choice for people that want to shave one pass and get to work, teamed up with Feather Blades black label 5 packs or yellow label 10 packs.
The Futura is almost $100 so that wasn't going to happen unless I knew I would like it so when I saw this clone for 12.64 I couldn't resist and I'm very happy with it. The design really is unique and but seems to work on this knockoff which appears identical to the Merkur version. The blades "dorco" it comes with are terrible at least for me and once I put my feathers in it provided a great one pass shave on setting 3.
My one knock on the razor is what makes it so handy especially for beginners the bulky head, it is near impossible for me to get right up tight under my nose so I use my HD for that otherwise it is a great razor. And I'm buying three more for presents. The Merkur version is supposedly heavier but that might almost be too heavy, this Razor to me is heavy enough, almost too heavy.

Women if you are considering a double edge razor this is the one it will give you great results safely and easily just put the razor on setting one. after the you recapture the razor cost for about 10-12 dollars a year you can have an ideal body shaving solution

reviews.title: Wunderbar!
reviews.text: For an adjustable straight blade, this is nearly impossible to beat.  You also get 5 Dorco blades; Dorco are some of the best blades you can buy.

reviews.title: Horrible shave. Cuts and razor burn all over my face.
reviews.text: Omg I've been using Gillette fusion for years and decided to try this and eliminate razor bumps. I now have razor burns all over my face and neck along with multiple cuts. What a waste of money and time. They don't even give directions on how to load althe blade!!!

reviews.title: A Sturdy Built Adjustable Double Edge Razor 🪒
reviews.text: I purchased the razor after I no longer could buy replacement cartridges locally for my Schick Hydro. Also,  I contemplated for years about trying a double edge razor but felt nervous about cutting myself. However, since this razor is adjustable gave me the confidence to try one. At first, I used the number one position to avoid any mishaps. After a few uses, I felt confident and used the razor at the number two position. I finally found for myself the sweet spot for a close and smooth shave is slightly past the number three on the adjustment dial. In conclusion, I am happy with the razor for the price and will never use any other type of razor as long as the double edge razor exists.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: It’s the best safety razor I own...

reviews.title: drop it and it falls appart
reviews.text: You have to put the razor in the lid then snap it  on the base. When you want to clean it you have to take it apart the reassemble it, If you thought it would screw open and shut think again.

reviews.title: What a Bargain
reviews.text: I have been using this razor for several weeks now and just love it. However, I'd shy away from the blades that came with it. Adjusting the blade is a breeze and it just plain looks classy.

reviews.title: Flexible shave settings; Use towel to assemble and diasseble razor to avoid cutting fingers.
reviews.text:
Pros
1) Easily adjustable aggressiveness of shave (such that less passess of the shaver over the face is needed to produce a clean shave when set closer to 6 of 6 where the safety guide is farther from the blade edge as compared to 1 of 6 where the safety guide is closer to the blade edge.

2) Silver plating of shaver works well with trig silver plated edge blades to avoid: skin irritation, skin inflammation and shaving skin leading to bleeding.

3) Sleek form.

Cons
1) Need to use a heavy face towel to remove head of shaver from body to clean blade and shaver body. Also need to use said towel to assemble shaver: On towel razor cover is first placed, then razor blade, then press razor mount attached to handle onto cover sandwiching blade. This way of assembling and disassembling razor avoids cutting fingers.

2) Razor mount on handle has two holes where debris from shaving can get caught as well as residual moiture from wet shaving. Need to rinse mount, then wipe dirh paper towel, then dry with hair dryer.

reviews.title: Good value but slippery.
reviews.text: Just got it in the mail. Did not want to spend excess money for something I might not like, so I figured the knock-off would be the way to go for now.  It feels hefty with most weight directed toward the head which I believe should help in the razor doing the work. I will say it’s very slick silky feel in the hand might be a negative. Instructions say to operate with dry hands, which I did but it seem to slip out of my hands unless gripping it well.  Have not shaved with it yet but already cut my thumb while adjusting the settings with a blade installed.  My dumb mistake.  I was attempting to mimic adjusting during a shave session.  In attempt at seeing which setting I might like. I would suggest for anyone that needed to adjust this razor to hold it upside down on a towel or washcloth then turn the handle to your desired setting. Don’t hold the top as it’s likely to slip from your holding fingers and cut you.  Be careful.  I’m glad I purchased this razor at the price I paid before spending way much more money on the German made one. Seems like a good razor overall for the value.

reviews.title: Gives me a predictable shave exactly when I need it ...
reviews.text: Gives me a predictable shave exactly when I need it. It is a bit bulky on the head and the handle may seem a bit slippery but all in all it provides a quality shave.

reviews.title: Great razor that can be as gentle or aggressive as you'd like it to be
reviews.text:
This clone of the Merkur Futur adjustable was way better than I expected. I've been using it for legs and underarms, primarily, for about 4 months. I find it to be built with good quality -- it provides a close shave, adjusts smoothly, and the head seems to be set pretty even on both sides. I found no issue even when shaving more oddly-shaped hills and valleys. I feel that the razor has pretty good grip as long as your hands aren't slathered in soap. It also comes with 5 high quality Dorco blades in a little plastic dispenser that can also be used to store used blades in a separate compartment until you're ready to dispose of it.

To clean and change blades, you have to be a little careful because you need to pop the top cover of the razor's head off to do it. I grab the handle firmly and turn the razor as though it's a tiny hammer, then use the other hand to gently pop/pivot the top cover off with the thumb and index finger.

One thing I learned from YouTube is that this and other Futur clones like the QShave -- you can adjust the head's gap width further, if you prefer a more aggressive razor. When you get to level 6, squeeze the entire razor head together then twist the dial clockwise again -- you will bypass the locking mechanism and reach level 1 again. You'll see the gap widen on the head. In this "mode", level 1 technically becomes level 7 and so on. I prefer mine this way, although the razor's perfectly good as it comes out of the box. To get back to the default gap width settings, move back to level 1 (i.e. 7), squeeze the head again and twist the dial counterclockwise until you're back at level 6.

I've not ever used a real Futur so I don't know how well it mimics that one in actual performance but, from what I understand, these clone razors mostly vary from the original in weight and minor design differences (apparently the original Merkur patent expired, making way for these clones). What I can tell you is that this one's a great deal for the money.

One other thing to note: This week, I began to really understand that we have a major condensation issue in our bathroom (There's no exhaust fan and only a tiny window.) While some other metal items began to show some rust, this razor is one of the few things that doesn't have a single spot on it anywhere. Given the price, that's definitely evidence of higher quality materials than I would have expected.

reviews.title: Manion M5 Matte Silver Adjustable Razor
reviews.text: Slipperiness issue solved by wrapping orthodontic elastics (dental rubber bands for braces) onto the handle. The razor itself has uneven blade reveal; thus one side provides a milder shave and the other side a more aggressive shave, Not a problem if used as a single edge, but the adjustability feature is mitigated. A fine value overall with regard to price / quality.

reviews.title: Close shave
reviews.text: Closest shave I've ever had without irritation. The smooth metal finish is slippery So be careful but other than that it's solid and well built.

reviews.title: Buy it!
reviews.text: I am 66 years old and this is the BEST razor I've ever used! It shaves close, and very fast. I can't recommend it highly enough. It comes with a bikini attachment, but it shaves wonderfully without it! Don't hesitate to buy it.

reviews.title: My first electric shaver - a win!
reviews.text:
I received this product as a gift in Nov 09 and have been very pleased with it. I have never had an electric shaver before, and thought it might be a time/energy/money-saver. So far, so good! It seems to work better dry, rather than wet, which is actually better for me (shower is too small to shave in anyway!). I learned to go slow, otherwise it needs a second pass, but I don't expect other electric razors are much different. The trimmer works pretty well, although I'm still getting the hang of it. The charger is small and tends to be unobstrusive; the cord is long, which is nice.
Overall, I've been very pleased to not be using a razor and cream!!

reviews.title: WIfe disapponted - I know Panasonic can make better products than this!
reviews.text:
Looking forward to Panasonic creating the same quality razor for women as they do for men.

This 'top of the line' electric razor was encased in plastic and hard to open. The materials used in the razor itself unfortunately matched that lack of quality. Cutting heads were rudimentary and did not provide a close or even shave. My wife is going back to a blade not because she loves it, but at least it works as an effective tool.

reviews.title: I want my Remington back
reviews.text:
I had a Remington wet/dry that finally got outdated and I thought to replace it. I did a lot of research and decided to try this Panasonic. I was really excited when it got here and the first time I used it I loved it and was all set to write a raving review. But I thought I should wait a couple weeks. I am very unhappy with it, as it turns out. I wish I had my Remington back (or got a new Remington). Yes, the Remington is a little louder. But this one is loud too - you can hear both of them from many rooms away. So really, that's not a big deal. I ended up getting another Remington and it is much better.

What got me is two things with this one:
