1) The sides that hold the razor in place are taller than the razor area. So when you go to get a close shave, the sides of the unit scratch/scrape your leg. It is plastic, so no blood drawn or anything, but it doesn't feel good. Has no one else noticed this? I went to do a quick shave on my calves and ended up with stripes from the sides of the unit scraping my legs. And it didn't even get the little stubble. I didn't think the Remington was that fantastic, but it did better than this. Someone suggested to put the side extenders down. I do see the extenders (per the angry reply to this post), but they are down all the way. The won't go any farther down. So your angry post is not applicable. If I did not apply force to my leg with the razor, the length of the stubble it left was ridiculous. Again, maybe very long stubble is just the way it is, but not with my two Remingtons. They got much closer without having to apply force.

2) It vents out the cut hair once in a while all over your leg. You could be shaving along la di da and all of a sudden get a huge area of hair. I thought "I already went over that area?" but it was just the hair that got spit out from the razor. It is a little gross, I'm always wiping the cut hair off my leg. I've had two Remington's that did not do this. The Remington might have left the hair on the ground, or kept it in its head for me to clean later, but I prefer the Remington that didn't spit out puffs of hair on my legs incrementally. Again, the angry reply to this about including a vacuum is a bit silly.

Another disadvantage is that this one's charger plugs straight into the outlet, instead of having a cord. So I have to find an outlet to leave it in to charge that my kids won't get to. I like the cord better, so I can plug the cord in behind my dresser and have it sitting on top of my dresser in the little charger base. This one, you have to put the razor in the charger directly into the outlet. I could deal with this though if it didn't spit hair out all over my leg or leave scrapes down my leg. Yes, you could get an extension cord, so it's not that big of a deal. Just one more thing to add to a poor design IMO.

reviews.title: Great razor
reviews.text: I've owned several electric razors in the past and this one is by far superior to the others.  It gives you a close shave without causing any nicks or burns like my old one.  It can be used either wet or dry and works well both ways.  I also like the fact that you can change the angle of the head to shave your armpits vs your legs.  Of course it will not compare to a regular razor but for a quick convenient shave, it works great.  You will need to use it every day or two if your hair grows quickly. Well worth the money in my opinion.

reviews.title: Not a good basic shaver
reviews.text:
At 62, my shaving needs are basic, so I have nothing to say about the bikini features, for obvious reasons. I don't particularly care to shave in the shower either. All I want in a shaver is for it to work well for my armpits and legs. I have sensitive skin, so I took a chance on this shaver; it's my first Panasonic, and my last.

The reason this gets two stars instead of one is that it does do a good job on my legs, and the shaver holds a charge well, for several shaves. That said, my primary concern is shaving my armpits without irritation, which it does, and without having to keep going over the same area to catch all the hairs, which it doesn't. You would think that with the rotating head that this contour feature would help, but this shaver will not give you a close shave under your arms without several passes. I don't even have thick hair, so if you do, I certainly wouldn't recommend this shaver.

reviews.title: Good for occasional use...
reviews.text: This is a pretty shaver; a nice lavender/purple color.  I like that it has the base that you can leave it on while unplugged, so storage is not an issue.  It shaved pretty close the first time, although, I would have to use it everyday to keep my legs looking halfway decent, and that is too time consuming.  It does not shave anything like a manual razor, nowhere near as close, so stubble is a constant.  But I do suffer from dizziness problems, so it will be good to use when I am too dizzy to risk getting into the tub/shower, or for quick shaves when going somewhere.  I will have to charge it before every shave, as the charge ran out halfway through my second shave, so I have to think of this ahead of time, no big deal.  It is a nice electric razor for what it does.  It did not scratch or cut my skin, even around my knobby knees.  Just keep in mind that electric razors do not shave anything like a manual shaver; no where near as close.  If you grow hair quickly like I do, this is an issue.

reviews.title: Really happy with this shaver
reviews.text: It's worth paying a little extra for this one. It's much much better than the last shaver that I had.

reviews.title: Would never go back to regular razors...
reviews.text:
I've had my eye on this for awhile, and finally got it for Christmas. There were a few key reasons I wanted one. First, wet/dry shaving sounded great, especially dry without wasting water down the drain. Also, the cost of replacement razors for the venus and similar items is ridiculous, so I'd only change it when it got very dull. Plus, I hated throwing away the plastic and adding to the landfills. The reviews of this seemed promising, and I'm glad to say, I definitely agree!

The razor works really well dry shaving, and its so much easier then balancing precariously on one foot in a wet shower. Not too loud, once you get started you forget it's making any noise. According to the package, you need to replace the foils once a year and the razors in the machine every two years. Imagine the money I'll save! Especially if you go thru multiple 8 packs of venus razors a year. It doesn't get as close a shave as the regular razor, but thats not a big deal for me. I'll use this on a normal basis and get out the razor when I need the babysoft feel.

I'm a big fan, and will never go back to using traditional razors full time.

reviews.title: Fabulous!
reviews.text: I was tired of nicks and the hassle and waste of water (I live in the desert) of shaving with a blade. I was wary of buying an electric shaver because I have purchased both shavers and epilators in the past and found they did not work and/or were just too painful to use. Having read the reviews for this one on Amazon, I decided to take a chance since the price was right. All I can say is, WOW! I am really impressed by this shaver! It is not quite as close as a blade shave, but really good. I saved a lot of time, and a lot of water (even better!). It also did not irritate my underarm area like some electric shavers I have used. I am very pleased with this purchase and would highly recommend it to others.

reviews.title: Save Your Money
reviews.text: I was not crazy about this product, 3 months later it hardly works at all. I have to go over the same patch of skin before it cuts all the hairs which leads to skin irritation also it's not a close shave so while it worked I found myself having to shave every morning.

reviews.title: Great Little Shaver
reviews.text: This shaver works extremely well.  I get a very close shave and it leaves my skin silky smooth.  It is easy to clean.  I have very sensitive skin and have had no outbreaks or razer burn from this little shaver.

reviews.title: Excellent Woman's Electric Shaver
reviews.text:
This is the best electric shaver I have ever owned. Besides the fact, it is one of the best electric razors. The price is more than fair beause I have seen the same exact item for more money than what Amazon charged for it.

I would definitely recommend this item.

reviews.title: great for legs!
reviews.text: I love this shaver. It charges quickly and stays charged. It removes leg hair painlessly without leaving stubbles. The grip is easy to use and it is easy to clean. I travel with it as well. I didn't find it that helpful for removing underarm hair but it is fantastic for the entire leg, leaving a soft smooth feel to the touch.

reviews.title: Great choice for girl's first shaver
reviews.text: It was time for my granddaughter to begin shaving. But razors can be a bit scary with sharp blades! Even though an electric shaver does not shave as close as a blade shaver, it also does not nick! This is a pretty device (I ordered the mauve, kind of a pearlized pink color) and is easy to hold and to use. It holds a charge for a long time. For closest results, go over the area in every direction and do both straight and circular movements.

reviews.title: Highly Recommend!
reviews.text: I have never written a product review online before but my experience with this razor definitely deserves one!  I recieved this on Wed, charged it and began use on Thursday.  I have used it once wet and one dry and loved the results of both!  It is quiet, provides a close shave already (claims to improve over three weeks!), and gives me NO razor burn.  The whole reason I bought this razor was to try to find something that would eliminate razor burn for me and I had yet to succeed after trying all kinds of epilators, razors, depilatory creams, and other electric razors.  This finally has me happy!  It may not give the perfect smooth shave that my sister always seems to get right out of the box but I'm very satsified!  I was never able to shave more than twice a week before so this will give me a closer shave with a better experience hands down!  Can't wait to see what happens after the 3 week point!

reviews.title: works as promised
reviews.text: It was nice that it came already charged. I have used it several times and have not needed to charge it. I like that the charger is small. It sits in the outlet and the shaver sits on top of it. I have a small bathroom and I don't want another bulky base and extra attachments laying around. I use it when I don't have time to shave. You can still feel the hair but you can't see it.

reviews.title: Short Battery Life
reviews.text: Very nice razor, close shave, but battery life is nowhere near Braun's. And the fact that I can't leave it on charger is very annoying.

reviews.title: Cool Shavings!
reviews.text: I used to use a Braun Flex shaver and had been disappointed with the shave I received. I had been wanting to try the new Norelco-Phillips shaver for some time, but felt since I had paid so much for the Braun, I would wait until I got my money's worth from it. Then when I ran across the deal from Amazon, I purchased the Norelco-Phillips and have been satisfied with the shaving experience ever since.

reviews.title: Gets the job done.
reviews.text: Works with regular shaving foam if you don't want to use the pricey Nivea cartridges, has no problem getting wet in the shower. This is what I needed, and what I got. The shave isn't as close as a blade, and you'll want a mirror if you use it in the shower. The battery life is fine and the device styling is a little nicer than that of most electric shavers.

reviews.title: perfect
reviews.text:
Love the fact you can use this in the shower..makes my mornings go a lot faster

Great close shave and the gell is just right..letting the blades glide across you're face

Good battery life but why not just charge it after every use

reviews.title: I always come back to Norelco.
reviews.text: this time I sticking to Norelco! I've had the Remington shavers but didn't use them for very long. I've used Norelco longer and a blade sometimes, but this particular "Cool Skin" shaver is great! I think the green color makes it look cool too. I have an older "Advantage" that I have used and liked it very much. The "R" brand shavers are loud and vibrate too much. I let my beard go for 3 days and the "R" shaver didn't even get all of it even with a preshave lotion. I did the same with the Norelco and with the lotion it got it all the first time! Price and features are great. I will probably give my old Norelco to someone, or just keep it for old time sake. :) I'll put the 2 "R" shavers out to pasture. This new one works great in the shower too. I've even used shaving cream with it, but the lotion is better. I think it would be nice if the lotion came in a bottle. The cartriges work great too. Great for travel. Buy this shaver and you'll like it a lot.

reviews.title: He actually uses it !!
reviews.text: My husband HATES shaving. He usually gets a rash and ingrown hairs. So he shaved once a month whether he needed it or not. Well, he LOVES this shaver. He says it is gentle, gets all the tricky spots and no rash. And the gel smells nice, not overpowering. While he still doesn't shave every day (must be a guy thing), he uses it without prompting pretty regularly. Definitely a great find.

reviews.title: Shaves as good as a blade - if you use it right
reviews.text:
This is the second 7775X I have owned. The first lasted two years, but this after extensive travel all over the world. It does shave as well as a blade (I use one of those too now and then) if you use it right (see below)

Positives

1) Quick charging - less than an hour to charge.
2) Long lasting - I have used it 10 days without recharging - great if you travel.
3) Seems to prevent ingrown hairs - which is something I run into when I use a blade consistently. My face definitely breaks out much less when I use this razor versus a blade (and I own some expensive blade razors as backup!).
4) So long as you follow instructions (don't press down hard, but when you need to go over an area again, do it firmly) you won't get nicked. Nice when you are in a strange hotel in a strange time zone and waking up jet lagged but needing to look presentable for a meeting.

Negatives

1) A little expensive
2) The shaving lotion cartridges are also expensive - I would just buy a can of regular shaving Gel (eg Edge).
3) Constantly pressing the dispenser for more gel can be a pain in the neck, and you go through those cartridges quickly (one every 10 days at most). If I wanted to redesign this shaver, I would junk the shaving dispensor entirely - I now find it just another moving part with the hinged lid.
4) I wish Norelco had come up with a way for this razor to use a standard charger jack of some kind, as having another electrical cord when I travel (especially one that does not work with multiple corded adaptors such as I-go) means I don't take the cord unless I am gone more than 8-10 days.

To use this right, just buy regular shaving gel, put it on your face, wait 30 seconds for the gel to soften your beard, and go for it. This is the best way I have found to get a clean, smooth shave. Gel in a can also has the advantage of going on thickly (versus the Coolskin dispensor) and not drying up quickly. Also, having a can of the shaving gel when you travel (checked baggage of course) can also come in handy if your Norelco runs out of charge, and you need to switch to that backup Gillette you should have in your shaving kit (I travel with enough cords as it is).

reviews.title: Not a close shave...older model works better
reviews.text:
I ordered this shaver for my husband to replace his older Norelco Quadra. After trying it, we are returning it to Amazon. He says his old Quadra works better than the 7745X Cool Skin. It looks cool and the wet shave is nice but it takes longer to shave and the closeness is not up to expectations. The new shaver should shave as good or better than the older models. The shaver does work so if you don't have an electric shaver, this might be okay for you. My husband has dark, coarse hair and the blades just weren't "cutting it." Fine, light colored hair might improve the performance but, my advice is to look for something else in the same price range.

Maybe our expectations were too high or the shaver really is lacking in performance. There was only one review when we made the purchase and maybe this will help others out there before money is wasted.

reviews.title: Works as Advertised
reviews.text: The Norelco 7745X works as advertised... I've only had it for a few weeks though. Cleaning is easy and it shaves close.  The colors are a little bit loud.

reviews.title: Great shaver.
reviews.text: I bought this shaver for my boyfriend for Christmas this year, and I was really nervous because he has never used an electric shaver and I really wanted him to like it, even though I bought one in the mid-price range. I thought this would be perfect because he shaves in the shower, and I didn't realize most shavers aren't for use in and out of the shower. He has used it about four times, and even though the manual says it takes around 3 weeks for your face to adjust, so far it is wonderful. He has fairly corse, stubbly black hair that needs shaved often, but with this razor, his face feels just as smooth as I'm used to. He says it doesn't get quite as close as if he were to shave up and down with a razor, but it's not a huge difference, and we won't be returning it. I think it was definitely worth the price I paid on Amazon, and I hope it becomes available again so other people have the opportunity to try it.

reviews.title: After 30 Years of Electric Shavers This One is Tops
reviews.text:
I've used electric shavers for a long time. Some were good (gave a close shave) for me, some not (I have a medium weight beard with light brown hair color). Although I never experienced much discomfort with electric shavers, starting about two years ago it was, for whatever reason, becoming a constant problem. I was getting a constant neck rash no matter what shaver I used. I saw this shaver, tried it and love it.

The closeness of the shave is as good as I got from a prior Norelco Quadra, and I thought that was the best I had had from electric shavers. I use the Nivea lotion and I have had absolutely no irritation since the day I started using the shaver. While it may, as some reviewers have noted, take a little longer to shave then with a razer, I have not noticed any increase in shaving time over prior electric shaver.

I did run into one problem with my first shaver. A small plastic part became dislodged (the part where the lotion comes through when you push the pump) and try as I might, I could not reconnect it. I contacted Norelco service and they directed me to their service center. Although it took too long (about 6 weeks), they sent me a brand new shaver without cost, which I've used ever since without incident.

If you suffer from any kind of shaving irritation, I highly recommend this model.

reviews.title: Norelco 7745X Cool Skin Razor
reviews.text: A great razor, especially if you like to shave in the shower. I somehow lost a previous, earlier model that also dispenses lotion, which is why I bought this one. The battery would still hold enough charge for 2 weeks of shaving even after 6 years if care is taken to fully discharge the battery each time. I anticipate this one will be the same. It currently holds a charge for 3 weeks of daily shaving. The improved design of the top of the razor ensures no water residue remains if hung to dry, and I really like the anodized aluminum vs. the plastic of the older model. The gel packs last awhile, but I use shower gel as a lubricant so they're only used for traveling. I don't care for the case as much, and the replacement blades are expensive (~$25-30), but once a year isn't too bad and still cheaper than disposables or blades in the long run. A great razor!

reviews.title: Close as a blade, but..........
reviews.text:
I bought this shaver about a month ago because I'd had enough of cutting myself with my viscious Gillette Mach 3. Three cuts at a time on the cheek. Ouch! The good news is that I have no regrets buying this Cool Skin and I don't think I'll be going back to the Mach 3 any time soon. Like all things tho', pluses and minues:

Pluses:

- No more cuts.

- Shaves as close as my Mach 3 with less irritation.

- I can shave in the shower, which is absolutely essential!

- Great looking shaver, and one of the few that doesn't look like my grandfather's. The green color is super cool, and my wife thinks it has even improved the look of our shower!

- Very easy to clean under running water in the shower.

- One charge lasts a week or more.

Minuses:

- Takes about 2 - 3 three times as long as shaving with a razor.

- I don't mind the cost of the Nivea refills that much (see below), it's the waste generated by the empty sachets that bugs me. I wish they could have developed a refillable dispenser.

- The "advanced LED display" is pointless. Do I really need a flashing light to tell me to rinse the razor at the end of every shave? Or do I need another light that flashes to tell me I can pump lotion just after the thing's switched on? If it weren't for that great green color I could have bought the cheaper model and got the same functionality at less cost. All you really need is a low battery indicator.

- You pump the lotion or gel onto the heads with your finger or thumb. Using my finger gave me cramp in my hand. Using the thumb is OK. A more advanced design would be to have the lotion delivered automatically.

Finally, what about the lotion and gel? I found both worked fine. At a pinch I've also used ordinary shaving foam and even just water applied directly to my face. The most comfortable and closest shave is however obtained using the gel or lotion. I just bought a 5-pack of lotion at Target for $4.99. One sachet lasts me 4 or 5 shaves if I'm fairly frugal with it. So that works out at about 25c per shave which isn't too bad.

All in all recommended for those who are thinking of switching from razor to electric.

reviews.title: Happy husband
reviews.text:
I bought this to my husband for christmas over a year ago, and he still uses it. Works well. He does not use the foam packets so much. just shaves under the shower.
I prefer when he uses a manual razor, but this one still does a good job.

reviews.title: Wife really loves it
reviews.text:
Facial Steamer Nano Hot Steam Face Spa
Device Ionic Face Steaming Machine for
Home Facials Personal Moisturizing
Humidifier Vaporizer for Skincare Beauty

My wife has always wanted a facial steamer but didn’t want to spend too much money. So,
when I saw this one, I thought it was a good deal. So far, she really likes it. She says that the
water heats up quickly and the steam coming out is smooth and doesn’t spit out any water. The
little cool mist steamer will come in handy on hot days, which will be coming up soon here in
California. Even though this comes with some acne removing tools, my wife has not used those
so cannot comment on whether they are effective or not. The steamer and mister, however, has
been something she really loves. Five stars

reviews.title: Small Unit, Good Results
reviews.text: This is a small facial steamer that has had good results for my daughter.  She has another unit that must be 3X bigger than this unit.  This unit is approx 5.25" x 3.5" x 6.5" tall.  This is a compact unit.  We were first concerned that this would even work but after a few uses she is very happy with the operation of this unit.  The unit includes the main compact unit, a small hand held unit, a mask, and 4 tools.  I took 1 star away because of the price.  She is happy with the results.

reviews.title: Quick and powerful
reviews.text:
It took a while to get this review together because there was a lot to unpack with this steamer and all the little extras that came with it; however, what stood out most was how fast and powerful the steamer itself was.
If you were thinking you'd be waiting around a few minutes for this thing to start producing steam, chances are you will be pleasantly surprised by how little time it takes for the device to start pushing out huge clouds of steam.

While the instructions that come with this set are detailed, using the device itself is fairly straightforward.
Simply be aware that the steamer needs to be plugged in to work; moreover, you will probably want to sit during its operation so that you can lean over it for a few minutes at a time.
That means you'll want to find a place where you can use the steamer comfortably, like a vanity or desk.

Other things to keep in mind as you consider buying this.
- The handheld, portable, rechargeable device is a mister, not a steamer. This wasn't completely clear on the product page, so I want to make note of it. Basically, the mister is a nice thing you can use to cool down and refresh your skin on the go.
- Packaging suggests two different types of water for the steamer and mister respectively.
- steamer = distilled water
- mister = mineral water
- The steamer's cord is about 2.5 feet long, which is something you will want to consider when decided where you will be most likely to use the steamer.
- The little extras like the headband with ears and the pore clearing tools make this a great gift to give someone who enjoys getting and giving facials.
- The manufacturer warns that acne which is hormonal in nature will not respond to the steamer
- The instructions also advise you not to use the steamer everyday, nor more than 10 minutes at a time. Accordingly, this is not something that is likely to add a lot of time to your daily routine, but rather something you will use once or twice a week.
- The steamer itself is cute and compact, so you're not giving up a huge amount of space if you get one, nor do you necessarily have to hide it in between uses, unless you want to.
- The steam produced is hot/warm and there is no way to control the temperature; however, you can easily regulate how you experience the heat simply by adjusting the proximity of your face to the steamer.

Overall, I would definitely recommend this facial steamer set as a purchase for yourself or as a gift for someone else who enjoys giving/getting spa treatments at home.
I personally like the effect it had on my skin; I felt as though using it has helped with both the cleansing and hydration of my skin.
Once you get through all the instructions, procure the right type of water for your steamer, and identify where you want to set it up, you will be rewarded with an easy to use machine that produces copious amounts of steam very rapidly

reviews.title: Obsessed
reviews.text: I absolutely love this little facial steamer. It's a great size, and heats up the water very quickly. I also like the tools that this comes with. I feel like the headband is a touch too small for my head, but it is very cute. After using it, my skin felt hydrated, and it really opened up my pores. The reason I'm giving it 4 stars is because I wish the cord was a bit longer and thinner. I have to use an extension cord to use it at my makeup desk.

reviews.title: Makes the skin hydrated as well as Soothes the sinuses
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:3ea33c1e85d8d1a8ce0fd7987edd95af]] This facial steamer heats up very fast (in 30 seconds as stated in the manual) and is very easy to use. You just add some distilled water or purified water and turn the steamer on. Since the steam is hot, you need to make sure that your face is away enough from the nozzle. I like how the steam comes from in a direction that covers the chin and the face. There is no option for adjusting the level of the mist but I do not think if it is necessary since the amount of steam is good in this device. There is a manual inside the package that I found really helpful. The water tank is small and for me, the tank size is holding the exact amount of water I need for each usage.

The steamer can be used for hydrating the skin. It also opens the pores and is good for deep cleaning. But the thing I really enjoy while using this steamer is that it is very helpful for my Sinusitis. The warm mist soothes and moisturizes my sinus passageway. So, for me, it does not only make my skin hydrated but also unblocking my sinuses.

I also like the portable facial steamer. It is chargeable which is great. It is not that much powerful and it is releasing cold mist but, it is a good size for travel. Surprisingly, this tiny humidifier works for about 10-15 minutes. The other things in the package are also useful to have a nice Spa at home.

All in all, I like the whole set and to me, each part of this package is useful and works great.

reviews.title: Nice facial steaming product, with handy portable misting device.  Decent performance.
reviews.text:
At a spa, people go into the steamroom to relax muscles, open pores and detoxify. This device isn't a steamroom but aims at providing some of the same benefits to the face and neck, especially regarding the skin and pores. This device heats water up to 104F, creating steam which is expelled from the golden rim outlet. Unfortunately it cannot handle the addition of essential oils, recommending only pure water (or weak alkaline mineral water). This would have been a fantastic ability and function. Oh well. As a pure water streaming device, it is a decent performer.
It does not take long for the water to heat, about 30 seconds, and a fine steam mist is pushed out from the device. The optimum range is 6 - 10 inches away from the steam port. It's a good idea to start at a 12 inch distance, and ease your face closer, gauging your skins tolerance.
(Note: you can add toner that can fully dissolve into the water. The key is not to have a viscous liquid, or particulates, which will adversely affect the atomization.)
We found operation to be fairly simple and easy. Fill the water reservoir, put reservoir back into it's position. turn on the unit, within 30 seconds face gets a spa steam experience.
Is this as effective as those face steamers that have a cone that keeps the steam contained? No, because the steam floats freely. But, it's also multiples cheaper than those kinds of facial steaming devices. This is a credible portable device, that is compact enough that it won't take up much counter space. It's minimalist design will allow it to fit in with most decor.
It's easy to set up and operate. It's acceptably effective.
Also included with this steaming device is a mini misting device, a headband (with pink ears) and 4 skin care tools (intended to help treat acne and blackheads).
The mini misting device does not heat the water you put in it. Rather, via a rechargeable battery, a touch of the button will spray a fine mist. This is very handy in the summer, when you might want a spritz of water to help cool you when outside. It's an apt complement to the facial steaming device.
I found the facial steaming device to be capable of decent performance. If you have realistic expectations, you'll enjoy this product. And, I like the rechargeable portable misting device.
Overall, a worthwhile product set, which earns a 4.5 - 4.75 star rating, with a high level recommendation.

reviews.title: Works Well
reviews.text: I love using a facial steamer on my pamper routine days.  The one I was using broke and I needed a new one.  This one is compact and does what I need.  At first I thought it was going to be too small, but it works just a well as my larger one did.  It is easy to use and easy to set up.

reviews.title: I love it!
reviews.text: Awesome product! Great for hydration and opening pores. Gets face ready for serums and moisturizer. Love it!

reviews.title: Worth It!
reviews.text: Great product. Great quality.  Exactly what I was seeking!

reviews.title: Small but powerful
reviews.text: The steamer is easy to use. It warms up the water quickly and it is powerful in delivery the steam. I do wish that there was a way to adjust the nozzle but overall very happy with the product. I really like that it is small and it also included a mini humidifier as a bonus.

it heats up really quick.
