reviews.title: Good product
reviews.text: Good item for the cost would recommend

reviews.title: diminishing breakouts
reviews.text:
Not Or Omega 7 Sea Buckthorn Oil Soft Gels,
Anytime Face Wash, Facial Defense Oil, Skin
Cleansing, Skin Moisturizing Core Kit

This facial cleansing wash, with sea buckthorn oil soft gels, and the facial defense oil, seem to
work really well together. My wife and daughter both get small bouts of acne during the month
and they’ve both benefited from this kit in diminishing breakouts and gently keeping their skin
clean and moisturized. They both want to continue using it and see if the soft gels make a
difference, but until then they are very happy with this kit. Four stars

reviews.title: a more natural approach for acne prone skin
reviews.text:
I am familiar with the Sibu brand as years ago I used to purchase the sea buckthorn oil supplements and was taking that religiously. I stopped just because I started to take other supplements (pre-natals, etc) and it was just getting to be too much I had to cut out some of the stuff. I still really like the Sibu brand and their sea buckthorn oil. I was pleasantly surprised to see that they now have skincare products. My favorite product in this set is still the sea buckthorn supplements (60 softgels). I am still nursing so I won't be taking this just yet but I am just about done nursing. The supplement is one thing I am looking forward to taking again. The expiration date is listed on the bottom of the bottle. I do like the smell of the anytime face wash, and it did a nice job of cleaning my face without drying it out. I do get little hormonal breakouts here and there so this is a good face wash for me. I've also tried the daily facial defense oil, a zit zapper, but it is too soon to tell if it works. It does a great job of moisturizing my face for sure as it is packed with different kinds of oils (argan, sea buckthorn, lavender, patchouli, etc). All these products are made in the USA, which I know is important for some people. This is a good set of skincare basics for acne prone skin. I like the more "natural" approach with the use of sea buckthorn oil and less harsh chemicals. This product came in a cardboard box (like a subscription style box) and placed inside a bubble foil zip loc style bag. Good packaging.

Some improvements ideas for this product:
- supplement does not say how many to take per day. The supplement facts says "serving size 2 vegan softgels", so I am assuming that means I should be taking 2 pills a day, making this a 1 month supply. There is no information pamphlet in the box or in this Amazon description page
- ingredients list for all 3 products should be listed on this Amazon page, I didn't see it listed yet
- brand name is confusing and made it hard for me to do more research on this product. Is the name of this product line "NOT OR" or "Not for him or her" or "NxO" or "NO"? It is just all very confusing. Either way, all of the names still made it hard to do a search because it sounds like it is a part of a sentence.

reviews.title: Great Set
reviews.text: This is a very nice set of sea buckthorn oil products.  Sea buckthorn helps with a variety of skin problems.  My main issue is age spots.  The topical products help right away, by softening and moisturizing your skin as well as reducing any redness or discoloration.  The soft gels help long-term by repairing from within.  I think it's a great idea to have an entire set of products packaged all together.  It's nice not only for yourself, but would make a great gift for somebody this holiday season.

reviews.title: Three piece set
reviews.text:
Not or Omega 7 skin cleaning kit comes with soft gels, face wash and a facial oil. The three products work together to give soft clear skin. The face wash has a nice texture, and the facial oil is light and absorbs quickly into the skin. The gels are easy to swallow.
Have i noticed any change in my skin? It feels less dry and tight, but I have not noticed any improved glow or anything.

reviews.title: Sea Buckthorn Beautiful
reviews.text: This Omega 7 Buckthorn set offers an outstanding trinity of products that tend to the basic necessities of body care. I love the unboxing and presentation of this set which can be a true gift for either yourself or another. I think that for anyone with normal to dry skin, this set would be a dream come true. As someone with oilier skin, I found that the cleanser and oil caused my face to breakout. I think if the cleanser didn’t have the exfoliant, my skin would be able to handle the product much better since it’s often the microscrubbers that get stuck in my pores and cause breakouts. Alternatively, using it as a bodywash/body oil is my new staple. I have been using one of Sibu’s other softgel supplements for well over a year and have been very happy with the internal hydration support and these softgels included in this set work just as well.

reviews.title: I'm With Everyone Else
reviews.text:
I would love love love this product if it were much cheaper and had reduced packaging.

The face wash is refreshing. It doesn't dry out my face and leaves a clean, bright scent.

The daily defense oil is absolutely lovely. I'm a big guy, so 7-8 drops is what I need to get the job done. It leaves my face feeling hydrated and smelling great all day long. It's really the star of the show.

The Omega-7 capsules remind me a lot of some enteric coated fish oil capsules I've used in the past. Easy to take without any fishy burps. I just can't say that they make a difference to my skin. That's the hard part about using preventative products. I know I haven't had a major breakout, though.

The box in which these things came is huge, could maybe fit 3x these products. My mom ended up using the bag for makeup, so there's one way to reuse.

At $90 for the set, this stuff isn't cheap. Quality facial products in general aren't cheap, but this is definitely on the higher side of things from what I've seen. I don't think I'd personally get this again, but it may be great for someone with much more severe acne who's tried everything else.

Edit: Still using the products much much later. A little does go a long way.

reviews.title: 3 ways to help the skin
reviews.text: The packaging on this kit is quite nice! I actually thought it was something else at first. Out of the 3 products, I love the face wash. It smells great and is a light exfoliator. The pills are a nice add-on, but I am not sold that they do much.  The oil, not lotion, takes a bit to get use too. It absorbs quickly and once it is on, it doesn't feel greasy, just not as much moisturizing as I was hoping for.  Nice product if you have normal skin, I tend to need something a little thicker for dry skin.

reviews.title: Good quality skincare!
reviews.text: So let’s see how this works! My skin isn’t super sensitive and I just need a good, simple routine and this one seems to fill the bill!

reviews.title: Mixed Message
reviews.text:
Some organic ingredients: 30 day supply of organic Buckthorn softgels 500 mg each.
Facial defense oil: mostly organic oil.
Anytime face wash: lots of chemicals in this one with citrus, lavender, and others for fragrance.

All of it comes in a lot of plastic (although the dropper bottle of oil is glass).

Looks like something from the movie "The Graduate" where the guy told Dustin Hoffman to get into plastics. He could have also added cardboard. Ugh. Everything here will go straight to a landfill. Then it will leak into the ocean and pollute fish. Then we'll eat it.

I guess they are charging a small fortune so that you pollute the planet quicker. What's wrong with their noses? The people who put this together must have lost their sense of smell using dryer sheets or something.

Why use any organic ingredients if the rest of it is horrible?

reviews.title: Omega  natural
reviews.text: Nunca he visto un producto que sea tan natural como este. Mi piel y las de mis hijas se ha mejorado rápidamente ya que la piel lo absorbe y me encanta porque es muy lindo.

reviews.title: Awesome set
reviews.text:
I am extremely impressed with this skincare set. First off, it is presented like a gift, which just tickled me. Everyone likes gifts, and receiving this in the box with the cool carrying pouch and the compliment on the inside of the box felt great! I really like this touch. Second, the products themselves are great. Obviously, that’s going to be the major selling point, but it always helps when companies treat their customers really well from the onset, so I was already feeling great about these starting out.

Now about the products. The soft gels are awesome. Those are what I started first. I actually got this kit because I have dealt with blepharitis for over ten years. If you have ever dealt with this then you know how extremely difficult it is to treat. I’d heard that one effective treatment at controlling it was sea buckthorn, so I was willing to try this. The soft gels are medium size gels, and I started those right away, the day I received the kit. They are easy to swallow and I simply added them in with my other supplements.

The next morning I started the wash. The wash has been nice. I use it both on my face and to cleanse the eye area thoroughly, and on my back where I have some issues with adult acne. My back hasn’t really had any change with this product, but my face and eyes have seen some benefit already. My eyes in particular are looking a ton better, which is not only a relief but also super amazing. I am going to continue to use these products and we’ll see how things go. I hope that there will be continued relief and improvement the longer I use them.

I admit that I haven’t used the oil on my face, mostly because I am just not used to an oil on my face, but I have used it on my body and I like it. It has cleared the chronic breakouts I used to get on the back of my neck and so far they haven’t started reappearing. These products are winners, and the fact that they address more than one issue is so awesome. Given the progress I’ve had so far, I definitely recommend this kit.

reviews.title: ingestable capsules, topical oil, and face wash; really expensive, may not be worth it for some
reviews.text: This buckthorn oil skincare kit includes three items: 60 softgels of buckthorn oil, a dropper bottle with a topical oil that includes a bunch of different stuff, and a facial wash. I think, for me, this kit is probably not worth the expense. I do like the buckthorn oil capsules, but I’d honestly rather consume buckthorn oil as a food as it’s pretty tasty. The moisturizer and face wash are fine, and I don’t have any complaints about them, but my face is naturally very clear, and an inexpensive moisturizing cream works fine for me. These items didn’t make my skin clearer or more supple than it already was. Thus, it’s not worth $90 for me. My wife’s skin is also quite clear, and my 14 and 9 year old children also have clear skin, so I don’t think any of us can benefit in the way that someone who might be prone to acne might benefit, in which case maybe this stuff is worth it if it’s as effective as other products and easier on the skin.

reviews.title: Cleaner is excellent.  Rest ok.
reviews.text:
The cleaner works well and dispenses easy in a pump bottle.
It smells like the orange cleaners I used to use.

My face didn't decline or improve using these products after 2 weeks. It was in very good condition for a 60+ year old woman in New England.

The moisturizer goes on in drops. I found it easier to directly put drops on my face and then rub it in. It was a bit too greasy for me and it didn't absorb completely. It worked best on days I was wearing makeup. It seemed to meld in with the make up. If I wasn't wearing make up the moisturizer was still on the surface of my face hours later.

I would buy the cleaner again, but not the pills or moisturizer.

reviews.title: Nice Brush | Similar To We Love Doodles & Chris Christensen | Cats Love It | Easily Removes Hair
reviews.text:
If I didn't already own & love my "We Love Doodles" brand of big slicker brush, I would have rated this brush at a full 5 stars. Mostly what I don't care for this brush vs Doodles is the handle. It is a little more narrow so it's a teensy bit less comfortable to hold. I have carpel tunnel. I've been grooming cats furever & grooming English angora rabbits for well over a decade.

🐱For short & long haired cats, this brush works great. All of my cats love this brush & my Doodles brand. I can comb all of them except for my 14yo with a surprising amount of pressure & they roll around purring. Tons of hair gets picked up into the tines. Unlike some styles of brushes & combs, no hair is left behind around the tail area.

Removing the hair from the brush is very easy. Start at one side from the bottom to loosen & then move to the other side. Keep working your way around & it comes off in a nice clean loose mat. I actually prefer these types of slicker brushes over self cleaning brushes & also over brushes with the little plastic beads on the ends. If you groom your animal properly, you don't need little balls on the ends of the tines. Those balls also make it a little harder to get the brush fully clean of hair.

Cosmetically, I like the look of the brush much better than my Doodles brush. This is all black with a more classy branding in small white text on the head. We Love Doodles is blue & loudly brands itself on the head.

🐰I don't recommend this style of brush for English angora rabbits. I have found that my bunnies seem to be more comfortable with smaller slickers with shorter tines. You'd think with their dense long coats, you'd want something large with long tines, but it really doesn't work out like you'd expect. At least for me it doesn't.

🐰Recommended for English Angora Rabbits:

🐇Doggy Man sells a small soft slicker brush that my angora rabbits love. It's plastic with a little blue ring on the handle. I have purchased/replaced many of them over the years (the handles tend to break over time) because they work well.
🌐 https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B001NLVFH2
🐇I also recommend if money allows, a small slicker brush by Chris Christensen. I've never needed to replace it.
🌐 https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B00GU7NKWW

Personally, I'd shop between this brand (Rita's), We Love Doodles, & Chris Christensen. Chris Christensen is top of the line. They are worth it if you have the money. We Love Doodles has been the middle ground for me. They were originally priced at $50, but the price has been a lot lower for a while now. Rita's is currently the second most expensive & probably the lowest quality of the three brands. However, I've been using it for the least amount of time, so it's too early to know for sure. So far I like all three.

Chris Christensen: Made in Germany $75.99
https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product//B00KAJTPZ2

We Love Doodles: Made in USA $29.99
https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B09G981NXV

Rita's: Made in China $36.99

reviews.title: Nice brush!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:a512021e054bf612bf9a5fb5fc70a2c2]] Nice brush, easy to use, easy to clean, everything I wanted and needed in a brush, and our puppy is loving it! At first a bit hesitant as it is a little rough but that didn’t last long and as you can see she is throughly enjoying it!

reviews.title: Good for shedders
reviews.text:
Now that we are in the summer time our furry friends have begun to shed. This brush is doing it's part to ensure all that loose fuzz doesnt end up on my floor or in my air vents.

Its durable plastic with metal bristles. Catches the loose fur fairly well with hurting my dog one bit.

reviews.title: Great pet brush but difficult to clean
reviews.text: This is a great pet hair brush and both my yellow lab and my cats love it. It has much longer bristles/pins than previous brushes I've used so it can hold more hair before needing cleaning. This also is the cause of the negative for this brush though which is that it is quite hard to clean out compared to brushes that have an ejector feature that pushes the hair off the bristles. If this brush included that feature it'd be perfect.

reviews.title: Works wonders
reviews.text: Have an Aussie and this is a must have during shedding seasons.

reviews.title: Decent slicker brush
reviews.text: I got this brush to use on my maltipoo dog.  The brush does a good job of fluffing up her hair and making her feel super soft.  The brush seems like it is well made. She doesn't fight me on brushing her with this brush the way she did with another brush we had that has those little balls on the ends of the pins.  I was concerned that she wouldn't like this brush because the ends are unfinished pins, but she seems to like this brush better.  It also seems to go through her hair with greater ease than the other brush we had been using.

reviews.title: Extremely handy
reviews.text:
I bought these because my pup has weeping eyes, which can cause problems if not taken care of and can also stain his fur. I bought these to help with his weeping eyes and have used them consistently for a few days now. I didn’t really think a wipe would make that much of a difference, but he doesn’t look like he has weeping eyes at all unless you look closely.
Aside from weeping eyes, I have found these to be quite handy all around. My pup sometimes has trouble not getting a mess on his fur when pottying and these are clutch in those moments. It is also nice for cleaning off his paws after he has stepped in something that has gotten them dirty.
Overall, I bought these to help me with one purpose - taking care of my pup’s weepy eyes, but wound up finding them useful in a number of cases. These are definitely worth having and comes in a doubly-sealed package (the snap top and the tap that lifts up and reseals after use), which keeps them fresh. I highly recommend these and will purchase in the future.

reviews.title: GREAT for De-knotting Long Fur - Pricey
reviews.text:
We have an aging 16 year old long haired Main Coon cat, and she's starting to get clumps/knots in her fur. Our regular brush just wasn't really doing it well enough at preventing/removing the knots. So I figured I'd give one of these "lotsa fine bristles" brushes a try.

What a difference! In just about 30 minutes, across 2 separate brushing sessions, I was able to pull out a TON of old hair and clear out several knots. You really kinda have to do short strokes with this as there are SO many tines and it'll try to pull too much at once. But what a difference it makes!

Only complaint is the price - it's far and away the most expensive brush I have, for people or pets!

RECOMMENDED

reviews.title: Great Detangler!
reviews.text: Very large, great for any big doodle or poodle that has lots of knots and tangles. Its a very effective tool for starting a dematting process if your wanting to keep the majority of the coat. The long bristles make it easy to catch onto any knots.

reviews.title: works, but not self-cleaning
reviews.text: This has longer than average tines, so it got down into my double-coated dog's fur. The tines are a little sharp. But it is not self-cleaning, meaning there is nothing to press and collect the fur. Because the tines are extra long, I had to empty this brush less often than with some, but it still was a pain to stop and pull the fur off. I have gotten spoiled with the self-cleaning ones.

reviews.title: Long Pin Slicker Brush
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:3dbb4f6afc88c6e7ba97851f064ad126]] The brush is well crafted and the pins are longer than normal. It fit comfortably in my hand and easy to use . I could easily pull hair from the pins when done brushing.

Wanted to try this on my shih tzu and found that the long pins are not the best for his shorter puppy cut. and was awkward to use on that length hair. .Recommend for dogs with longer hairstyles as has a nice quality feel and pins attached securely.

reviews.title: We like it!
reviews.text: Including a photo of my dog for reference since she's a cutie and to show her type of hair -- as an Aussiedoodle she has a longer, wavier coat than some dogs which means our pup needs to be brushed every day. We ordered this brush after our old one gave out and we do like it! I felt like it got out her tangles without being too rough on her skin, but it was a little difficult to remove the hair from the brush after we were done with it since the bristles are so close together -- HOWEVER the bristle spacing is somewhat of a positive at the same time because it means it gave our dogs coat a very fine, very through brushing. All in all, we like the brush and I think it's very pretty!

reviews.title: does get a lot of loose hair, hard to clean
reviews.text:
I got the Rita's Pet Supplies brush for my Malinois, who sheds a lot. I had been using a brush bought from the pet store, that has the plastic tines with little balls on the end. This one was supposed to get more hair and be gentle, so I tried it.
It did get a lot of hair, and my dog seemed to be fine with it. She even turned over to let me get her belly, so no complaints there. Cleaning the brush was not that easy, as it has a large height and width, so pulling the hair out of it takes several attempts. Also, the price is about twice what I paid for my other brush, which did just as good a job. So, while this is a fine brush and does its job well, it does not justify a price that high.

reviews.title: Perfect shaped dog brush
reviews.text: The handle of this brush is perfect for brushing out a dog. And it cleans out very easily. I felt like it did a good job brushing out my aussie and got quite a bit of hair off of her. I would definitely recommend  this brush!

reviews.title: I want to love this but it needs improvement.
reviews.text: As my title says, I want to love this but it needs improvement. First of all, my brush came damaged out of the box, I was able to luckily bend the bristles back. This brush brushes great, it gets out deep mats, and does a great job on my Pomeranians thick hair. However, it is big, bulky, and very uncomfortable to hold. I found myself often holding it from the head rather than the handle because the handle hurt my hand. The handle needs a redesign, and I may even see if I can just remove it. The bristle are very long and are the kind that can stab your fingers, I used a comb to clean them out.

reviews.title: Great for my double doodle!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:49f61449a2ae453ec990179babbbc0a0]] This is the only brush that works on my Bernedoodle/Goldendoodle! He loves it as you can see if the video. Gets the rats and mats out efficiently. Strong enough for the knots but gentle enough for his sensitive skin. A win for all of us!

reviews.title: Large ,Pricey but works really well.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:efb67b6214cb220065ddee3a814cbb21]] I am always dealing with shedding year round especially our longer haired cat.

This brush is larger and gets the job done fade making everyone’s coat look soft and beautiful and best of all with its long bristles, it does a great job of pulling out any loose hair.

This is perfect for fluffy, longer haired pets.

It is a bit more expensive and that is why o gave it four stars but it may be worth it just to get all that loose hair off all out four legged friends.

The bristles are not too stiff and the pets enjoy their brushing with this new Rita’s slicker brush.

Very happy with our new brush especially because of the larger size and long very fine bristles, works really well.

reviews.title: True CC Big K Knockoff
reviews.text: I am a professional groomer and this brush is amazing! It looks, feels and functions just like the CC brush line. Just like with CC, the handle is a little small for my hand but I adjusted. This brush has cut my brush time almost in half!

reviews.title: Does the job
reviews.text:
I currently have an older wire brush I wanted to replace for better comfort of my cats. I chose a few different types to try out so my review will include comparisons. This brush was most similar in size and shape of my current brush It works just as well but has coated tips which don't poke the cats' skin. They also don't poke my fingers as I try to remove the hair! After a brushing session, this item is as effective at removing the fur as my old brush though I notice the cats aren't as perturbed by the bristles. The size and shape are comfortable, the weight feels decently balanced and the handle is a rubber material that fits well in my hand. I love the push button feature and don't know why I didn't try one of these out sooner. The extendable plate makes it so much easier and comfortable to remove the hair than trying to pull it out of the non tipped bristle brush and always pricking my fingers in the process. And, even if the plate/spring wear out and I have to manually pull out the hair from the bristles, they are tipped so not a problem! I will not go back to non-tipped combs! Overall, this brush works well and I am pleased with it. As a standalone, it's perfectly fine and effective. Having tried a few other types, though, I notice its button isn't as easy tp push as others. This will be a personal preference depending on buyers' strength limitations. The only other note is the price. It's still a bit more expensive than other similar brushes, so assuming all perform the same, I'd go with the cheapest you can find since this one doesn't really have any unique features to justify the higher cost unless you really need a more contoured handle.

Pros:
--Decently balanced. Maybe due to the larger handle, the weight feels more evenly distributed
--Rubberized grip and has some contour shape to it opposed to some others
--Easy hair removal (push a button and the plate will lift off the hair)
--About the same size as my current (old) brush
--Safety coated tips for better pet comfort and no accidental finger pricks
--Works as advertised and expected to remove hair

Cons:
--Higher priced than similar products
--Button takes more strength to operate (see tips below) compared to others
--The plate (at least on mine) doesn't quite extend past all of the bristles. Nothing that makes the brush ineffective just noting a difference between this and other models I tried.

Tips:
--If you have limited strength in your fingers, you might want to look at a button that has less resistance or a brush that has the release button on the handle to use have more grip control. I found this button takes more effort than 2 other brushes I tried out. The easiest button to push out of the products I used was the green and white brush from guliguli.
--If you need or want the more ergo handle, this one is definitely nice and out of the 3 I tried, was the only one with rubber and some shape as opposed to a straight plastic handle.

reviews.title: Average
reviews.text: This pet brush works but for the price, I have found other products that worked better for less.

reviews.title: Great brush
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:a08bbd2c4100f12adbb383964a0e228a]] I have 2 cats (1 calico not pictured with short to medium length hair and 1 black with extra long fine black hair pictured).
This brush works miracles on them. It is gentle on their skin yet removes the extra hair that’s shedding.
Both of my cats love when I use it, it’s their special spa moments.
The brush is priced well. Great quality.

10/10 recommend

reviews.title: Works amazing!
reviews.text:
She loves to be brushed with this Milo brush. When she sees I grab it she comes sit next to me.
And I love this brush because I can easily push off the hair without even touching it.
It’s a great design and easy to use.
Great quality too.

I definitely recommend it!

reviews.title: Great brush!
reviews.text: Great brush! I used it on medium hair dog and it was perfect. There’s a button where you can push and it pushes all the hair to the top of the brush. The brush itself is soft and won’t hurt your dog! Highly recommend

reviews.title: It IS a brush.
reviews.text: Our puppy is terrified of it, the cats like it...my son's dog is fine with it.  I'll just have to keep trying to find one that works well for our puppies coat (Pyrador).

reviews.title: Nice brush if your dog has fairly easy to comb fur
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:d8c4188c3a8b20cec0a8f90c9a1aa483]] So, this is a dog brush with a gimmick. Namely, it has a little push button on the back to clear the fur from the comb. The attached video shows you how it works.

I have a Cavachon (a cross between a Bichon and a Cavalier) which has hair, not fur. The hair is fairly fine and as it grows long, it tangles. The longer it gets, the more snarls it gets so we thought this brush would be great to make picking the fine hair clumps out much easier.

The Good:
1) The push-button cleaner actually does a good job, it lifts the back above the bristles and you can whisk away the trapped hair with your fingers.
2) The soft rubber tips on the end of the bristles are a lot less "poke-y" than metal bristles that are just cut wire.
3) The rubber handle is easy to grip and does not slip in your hand.
4) After just one brushing, my dog's hair is super soft, so it seems to work.

The Bad:
1) This is a pretty expensive brush to be as flexible as it is. The brush always feels like it is just about to break when I pull on snarl. There is no piece of metal or wood in the handle, and it is super flexible to the point that it feels kinda cheaply made. Without stiffness in the handle, you don't get much leverage on the tangles, so it seems to pull more on the hair rather than pulling through the hairball. I seemed to have to tug on it more, which might cause more discomfort to the dog than my wooden handle brush.
2) I don't really need to clean my brush that often, so having to pull out the hair in my old brush just wasn't that hard. I am more comfortable with my stiffer wooden brush with similar teeth for less $$$

The Ugly:
1) After the second brushing, a wire got bent to the point that when I used the cleaning thingee, the bristle got stuck beneath the cleaning thing and bent even worse as the cleaning thing retracted. I figure after several uses, there is a good chance that the cleaning gimmick will be useless.

Overall rating: B- I really wanted to love this brush. All the sadder that it failed to live up to my hopes. However, if you have a dog with fairly straight fur that sheds a lot but doesn't get too tangled, this might just be the perfect brush for you.

reviews.title: Nice product
reviews.text: Used this today on both my cats. Did a good job overall and love that it is self cleaning.

reviews.title: Cats love it
reviews.text: Easy to use, and bristle tips are coated slightly to be gentle. It gathers quite a bit of fur, and its simple to clean it, push the large button on the back, gather the fur and dispose of it.

reviews.title: Cats love it
reviews.text: Easy to use, and bristle tips are coated slightly to be gentle. It gathers quite a bit of fur, and its simple to clean it, push the large button on the back, gather the fur and dispose of it.

reviews.title: Keeps up with brushing my 8 cats and dog!
reviews.text: The bristles have a little bend to them which makes them more comfortable for brushing. The easy push button on the back pushes the hair up to the end of the bristles for super easy hair removal. No digging out the hair with this brush!

reviews.title: Tiny plastic covers on the bristles make it more comfortable for some cats
reviews.text:
We have three cats with three very different coats. The black cat's coat is sleek, shiny, and seems to have fine little hairs. He barely sheds, and his hairs are short. He loves being brushed with just about any brush, for the interaction, the petting/grooming connection and the scratching action. However, he doesn't really need to be brushed.
Our medium-to-long haired snowshoe Siamese seems to have sensitive skin; she Hates being brushed near her achy back hips, and will complain and whip around to smack at you if you brush her very hard at all. However, she sheds a TON, and needs to be brushed almost every day, so she doesn't get clumps by her arthritic haunches (she actually gets dandruff flakes that her hair clumps around and knots up). She is at least 14 years old (we're not exactly sure), and just can't reach to groom her back end well, any longer. She loves being brushed around her head and neck though, even with our old "scratchier" long-tined brush. You can fill that brush 2-3 times easily, brushing her for just a few minutes, every day. This one, because of the tiny soft covers on the ends of the tines, doesn't seem to dig at her as much, and you don't have to brush her nearly as gently - meaning that you get far more of the clumps out when they are small and just beginning to build.
Our orange cat has extremely soft, short-to-medium hair, and you hardly notice that she sheds much. But when you brush her with the longer-tined, flexible but sharper-ended bristles of our old brush, you fill up the brush surprisingly quickly, and she lets you brush all over. So, she may be the only one that we continue to use the old brush on.
Our old brush sort of pulls the bristles into it, towards the handle, when you push the button on the back of it, to clean it off. The bristles disappear inside the brush, and you can peel the hair off like a little blanket and throw it away.
With this new brush, it is more like the flat base with holes around the bristle seats, comes forward, to the end of the bristles; when you push the button on the back, it pushes that base away from the handle. This may end up bending the bristles less, and keeping the brush usable longer. But it also doesn't compact the hair and make it just "peel off" in one fell swoop. It is more likely that you will leave some hair behind on the brush with this one. It is Worth the trade-off of it being slightly less easy to clean well, because our older, sensitive cat likes it soooo much better, and she is the one who really needs to be brushed the most. This brush is lighter, and feels less substantial than our old brush. Again, we will keep the old brush for the kitten. But this new one is perfect for Sagwa (the old cat), and I am very glad we tried it. If it does crack or bristles break off, because it seems less sturdy, I would buy another one just to keep her happy.

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: Works well, doesn’t harm the cats and is easy with hair removal. Great buy!

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: Works well, doesn’t harm the cats and is easy with hair removal. Great buy!

reviews.title: Brilliant for blending wool roving!
reviews.text: I ordered this to help me mix colors with the wool roving I use for needle felting and it works perfectly! Win!

reviews.title: Helped with my dogs shedding so much
reviews.text: My dog at first was not happy about being brushed, but he has an issue anyone brushing him. After maybe about 9 minutes of us introducing the brush to him we were able to get a lot of brushing done. My dog has longer hair and it a little thick but fluffy towards the ends (see pics), the brush did not bother him, he actually enjoyed it after a while. The button on the back of the brush has helped so much with quick brushing and not having to sit there and dig each hair out. This has helped him not have so much shedding either. It has only been two days since we started but we already see the difference. My dog loves it and so do we!

reviews.title: Pretty gel colors
reviews.text: These are so pretty. The glittery look is great - especially for younger women. Personally, I like the marbled colors. So, the girl and I will split this set up and she can have the colors that are more age appropriate for her and I will have the colors more age appropriate for me.

reviews.title: Nice colors very small bottles
reviews.text: Packaging is nice love how the bottles shows what color of the polish your picking.  Nice color glitter polish however they are not long lasting.  I did my nails in both matte and gloss look and within two days my nails with the gloss top coat peeled off.  My base and top coat bottles were relabeled so I don't know if that had something to do with it or not.  The nail polish is thick because the glitter so you will need to give it a good shake or tap it a bit in your palm.

reviews.title: Beautiful vibrant dense colors
reviews.text:
All of the colors look amazing! I tested a few of them on a piece of white cardboard and can't wait to try them all! Unlike other gel polish brands this one gives you two ways of easily identifying the colors. Each bottle has a clear opening in the front of the bottle as well as a color sticker on top. Those are very useful but some colors are still hard to make out so I had to open them to get a better look.
I applied the base coat and found that it was a bit thicker than expected. I have used some other brands of gel base coats and the consistency is generally thinner. Most of the polishes are light but not to the point of being watery. It's the same consistency as non gel polishes which I prefer. However, some of the glittered polishes that have confetti included are pretty thick and almost chunky. I'm not sure that it will last long based off the consistency at first use. I hope they do last because those are some of the prettiest colors. I love that unlike other brands of gel polishes that I've used, this brand's glitter polishes are not opaque. It's both shiny and the color is dense. I put on the color A708 first cured it then topped it with A707 while leaving some of the color A708 visible. I then layered it with the color A712 on a diagonal so that some of the color A708 would still be visible. I know the colors are hard to make out from the photo. I realized after the fact that I probably should've started with the color A707 or A712 first. I used number A707 and A708 alternating on the remaining toes. I finished it by putting a gloss top coat and cured it with the UV light. I set it to 60 seconds for three rounds just to make sure the polish dried fully. It has been two days and so far so good. The photo of my toenails were taken on day two.
Overall, I love the color selections. They're very pretty hence why I chose to use 3 colors at once. I can't wait to use the rest of the colors. I really love this set. I like to paint my toes colors that are compatible with the current season so these color selections are perfect for my seasonal needs. I wish the bottle sizes were a bit larger but I guess they make up for that by offering a lot of color selections. Also, you don't need to cake on the polish. A little goes a long way. The only drawback is that the base coat seemed a bit too thick so it may not last, but it could be an isolated case with this set. It's not a deal breaker though, which is why I'm still giving it 5 stars. Plus you can always buy another base coat.

reviews.title: Love the colors
reviews.text: Loving these colors! Easy to use, everything is labeled. My only flaw I saw was the UV/LED light needs to run a few times to really dry the nail when the color and top coat is added as its smaller than my hand so you have to move it to get all of them done

reviews.title: Excellent quality. Very attractive assortment of confetti and glitter polishes. Small bottles.
reviews.text:
This is an excellent assortment of attractive, quality gel polish, with base coat and top coat (no wipe and matte versions). The presentation elevates the worth as a gifting option. One of the best features of this set is the bottles of gel polish provide actual representations of the contents. On the top of the cap is a sticker with proper identification of the color (including glitter, confetti varieties). Also on the front label is a small circle, under the brand name, that has an accurate portrayal of the contents. Finally, a company gets it. So, this boosts the rating a bit.
There are 16 varieties of gel polish, with confetti and glitter. The consistency is perfect, allowing application of a thin coat. An ability to apply a thin coat is essential, mainly due to the size of these bottles. They are 5ml (0.17fl oz), which is one of the smallest bottles of gel polish that i've encountered. This is actually a disappointing size/quantity of gel polish. Based on the size of the bottles of gel polish, I consider this to actually be a sampler set, as opposed to a set of 'normal' sized bottles of attractive confetti, glitter gel polishes. So, i guess one can try out the various polishes and then purchase larger bottles of desired varieties.
Overall, I feel all of the polishes in the assortment are visually attractive. The polishes are excellent quality, and produce excellent results. The significant other tested the gel on some fake nails, and i commented that it looks beautiful as is...no need to add any embellishments/decorations. She agreed., but said she'd combinde solid color gel polish with these, and add some rhinestones or other decorations, maybe create a marbled effect. I could see her creative juices flowing.
The product does match the descriptions and pictures in the product details. The presentation is effective and top notch. We like the quality and consistency of the gel polishes and base/top coats. If the bottles were at least the size of the base/top coat bottles, this set would be perfect and worth 5 stars. As currently configured, based on observations and impressions, this quality 'sampler' set earns a 4.5 - 4.75 star rating, with a high level recommendation.

reviews.title: Good glitter set
reviews.text: I have the 'confetti' set and I have to say I like almost all of the colors.  The colors with the thread in them aren't my favorite due to the texture but the overall look is cool.  Nice thickness on the polish.  Good applicator brushes and normal dry / set times.  You do get base, top and top matte coat.  Really good sized set for price point.

reviews.title: So many options!
reviews.text: I've never tried this brand before so I was curious to get to testing. Before using on my nails, I did some testers to see the outcome. The confetti ones I did on a white gel color base, one of the glittery ones I did on black. I love the grey with the subtle glitter in it. Everything applied smoothly with no lumps, except for one (not pictured in the testers, but the top left in the company's photos). That one was a bit odd but maybe for someone with more finesse and skill. This set comes with base, top and matte top coat. The glittery ones have good pigment to them, you don't need any color base to make them pop. In my photo, I only did one coat. I was pleasantly surprised by the outcome. I do my own and some friends' nails on a regular basis. They're super excited to do the green for St Patrick's day and the confetti ones for Easter. This is such a great set, with so many options, I'm very happy!

reviews.title: Takes a long time to dry
reviews.text: So I used a salon uv light for each layer. The polish was still pretty sticky. I had it set on 120 min for each layer. After all 4 layers I had to do it 3 extra times. It still wasn't a solid dry. But I do love the glitter colors. I gave it a 3 because of the drying issues.

reviews.title: Beautiful set!!
reviews.text: This has such nice colors and glitters! There are only about 2-3 regular colors. The rest are glitters and I did notice there were a few that looked like duplicates but will have to see how they apply once in the nail. I love the variety of the set and you don’t need to do multiple coats. I also like that the bottles have a little clear circle to preview the color!

reviews.title: Used it twice and the polish started coming off after a few days.
reviews.text: I would not recommend. I have used cnd shellac for years and this does not even compare.

reviews.title: Beautiful and unique set
reviews.text: I've used the white with blue and red sprinkles, which looked super cute with a red and blue triangle shaped french tip, the blue one with fibers, which was cool, and the lighter green glitter which was gorgeous. I'm going with the blue glitter next. Great set, recommend.

reviews.title: Great gel color set
reviews.text: The colors in this set are amazing! They are easy to apply and dry perfect!

reviews.title: Some interesting Gel Polishes
reviews.text: This jiadel gel polish set brings some gorgeous unique colors to my collection. It is very pigmented, I only swatched it once and it was full coverage. Some of the polishes in this set are very unique like the ones with fur or hair on them, I’ve never seen polishes like that before. They apply smoothly and are a nice good thick consistency. Do not let the size of the bottle fool you, these gel polishes may be packaged small but they pack a good punch. I definitely recommend this to beginners or people who like adding unique shades to their collections.

reviews.title: Super tiny bottles that gave me an allergic reation
reviews.text: I am super disappointed in this set, the bottles are extremely small, like 1/4 the size of normal polish, so right off the bat they are hard to use because they are hard to hold onto.  The colors and glitters of this are lackluster at best and you never several coats.  I tend to have allergic reactions to gel polish except some brands.  I dont know which chemical it is that I have a reaction to but I had a allergic reaction in just sampling these on a few nails  - my cuticles puffed up and in equal parts itched and burned.  These are a no-go for me.

reviews.title: Awesome unique colors
reviews.text:
This is by far the most unique set of gel polishes I've ever seen... I only wish the bottles were bigger! They're only 5 mL, but you do get a lot of options so I think the price is still fair. Depending on your preference, you'll probably want 2-3 coats of each color; they're all pictured in 2, and do cure in 60 seconds. There are colored stickers already on the top of each bottle, but I don't feel they accurately show the colors inside, so I still added swatch dots.
The black velvet box does rub off on the white top / base coat bottles, but that's okay. One of these white bottles also had some color on it making me wonder if I possibly got a set that was returned, but I honestly like the color selection far too much to take off a star even for this.

reviews.title: Variety of glittery gel nail polish!
reviews.text: I really like how glittery these are! They'd go on quite easily. I did two coats and it look amazing. I also like the petite size bottle it comes with, perfect for training practices. They cure super easy nothing complicated. I've gotten glittery ones before and they were chunky and thick. These were very thin and not chunky at all. It's not hard to paint onto my nails. I enjoyed adding a little splash of color when painting the gel nail polish on with some extra bling to it.

reviews.title: Allergy
reviews.text: I am severely allergic to this I developed an allergy for whatever is in it and it hurts so bad I do not recommend at all the pain I’m in is terrible

reviews.title: I love this on my first try
reviews.text: Love these. I was confused at first that my base coat wouldn't cure well. I found out that each coat should be tacky for the next coat. The no wipe top coat is awesome. Did my first set for New Years. Gold on all, with an accent of light brown / dark brown. These are on my natural nails, which is why there is uneven lengths. My first try so I didn't take the time to shape and even the lengths.

reviews.title: Best shaver ever
reviews.text: Any Braun is the way to go. Best shaver ever, all of them.

reviews.title: Not bad
reviews.text:
After reading a lot of reviews i decided to go for it.
Overall i am happy with it, the only 'cons' are:
- have to cover an area a lot of times to get a really smooth shave. A 'quick shave' is simply not an option.
- it pulls on longer hairs. Best to use the trimmer first if there are long hairs or you are in for some pain.

Pro's:
- once done, the shave is incredibly smooth
- love the trimmer
- no burn, the adjustable speed really helps
- cleaning is a breeze
- battery life and charging speed are great

reviews.title: BACK TO PANASONIC
reviews.text: SEVERAL YEARS AGO I HAD A BRAUN I LIKED VERY WELL.  SO I THOUGHT THIS NEW SERIES 7 WOULD BE THE LATEST AND GREATEST.  I COULD GET A BETTER SHAVE WITH A JOHN DEERE COMBINE.  LAST YEAR I BOUGHT A 4 HEAD PANASONIC NANO TECHNOLOGY ETC AND SHAVES OK BUT IT IS VERY CLUMSY AND YOU HAVE TO WORK TO GET A GOOD SHAVE.  I HAVE TRIED NORELCO ALSO AND SENT IT BACK AFTER 90 DAYS.  I WENT BACK TO THE BEST RAZOR I HAVE EVER OWNED, A PANASONIC ES 8109, IT IS A 3 HEAD NANO TECHNOLOGY ETC.  BEST SHAVE EVER AND YOU DON'T HAVE TO WORK AT IT.  MY OLD ONE FINALLY GAVE OUT AFTER MANY GOOD YEARS.

reviews.title: great shaver
reviews.text: This shaver works great.  Close shave. No irritation.  Simple. I doubt I will ever go back to using a blade.

reviews.title: great shaving experience
reviews.text: Braun 7-790cc: Very gentle, fast, and close shave. Compared with my old shaver (Philishave HQ7415) 10x less skin irritation and 3x faster. 5 stars for the shave. Positive surprise: You can charge the shaver without the cleaning station, only with the cord, and also use it with the cord when it is not charged. Disadvantages: a) Cleaning with tap water is clearly more tedious than for simpler shavers (like my old Philips). b) Doesn't bother me, might bother others: It is louder than other shavers. c) "Hidden" costs: 5 USD/month for the cleaning cardridges and from the 19th month of use on additional 2 USD/month for the replacement shaving unit when everything is used as recommended. But compare the high cost for Gilette Sensor blades plus foam! d) Pure cosmetics (but if you buy the Mercedes you want perfect design): The lowest bar indicating battery charge has a red frame, so red = bad. At the same time, the best bar for hygiene status has a red frame as well, so here red = optimum. Doesn't make sense. In addition the green LEDs on the cleaning station have some cross-talk. If one is on, you can think two are on, at least in a dark bathroom as some light is bleeding through...

reviews.title: Great Shaver
reviews.text: This replaced a 12 year old Braun shaver. All I can say is wow. Best and most comfortable shaves ever.

reviews.title: Braun Series 7
reviews.text: This was a Xmas gift for my son.  He absolutely loves it.  When he first used it, he said that he was getting razor burn, but as he got use to it, that went away and he now gets the best shave he has ever had.

reviews.title: Best Shaver Just Got Better!
reviews.text: Okay, so the title of the review is a little excessive, but I really do love this shaver.  I used a older Braun 6-series for 5-6 years before it died, I think it may have died because I wasn't thinking and I used it in the shower.  Then again, maybe it was a subliminal attempt to get myself a new one for Christmas!  While I understand that the improvements are said to be slight over Braun's last flagship model, the difference is drastic and noticeable over my older model.  I have a fairly sensitive face, when it comes to shaving, and the old Braun, while it gave me the best electric shave I had experienced, tended to occasionally pull a bit and would miss a hair or two.  This new Braun is much, much smoother in its shave.  It has not pulled at all.  I love that it has three power settings.  This 790 rarely misses a hair and is clearly a closer shave.  The head of the shaver has more mobility, which is a big plus for someone like me who has a very angular and chiseled face reminiscent of the gods of Olympus... just kidding.  Though, it is a significant improvement from the older models and a handy feature.  The rubbered grip is also a big improvement, as the old one could be a little slippery at times.  I prefer the new travel case as well.  The old one was plastic and snapped shut, and it always popped open in my bag when I traveled.  The new case is zippered and is a firm-formed material, which makes it much better for travel.  The digital display on the shaver handle, showing battery life and "hygiene," is much more informative than the old display and is, again, very useful in travel situations.  It is much more clear in the documentation of this model that you can clean the head with water, again, good for travel.  I really like the new design for the replaceable foils.  The old foils broke apart into 2-3 pieces, which made it a little more inconvenient for cleaning and maintaining the sharpness of the blades.  This new foil is one piece and is virtually entirely enclosed, it would be hard to quantify, but the design seems to allow for a better cleaning through the base station.  Finally, the base station is different from the old one.  It is slightly less intuitive.  I had to make sure I read the manual to make sure I understood what various flashing lights meant, as opposed to solid lights.  The cleaning process was much more clear with the old model, you put it in the base station, clicked the button into lock and the shaver said "cleaning," and displayed the amount of time remaining.  The new base station does not display this kind of information, instead the manual explains the various cleaning times.  Getting it to start the cleaning is a little less straight-forward, and you can guess how much cleaning time is left by observing the rate at which the "hygiene" bars replenish.  One really good thing about the new base station, is that it seems to go to sleep after being unused for a while.  It wakes up upon pushing a button or replacing the shaver, this is a big plus because it saves energy and that is always good.  The long and short of this review is that I love this shaver.  I loved my old Braun, but this new one makes me wonder how I put up with the old one for so long.

reviews.title: Smooth as ever.
reviews.text:
Despite of what some people have complained about, the new 790cc-4 is many times better than my old Braun 7075 Syncro model. The first thing I noticed is how SMOOTH and COMFORTABLE it shaves, comparing to the Syncro, not to mention the shaving head no longer heats up like before. The Syncro used to occasionally cut my face at difficult to reach places, such as under the nose and by the neck, because I had to do several passes until it would shave properly. The 790cc-4 requires less passes and feels *extremely* comfortable in comparison. This also means I now shave faster than ever, and this used to be hassle to me. The body shape has changed but I still find it easy to hold, so it's fine with me.

But there were some things I didn't like:

* The sideburns cutter is at an awkward angle and make it difficult to see what I am doing. It also has it's own button now, which used to be the same as the power one in older models. I don't think this is an improvement.

* Locking the shaving head now has a separate button that is difficult to reach, and requires me to use both hands to do it. It looks rather flimsy, so I try not to use it much, afraid that I am going to break it - one person claimed to have already broken it in another review. Locking the head used to be simple with older models, so again, this was not an improvement.

* The base is now "automated", meaning that it decides on its own when I should buy a new cleaning solution cartridge. The buttons become unresponsive - it will simply stop working until you do it. Last time this happened, the cartridge was still a quarter full but the base would not let me use it anymore. Again, I don't think this is an improvement - it should let me decide.

* The shaver has built-in battery and "hygiene" displays, which may sound good at a first glance. The hygiene one measures (I assume) how long you took to shave, and then uses that information to tell the base for how long it will keep cleaning it. The older models would simply run a cleaning cycle and get done with it, while the new 790cc-4 wants to make it longer if I took longer to shave. This may sound like wasting cleaning solution when the amount of shaved hair from my face will not increase if I take longer to shave, so therefore it shouldn't take longer to clean. Sounds gimmicky to me, and perhaps makes you buy new cleaning solution cartridges more often?

* The shaving head has (so far) been impossible to find at local stores. From this I could assume that this shaver is not selling much, and that worries me because it wasn't cheap and I plan to use it for years to come. Braun claims the blades should last for 18 months of regular use, but my beard is so hard that they don't last even 10 months. Lack of offer might also mean that the head will remain expensive for lack of competition. Major stores here in Chicago (Target, Sears, etc) are not selling this shaver, so it makes sense they don't sell the shaving head, and that kinda worries me.

* The power button is tiny and hard to reach. The button sits at my thumb's 1st knuckle, and that's what I have to use to reach it. Used to be easy to turn those shavers on and off with the older models, so this was not an improvement.

* The manual suggests I only shave before taking a bath, which in my personal experience produces the worst shaving ever. People who have posted negative reviews here may have fallen for this. Shaving about 10 minutes *after* a bath works like a dream to me, and also causes less skin irritation.

Despite the rather questionable "improvements", I still like this shaver a lot and will not ever come back to my older Syncro - even because Braun has discontinued production of its shaving head, so I can't buy it anymore. So in a way, Braun has forced me to buy this new one and I don't regret the money I have spent on it. For what matters, it shaves closer and smoother than anything I tried before. Some people have complained that it is too noisy, but I find it much quieter than my older syncro. Conversely, the cleaning base is actually louder, but that doesn't matter to me.

All in all, I strongly recommend the 790cc-4 to anybody who can afford it. I took a star down for the issues above, but those do not keep me from having a perfect shave, faster and smoother than ever.

reviews.title: Question
reviews.text: I just received my razor and I noticed it does not come with a clear plastic cover to protect the foil as my previous razors have is it missing??

reviews.title: Works excellent
reviews.text:
shaving with a regular blade -- no matter the blade, always gives me red bumps and ingrown hairs. I am able to get just as close a shave with this razor with none of the bumps or ingrown hairs.

I would recommend this to anyone thinking about buying it. Although the lesser model's I imagine work similarly well.

reviews.title: The Only Razor I Use Now
reviews.text:
You’ll want to watch a youtube video on how to load the blade. It’s different. Go to Barber Turko’s channel. Or just search “sedef razor” and something will come up. Be careful - It requires a little extra caution. The razor is super light, super thin (because there’s no complex blade-loading system), and easy to use. My fancy expensive razors are just sitting in my scuttle; they’re basically decorative now.
Ten skulls.

reviews.title: Returned. Horrible design.
reviews.text: Came unsharped.  Made cheap.

reviews.title: ... a professional barber and this is by far my favorite replaceable blade straight razor I've been able to find
reviews.text:
I am a professional barber and this is by far my favorite replaceable blade straight razor I've been able to find. I like it for several reasons.
super low cost. the width and the height makes it ideal for shaving the upper lip of clients as matter of fact it has the narrowest blade holder of any razor of it's type that I have found. It's well balanced but light weight. It's very durable I had one that lasted over a year and it finally broke when I dropped it on the ground. However, I used it for multiple face and neck shaves a day 6 days a week for a year. I know several professional barbers that use this razor and only this razor. Load it from the point and side it in towards the heel.

reviews.title: Plastic is plastic
reviews.text:
Not sure about sturdiness, or even to take this seriously. You have to slide blade in, there is no lift up piece that holds blade. Unless a tool, but your fingers are in for some slices.

Also as stated is plastic. This is something I ng where your choice of blade is gonna matter and this merely holds. Save your money or spend it on shave soap. Even to the beginner, this would seem like a toy.
BUT, GREAT for travel. Dont worry about leaving a good razor at hotel.
You can still have your shave and and not worry about being left behind.

reviews.title: Good, sturdy razor
reviews.text: At first I was confused how to get the blade into the razor. There's a little hole at the tip that you slide the blade into. It's much easier to slide the blade in if you break off the flaps at the ends of the disposable blade. To take it out, push it out with another object like the corner of a table.

reviews.title: Half blades recommended
reviews.text: For a barber this is a thin and lightweight option for tight spaces and design work. The problem comes in what you load it with. Using a blade you have to snap first is not suggested as the bent corners will need to be clipped off for a flush fit. Also sliding any blade in here is a rough fit from how close the metal has been pinched. Best option is to lay the holder on a flat surface and use a wall to push the the blade into place

reviews.title: Overlooked and underrated. Turkish razors have been a barbers staple since disposable blades
reviews.text:
Nearly every barber straight razor hot shave Ive had (before the ban on reusable blades), Sedef made an appearance for certain tasks. This should tell you something. I have no trust in reviews that obviously have no knowledge of the product type or experience with said "thing" prior to purchase. I have my great grandfather's straight razor. I can hone for shaving or woodcarving. I have lathered and shaved with a dozen knives. A few old timers may remember some of the DE blades in my picture, some are NA NOS and older than half the readers here, all are part of my rotation and stash. have used a half dozen straight edges. I have used all the general styles of shavette. I have EIGHT razors of this turkish style. Two sedef and six Ali Biyikli (identical except for name in same location, they may come from same factory for english and turkish markets for all I know). Why so many? Because they are cheap, thin for travel, the easiest to index your angles; be that your blade angle in a pinch grip, or just feeling where the blade is by the straight shank. The turkish is my favorite. It may look cheap, it may be cheap, but it is a great shave and due to its thin flat design, they get into tight spots more easily than any other shavette or straight razor. Do you get the picture that I did not just unbox my first shavette and try it a few times?
Loading blades is super easy, and if someone says dangerous, they must be using the wrong methods to get blades in or out. These are meant for half DE blades, which can also be found already halved, as shavettes have been standardized for a long time. This review is long enough, you can find a video on how to if you cant figure it out without putting your fingers somewhere dangerous (this includes premeditating a slip and the resulting unintended motions to follow, much like carving or cutting).
I have found sedef not only better in feel, as flat straight and thin is very easy to index. But it also very versatile. I have managed to run all kinds of blades. Some require modification to the blade; some to the razor holder... This i WILL explain but the pictures really should be self explanatory if you know what you are doing. I have never received one with a loose hinge, as they have a rubbery plastic spacer for friction. However i have had some loosen up over the years. But had no problem peening it with a light tap, or with vise grips and a light pinch. However with propper use, a loose handle is a non issue since it is always held in its position by your hand positioning. I no longer tighen a worn out one, as it makes it easy to switch positions and corresponding grip one handed. Much like a butterfly/ balisong. If you have used one, you know that you NEVER grab or use it by the handle (shavette or straight). You hold the tang only, and the handle is simply a blade cover you cannot lose because its attached. When they get loose, i leave it that way for ease of one handed use, and simply lock it closed for travel by slipping an O-ring over the smaller end. This is mostly so it doesnt open in a roughly treated bag and lever the plastic handle in a way that will break it.
INJECTOR BLADES- the friction fit will hold it while shaving. However it does not fully seat, making it (actually) dangerous to load and adjust exposure. It will have no exposure and be stuck inside holder if you try to seat it fully. The solution is a shim to raise exposure height. I dedicated one razor to injectors. It has a metal shim. My primary is a stainless piece, but you may have troue finding the right size... So put a used (injector) blade in a vise leaving an even reveal and file or dremmel the exposed part away. Now you have a shim. I am away from home writing this but made a quickie from wood that shows better on camera anyway. Hence the small travel kit in pic (sedef, blades, diy folding brush, alum and tabac in a chapstick tube fits in a sunglasses case. If the picture does not make it obviously clear what to shim my words may be no help. Those who get it, may see why I like these. Injector blades are great if you have thick wire hairs or want better longevity.
I also run Personna "hair shaper blades" (the 2.5" type shown) the other hair shaper types can be used too but may require a shim as described above. All you need for this style is how to safely de-spine a single edge blade,as picture shows half removed. My method is not shown on youtube but should be, as i have found safer easier ways than the end nippers.. But point being that its quite simple, and then you simply install the blade. This makes it identical to a vintage WECK razor. The longer stiffer blade makes it handle and shave the closest to a straight razor out of any style of shavette I have used. The blades cost more (as do injectors, but I have plenty of usable vintage) however they shave better (especially if you have thick beard or very tough hairs) and tend to last more shaves. However if you do this BEWARE, when you close it, the blade will protrude from handle. Remove the blade for travel or of you leave it somewhere pets may bump it. If you have kids, surely you shouldnt leave it where it can be reached, whatever blades you run.
I even run Gem (de-spined) blades and a similar style thats a bit thicker, no spine and a square hole. Point being; these things are incredibly versatile.
Six dollars is avg for a 2 pack, but a great deal given their versatility and ease of use with little learning curve. A straight shank really helps. Scales are thin for travel, and if you drop it and it breaks, consider it a Kamisori now and get another set. Its not like theyre expensive. But dont judge by its price!
However, if this is your first turkish, I suggest getting used to it with half DE blades as intended.

reviews.title: Quick and easy to change the razor
reviews.text: Light and well balanced. Quick and easy to change the razor.

reviews.title: would not recommend for beginners
reviews.text: So this is partly my fault since i hadn't used a shavette before, but loading the blade was difficult and I cut my finger, when I finally did get it in it was crooked and there's really no way to fix that with out cutting yourself, it is cheap but nothing was broken or damaged, I think if your familiar with using a straight blade this could work well but I would not touch if your new I made that mistake

reviews.title: Great for line ups
reviews.text: These little blade holders are about as good as you can ask for. Loading is a bit hairy but worth the extra caution as the thin profile makes extremely low blade angles possible. Not sure anyone could design a better way that keeps the frame this thin. I've tried about every replaceable blade shavette\barber razor out there and for me it's these or a real straight even feather and kia didn't cut it for me.

reviews.title: Not good
reviews.text: Not good, you have to make an opening for tge blade to fit right but not too much, both won't hold a blade anymore. Get something American made better.

reviews.title: NOT FOR BEGINNERS
reviews.text: If you are new to shaving with shavettes or straight razors these ARE NOT for you. There are many other shavettes much more suitable to learn with. There are no safety features. The blades must be pushed into the razor from the front, and it is a very tight fit because it has to be very tight in order to secure the blade in place. The blades are removed by using a flat object to push the blade back out. The Turkish razors are smaller than most standard sized shavettes, and the blade base is narrower than most shavettes. I happen to love them, and the price is right. They're cheaply made but rather sturdy. I use small needle nose pliers to gradually push the blade into place from the front, then refold the package the blade came in and lightly/gently push down on the blade against the blade wrapper on a counter to ensure that the blade in uniformly lined up evenly. I remove the used blades by holding the razor in place with one hand while using the blade of a small knife to push out the used blade from the rear blade edge.

reviews.title: Okay

I bought this beard leave in conditioner for my husband. I wanted him try something different he's used beard oils and beard balms I wanted to try out this beard conditioner to see how it would work out for him.

He didn't like it at all unfortunately, it was heavy on his beard and left a white residue that never absorbed he ended up having to wash his beard he was not happy. I was really hoping it would work out and make his beard nice and soft and fluffy like leave in conditioners for the hair on your head . It doesn't have a scent.

He gave it 2 chances each time no success so he decided not to try it again.

reviews.text: This product helps to keep the skin under my whiskers free from drying out and causing flaking. Does the job!

reviews.title: Hooray for no more flakes and softer beard!
reviews.text:
I have really been enjoying the Bannerman beard leave-in conditioner. I purchased it with hopes it would help with summer flaking that hit as soon as the weather started staying about 100F. Even increasing my water intake hasn't been enough to compensate and I began to notice skin flakes coming out when I touched my beard, not only embarrassing but it began to get itchy as well. This 'beard-aide' product has helped tremendously in keeping the flaking to a minimal and I'm hoping that by continuing to use it, it will help reduce the flaking to nil. I apply just after showering and my beard and underlying skin are still damp. Once I have it dabbed in with finger tips, I use my comb and boar's hair brush to blend it thoroughly. With the residual on my hands, I've even gone ahead and rubbed it through my hair and have noticed its making my hair more manageable as well - definitely plus. Overall, I would recommend this product to guys that need a beard condition up and beyond the regular application of a beard oil to help fight dry whiskers and the skin underneath.
I am an Amazon Prime shopper that reviews products like this in exchange for a fair, honest and thorough review. Even though I received the Bannerman leave-in beard conditioner at a reduced cost, I have rated and reviewed it without bias, based solely on my experience. If at any time my opinions change about its effectiveness to condition my beard, I will update my review accordingly. If my review helped you in making up your mind about whether to purchase this product, [...], it tells the seller that folks are in fact reading reviews like this. Thank you!

reviews.text: I like that tis product leaves the beard very soft and manageable without being oily or greasy. I like the results, and I'm sure some users like that it is scent free, but I prefer a masculine scented product.
