I usually use Listerine TotalCarePlus but that burning sensation as you're swishing it around in your mouth can be overwhelming at times. I decided to Try the Colgate Total Advance since it prominently said 'No Alcohol'. I do like the product. It gives your mouth that clean feeling that brushing alone can't give. It doesn't burn like the Listerine product does. You can also have your coffee right after without that 30 minute wait. The spearmint flavor is very refreshing and the fresh breath lasts a long time(if you don't eat or drink coffee).

Still it does not have the floride and the enamel protection that TotalCarePlus has. I think after this bottle is consumed its back to the burn.

reviews.title: Feeling Clean
reviews.text:
I like this product & feel safe using it. Makes my mouth feel very clean.
A trusted product. Hope you like it, also.

reviews.title: It's....a mouthwash
reviews.text:
How do you review a mouthwash?
I mean, it is what it is. It doesn't taste all that good as fatr as mouthwashes go, though it's not bad per se either. Can't say it does particularly any better or worse than normal either. It just....is. It's not special.

reviews.title: Forget the old stinging stuff
reviews.text:
This mouthwash gives you a great taste and no sting. I've used it nearly every day for a month, and it feels great. The bottle is enough to last 1 person over 2 months if used twice daily.

The taste is forgettable, nothing fancy, but not bothersome either.

reviews.title: mouth wash
reviews.text: I didn't find this mouth wash amazing nor horrible.  The taste is ok, and it gave me that clean feeling.  But I didn't feel any strong urge to buy this item again.  It does a decent job.

reviews.title: No burn
reviews.text: This mouthwash does it all - cleans, freshens, and protects - I just miss the really  lean feeling I get from other mouthwashs. There is no "burn" of alcohol here and I leave the bathroom not feelin as sparklingly clean as I'm used too. Otherwise, it tastes fine and works a lot better than nothing!

reviews.title: A Very Strong Minty Mouthwash (Not Typical for Most Alcohol-Free Moutwashs).
reviews.text:
It is hard to get excited about mouthwash, but for me, it is especially hard to get excited about alcohol-free mouthwash. I tend to prefer antiseptic to antibacterial mouthwash (this one being the latter), but I was asked to try it.

Part of the reason I am skeptical of non-alcoholic mouthwashs, frankly, is that the taste on many of them just isn't very strong. I want a strong mint, and most non-alcohiolic mouthwash I've tried is rather wimpy. This Colgate Total Advanced Pro-Shield Wintermint, though, is surprisingly bold in flavor. I could feel the mint in my mouth for about an hour after I used it (something I usually only get from Listerine or Act antiseptic mouthwash).

As far as really cleaning my teeth, I'm afraid I am going to stick with the alcohol-infused antiseptic mouthwash, mostly because my problem is not bad breath, but wanting to prevent gingivitis and the like. But if you want a good antibacterial (to head off bad breath and kill bacteria without drying your mouth out with alcohol, I would recommend the Colgate, particularly if you like a bold flavor!

reviews.title: Colgate Total Advanced Pro-Shield Mouthwash
reviews.text:
I have been looking for an effective mouthwash that was alcohol free because I'm tired of the "alcohol burn" that comes with many of the top-named brands. When I saw this new product from Colgate I thought I would give it a try.

This new Colgate Total Advanced Pro-Shield is alcohol free and is advertised as an anti-gingivitis, anti-plaque mouthwash. The product I am reviewing is the 16.9 fluid ounce plastic bottle manufactured by the Colgate-Palmolive Company and comes with several fairly remarkable claims:

~ 12-hour protection against germs, even after eating and drinking.
~ Kills 99% of germs; kills germs that cause bad breath, gingivitis and plaque.
~ 24/7 protection.

When I first saw the 12-hour protection guarantee and then noted the 24/7 protection promise I thought, "Wait a minute!" I may not be a math wiz but those two claims don't add up. After closer observation I spotted the following recommendation: "Use 2X daily."

A couple of things I did like about the container were (1) the plastic cap came with a safety sealed wrapper around it so the consumer can be assured that there has been no tampering (most mouthwashes come this way, but not all do) and (2) the cap just screws off; no tricky push-it-down and turn or press-in-the-sides and turn caps. Also, if you flip the cap over so you can see the inside, there is what appears to be a cap inside of a cap. I doubt it was intended for this, but I found the inside cap could be used as a measuring cup for dispensing the right amount of mouthwash. I suspect the double-cap design is intended to prevent leakage but I thought it also worked well as a dispenser, too. Even though this was a 16.9 fluid ounce bottle, I was surprised just how quickly I used it up; even when using the nifty pseudo measuring cup I had discovered.

Now let's get to the questions most consumers have when they shop for mouthwash: (1) Does it work as advertised? and (2) what does it taste like? The product I have has a flavor that Colgate-Palmolive calls "Wintermint Rush." Now considering that I was looking for a mouthwash without a burning sensation or one with a soda pop taste I was hoping this "Wintermint Rush" also wouldn't taste like the title...a RUSH! After trying it out it definitely didn't have a burn or a rush. This mouthwash has a very mild, rather dull taste; no fizzing, bubbling, burning and definitely no rush; exactly what I was looking for. After rinsing I found the mouthwash left a fresh, clean taste in my mouth. But does it work like advertised? As far as I am concerned it does. My breath stayed reasonably fresh and free of an undesirable smell. I found the mouthwash left a pleasant coating on my lips, which my wife also found appealing. This new Colgate-Palmolive mouth also comes in two additional flavors: "Peppermint Blast" and "Spearmint Surge."

The label comes with many of the usual list of active and inactive ingredients, warnings and and so forth. This product is intended for use by adults and children over the age of five. One recommendation I thought was excellent was children 6 years to under 12-years-old should be supervised while using this mouthwash.

Overall I was pleased with this new Colgate Total Advanced Pro-Shield mouthwash. The fact that it doesn't have a strong flavor wasn't a problem for me. The taste isn't impressive, but then I don't purchase mouthwash for its jazzy flavor like I would the smell of my cologne. I buy it because it works; because it:

~ Kills germs.
~ Significantly prevents and reduces plaque.
~ Helps prevent gingivitis.
~ Has no alcohol burn.
~ Freshens breath.

Postscript (17 March 2014): I finished my bottle of Colgate Total Advanced Pro-Shield mouthwash this morning and I'm still impressed with this product. One additional note: I have been for decades prone to having minor, yet still annoying sores on the inside of my cheeks (not my gums) because of some prescribed medication I'm taking. After using this product for only a few days I haven't had another sore in my mouth. This was completely unexpected; what a relief.

reviews.title: Good Taste and Good Protection
reviews.text: I already finished the product and really liked it.  It has a clean taste and it was no problem swishing for 60 seconds. That is partially because it is alcohol free which I like. It also helps fight a varieity of potential oral problems. My wife tried it and also liked it.  I think most people would. Give it a try.

reviews.title: Ok but not for my taste
reviews.text: I was excited to try this to see if my mouth could feed refreshed without burning, but overall I don't like the taste. It seems to last for a long time afterwards and that's enough for me to look elsewhere.

reviews.title: It Works for What We Wanted It For
reviews.text:
Husband grew a beard the moment he was put on blood thinners, and he has it trimmed once a month, but sometimes that's not enough. When it gets too long he tends to look like Grizzly Adams. I would keep it tamed with scissor trims, but this works much better; he can do the trim himself, and also trim his cheeks and mustache. We haven't tried it on trimming the hair at the back of his neck.

He does not like that it is only USB and you have to buy a wall plug adapter to charge it. I guess they figure most people have them now, but it should come with one. That's just being cheap.

reviews.title: New Maybuy Beard Trimmer Takes On Viking Beard -- And Wins!
reviews.text:
The gene pool I come from has Viking, German, and Irish heritage -- resulting in a stiff wiry beard that defeats most cordless beard trimmers. To give this trimmer a good chance I followed the directions and charged it for the two hours as recommended in the instructions. My only gripe about this process is that you're supposed to keep an eye on it so that when charging is finished you can disconnect it immediately to avoid potential damage. Since the unit has no automatic shut-off when charging is completed, you have to keep an eye on it to avoid a problem. That is my only gripe about this unit. Not too many people have the time flexibility to watch it for the two hours (or less) it needs to charge.

While it only comes with 3 attachments that is all you really need. The shaver itself is large enough to give you a firm grip and the weight implies quality. The initial effort at trimming my beard was highly successful. I grew it for two weeks to make it a challenge and then shaved it without using any attachments. Normally (with other units) I would have expected difficulty in shaving it flush with my skin. Typically this type of trimmer is not powerful enough to plow through my beard, pulls some hair out instead of cutting it, and sometimes nicks my skin. None of that happened here. Evidently the battery and the motor are up to the task.

This is by far the best cordless trimmer I have ever owned and used. Highly recommended!

reviews.title: Nice smooth glide and large head makes for easy work
reviews.text:
The Maybuy Professional Hair Clippers/Beard Trimmers comes with 3 attachments, 1/2 inch, 1 inch and 1.5 inches. These attachments simply clip onto the head of the trimmer. You also get a small brush for cleaning the trimmer and a charging cable which strangely is a USB to Mini-USB even though at the bottom of the trimmer is your standard two prong charging connector.

Being able to charge this via USB however is great for when you need to go on holiday/vacation as you always have some kind of USB charging device with yourself (usually for your phone). On a full charge the clippers will roughly 2 hours and are small enough to just throw in your toiletry bag.

The trimmer itself has a nice shape to it and is comfortable to hold in your hand. It also has a fairly nice heft to it, not too light but not heavy. It feels like you are really holding on to something though.

The head is fairly wide which can be a plus in that it doesn't take long at all to trim an area but can also be a bit of a minus depending on the shape of your face if you are trimming your beard. The action is smooth and has a nice gliding feel to it, even if you don't use any attachments and just use the trimmer head itself.

It would have been nice if the Maybuy came with some sort of carrying case to put it, the attachments and the charging cord in but it doesn't unfortunately. The attachments do come in a small Ziploc bag.

Overall a nice trimmer, nice action and makes quick work whether trimming your beard or hard to reach places like around your ears.

reviews.title: Basic, Good Quality, Affordable
reviews.text: So I got this out and started using it on my husband immediately.  It is perfect for cleaning up the hairline.  My husband can't stand hair on his neck so it cleans it up very well.  If you plan to use this to give yourself a haircut, your hair will be very short when done.  It has clips but they aren't for longer hair.  My best friend cuts his own hair but his is longer and he uses a kit that has more clip sizes with it.  If you are concerned you might not know how to use this well, get online and watch videos.  There are so many that will tell you exactly how to do it and how to use clippers.  If you keep your hair very short, this is good to use so you don't have to go back to the barber too quickly and can keep a big haircut at bay.  Look at the width of the head and make sure that suits you.  That was something I hadn't considered until I started using this.  I think it would be easier to maneuver for the beard if it were just a little narrower.  However, with the size it is you can cover more area much more quickly, with fewer passes.  It removes the hair very well with just one pass.  It doesn't come with oil but it is recommended you oil this as needed.  It comes with a cable for charging.  Because it is cordless, I can use it anywhere and that is very convenient.  The clips go one easy and can be a little harder to get off.  The more I'm using them though, the more accustomed to it I am becoming.

reviews.title: Small is useful
reviews.text:
Surprisingly, this small set, probably the smallest I've seen so far, comes with everything you will need for beard-mustache trimming and it could be used for hair styling for as long as your hair is not too long.
I would recommend it as a travel option because of its small and efficient size but it could also be used as the default beard or generally facial hair tool if your needs aren't anything special.

reviews.title: Keeping me well trimmed
reviews.text: I have been making the wife happy and staying trimmed... I never use a clean shave razor, but have been keeping a close shave with this trimmer. Working great, light and maneuvers with easy

reviews.title: Great for home or the salon
reviews.text:
There is plenty to like about this kit.

First, I like that it's USB powered. That is a major convenience, especially if using in a professional setting. Secondly, it's got good balance and is ergonomically pleasing. A great feature when you're using is regularly.

My sister, who has been a professional for 20 years, said it was among the sharpest (in a good way!) she's ever used. Obviously this is huge when working on customers and you don't want to pulling hair out when you're trying to cut it.

With the myriad attachments, there's really very little you can't do with it. And best of all, the price is great for what you get. I would recommend this for both home and professional settings.

The battery length isn't great, but it also charges really quickly, so it's simply a matter of being mindful of it.

reviews.title: Great quality
reviews.text: This is an excellent budget brand for a nice quality set of clippers. They work very well on toddler hair, which is mostly what I've used them for so far. It charges super quickly and lasts for quite a while. I also really love that it's cordless, which makes it very maneuverable. I've found that it also works reasonably well for face shaving. Great set--highly recommended!

reviews.title: Perfect Ergonomic Design
reviews.text:
I always wondered how you can see some guys always sporting a three-day beard. It seems with my beard it is either too short or way too long. This product provides an excellent choice of three beard trimming guides (guides reflect the following numbers on the guides -- 1/2 and a 1 and a 1-1/2). You may want to consider starting with the highest number and working your way to the smallest to make sure you don't do what I did and make your beard immediately shorter than you anticipated. However, with that said, I found the perfect combination for me is using the 1/2 which is the equivalent of a three-day beard.

When receiving the product it shipped with a travel guard for safety. With no guard, you could easily shave your head. The quality of the product is superb with excellent weight and is easy to use. On/off switch is an easy up for on and down for off. A green light comes on to let you know that you are in business. It is a quieter device than most. I charged the battery when it first arrived using the provided USB cord and it is still going strong. NOTE. You must have an AC adapter that you can plug into the wall. This device does not come with it. However, you can charge on your computer or other charging device.

I love it! You will not be disappointed.

reviews.title: Great Scissors.
reviews.text: I bought these for my daughter and she LOVES them.

reviews.title: Does a good job with the guards on but is not good for shaving beard off
reviews.text: This product is easy to charge and easy to use. It does a nice job of trimming my beard with one of the guards on. However, there are limits to the length you can trim since it only has four small guards. So if you want your beard a little longer, this is not the product for you. Also, if you are looking for a product that can be used to take off your beard completely when you are preparing for a clean shave, this is not the product for you. It is best used for trimming with a guard attached, which it does nice and evenly.

reviews.title: Scissors that really work!!
reviews.text: These scissors worked exactly how I expected a top of the line professional scissors to work.  It is fabulous.  Sharp and felt good in my hands.  And they are beautiful.  Thank you.

reviews.title: Four Stars
reviews.text: Great for the price!

reviews.title: Great little clippers with good performance
reviews.text: This is a great little clipper and it performs exactly as expected. My favorite feature is that it is rechargeable so it is used without a cord attached. I've had one with a cord for years so I really appreciate the difference.

reviews.title: Small and portable for trimming
reviews.text:
I always cut my husband's and sons' hair, so having a wireless option sounded great. It works well as a trimmer, and charged up easily via the USB cable. It's handy for a quick beard or mustache trim as well. It comes with some guards, but not the ones for cutting hair that go up to an inch. This is for trimming, not for buzz cuts unless you're skilled with using the comb as a guard.

It's fairly quiet, but not whisper silent or anything. It doesn't come with oil, but needs to be oiled.

I found it interesting that on the bottom where you charge it, there is a slot for a traditional cord and it says "two-way" but this only includes USB. I think this is the budget version of the clipper. It's odd that it doesn't include a cord if it has the ability to be charged two ways. I have a zillion USB cords laying around the would work with this, but no standard cords. Those are specific to the wattage of a device.

This is a handy extra tool to have along with my regular clippers. And it's portable if someone needs to trim facial hair and travels.

reviews.title: Really nice!
reviews.text: Fine product, fine customer service. These feel great in the hand and are very easy to control. I have used more expensive products that were not as nice.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: These shears are awsome

reviews.title: giftable
reviews.text: I am familiar with the Kopari brand.  They are considered a "clean beauty" brand with less toxins than most.  This is a fun little set.  Not overly sticky.  Imparts a nice shine and makes lips feel good.  Non drying.  These would make a great gift.  No complaints or regrets.

reviews.title: Fun flavors!
reviews.text:
First, I did find these to be thinner than my original coconut glossy. They still add shine and a ton of sweet flavor, but go on a little heavy out of the tube.
The texture after it settles is nice, shiny and not drying. The scents are fantastic, true to the super sweet scents of my much younger years.
It's a fun way to mix up my lip balms!

reviews.title: Great worn alone, not flavored as claimed
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:9631aea0bf38c5a9cf1cb4863ad4559e]] I might be the only one who didn't like these. I just didn't notice much of a flavor at all and the texture was more like an oil which drove me nuts throughout the day. I kept worrying about it sliding around and moving my lip color underneath. I personally prefer a more sticky gloss. I did like how fine the glitter was so it didn't bother my lips or create any tingling effect. The scent was by far the best part and I loved the strawberry.

reviews.title: Cute gift
reviews.text: looks just like the picture. Nice scent and keeps your lips glossy. A little pricey to me for what you are getting.

reviews.title: ALMOST perfect!
reviews.text:
I'm a lip gloss connoisseur and my favorite was this tube that looked like this one at a retailer that doesn't sell it anymore (MAN!) - it was sheer, tasted/smelled great and most importantly - was NOT sticky! This is the closest I've found to it.

PRO: The lip gloss smells and tastes great and it definitely helps with moisture and keeping lips soft. I like to put a little dab over lipstick so it has a tad bit of color.

CON: It's a little sticky - just a tad. Also - the colors are meaningless because it doesn't add any color that I can tell.

Verdict: Recommend - a very nice lip gloss

I pride myself on honest, accurate, helpful reviews.

reviews.title: Smells like bubblegum and non sticky!
reviews.text: This is the perfect lip gloss. It smells amazing but best of all, it doesn’t feel sticky! It feels hydrating but doesn’t give that sticky feeling that most lipglosses have. I love that the brand is vegan. I am very happy with this purchase and would buy it again.

reviews.title: Cuuute
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:c63a2bc18391c6be70754b10ccce4080]] The lip gloss kit arrived fast and packaged well. It was brand new.
Love the color of all 3 of them.
Not super sticky and your hair doesn’t stick to it like to a magnet.
Smell is nice, reminds me of childhood lip gloss smells. Love it.
Lip gloss stays on for a while, doesn’t run. If you accidentally taste it, it doesn’t taste bad. Just as it smells.
10/10 recommend.

reviews.title: Moisturizing ingredients
reviews.text:
Kopari, you have done it again.
Fabulous little set of Lip Glosses, super moisturizing and contain Coconut Oil, Shea Butter and Squalane for those dry peely cracked lips.

They go on nice and smooth, just gently squeeze the tube, and a little bit comes out, but don’t press too hard. They look great over a Matte Finish Lippy, or just on their own, of the three flavours (grape, bubblegum, and strawberry) my fave is the strawberry, which has a reddish pink tint, but isn’t strongly coloured, and goes on sheer. It wouldn’t affect the colour of any lippy you were wearing underneath.

They aren’t sticky, and i no longer have long hair that clings to such products, so i can wear them without that happening anymore.

I like this set a lot, it’s a beautiful presentation, as are all Kopari products I’ve had, and could easily be gifted to any Lip Gloss lovers out there, of any age, in my humble opinion.

reviews.title: Great size for carrying in small bags. Great scent!
reviews.text:
Things to note about this lip gloss:

- They are small. They fit nicely in my small fanny pack with my phone and wallet.
- They have a nice delicious scent.
- They are flavorless.
- They're not sticky, I feel that makes them last a little less on lips but that makes them way more comfortable to wear.
- They dont really add any color to your lips.

For reference: they are not like your typical VS gloss, they are way less thick.
I love them and recommend them to use on top of a lip tint or lipstick.

reviews.title: Smells good and kid friendly
reviews.text: All 3 lip glosses show up a clear color on my lips. They are not sticky, I wish they were a little thicker. They smell just like fruit and bubble gum. The lip gloss seems to be kid friendly, I gave one to my daughter and she enjoys the feel and scent. I haven't calculated the time frame of how long it will stay on but it seems to only last for a short period of time.

reviews.title: Delicious flavors
reviews.text: Sweet and colorful

reviews.title: The perfect bit of shine to add to your lips
reviews.text: I love this trio of lipgloss. The flavors are very popular fragrances of strawberry, grape, and bubblegum. The strawberry and the grape do appear to be slightly tinted, but you will not notice any real color added to your lips from them. You can use them alone and enjoy just a nice clear gloss over your natural color lips, or you can put it over top of your color and no worry of changing the color of your favorite lipstick. It will add a very nice bit of shine and a little bit of a sparkle to whatever color you decide to slide it over.  I do not notice a lot of flavor to these glosses, but the color is very pretty. I feel that the box is a nice addition if you plan on giving these as a gift. I think this would make a wonderful gift for a young girl or even an older lady. I can’t imagine anybody that wouldn’t enjoy receiving this trio of lip glosses. I felt the price was extremely fair and that the quality of this item was very good.

reviews.title: Super cute
reviews.text: The packaging of these lip glosses is so cute and girly. The packaging actually reminds me of a package of bubble gum (I'm sure that's the point). It would make a great gift or stocking stuffer for a tween or teen. It's slightly sticky, but not as much as other lip glosses that I've used. If you or someone you know loves lip gloss, this set would be perfect.

reviews.title: Perfect for Young Ladies
reviews.text: This is a super cute set. The colors are cute and great colors to have. The gloss itself goes on easily, has good flavor, and feels good on. I (and my daughter) haven't had any issues with reactions or anything like that. I would say this set is definitely geared more toward girls than for adults (though you could totally wear it if you just want a gloss on for an hour as a woman.) It's not long lasting, but my daughter doesn't really care about that.

reviews.title: Hydrating sheer shiny glosses
reviews.text: I am a big fan of Kopari products- I trust them for being clean and free of bad stuff. These lip glosses are in line with the brand. Made with coconut and shea butter, these aren’t the slimy sticky glosses of 90s. They are shiny, lightly pigmented, and cruelty free.

reviews.title: super cute
reviews.text: They're quite small, but I don't really mind. They all smell lovely and don't get very sticky. They also don't "gum up" like cheap lip glosses do.

reviews.title: 2 of 3 have micro glitter (mica). Sticky but not too bad. Moisturizing
reviews.text:
These come in a nice little box. They are vegan and cruelty free. They are made in the USA. I included the ingredients at the end of my review.

You get 3 glosses - a peachy pink toned one that has a strawberry scent/flavor, a more reddish toned one that has a grape scent/flavor, and a clear one that has a bubble gum scent/flavor. As for the scents, I can notice them when applying, not so much after the fact. I don’t really notice a taste really with any of them, but the ingredients do list “flavor” not fragrance/parfum, so it made me think they’d have more taste to them. Not a big deal to me though.

While 2 of them are tinted in color, the color doesn’t really translate onto the lips. They just look clear on the lips. They don’t change any color of lip liner, lipstick, matte lip, etc. that I put them over top of. So, I liked that.

The formula is nice. It feels nice and moisturizing on the lips, which I expected given they have nice moisturizing ingredients like Shea butter, coconut oil, and squalane. They do feel SLIGHTLY sticky on the lips. I hate sticky glosses, but these aren’t overly sticky. They don’t feel thick or heavy on the lips. They feel nice and thin. They are glosses so they do need re-applied often throughout the day. But, I’m used to that with glosses so it’s not a big deal to me.

The strawberry and grape both have micro glitter in them (mica). I didn’t know that when I got these. None of the product photos showed they had glitter and the description didn’t say it either. I’m not a fan of micro glitter in my lip glosses. I feel once the gloss wears off, it usually leaves behind glitter particles on my lips. Also, they can sometimes feel gritty. So, I was surprised when I saw 2 of the 3 had glitter. Luckily, these didn’t feel gritty. I do notice that there are some glitter particles on my lips after they wear off, but it’s not too bad. The glitter particles are really small, so it’s not really noticeable. So, I don’t mind that.

Overall, these are nice glosses with a nice formula. They might seem pricey when seeing the $30 price tag, but you’re getting 3 tubes of glosses, so that’s only $10 a piece, which isn’t bad. Of course, it isn’t great. You can get a tube of gloss that looks great and works just as well from the drugstore for slightly less. But, the ingredients might make it worth it to spend the little more. I give them 4 stars.

INGREDIENTS
Bubblegum Lip Glossy:
Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil*, Isododecane, Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer, Flavor, Sorbitan Oleate, Squalane, Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer, Tocopherol, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E Acetate).

*Organic/Biologique.

Grape Lip Glossy:
Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil*, Isododecane, Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer, Flavor, Sorbitan Oleate, Squalane, Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer, Tocopherol, Ricinus Communis (Castor) Seed Oil, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract, Mica, Titanium Dioxide, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E Acetate), Iron Oxides (CI 77492), Red 33 (CI#17200), CI 15850 (Red 7), Iron Oxides (CI 77499).

*Organic/Biologique.

Strawberry Lip Glossy:
Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil*, Isododecane, Ethylene/Propylene/Styrene Copolymer, Flavor, Sorbitan Oleate, Squalane, Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer, Tocopherol, Titanium Dioxide, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract, Calcium Aluminum Borosilicate, Ricinus Communis (Castor) Seed Oil, Mica, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E Acetate), Iron Oxides (CI 77492), Red 27 Lake (CI 45410), Silica, Tin Oxide.

*Organic/Biologique

reviews.title: Very glittery
reviews.text: I have a lot of orange-gold lip colors because I find them very flattering on me, so I was pretty confident I would like this. The color is beautiful and not too orange. While I like the gold too, the specks of glitter are larger and more obvious than I would like. Some gold is really fine and distributes as an even shimmer across your lips. With this, you can see the glitter speck here, the glitter speck there, the glitter speck falling a little outside your lip line that will probably end up somewhere else on your face. It has more of a youthful, party feel. I agree it's a color reminiscent of sunsets, suited for summer and fall. The case is possibly the most sophisticated I own though. It has a nice weight, and the magnetic closure feels good. It came in a box and in a sealed bag. I don't mind wearing this color, but I wouldn't buy it again. I think I like the case the best, but I'm not convinced the color or feel add up to make it worth $38.

reviews.title: Happy with it!
reviews.text: I had never heard of this brand before and was also looking forward to trying this out because I feel like this shade wouldn't look right on me- I guess because I have a lot of redness in my face. So getting the opportunity to try it out allowed me to branch out and try a shade I would normally be hesitant to buy. I'm glad because I love it! I ended-up loving the shade and how it looks on me. It applies so smoothly and even feels moisturizing while wearing. I love the shimmer- it's enough to be noticeable and pretty but isn't too much. Happy with it!

reviews.title: Moisturizing & Comfortable, Good Coverage, Pretty Red-Nude w/ A Sparkly Coppery Finish On Me
reviews.text:
This Emilie Heathe Lip Atelier Lip Colour in Shimmering Burnt Orange (Public) comes in a premium tube with a magnetic lid. I don’t notice any scent while wearing this, but this has “flavor” that I can smell if I hold the lipstick under my nose. The formula glides on smoothly and has a comfortable, moisturizing feel. My lips don’t get dried out or irritated, and the color doesn’t bleed even though I have dry skin. This is semi sheer yet buildable and even one swipe gives me good coverage that wears well. This does have sparkles that I need to remove after wear.

This lipstick is really pretty. It looks sparkly and shimmery and has become a favorite for evening as it’s more glam than my subtle everyday pinks, yet it doesn’t look overdone or too intense. I wouldn’t normally choose an orange shade, but this turned out to be really flattering with my light skin with an olive hue. While “burnt orange” is a fair description of how it looks in the tube, on me this looks less orange and more like a reddish/nude color with a sparkly finish that’s coppery. I think natural lip color and how much is applied will affect the look. Love it. 5 stars.

reviews.title: Shimmery, long lasting
reviews.text: First time I've heard of this brand so I was eager to try something new.  I'm happy with the color as it's not too bold or dark but more natural.  It's not a super soft lipstick, it takes a few passes to really get good coverage.  It has a texture to it because of the shimmer it has.  I like that it lasts really well throughout the day and doesn't come off on everything!  Seems to be high quality and I like the way it looks.  Very happy with it.

reviews.title: Totally 90s orange sparkle lips
reviews.text:
Such a gorgeous color that can work for everyone. It reminds me of the orange glowing sunset when the next day is supposed to be hot. The glitter in the lipstick is such an amazing addition to the already beautiful color. The gold really shines in the light without looking dated. Using this without liner as an ombre lip is great for those who want a more subdued look.
Even with ultra fair skin this shade doesn't look overdone because the pigmentation is nice and light. It seems to last really well even though it's more of a glossy hydrating formula.

reviews.title: Feels great, but does not last long
reviews.text: I love the way my lips feel and look with this lipstick, but I took away one star because it does not stay put as long as I would like and needs touching up more frequently than I would like. It also requires a bit more caution as it will rub off and smear (on me anyway) more easily than my usual long-wearing brand. But the the feel is so moisturizing and the color flattering that sometimes it’s just worth it if I’m all dressed up and want to look my best!

reviews.title: It's nice, but...
reviews.text: Very nice color. It's somewhere in the bronze family. It works with both pale and tanned skin. The lipstick feels a little gritty on my lips from the glitter. The case is cool although I'm not sure why the cover is magnetic - so I don't lose it? It's a very nice color, but I'm just not comfortable with the luxury price tag on this one.

reviews.title: My Lips Are So Striking!!
reviews.text: The luscious beauty of this shimmering succulent orange brings out a daringness to my lips. It’s bold, depending on my fashion style, and yet ultra complimentary and tasteful. It also has amazing texture, plus it generously hydrates, while offering me beautiful, moisturized lips.

reviews.title: Shimmery Tangerine Color- perfect!
reviews.text: This Emilie Heathe Lip Color is a really good product. The lip color is a great color, peachy orange with a very small particle shimmer dust with gold flecks. It is a great color. Not over the top. The shimmer isn’t tacky shimmer, it’s just a hint. I also love the magnetic top to the lipstick case. It snaps right back on with ease. Love the lipstick!

reviews.title: Love the idea
reviews.text: I'm interested in purchasing other colors from the line because I LOVE the overall formula, but the shimmering burnt orange shade wasn't flattering on me. The orange base was pretty and built up nicely with additional layers, but the actual shimmer particles imparted somewhat of a gritty texture to my lips and looked almost *too* sparkly. I think I would prefer finer shimmer. But otherwise, the product was very moisturizing and lightweight on the lips. If I can find a more suitable shade, I think it would be a very convenient and glamorous on-the-go product!

reviews.title: Emilie Heathe’s Lipstick…fabulous and beautiful!
reviews.text:
Emilie Heathe’s Lipstick in Public/Shimmering Burnt orange is fabulous! This lipstick gives me the right “pop” of color I have been looking for and has beautiful little sparkles in it, making it perfect for both daytime and night time (or even special occasion use!) There is so much to love about this lipstick!

1. This lipstick glides on easily, is moisturizing, and really complements my fair/ivory (with pink undertones) skin color.
2. It feels creamy and luxurious, while not feeling heavy, and feels perfectly natural on my lips no matter how long I was wearing it. It did not look orange on me, considering the name (shimmering burnt orange), but was a very pretty shade of medium peach. Love it!
3. The packaging for the lipstick is extremely chic, being black and sleek. The lipstick tube has a lid that “locks” together with the base, making me feel like the lid is more secure in my purse than a conventional lid. I have not used a lipstick with this kind of packaging before, and it is a nice detail. It is all about the details!
4. The icing on the cake is that this lipstick is vegan, cruelty-free, made in the U.S.A, and packaging is recyclable and sustainably so you can feel good about using this product in so many ways!

Hope you get a chance to love this great lipstick by Emilie Heathe, as I much as I do!

reviews.title: GLITTERING burnt orange
reviews.text:
This review is for the Emilie Heathe - Lip Atelier Lip Color in the "Shimmering Burnt Orange" shade, which I believe is the only "shimmery" color offered at the time of this writing. It comes in a super-chic understated black box, and the tube itself is also black and square with a magnetic lid.

Calling the shade "Shimmering" is an understatement - this shade is GLITTERING. The pigment itself is a really pretty Sienna / rust color that's very flattering on my pale, warm-undertoned skin, and it has a LOT of coppery-gold sparkle. It glides on my lips smoothly and puts down a good amount of pigment (and glitter) in a single swipe. It does feel a little bit gritty from the glitter, but doesn't leave my lips feeling dry at all. It does have a little bit of a scent that smells floral to me.

My problem with this particular shade is that the pigment fades (as all lip colors do) throughout the day, but the glitter does NOT. The glitter just migrates outside of your lips and you end up with it all around your mouth. So I personally wouldn't order this shade again. However I like the formula overall, and the fact that it's made in the USA from clean ingredients, so I'm sure the other (non-glittery) colors are amazing and would be right up my alley!

reviews.title: Very sparkly
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:d6cdea0c18d5911b6ddf88504e4441c9]] I expected a subtle shimmer with this lipstick, but it actually gives off more of a glittery look.  I don't like to draw that much attention to myself and the color wasn't quite right for me, so it was a bit much for me at first, but I've figured out how to apply it to best suit me.  I found that if I apply a light layer of this over my usual lipstick it adds just a bit of deeper color and a shimmer to give it a nice new look.  I also found that if I do just a light layer of only this lipstick the color is more subtle and suits me better.  If you like a lot of sparkle, you will love this.  It really leaves your lips shining in the sunlight.  Occasionally some of the glitter flakes will land on other places on you too, but it isn't really bad about that.  The lipstick itself is a really good lipstick.  It glides on easily and evenly and feels moisturizing.  You will notice more of the moisturizing feeling if you apply it heavily, however, so with my light application that feeling doesn't last long.  The color doesn't seem to last any longer than any other lipstick, but I never feel like any of them last too long.  The case of this lipstick is actually quite nice as well.  It is very smooth and shiny and has a bit of a weighted feel to it.  The lid is magnetic so as you go to close it up the lid snaps itself shut.  I appreciate the lack of parabens, sulfates, etc and that it is cruelty free as well.  Due to the fact that the current listed price of $42 for one stick of lipstick is quite above budget for me, I will not purchase this one again.  However, if that weren't the case, I would be interested in trying some of the other shades.

reviews.title: Warm and bold color

reviews.title: Nice chair
reviews.text:
Very nice chair. This one competes nicely with the one at Cosco that my buddy bought for $1800. I’m 6’2” 280lbs and it fits me comfortably. And you can sit in it as a regular chair when you have it off. Now instructions how to operate are definitely not clear so you have to fiddle around with it to figure out. And instructions how to put together are not clear either. I’m a carpenter with mechanical skills and you need moderate critical thinking skills to put together. The delivery company delivered it where I wanted it but you have to put sides on and plug in pads. Kind of hard to push air tubes into fittings too.
When you have it together and you get it working it messages your back nicely, sometimes it’s alittle rough on lower back but all in all great on back. Feet massage is ok too. Now the arm and calf air bags can get out of control so sometimes I’m not up for the torture and I leave them out and with no clear instructions how to maneuver the hand held remote, I just leave my arms and legs out, But like I said sometimes i can take it. The air bags adjust to your arms and legs but they are strong! But with that said I’m glad I bought it very nice great message, glad I bought.

The power is just perfect for my lower back. Other than that the neck position is perfect too. I love it so much. I would recommend to whoever looking for a massage chair.

reviews.title: More pain inducing than pain relief
reviews.text:
Where to start with this thing...?

Pro's first I guess:
- Sturdy and well made. It was relatively easy to put together once it arrived, but somewhat cumbersome trying to do so alone. Assembly involves raising up the back seat from the bottom portion (Akin to folding up the back seat of a car) and sliding on the arm bolts into a groove, attaching the hoses, rotating it onto a metal beam, and screwing in a screw into the wooden beams of the arm through a hole in the seat.
- The S-Beam thing was pretty powerful, so it is definitely capable of doing that "deep tissue" massage on your back but is more of a Con in this case (Will describe later)
- Leg supports were "extendable" (according to the exact words of the images on the product) in that if you pushed hard enough, the spring loaded support would give out a bit and allow the leg segment to go out a bit. If you didn't apply constant pressure, it would spring back into place (somewhat of a con / misleading feature advertisement)

Now for the Cons:
- The biggest and most glaring issue is that this thing *caused* more pain than helped it. The S-Track system they have in place is falsely advertised in their product video and images showing the track going all the way down to the hips / buttocks. This is not even remotely the case. It stops right at the lower back. I have attached a screenshot of their product demonstration video and a red line indicating exactly where the S-Track system *actually* goes down to. In addition to the fact that the massage is not complete, this is compounded by the fact that the product description lists the width of the rollers as "adjustable" This is also *not* the case as there is no method to do so from the user's controller. The rollers DO adjust outwards and inwards, but this is a feature of the massage, not something that the user can control to suit their needs. A bit of deliberate misleading on their part, if you ask me.
- As stated before, the S-Track system isn't at all what it advertised as. We also had no indication of this "Body Scan" feature working as intended. The instructions state that when you turn on the unit, it scans your body. There is no LED / Indicator / noise / alert / etc. indicating that this is taking place. When we turned on the unit it just immediately going into a default massage mode. There were no buttons to invoke this feature manually, nor any way to adjust the body settings. You get a rudimentary "Massage Type", "Intensity Setting" and a "manual mode" where you can direct the S-Track to a specific spot up or down the track. Thats all.
- The Air pouches that are used are WAY too strong. I had this thing turned all they way down on the "Intensity" setting and the air pouches were still cutting off circulation of our wrists on both myself and my fiance who used it. The feeling is akin to placing your entire arm in one of those blood pressure arm cuffs filled to the maximum - It is not enjoyable and it does not feel relaxing, it just borderline hurts and feels like someone is squeezing your arm. There is a video review by TJ that said the same thing, so it certainly isn't just our unit.
- There is another set of air bags that attempt to press on your shoulders, which actually did feel nice, but the sheer force on the bags hold you in place unless you make a concentrated effort to get out - this wound up causing problems later on when the S-Track got to the upper back / neck area as the rollers were so far forward for the massage it was genuinely stabbing us in the back with the air bags keeping us held in place unable to relieve the pressure of the rollers digging into our spine. The situation is hard to describe in words sadly, but effectively think it of as someone digging their elbows into your back as hard as they can with another person holding you in place unable to move - in most situations on massage chairs you can just arch your back a bit to get off the rollers until its in a more comfortable spot... You cannot do that while the shoulder bags are inflated as they hold you down pretty well. A relatively fit person like myself can get out of it easily, but this might not be the case for people purchasing this unit for medical reasons / disabilities, or for the elderly = Just something to keep in mind.
- There were no pre-drilled holes in the chair where you were required to put the screws. I triple checked the instructions to ensure what I was about to do was correct.... You must force the provided screws through the leather lining and into the wood beams inside the arm segments manually. I was not happy that I would be potentially tearing / ruining the leather to follow their instructions. If I need a screw there, pre-drill it during manufacturing.
- Chair has built in speakers on the arm units, but I have no knowledge on how to use them, nor were they listed in the instructions. There was no instructions to download any apps for the unit, nor did I see any bluetooth devices on my phone pop up while in discovery mode, so they seem to be for the chair itself, but we never heard anything aside from some female voice in what I assume to be French when we plugged the thing in the wall. There were no 3 millimeter jacks or any other type of audio jack on the chair itself.

Problems specifically related to the *SELLER* and not the chair:
There were numerous shipping issues we encountered with the third party seller here. Not only did we receive our chair, but we received half of another customer who also bought this chair in my area. We contacted the company that delivered it and we made them aware of the mistake but they had told us the earliest they can come back would be in another 2 weeks and we would have to hold on to it until then - despite that they had just been to my residence 3 hours ago. So essentially I was stuck with a 120 pound box that was about 6 feet in length in the middle of my living room. Luckily I found the intended recipients Name, Address, and Phone number and gave him a call. He was happy to come pick it up from my residence. I didn't have to wait 2 weeks to get it out of my house, and he didn't have to wait 2 weeks to get something he had paid for. The fact that the recipient had to pick up his own delivery from me because the company would not fix it in a timely manner was unacceptable. During the return process for this, I ended up having to call Amazon support today because they had come to pick it up July 9th (July 25 at time of writing) and I had not been refunded my payment even though the product was picked up from my residence more than 2 weeks ago. The support agent called them and confirmed that they had the product, but it has been sitting in the warehouse for a week and was scheduled to be sent back to the retailer sometime soon - effectively artificially delaying my refund until after the due date of my first payment on this. Props to Amazon though as the agent processed the refund for me anyway, as she confirmed they had gotten the product back from me and the third party company were just being slow. Otherwise I would be missing a credit card payment on my Amazon card for a product I technically didn't owe money for anymore.

Overall, we were not happy with this unit at all as we were suffering more pain simply using it than dealing with our existing pain so we returned it. The headache alone from the delivery company responsible for this seller is more than enough for us to void any future consideration of these products.

reviews.title: Airbrushy, beautiful foundation application!
reviews.text:
First, let me get a disclaimer out of the way ... I received this item at a discount in exchange for leaving an honest, unbiased review ... but I'd buy another one at full price if I needed it. I hate having to wonder whether a review is legit or not as I, too, do a LOT of Amazon shopping and use reviews to make purchase decisions. So I'll ALWAYS tell the truth, even if it isn't pretty. So here goes!

I've tried the original Beauty Blender and then tried a few mocks to see how they'd compare - in an attempt to save some money after having to trash the original after 3 o 4 months of use for sanitary/skincare reasons. All the others did an OK job, but nowhere near how nicely the original applied my foundation ... until this one!

I'm so stoked that I found a reasonably priced substitute for the original Beauty Blender (who wants to pay $20 if they can get away with pay8ing $8-ish for the same result?). This one really feels like the expensive ones, is also latex free and applies my foundation like a dream - airbrushy, smooth, consistently blended. I love it! Definitely buying a few more to have on hand as I have to replace them. There's no weird scent or anything, in case you've ever had blending sponges that did have an odd smell. I'm not sure what benefit the vitamin E infusion is, but I'd buy this sponge either way.

reviews.title: Good BB Alternative
reviews.text: Having tried the Beauty Blender sponges, I was looking for a cheaper alternative.  This one fits the bill VERY well.  It's the closest in texture to the BB that I have found yet.  I find that it blends my makeup just as well as my BB at less than 1/3 of the price!  It's just a little bit larger than the BB, compared side-by-side, when wet and I like that.  I will definitely be repurchasing when I wear this one out.  I have had it for a week and used/washed it four times, so far, and it's holding up perfectly.

reviews.title: A dupe for the brand name blender? in my opinion, YES!!
reviews.text:
This is absolutely the softest beauty blending sponge I've ever used, and I've used a lot! Most makeup sponges are much more dense and don't soften very much, even when it's filled with water. This sponge is so incredibly soft and squishy right out of the package! I washed it before using it the first time and it did nearly double in size. This sponge doesn't soak up much of my makeup and it bounces across my skin easily making my skin look flawless. So far, I've used it mostly for my foundation, but I've used it a couple times for my undereye concealer and powder. I've also used the pointed end around my nose and in the corners of my eyes to make sure everything is covered. I'm very happy with this beauty sponge, it does everything it says it will and it does it extremely well! If you're looking for a less expensive 'beauty blender' - this is the one for you! It's by far the closest to the brand name one that I've ever used. I'll be buying a couple more of these just to have them on hand.

* I received this beauty blender at a slight discount (not free) and my review is my own ~ I have absolutely no reason to give it stars it doesn’t deserve. When I shop online, I rely heavily on the reviews of others, so I make sure that when I write my own, you get the full story. If it’s a good, bad or ugly product - I will always review it fairly. I hope you have found my review helpful!

reviews.title: Most blenders I've tried feel like I'm punching my face lol but being a makeup ...
reviews.text: Was extremely soft!!!! Most blenders I've tried feel like I'm punching my face lol but being a makeup artist I loved the texture and the coverage from this product

reviews.title: Great for blending, contouring, and precision application of makeup!
reviews.text: This make-up sponge feels good in the hand--not too big and not too small. It is perfect in terms of being able to not only apply my foundation with the wide end, but also using the narrower sides and end to blend and to cover those smaller areas around my mouth, nose, and eye areas. I recently invested in some more expensive, quality makeup, and decided that I needed to find a quality sponge.  This sponge will last, It  also cleans easily--I just wash it with a gentle hand soap and rinse it out. It does not lose its shape--it is a nice firm sponge. It also does not smell "funny" like some sponges do after they have been used and washed a few times. This sponge is a great addition to my make up, and in fact is the best blending sponge that I have found yet. I did purchase this sponge at a discount for purposes of providing an honest, unbiased review. I like how well this sponge works for me, and will continue to purchase it.

reviews.title: Blends makeup beautifully once you know how to use it!
reviews.text:
I have had my eye on the makeup blender sponges at Ulta, but just couldn’t justify the price. This is a much more affordable option and it has a very feminine, pretty packaging. I haven’t tried the more expensive ones to be able to compare them, but I can compare the effects of this sponge compared to my nice makeup brushes.

The first day I used the sponge I was really disappointed. I dipped it in water and dried it off on a towel and put my makeup (It cosmetics CC cream) on the big end of the sponge. Then I used it to rub the makeup all over my face. And there was very little makeup on my face! I was thinking I wasted my money. Then I put the sponge in water to try to clean it off and it expanded A LOT – over half an inch in length and so much in diameter that it would no longer fit inside the packaging. I figured I had used it wrong. I put on my makeup with my brushes and decided to seek more information on how to use the sponge for the next day.

Before trying my sponge the second day, I watched a few YouTube videos on how to use makeup blender sponges. I had definitely used it wrong the first day! It is important to get the sponge really wet and fully expanded before using it to help keep the sponge from absorbing your makeup. So this time I let it fully expand in the water then blotted it dry on a towel. I then used it to blot my makeup all over my face. This technique actually worked.

After playing around with different methods, I have found that spotting my face with the makeup first and then using the sponge to blot it all over my face before the makeup dries works the best. I get a nice airbrushed finish without streaks from a brush. It has also helped me to not put on too much makeup. With the brushes I was having problems with having excess makeup in certain areas of my face. By the end of the day my hands and everything in sight would be covered in makeup! With the sponge I haven’t had this issue – it seems to really help the makeup blend into your skin and not just pile up and rub off.

I did receive this item at a discounted rate in exchange for an honest review. I have been pleased with how it works and would recommend it to others who are interested in the makeup blender sponges but don’t want to waste money on the pricey name-brand ones or on cheaper ones that don’t work.

reviews.title: First time user, impressed with application
reviews.text: This is my first time using a make up sponge and I was highly impressed. I used liquid foundation a couple years ago and couldn't get a smooth application. My fingers left my face looking splotchy and a foundation brush applied the liquid too thick. I switched to mineral powder foundation for better coverage, but it's messy and not travel friendly. I saw this and wanted to try it. I have no other brands to compare it to, but these are my impressions: It's about the size of an egg, it becomes nice and soft and expands when submerged in water. I watched a couple youtube videos for guidance on the technique. I put some liquid makeup on the back side of my hand and dabbed it on. I had great coverage after about two layers. The sponge provided good coverage for my redness and I did't get any irritation like I normally do from my mineral brush sweeping across my sensitive skin. Overall, I am really glad I purchased this. It seems like a good density sponge that won't fall apart. The packaging says this should be washed after every use and replaced every two months. I'm looking forward to taking it on my upcoming trip and not worrying about all my mineral make up sifting through the lid during travel! I received this item at a discount in exchange for my review, but will definitely purchase again before paying 3 times the cost for other brands sold at high end make up stores.
