reviews.title: LOVE! super gentle manual exfoliation & chemical peeling product; deep cleans pores; brightens
reviews.text:
I am a big fan of Neogen products, and Korean beauty products in general. I like that the focus tends to be on creating healthy glowing skin through skin loving ingredients and maximizing hydration, which all skin types need. I find these Neogen Real Charcoal Pads to be super gentle, non irritating, lightly hydrating, skin brightening, deep cleaning of pores and other yucks on skin. Skin never ever tight, never irritated-- and I have non acne ROSACEA (flushing, telangiectasias/veins).

I hadn't used the charcoal pads before, so I was excited to try them out. There are 60 pads in a tub with twist off lid. One side of the pad is quilted and you can clearly see the charcoal coloring. The other side is smooth. The pads are quite damp, and there is plenty of product on them to take care of face and neck, or to use as a targeted "sheet mask". Avoid eye area.

DIRECTIONS: AFTER washing, swipe the quilted side over skin. Then use smooth side to pick up traces of impurities. Pat in remaining essence.

When I swipe the quilted side over my skin, I like to go slow with gentle pressure. I allow the product to sit for a moment before flipping the pad over to the smooth side (more white than gray) and wipe my face. One side applies the product, the other helps to lift up what is left behind- or brought to surface as it is meant to draw out impurities and wastes. I noticed that the pad was turning tan/brownish leading me to believe that these pads were indeed doing a good job of breaking down excess junk on my face (despite a good cleansing) and leaving my skin feeling supple and clean. I followed the directions that state to pat in any excess product. I wasn't sure whether to follow up with my hydrating toners, or skip to my Hyaluronic Acid (HA). Since skin needs moisture to make HA most effective, I patted on one layer of hydrating toner, a layer of HA, followed by a moisturizer to help lock in the hydration. I think this charcoal pad is gentle enough to use daily, if need be. However, I have been using it 2-3x a week. You will have to experiment to see what is best for you. My 40 something rosacea skin is sensitive, leans dry, and loves hydration. My skin can use an occasional boost in really purifying my pores and such. I am really liking this product so far. It just feels so gentle on my skin!

Benefits, and a little more info:

--Charcoal powder and peeling essence ingredient- Hydroxycinnamic Acid- provide double synergy effect for absorbing sebum and removing dead skin cells. The charcoal powder delivers gentle yet effective adsorption benefit to draw impurities, while hypoallergenic peeling essence (Hydroxycinnamic Acid) provides manual exfoliation combined with chemical peeling effect.

--Manual exfoliation + chemical peeling effect.
--With two sides including Charcoal pad that cleanses the skin and embossing pad that softly purifies the skin.

--Pore clarifying: Formulated with natural Peat water and Charcoal powder(310 ppm), these help to draw deep-seated sebum, whiteheads, dead skin cells, and other impurities out of pores for smoother and clearer skin.

--Gentle Peeling: NEOGEN PHA™ removes dead skin cells and impurities without irritation or discomfort while leaving the skin radiantly hydrated and smooth. Softly cleanses skin, non irritating.

--Skin soothing: TECA™ formula (Titrated Extract of Centella Asiatica) delivers a soothing benefit to skin; helps create bright skin. Centella Asiatica has potent antioxidant properties, rich source of amino acids, and there's additional research showing that it's a good hydrating ingredient to soothe upset or compromised skin.

INGREDIENTS:
Water, Butylene Glycol, Glycerin, Dipropylene Glycol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Betaine, Panthenol, Charcoal Powder(310ppm), Peat Water, Asiaticoside, Madecassic Acid, Asiatic Acid, Acacia Senegal Gum, Cetearyl Olivate, Sorbitan Olivate, Allantoin, Gluconolactone, Citrus Aurantium Bergamia (Bergamot) Fruit Oil, Glyceryl Caprylate, Tromethamine, Ethylhexylglycerin, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Xanthan Gum, Propanediol, Citric Acid, Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose, Alcohol, Rutin, Hydroxycinnamic Acid, Potassium Sorbate, Disodium EDTA

reviews.title: Very Hydrating & Lightly Abrasive
reviews.text: My husband and I have both used these. I like the hydration, the hydroxy acid and the feel of these -- I do not find them too abrasive at all. I find them to have a very mild exfoliation and very mild peeling effect. My husband says that his skin tingles after shaving because they have alcohol (this has very little alcohol because it is listed way at the end of a long list of ingredients). So, I like them much more than he does! These pads are embossed with charcoal powder that draws impurities out of the skin and clarify the pores. My skin feels extremely soothed and hydrated after using these!

reviews.title: Irritates my skin...
reviews.text: These aren't pads to clean make-up off your face(at least not fully), like I thought they were. They're actually supposed to be used after washing your face. After wiping your face with them, you're supposed to press any excess liquid onto your skin and then leave it. I found them a little too harsh for my skin. On the front of the container, it says they're soothing, but I didn't get that at all. My face feels scratched and irritated after I use them and I for sure don't like the feeling of leaving the liquid on my face. It's an itchy feeling and it makes me anxious, as opposed to soothed. I ended up having to wash it off after each time I gave them a try. I like the idea of them, but unfortunately, they're not for me.

reviews.title: NEOGEN DERMALOGY REAL CHARCOAL PADS
reviews.text: These NEOGEN DERMALOGY REAL CHARCOAL PADS are easy and convenient to use. They are a nice size and are very moist. One side is exfoliating and the other is more cleansing. They leave my skin feeling clean and hydrated. I don't know how effective they are for drawing out impurities but they do work well for cleansing. I have not experienced any irritation or sensitivity after using these.

reviews.title: Very refreshing and great quality
reviews.text:
NEOGEN DERMALOGY REAL CHARCOAL PAD 5.07oz / 150ml (60 PADS)

These wipes are absolutely amazing! I was super impressed by how refreshing these feel. Despite the charcoal, they are rather smooth but do exfoliate to a degree.

First off these are very well packaged. They come incredibly moist, not dried out at all. Hard to believe there are 60 of them in the container but there are. Despite it being a little small one wipe is more than enough for a single usage because of how much moisture is in each wipe.

These work really well on dry skin. They don’t feel oily but they certainly feel like the ingredients are being absorbed by my skin. They have a faint scent that is pleasant and just as refreshing as they feel. The price is fair given how many you get and the quality of them. All around this is a great product.

reviews.title: Very Soothing and Excellent for a Quick Pick-me-up!
reviews.text:
I’m loving Neogen products, and these pads are no exception. They’re perfect if you’re not into a big cleansing routine on a relaxed day, but you still want your face to feel clean and fresh. They’re soft and gentle, but they are very capable of exfoliation, depending on the amount of pressure you apply. Very easy to use, and they’re incredibly moist, leaving your face feeling clean, hydrated, and bright.

These pads work well as a toner and are very soothing to slightly inflamed skin. There are 60 pads in the jar, and they appear to be larger than others I’ve tried. I think its good value for the current price of $22.00.

reviews.title: Great for sensitive skin
reviews.text: Kind of expensive, but when I use this my face breaks out a bit as it releases toxins and then glows the following day. I am highly sensitive to ingredients and this doesn't make me break out. I expect this from K Beauty. Rarely fails. It just works.  The pads are rough to exfoliate which is great. I feel like I could cut the pad in half and stretch the cost per use.

reviews.title: Seems To Make a Difference
reviews.text: I have sensitive skin, and also with some health issues, my skin has really not been looking good lately.  I also have very dry skin.  These charcoal pads seem to be making a little difference in my skin. First off, it hasn't irritated me.  I've been using them like every other day or so to see how that goes.  My skin feels a little softer after use, and looks nice.  I like that it's a very subtle, faint scent, barely there.  Strong scents tend to bug me.  These barely have a scent, just a soft, fresh scent.  It doesn't linger with you after you are done cleansing your face.  I also like how handy it is to have a little jar of pads, mess-free, they are not over-saturated and dripping, yet also not dry.

reviews.title: OK Facial Pads.
reviews.text:
These charcoal pads are intended to be used after you wash your face. They are suppose to draw out impurities (charcoal side) and soothe the skin for a "healthier" look.

The pads are premoistened and have plenty of moisture. It is easy to discern the charcoal side. After about 3 weeks of use every 3rd day, I cannot see the difference in my skin. I'm not a heavy make-up wearer, so this may be a factor in trying to see noticeable differences when using/not using the Neogen charcoal pad.

reviews.title: Great cleansing pads.  I love them.
reviews.text: I love these pads! They remove everything off my skin before washing with a cleanser. My pores feel so much cleaner and healthier when I use this.

reviews.title: Would buy again!
reviews.text: This is an amazing product! My skin feels fresh and the pads aren't too rough on my skin! I use it every alternate night right before bed, and I pair it with SOME BY MI AHA, BHA, PHA 30 Days Miracle Toner, and people have really started noticing a difference in my skin. Totally worth it!

reviews.title: Excelente producto
reviews.text: me encanta este producto, lo uso para desmanchar mi piel por exceso de sol y funciona, mis manchas han disminuido y mi piel se ve más brillante

reviews.title: An average exfoliating experience
reviews.text: I enjoy NeoGen products and was eager to try out this new addition to their line. I was surprised too say the least when I opened the package. Though it does have a charcoal pad, I didn’t quite get the same feeling as when I use the Lemon or Wine pads. It’s like a 3rd or 4th step exfoliating product. Honestly, I wouldn’t purchase this particular one again as I love their other pads so much. But if you need an additional exfoliating product then definitely buy.

reviews.title: Great toner pads!
reviews.text: Great pads for cleaning your pores! I did not break out when I used this!

reviews.title: Good Results, Good Men's Lotion
reviews.text: My husband has dry skin that is worsening now that cold weather has arrived and the heat is on in the house on pretty much a regular basis.  I ordered this cream to help moisturize his dry skin as well as  reduce his fine lines as he approaches the 'big 5-0'.  He's not yet noticed a reduction to his fine lines though he hasn't been using the lotion very long but he is pleased with the job its doing to help moisturize his skin.  His skin isn't nearly as dry as it had been so he is experiencing some much needed relief.  He is a fan of the scent and he's happy that the lotion doesn't cause his skin to look or feel oily/greasy.  He's definitely satisfied with this cream and the results he is receiving thus far.

reviews.title: Pricey but Decent
reviews.text: This has worked pretty well for me over the past couple weeks of use, but I can’t say it’s worked better than products that cost half the price. It’s a good moisturizer, but it’s overpriced.

reviews.title: a face cream for use in the colder seasons
reviews.text:
I like that this product uses oils like jojoba, cocoa, and coconut. This face cream is a little on the richer side (but not super rich and thick to where it is hard to soak into the skin). It is a little on the oily side but the thing is now that the colder/fall/winter season is coming, this is the perfect face cream for the season. I would not recommend this for use during the hot summer but your skin will drink this up in the cold. The scent was light and pleasant, but that always comes down to personal preference as some people are very sensitive to smell.

It says this cream has collagen and stem cells, but I’m not sure how much of those ingredients are used in the formulation. It would be nice to know because if there is too little then there is probably very little anti wrinkle benefits. As with all beauty/skin care products it takes time to see the results. Overall this is a good face cream if you just want something moisturizing for the face but I can’t say yet if this will help erase or prevent any wrinkles.

reviews.title: If you sleep alone
reviews.text:
The marketing blurb says, among other things "this advanced man face care formula features stem cells to help revive the skin and smooth out imperfections".
Well... stem cells, if they were actually somehow harvested, in a cosmetics container, mixed with various other chemicals and ingredients would necessarily be dead and therefore completely irrelevant. And, of course, the stem cells would help address things such as diabetes and repair of heart muscle following heart attack, not zits on one's face. Not to mention that they would need to be live and 'human' cells. So, I am quite confident that the 'stem cells' claim is untrue and, even if it were true, the presence of dead stem cells in a cream would make zero difference.
Other than that, the cream is thick and, as other reviewer found, it has an aggressive smell that reminded me of Noxzema. So I would never use it during the day. At night, it would probably be Okay if used by men who sleep alone.

reviews.title: Leaves skin SO Soft, BUT Propylene Glycol is the 2nd ingredient (water is 1st)!
reviews.text: This moisturizer leaves my boyfriend's skin so soft feeling. The scent is light and very likable! When the container arrives, it is in a box that looks really nice. After you unscrew the cap of the container, there is a plastic moisture cap that lays on top. That is good to keep on the container because it keeps keep moisture inside. It has a little finger handle on the top so it is easy to remove. It's a great product but it costs a bit too much as there are other products that can do the same for a lot less.

reviews.title: Moisturizes without a strong scent
reviews.text: This works well on my face.  I apply it around my face and neck and gently rub it in.  It goes on a bit thick but rubs in nicely leaving my face smooth.  The scent is mild and pleasant which is key for me since I don't like strong smells -- especially chemical smells or perfumes.  Hoping that it works well to limit wrinkles as my faces ages -- we'll see.

reviews.title: Men's facial moisturizer with new ingredients!
reviews.text: This facial moisturizer from Gentlehomme is a combination of effective ingredients for revitalizing, rejuvenating, and hydrating men's skin. Collagen and other herbal oils in this lotion are effective ingredients for reducing redness and long-lasting moisturizing. The cream has a medium thickness and absorption rate. It takes a while to be completely absorbed on my skin and it does not keep any residue on my face. However, it has a long-lasting effect which I can feel for a few hours. My skin becomes smoother when I use it once a day. The cream has a gentle scent. Although I feel some minor burning or stinging at the moment I apply the cream on my dry skin, the feeling fades away after a few minutes. I am also not sure about the impacts of stem cells on my skin yet, as I may need to use it for a longer time to see the promised positive effects on skin wrinkles and dark spots. Lastly, the cream is ordered by Gentelhomme (USA), but it is Made in China, which I was not expected initially, although I liked the quality and hydration performance on my skin.

reviews.title: Great Product that Works
reviews.text:
Face Moisturizers, and cosmetics in general, are typically not available or made with men in mind, so it is refreshing to find a beauty-type product and product line that caters to the male of the species.

Gentlehomme is certainly on to something pleasantly surprising, with a face moisturizer that contains many of the ingredients you might find in higher end products for women, such as collagen, oils, Vitamin E (tocopherol), butters, and stem cells. The ingredient list is rather exhaustive, with probably more than you need, but I have to appreciate a product that is well thought out. In terms of just the 'butters' and 'oils', the face moisturizer includes Cocoa Seed Butter, Coconut Oil, Avocado Oil, Bitter Orange Flower Oil, and Jojoba Seed Oil.

Unlike a few other reviews I've read of this product, I did not find the product to be greasy at all. It absorbed rather quickly into the skin, leaving it soft and supple, with a slight glow, without being shiny. It does have a thick, white, whipped consistency to it, similar to other facial creams out there, such as Noxzema. Also being four ounces, rather than two ounces, which is more standard for facial creams, makes this a pretty good value, overall.

Also unlike other comments that I've read, I did not find the Neroli scent overpowering or unlikeable at all. With that said, however, I found the smell to be very faint and more non-descript than of Neroli (Bitter Orange). The fragrance is very light--not necessarily masculine as one might expect, but almost indistinguishable from most other products out there--not offensive but not remarkable. My nose is rather sensitive to scents and I would have difficulty picking this one out in a crowd.

I like that I didn't have any negative reaction(s) to the product--no burning or stinging, rash or inflammation, and the moisturizer held up all day and all night without any discomfort or facial skin drying. It applies easily and a little of it can go a long way.

I would like to know a little bit more about the stem cells that are used in this formulation, as the literature and description is rather empty. Stem cells are supposed to help renew and rejuvenate the skin to make it look younger. Are these plant or fruit stem cells (most likely) or human stem cells? Live or extracts? Similar questions about the type of collagen that is used might also be beneficial, or worthy of a more detailed description or commentary.

It's advertised promise to moisturize, revitalize and hydrate seems spot-on. I rate this product as an excellent choice in the limited field of men's facial moisturizers.

Recommended. Made in China for Gentlehomme, LLC, U.S.A. Certified Vegan.

reviews.title: rich
reviews.text: I like the smell. It says it’s Neroli essential oil. I would simply describe it as a midl cologne-like scent. The moisturizer is rich, creamy and silky. I would not describe it as a light moisturizer although it’s also not as heavy as some. However, I reserve it for situations that call for a strong moisturizer. It absorbs well and I like the container.

reviews.title: excellent hydration and not greasy
reviews.text: I sometimes get a bit of dry skin on my face after a shower, if I have not used any moisturizer or facial lotion.  It gets to the point of flaking in some areas, and needless to say I would like to avoid that.  This cream is really nice.  I have used many creams for my face over the years, but I think this is my favorite so far.  It is thick and moisturizes without being greasy.  It also saturates very well, and quickly.  I hate creams that you need to work into the skin for a long time, and this one is not like that at all.  I find that a little goes a long way, and so I just dab my finger in the jar, and put a few spots of cream on my face, then spread it around, and it seems like I hardly use any at all.  Even though, the amount that I use, does the trick, and I have not had any dry skin.  It comes in some pretty nice packaging, but most importantly it works very nicely, and delivers good results for daily moisturizing to avoid dry skin.  I do recommend it!

reviews.title: This lotion is good for everybody
reviews.text: This masculine smelling lotion is actually more feminine than you would think I've used some on myself men and women and nobody knows the difference in everybody likes it

reviews.title: Good & Hydrating
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:84a5ae506619190f739d3befcde0a9bc]] After about 2 weeks of use I can say this face cream has a nice texture and kept my skin moisturized throughout the day and doubles as a night cream. Be careful not to overdo it because a little goes a long way. If you use too much it feels greasy.
It's cruelty free and uses plant stem cells like some other expensive brands who use this technology. The only thing I didnt care for was the fragrance. Not a deal breaker but I personally just wasn't a fan. Although my wife said she didnt mind it and that it smelled masculine. All in all it's a good hydrating cream and is priced well. A great value, especially if not trying to break the bank on skincare.

reviews.text: I am absolutely OBSESSED with this product from Gentlehomme!!!! The scent is amazing and the moisturizing is out of this world!!!! 10/10 would recommend!!

reviews.title: GREAT BUY
reviews.text: I LOVE THIS SET IT MAKES MY HAIR SMELL SO GOOD AND IT IS REALLY SOFT AND SEEMS LIKE IT IS STARTING TO WORK I RECIEVED THIS PRODUCT AT A DISCOUNTED COST FOR MY HONEST OPINION AND REVIEW

reviews.title: 5 Drops Will Do Ya
reviews.text: This is a very nice hair serum that is targeted at encouraging and speeding up hair growth. The first ingredent, and therefore the  most prevalent ingredient, is a type of silicone. Silicone has become the most popular ingredient for anti-friz and shine serums for hair, not to mention a whole world of cosmetics like oil free moisturizers and foundations. It has a slip to it, which gives silicone the *feeling of being like an oil, but it is by its nature, is totally oil free. And of course, also by its nature, it cannot clog pores or follicles. Infact, the Dae Long Hair Growth Serum formula contains three different types of silicone as its first three ingredients. The active ingredients in the product, or the ingredients that actually enable the product to do its job of encouraging hair growth, are Jojoba Oil, Macadamia Nut Oil, Lupine Protein, and Panax Ginseng. These nourish and stimulate the hair follicles to remain longer in the anagen, or hair growing phase, and to produce healthier, stronger hair from the root. Jojoba oil is a skin compatible oil, meaning that it cannot and does not clog pores or follicles, closely resembles the body's own natural lubrication, and can be absorbed by human hair, skin, and nails. There is also extract of Macadamia Nut Oil, which moisturizes the hair cuticle and shaft and helps to prevent hair breakage and split ends. Hydrolyzed protein from the seeds of the lupine plant are very high in protein and contain 35-45% of all the essential amino acids. Panax ginseng is an extract from the panax ginger root and has nourishing and stimulating qualities that directly feed and nourish the hair follicle. I was fortunate to be able to order this product at a discounted rate in exchange for my honest and unbiased review. I have used a great many hair serums and products to stimulate growth of stronger, longer, and healthier hair. One of my favorite things about this Dae Long Hair Growth Serum is that it has a very pleasant fragrance! I also really like that the ingredient list is simple, straightforward, and does not contain weird chemicals or scary preservatives!!! It has a very clean  feel to it, not leaving your hair greasy at the last part that you want to be greasy, the roots. A few drops goes a long way I say five drops will do. While pleasant, It is also important not to over do it!. I have very, very sensitive skin and had no problem at all with this. Time will tell how much it makes the hair grow. I am also taking a good hair, skin & nails vitamin because as a Licensed Esthetician, I know for a fact that you have to feed and nourish INSIDE AS WELL AS OUTSIDE in order to truly grow longer, stronger, and healthier hair!!

reviews.title: Unfortunately, the serum is too heavy for my hair ...
reviews.text: Unfortunately, the serum is too heavy for my hair. It makes it look weighed down and greasy, rather than adding any volume.

reviews.title: No. Don't do it
reviews.text: This is a waste of time and money

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: The serum makes my hair looks full hair-volume. It also makes my hair soft.

reviews.title: I really really like the DAELONG products
reviews.text:
I have been using the serum for a little over 3 weeks. I really really like the DAELONG products. Take a look at my other review of the hair treatment. It’s absolutely amazing. However, the quick grow serum didn’t work for me. Its consistency was too watery for my taste. You can definitely tell it has argan oil in it. There is something else in this product that causes it to be a little soapy if you apply more than 3 squirts.

I usually apply this product to freshly washed, but dry hair. Just to allow it to have its full effect. I also applied it nightly and something during the day if I remembered before work. I did not lose any more hair while using this product but I also didn’t gain any hair as well. I wish I would’ve taken some pictures to really be sure that I haven’t had hair growth but I watch for growth every day. I can guarantee I am pretty sure. I like products with a thicker consistency so what works for me may not work for you. I have very thick no porosity hair.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Results are seen inmediately!

reviews.title: Very impressive for wavy/curly hair
reviews.text:
My hair is naturally wavy/curly and tends to be dry and on the frizzy side. I don't use heat tools other than for special occasions. It's also on the fine thin side. I have the length I want, it goes to the middle of my back, but i'm looking for something to help with making it thicker. I don't know if this product will help with that but willing to give it a try. It does say that it will make it fuller so that gives me hope. I have tried probably every hair care product there is from cheap to expensive. I have found some that i'm more pleased with then others.
So now the selling points of this product are:
~This serum will stimulate hair growth at an impressive speed; just after 1-2 weeks of usage your hair will start getting longer and fuller!
~The enriched formula of this serum will nourish your roots and follicles deeply, helping your hair become denser, fuller and stronger.
~This serum will offer deep moisture to your hair, making it look healthy and glowing. Your hair will soon be silky soft and tempting to the touch! Forget dry, frizzy hair once and for all.
~ Just 5-6 drops of this amazing serum will rejuvenate your hair, making look it good when you don't have time to wash.
My honest and unbiased opinion for the discount price I paid for this serum:
The fragrance is a light soft scent which I like alot. The serum is light and easy to apply to the scalp. I pump 2 drops on my finger tips and massage in a section of my scalp. Repeating this till my entire scalp is massaged. I also use it on the length of my hair to tame the frizz. Just 4 or 5 drops in palms and smoothed down the length of hair is all is needed. I am very impressed with this serum. I have been using it for a week. No noticeable growth or fullness yet but the texture of my hair is so much smoother and noticeably less frizz. Some reviewers have stated it made their hair greasy feeling. Maybe they used to much or have straight hair. Curly wavy hair is drier due to the natural oils from the scalp can not travel down the hair shaft like straight hair. I am very pleased with this product!

reviews.title: Requires dedication.
reviews.text:
This is different from every hair product I have used in my life. It smells good, a bit like men's cologne, not in anyway overwhelming. I have heard from beauticians that massaging the scalp in daily intervals helps stimulate hair growth. So I do believe this has the potential to work as long as you follow the instructions and do it it consistently for 2 weeks. I would love to tell you the results but it has been 2 weeks and I suck at sticking to a schedule in the evening. So I have only used it once a day if that for the last 2 weeks. It is not sticky or oily. The consistency is kind of like diluted gel, if that makes sense. Kind of like thick water.
If you found this review helpful please let me know. Thank you

reviews.title: Soft and Silky Hair
reviews.text:
I have been using this product for some time now and I can say it is a smaller container than I expected but it is because you only need a small amount for your hair. I put this product on after I shower and it makes my hair not only detangle but silky smooth. I feel my hair has gotten healthier after six months of breast feeding my little girl. I would definitely reccomend this to anyone with dry and damaged hair like mine.

DISCLOSURE*** I received this item for free or discounted to give an honest and unbiased review. I was not paid or compensated to give a rating or to even review the product. I do not have a relationship with seller, vendor or manufacturer. I give ratings based on my experience and expected performance with a product and not what the seller wants me to put. My first impression is based upon my testing of this product. This is my opinion AND I only change my rating if the product under performs or breaks over time. I am disclosing this in accordance with the Federal Trade Commission 16 CFR Part 255: Guides Concerning the Use of Endorsement and Testimonials in Advertising.

reviews.title: Daelong
reviews.text:
I just received the product a few days ago and just started using it. I will get back with a review in a few weeks to see if this product stands by it's name. It doesn't have an odor and I do like that
Ok I am back with my second review. It has been over 3 months now since I have been using this product. It has grown my hair and most of all thickened it. I will most likely keep using this product simply because it does work. It literally took 3 months for me to see the difference when on the bottle it says 2 weeks of hair growth. Did not happen. But overall I do like the product.

reviews.title: I've only been using this product for a week now ...
reviews.text: I've only been using this product for a week now. Not long enough yet to really give a full review. But so far I have noticed my hair is shinier, softer & looks healthier overall! I will continue to use & give an updated review after more use of this product.

reviews.title: Three Stars
reviews.text: Using product for 3 weeks, it's showing very little progress. Will continue to use and see mir results.

reviews.title: It works guy!
reviews.text:
I was excited to get my product when I received it in the mail. I was a bit skeptical at first when I received it because it said that it would help my hair grow so much within a couple of weeks. My hair texture is naturally wavy and coarse. I have dry hair most of the time, so I am constantly trying to find a product that will moisturize my scalp. I also noticed my hair sheds more as I have experienced thinning around the front of my hairline because of overheating.
When I used this product I noticed my hair was not shedding as much as it did before and I was slowly gaining restored length over the weeks. My hair has improved in shine and has a more radiant, healthier look. I have seen an improvement with moisture co'nsistency, hair length, and volume. My hair in the weeks of use has improved, and I am glad to have been able to review this product.

reviews.title: I've been using it for a week now and I ...
reviews.text: I've been using it for a week now and I can definitely see improvement on my hair. It really works!

reviews.title: Magnetic Eyelashes
reviews.text: I am in love with this Magnetic Eyelashes kit. I have tried other brands before and really hated the eyeliner. This set has a precision eyeliner. It goes on so smooth and straight really love it and it makes the eyelashes to stick easily. I will say that you have to really apply a good amount of eyeliner all the way across the lid for the magnetic strips to work. It does take time and practice to really get the hang of these, but I find them so much easier than glue.

reviews.title: Big difference!
reviews.text:
This was my first attempt to do faux eye lashes. I can not compare to other systems, but I love it! I use the 3 pair from the top. The look was pretty normal. I had to cut a little of them because it was too long for me.
The set comes with 2 eye liner, tweezer and 5 pairs of lashes.
The liner was easy to apply and then the lashes were stuck on it fast and easy.
I went to a barbecue with them and they stay in the entire time.
After, they go out without any pain. The eye liner was a little hard to take off. I use my makeup wipes in a hard way. Haha.
It did not make my sensitive skin hurt or burn.
I love it!

reviews.title: Nice selection, super easy to use.
reviews.text:
I've always struggled with gluing in lashes and I had pretty much given up. I ordered these after a friend was telling me about how great magnetic liner is. I was super impressed with how easy it is for me to put these on.
I'm kind of confused on whether the liner is sticky and magnetic or just magnetic. I didn't let it dry all the way before doing my eyebrows and my lower eyelid got stuck to my upper eyelid. I couldn't tell if that was magnet pull or glue.
Whatever the liner is, it keeps those lashes in place and they are so easy to put on. The brush is thin but the thicker the line, the better it will hold on to the lashes.
My eye lids are sort of small and I think I'm going to have to trim these a tiny bit or the ends can be a little jabby in the corner of my eye.
I haven't worn these out yet due to the pandemic but I know the liner lasts because you have to scrub hard to get it off. These should last all day.

reviews.title: Much easier than glue
reviews.text:
This was my first attempt at using magnetic lashes; there's a small learning curve but oh my gosh - SO MUCH EASIER than the old eyelash glue that comes in a separate tube! They're very comfortable; like any false lashes they feel weird at first but after about 15 minutes I forgot they were there & wore them all day with no issues. They stay where you put them, they DO NOT budge until you pull them off. No glue melting or flaking off into my eyes, obviously that's a big benefit! They look super dramatic with pretty minimal effort.

I'm a little confused as to why it's best to apply the lashes when the eyeliner is half-dry but still tacky; is there glue built in to the liner as well as magnets? If you need to reposition them, you have to reapply the eyeliner because the lashes pull some of it off. The eyeliner is very wet when you apply, so be careful - you have to shake the tube before application, and when I pulled the brush out, it spewed eyeliner onto the wall (and it was not super easy to remove). So that's the learning curve part, now that I know I'll avoid those pitfalls.

The one unavoidable pitfall is that it is INCREDIBLY difficult to remove. I used 3 different types of makeup remover & still needed to scrub my eyelids for a good 10 minutes to get all of it. Not great for the eyelid wrinkle situation! I slathered on a lot of pampering eye cream afterward because my lids were a little sore. I guess I'm glad it won't wear off halfway through the day, but geez!

Anyway, despite the difficulty removing it, I'm pretty smitten. I used to love wearing false lashes but they were just so much trouble that I gave it up a few years ago. Now I love them again & they're easy!! I want to order all the lashes!

reviews.title: Better than most
reviews.text: These magnetic lashes are better than most at this price point. I like that two tubes of eyeliner are included, and several different sizes of lashes. It would have been better had there been some instructions. I recommend using several coats of eyeliner, and make it longer than you think it needs to be, to insure the corners of the lash do not curl up. The eyeliner stayed very well, and I got a full day of wear out of them!

reviews.title: Not the best prefer regular lashes
reviews.text: Not sure about these lashes they are very much long and thick but the magnetic application is whats throwing me cause I cant get them to sit and stay for long.  A d I'm really good at applying my regular lashes.  The magnetic lash glue kinda sucks I used my regular glue and it worked better for me the magnets work cause when I put them back in the case they clasp on quickly but  not on my eye lids. I dont really like them

reviews.title: Both glue bottles was dry
reviews.text: I obviously liked the shape and length,  However both bottles of glue was DRY DRY...

reviews.title: Stick very well.
reviews.text: Let me start by saying I am not a super girly girl. Fake eyelashes are newer to me. I have tried several different kinds and let me tell you these magnetic ones make the whole experience so much better. The magnetic eyeliner is amazing once you put that stuff on it is not coming off. The only down part of that is you need to make sure that you put it on correctly and exactly how you want it. The lashes stick to it incredibly well. I had major doubts about how well magnetic lashes would work and I would recommend these to anybody I was extremely impressed and surprised. It comes with four different types of lashes so it’s a nice variety none are overly big but they are all a little bigger than what I preferred they definitely aren’t natural. If I could get these a little smaller in a more natural look I would say they would be absolutely perfect!

reviews.title: Not for asian with straight eyelashes
reviews.text: These don't work if you are korean and don't have eyelids. My eyelashes are straight and when you put these on, you can see my straight lashes. Also some of them are over the top and not right for me. I liked 2 pairs but it was so hard to put on and it looked good except my straight lashes that show.

reviews.title: Messy
reviews.text: I ordered these lashes fior my wife with intentions of applying with ease. She was not pleased. The eyeliner was messy. The lashes hurt her eyes. We don’t recommend this product.

reviews.title: Look nice, lashes too long for my lash line
reviews.text:
This is such a cool concept that I’ve been curious to try ever since I heard about it. The magnetic eyeliner makes the small magnets on the falsies attach to your lash line. I’m going to need more practice with these and possibly cut them, because as they are they’re too long for my lash line, which causes them to poke me as I blink (NOT comfortable). They look good but like I said, not comfortable for me. I’ll work with them and see if I can somehow get them to be a little shorter. It’s difficult to cut because the magnets are placed a certain distance apart. That being said, the liner works great and the magnets attach easily to it.
I’ll update when I figure a way to make them work for me better. As they are now I could wear them to events but definitely not a whole day.

reviews.title: Don't stay well
reviews.text: I had to keep reapplying the magnetic eyeliner especially in the corners of my eyes where it maybe got wet from blinking. I didn't feel like I could go out without the eyeliner in my purse to reapply everything I had a mirror. To me that just too much work then and you might as well use regular lashes.

reviews.title: Better than dealing with eyelash glue!
reviews.text: This a fairly decent set of magnetic eyelashes. I will say I have tried a few different brands. The plus side about these is that they have several small magnets, making it easy to manipulate to your eye shape and have them fit correctly. Some brands only have 3 large magnets and the placing is hard to get a good fit. The second positive about these is that it comes with two bottles of the eyeliner which is awesome since you can keep reusing the eyelashes. I will say that these eyelashes are pretty long. I wouldn't say any of them are "natural" looking because of the length. I've toyed with cutting a pair to see if I like them better but am afraid I'll throw off the look...over all though I would say this is a good set. And I love magnetic eyelashes far more than dealing with eyelash glue!

reviews.title: Magnetic Lashes
reviews.text: There's a little bit of a learning curve with these magnetic lashes, but they're a bit more forgiving than adhesive lashes. You can remove them and reposition them (although you may need to reapply the magnetic liner). Most of the lashes needed to be trimmed just a bit in order to fit my eyes. They do pump up the drama when worn with a bold eye shadow.

reviews.title: Interesting, but you feel then on your lids
reviews.text: I have sensitive eyes and surrounding tissue. They looked nice and natural, but I could totally feel them the entire time and it made me itchy...so if you aren't sensitive to things near your eyes, then these are good, but for me, I wanted to pick at them all day.

reviews.title: Cant go wrong
reviews.text: Awesome product very happy

reviews.title: SO easy to apply!
reviews.text:
Let me start by saying that these are the easiest eyelashes that I have ever used. I was definitely skeptical but it really is as easy as swiping on the magnetic liner and then sticking the lash strip in place. THAT IS IT! No fussing, no dropping, no glue, no 2-piece magnet that you can't ever position properly. Just swipe and stick. Pretty much foolproof! I did find them to be a tad heavy on my eyelid but I imagine that is because I'm not used to wearing false lashes (because I could never figure how to get them on properly...haha) and I'm hoping it will get better with time.

Ok...now onto the negative. While these lashes are absolutely amazing, I was appalled to receive a used tube of eyeliner in my pack. You know the black thread rings that mascara tubes develop after you have removed and inserted the wand a hundred times? That's exactly what the eyeliner tube looked like. A 'sealed' package that contained a used product? Not really sure how that happened. Luckily, the kit contains 2 tubes of eyeliner so I went against my better judgement and decided to try the tube that was obviously (maybe I should say hopefully) not used.

Other than receiving a partially used product, these eyelashes are AMAZING. So easy and fun, I highly recommend!!

reviews.title: They are great
reviews.text: They looked good.

reviews.title: Beautiful, easy to use!
reviews.text: Very easy to use! Will buy again!

reviews.title: Horrible!!!! BOTH eyeliners COMPLETELY DRY !!! y dried out to the fullest , like nothing in there!
reviews.text: Both tubes totally dried out to the fullest !!! Do not purchase total waste !!! I was shocked . Nevet ordered something in such bad condition

reviews.title: Feels clean
reviews.text: My hair felt clean after using it. There is no smell and lathered really well. It's safe for colored hair and has no bad ingredients in it.

reviews.title: Fantastic Shampoo!
reviews.text: Just Wonderful! This seriously has NO SCENT! They are not kidding around with the Fragrance Free on this bottle here! It truly smells like nothing! Not a hint of anything! Remarkable! This stuff lathers quickly and feels great!!! My hair and my scalp feel so clean! And, I love combining this with their Fragrance Free Conditioner! Amazing Product! Highly recommend!

reviews.title: nice
reviews.text: it's nice but not that moisturizing but the conditioner that goes with this is awesome

reviews.title: Dislike it more than initially
reviews.text:
I’m revising my review and deducting a star. I’d already mentioned how CRAZY BAD this burns if it gets in your eyes, but it bears repeating. The new info here is that 2 of my household users get an itchy scalp after use. Maybe “diamond powder” as its 9th active ingredient isn’t such a fabulous idea??
* * * * * * * * * * * *
Ingredients: Aqua (Water/Eau), Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate, Cocamidopropyl Betaine, Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate, Cocamide MIPA, PEG-120 Methyl Glucose Dioleate, Glycol Distearate, Diamond Powder, Emblica Officinalis Fruit Extract, Salvia Officinalis (Sage) Leaf Extract, Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seed Extract, Hydrolyzed Quinoa, Brassica Campestris (Rapeseed) Seed Oil, Sesamum Indicum (Sesame) Seed Oil, Silicone Quaternium-16, Polyquaternium-7, Undeceth-11, Butyloctanol, Polyquaternium-10, Undeceth-5, Cinnamidopropyltrimonium Chloride, Phenoxyethanol, Acrylates Copolymer, Disodium EDTA, Glycerin, Citric Acid, Sodium Hydroxide, Methylchloroisothiazolinone, Methylisothiazolinone.

I ended up liking this shampoo more than I thought I would. I took a spin around AIIR’s website, a brand I was previously unfamiliar with. Their lane is definitely upmarket holistic+New Age-y. (And those two words shouldn’t be mutually exclusive IMO). I’m personally not a big fan of hawking diamonds and crystals and other marketing hooks that aren’t scientifically proven to actually benefit hair. It jacks up the price point and borders upon gimmicky. However, finding truly fragrance-free shampoo is not an easy task… and this indeed is. And they are also Leaping Bunny certified, which I verified because I’m persnickety like that. Some small cruelty-free labels are still owned by a large parent company that isn’t Leaping Bunny certified, but AIIR is fully good to go. So high marks there.

The formula lathers a lot more than I expected. Here the Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate replaces the heavier sulfate that’s usually found in the first couple of ingredients; it’s a very mild surfactant, yet lathers quite well and rinses out easily. As a lot of newer generation brands are replacing now-vilified ingredients, I should note that the way we get Sodium Lauroyl Methyl Isethionate technically involves ethylene oxide— which is not without controversy. In 20 years it’ll also be replaced by something even better/safer. But for now, this is a very friendly cleanser option and my color-treated hair appreciates it.

My mom and have radically different hair types; she’s got thin, baby fine hair that needs body and I have thick, coarse, curls that I try hard to tame and de-volumize. Both of us didn’t have remarkable results after using this, but we did note our hair felt pretty soft. While I don’t know if I believe all of AIIR’s claims here about hair growth, I am impressed that the two of us could share a shampoo… as that’s something we can usually never do. This also preforms well in hard water.

I'm leaving it 4 stars because of how much it stung my eyes when some suds fell into them during my shower. And because of the diamond nonsense. You also don’t get a lot vs price point. But for gentle yet effective cleansing in a truly fragrance-free formula, yeah, not bad! Nice choice for a guest bathroom as this shouldn’t trigger many allergies, is gender neutral, and can work on all hair types.

This was a Vine item for me but unless I see some ‘wow’ factor pretty soon, once this is gone I don’t plan to buy any outright.

reviews.title: Love this shampoo
reviews.text: I actually love that this is unscented.  It cleans my hair really well also.  It's not heavy or anything.  Love using it with the conditioner.

reviews.title: Very nice shampoo
reviews.text:
This is a nice shampoo. It is odd not having a fragrance as I don’t believe I’ve ever used a shampoo that is fragrance-free, but there are benefits to this, such as helping prevent itchy scalp. I love the way the shampoo lathers my hair and leaves it feeling soft and clean.
Overall, this is a nice shampoo that provides a good clean, an excellent lather, and leaves my hair feeling and looking great once finished.

reviews.title: Left my hair Soft, Smooth & CLEANED⭐️👍
reviews.text:
Like that this is Fragrance Free. A gentle shampoo that thoroughly cleans my hair. Usually shampoos that throughly clean away build up will strip my hair of the natural oils and this definitely does not. It is for “COLOR TREATED” hair.
I also use this shampoo in combination with [[ASIN:B09TQ29M5H AIIR Diamond Hair Conditioner]]. The combination of both together leaves my hair feeling cleaning, smooth and soft. No frizzing at all.
It is a bit pricey, however, for a professional brand product, is does as described, and they are all priced around the same for 8oz.

THOROUGHLY SEALED: ✅
One thing that is important to me is how manufacturers seal & ship their products. This was THOROUGHLY sealed as you can see from the pictures. 👍👍👍👍👍

DISCLAIMER:
📝Yes, I am a VINE 🗣VOICE member. EVERY product that I review was selected by ME & ONLY ME. The product or item is ALWAYS something that is needed and will be used in my Home🏡 & Yard 🌱🍃, or for my FAMILY 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦& Pets🦮🐾🐈‍⬛, or JUST FOR ME 💁🏻‍♀️.

reviews.title: Fragrance Free - Thank You!
reviews.text: This 8 fl oz bottle of shampoo is just what I’m looking for. Not because it says it helps with hair growth or hair repair. I like this because it’s fragrance-free. I thank the maker for that! The shampoo comes in an easy to hold container. After wetting my hair in warm water, I squeezed a generous amount on my head. The shampoo lathers well and washes off easily, plus it makes my hair squeaky clean. I used a little hair conditioner before rinsing my hair again. I like how my hair feels soft and tangle free after drying off. The price, however would be expensive as an everyday shampoo. I see it more as a luxury shampoo which cleans and rinses well.

reviews.title: IT LEFT MY HAIR FEELING DRY & WEAKER
reviews.text: I have tried this shampoo for about a week now and I wish I could say that I liked it, but I honestly can't. The shampoo feels smooth when I first wash it into my hair, but after I rinse the shampoo out, my hair feels really dry, instead of feeling soft, which I was really surprised after reading all the great reviews. Even though my hair is treated, I have always had thick and smooth hair but lately my hair hasn't been as strong as it used to be and I was hoping this shampoo would help. I do like the fact that it's a vegan formula and that its ingredients include quinoa and sage. But, unfortunately, I have not seen any benefits from using this product, if anything it left my hair feeling dry and weaker. And for what it sells for at $34.00 for a small 8 oz bottle, in my opinion, it's not worth it.

reviews.title: Great if you have allergies
reviews.text: I have an allergy to scents and most shampoo.  This works out great for me and my hair looks so much better.  It has no scent. The bottle is very small and it’s pretty pricey but I gotta do what I gotta do

reviews.title: good shampoo
reviews.text: This is a good shampoo. it is Fragrance free and does not have a scent. it left my hair and scalp feeling nice and clean. it suds up nicely and I feel like it cleanses really good. i think it would be great for anyone who is sensitive to scents.

reviews.title: Diamonds don't play a role, but my favorite shampoo these days...
reviews.text: When I read the word "Diamond" at the front of this shampoo bottle, then of course I became curious to try it, but as far as I can tell, there's nothing that diamonds would do to benefit the hair, but I do see it listed as "Diamond Powder" on the ingredients list. The bottle actually looks kinda cheap, but it could be that this brand does not invest in fancy packaging, which will only increase the price of the product. This shampoo has no scent whatsoever, which I don't mind, less is more when it comes to products we use on our bodies/hair every day or almost every day. The liquid is completely clear and it lathers pretty good. But of all the shampoo's I've tried lately, I can say this is the one (when used with the same conditioner) that leaves my hair the best: soft, shiny, less frizz... it is only 8 fl oz and on the pricey side, but if you are curious like me, and need a change for your hair, give it a shot!

reviews.title: It's ok
reviews.text:
I have used a couple.of times the shampoo, as mentioned in the description it doesn't have smell and it does help your hair to be shiny. Now saying this I suffer from dry scalp and it have helped me with it (the cleansing part of the shampoo)
It's a good product but I don't think is worth 30 dollars a small bottle like this one

reviews.title: Not the best, not the worst
reviews.text: It cleans your hair, but I’m not a huge fan of how it made my hair feel or how it smells. It didn’t make my hair soft like I like my shampoo to do. It had more of a squeaky clean feel so if you like that, then this is for you. :)

reviews.title: Nice Product, No smell
reviews.text:
This review is for AIIR Diamond Shampoo - Fragrance Free Shampoo - Shampoo for Color Treated Hair.

This shampoo cleanses superbly and softens nicely. I wish it wasn't fragrance free though. I personally like a little fragrance with my shampoo experience. I also love the way my hair smells afterwards. It's nice and it does clean well but I wish they'd consider making a similar product that's scented with jasmine or lavender.

reviews.title: Not sealed
reviews.text:
My bottle was received not sealed. It has a seal, but it was stuck to the top of the bottle, not the bottle itself. So the bottle of shampoo was not sealed, and if I want to use the squeeze top, I will have to somehow remove the seal that is stuck inside the top.

I was interested in trying this product because of the crystal infusion. I was curious to see how that effects the shampoo, but honestly, I'm skeptical. I carry crystals with me and do believe in the energy they have, but I don't know how that translates to a shampoo.

reviews.title: Super Easy To Apply, Does A Great Job Smoothing My Fine Flyaway Hairs & Haven’t Had Any Issues
reviews.text:
I have fine hair that I blow dry to add style and smooth frizz. I can usually get the top pretty smooth but have some baby hairs and bad hair days where little hairs stick out and adding more of my regular lightweight gel or other products can weigh my hair down too much, make it look wet or dirty or make it clumpy or stiff. I don’t know why but I didn’t have high hopes that this Wing Fly Grl Mane Taming Wand gel would be anything special, but it really works great for my fine hair.

It’s about the size of a mascara and has a wand with a small foam type applicator (similar to one on a lip gloss) at the end. I just gently run it over the hairs sticking up on top and they just lay down and blend right in with the rest of my hair. I can’t say how this works for thick hair, but it sure is fast and easy to use and works great for me. My hair is very dark brown and I can’t see any sign of the gel, just the benefit of smooth hair.

I haven’t experienced any flaking, dryness, stiffness, wet look, burning or discomfort or issues with my hair color. This wand is convenient as it’s so compact and easy to carry in my purse on-the-go. The ingredient list is short and no ingredients stand out as worrisome health risks or as being possibly damaging to my hair. It works like a charm smoothing my little flyaways and so far has no negative consequences. I’ll update if I see any issues in the longer term, but it’s all good so far. Love it. 5 stars.

Ingredients: Purified Water, Glycerin 99.% Usp Kosher, Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6, Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, Ethylhexlglycerin, Provitamin B5 (D-panthenol) - Powder.

reviews.title: Loving My Hair!!
reviews.text: This succulent gel is my ‘saving grace’ when my locks of hair need that finishing touch of perfection! It is fantastic at pampering my hair with a subduing presence that sleeks down my unruly strains, trying to undo my groomed beauty. Just a thin layer applied to the top of my hair, graces my curls with a silky, nurturing caress - this gel works wonders!

reviews.title: Works surprisingly well!!
reviews.text: I was optimistically skeptical about this product, but it works just as described. It does tame (kinda locks down) those fly away pieces. Much more targeted, quicker, and requires less product application than hairspray. Doesn't leave hair hard to touch or greasy.  I did have to dip the applicator into the bottle before each little section of hair and am guessing I'll go through a bottle fairly quickly. Glad I gave it a try. Will use it on important hair days for sure.

reviews.title: Does its job well
reviews.text: A fantastic cruelty free item to have in your purse. Takes away any flyaways that are driving you crazy! During in the summer I tend to wear my hair pulled back in a ponytail, and always find that I have those stray flyaway hairs. This easily and quickly takes care of them. Just pull out the wand, tame them down using the tip of the wand, and go about your business. Holds for the better part of the day. You will need to reapply every now and then, but it doesn’t leave your hair feeling oily or weighed down. Definitely some thing I’ll continue to use.

reviews.title: Good hold
reviews.text: I have tried hair flyaway gel before that comes with a mascara wand but this one comes with a wand that looks like it would go to a lipgloss. I feel like the wand itself makes it messier to apply because you are just rubbing it on your hair instead of brushing it to catch all the flyaways. The gel itself works well but you just have to apply a little more pressure with the wand to get it to go where you want to go.

reviews.title: Small Wand
reviews.text: Maybe my flyaways are just bigger than the average person's, but I thought the wand was a little small. It's essentially the size of a lip gloss or mascara. I feel like the wand tip would work better if it had something like a mascara wand tip so it helps comb the wispies back. The stuff itself works well without leaving your hair looking or feeling oily. The application was a bit tedious and time consuming for me personally.

reviews.title: Works well
reviews.text:
I was surprised to see that the applicator is a lambs ear type brush versus one with bristles, but it does the job! It smoothed my fly always down and once dry (it goes on wet), everything stayed in place despite some light winds. It won’t work in heavy wind, but it provides a light hold that isn’t crunchy.

It won’t hold big pieces, and it doesn’t last all day. But having said that, it lasts a long time in normal circumstances. Keep it in your purse for touch ups.

reviews.title: It is okay
reviews.text: It doesn't keep my baby hairs down for too long. I wish it had a stronger hold

reviews.title: Didn’t work too well
reviews.text: This didn’t do much. I still had to use a little bit of hairspray to tame my flyaways. It did work but not the whole way.

reviews.title: Why is this so expensive?
reviews.text:
Ok so this entire tube fits in the palm of my hand. It’s like the size of a lip gloss or mascara. So, with that being said why is it so expensive???! I have a hard time getting past that fact. I thought oh boy this better be good for the price.

So this is pretty much identical in both applicator and size to a lip gloss. I like the idea of the applicator being mess free something you can carry in your pocket and do a quick touch up on the go without putting product on your hands. The gel itself isn’t sticky and doesn’t dry with any “crunch” to it. Which is both good and bad. It’s not overly heavy for quick touch ups. But for someone like me with really curly frizzy hair this provides no lasting power. You can hardly tell I used it a short while after application.

I’d say all in all you can buy a whole bottle of lightweight hair gel (basically all this is) for about 1/10 of the price. I wouldn’t purchase this in the future.

reviews.title: Decent Product
reviews.text: My daughter has fine hair and always has flyaways. This is helpful but I wish it had a comb like applicator rather than one that looks like lipgloss. She has sensitive skin and this didn't cause her skin to react to it when applied.

reviews.title: Why this tip!
reviews.text: I use this kind of stuff all the time for my girls. My 9 yr old is obsessed with the brush kind. This wand is ridiculous! I absolutely hate how it goes on and it doesn't work anywhere near as well as the brush. Who decided this eye brush would be a good idea?! Stick with the mascara brush! It works perfectly, this doesn't!

reviews.title: It Works!
reviews.text:
My hair is exceptionally fine and fly-away. So, when I saw this, Fly Grl Mane-Taming Gel Wand by Wing, I had to try it.

When I saw its size, I really thought it would not be enough to tame all my hair. It is merely the size of a mascara. But it did calm those fly-away hairs! It is clear, so it is not visible when it is applied. All I had to do was apply the product with the wand to the fly-aways, and they were instantly under control. Fly Grl also adds shine.

The ingredients are also supposed to condition and strengthen my hair, which is also very welcome. It contains glycerin and panthenol.

This handy product is made in the United States.

reviews.title: Worked well
reviews.text: The applicator is the type that you would normally see in a tube of concealer when similar products have a mascara type of spoolie. If you were trying to tame the hair in the picture shown above it would take forever but taming one or two loose hairs when you have it in a ponytail is super quick and easy. The hold lasts pretty much the entire time I have my hair up. As far as value, I think you'd be able to find less expensive options or even have the same results spraying some hairspray on your hand and smoothing it over your hair.

reviews.title: Pro: Doesn't Smell Con: Doesn't Work
reviews.text: I have another hair taming gel from some other brand that works REALLY REALLY GOOD but has a horrible smell that I simply can't stand. But this one has ZERO smell and doesn't work AT ALL.

reviews.title: Works on some hair
reviews.text: I have some baby hairs, some frizzy hairs and some wiry colored white hair that pokes out of my pony tail and blows up in the wind. This is definitely not for the frizzy or thick hairs. It did basically nothing on those. I used it on my very fine baby hairs and around my face and it smoothed the hairs down well and looked a lot more polished. I could have probably achieved the same result with hairspray. I assume this is for a quick fix and to carry in your purse. It only works on fine hair and not a great fit for me.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text:
I have fine hair with some thicker, unruly hairs and frizzies that refuse to cooperate on top. This gel definitely did the trick for most of the pieces I put it on but the thicker ones bounce right back up. If you only need to touch up a few hairs here and there, this is a great product for you. If you have quite a few, I would say maybe skip this product as the wand is so small that it would take way too long and isn't realistic to use on a daily basis.
I am confused about the price point; the portability and quality are fantastic but would be more willing to purchase again if the price was lower.

reviews.title: Works Okay. Very Expensive.
reviews.text:
My daughter has frizzy hair, especially in the summer, and she was really hoping this would help. Well, it did but she'd probably use the whole bottle in a few days.

This bottle is about the size of mascara. It's on the small side. The applicator was nice, it worked well and was easy to use. My daughter has frizzy hair and was really looking forward to this. It does work, but it's such a small bottle!

Overall, it works well but there's cheaper and bigger sized alternatives out there. This is so expensive for what it is, currently $19.99 for a little 8 ml bottle. I just couldn't justify getting this again.

This would be okay for minor touch-ups but if you have frizzy hair, try something else that's cheaper, and bigger.

reviews.title: Fly away no more
reviews.text: I was really surprised on how well this worked and a little bit does. Go a long way

reviews.title: No more fly-Aways!
reviews.text:
If you’re like me, and have ultra fine hair with a ton of fly-away strands around the frame of your face, then this is an absolute must have for you!
I am sooo glad a product like this exists as it’s been a true lifesaver for me!
Very easy to use, just lightly brush the wand over the unwanted fly away strands and lay them back against your hair,and they stay like magic!
I always spray a small amount of hairspray after using the wand, just to help lock it in, and have had no issues with my hair not staying in place.
This stuff is truly awesome and a must have for anyone with unruly fly-away hair!

reviews.title: Did not work  for me.
reviews.text: I was excited to find these and with the supporting research really hoped these would work. They did not. I do not consider myself to have high arches, but once I placed these in my shoes and adjusted several times, they just did not work. My arches barely made contact with the insole, let alone shift weight from the ball of my foot to my heel.

reviews.title: Doesnt make a difference
reviews.text: Read great reviews about this, but unfortunately it didnt really help me at all.  Regular gel insoles felt better than these inserts.

reviews.title: excellent product
reviews.text: I have placed these in every pair of shoes with a heel of 2 inches or more. There is no pressure on the ball of the foot. I'm very happy with the product. Fast delivery too.

reviews.title: Makes super high heels bearable
reviews.text:
Makes high heels bearable, not comfortable. They are not miracle workers but would recommend for improving comfort.

They were received timely and with no problems.

reviews.title: These are amazing
reviews.text:
I love my heels but you know how it goes--you wear them for a few minutes and then your feet hurt.

These have made a big difference in my ability to wear shoes that I couldn't even wear years ago! I'm so glad these exist.

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text:
I have really high arches so wearing flats a lot puts most of the stress on my instep as there is nothing to support underneath (like in an arched bridge) so wearing heels is actually more comfortable BUT eventually all the weight on the ball of my foot ends up hurting, too.

I have tried all kinds of inserts. The ones I had liked the most were [[ASIN:B0013DAI3C Dr Scholl's For Her Sole Expressions Insole, Women's 6-10, 3-Pair Package]]. They were economical and cute looking. I preferred the fabric over the gel because the gel ended up causing my feet to get sweaty and they felt slippery. I wasn't completely satisfied with them but they had been the ones I liked the most. I felt I needed more cushion, they helped but at the end of the night my feet still hurt and sometimes they made the shoe fit a little tight so I had to account for that when shoe shopping.

I continued my search and found these. I read mixed reviews but I decided to try them because the concept was different. Instead of trying ease the pain by providing more cushion to help absorb the weight, they were supposed to redistribute it. After all the cushioned inserts I've tried, I've found that this alone is not the solution.

They are more expensive, for the price I could get almost twice as many dr. scholls but I have to say that they have been worth it. I wear a 6.5-7 and I got that size. The shape resembles the outer shape of a small spoon with a fat handle and made of a flexible polyurethane plastic. I would have preferred that they be covered in a fabric because they make for less slip and sweating but at least its not on the ball of the foot where there is more shifting and could potentially cause blisters.

The thing is that they are tricky to place, they have to be placed just so in order for them to be effective so read the directions! They are placed with the bowl part of the "spoon" at the heel. About half an inch down it starts to get a little thicker and continues all the way down to the handle, which is what they refer to as "the bump".

You start by removing a small part of the plastic liner and placing the bowl about 3mm (1/8in) away from the end of your shoe at the heel part and keep shifting them up or down until they sit just right. Then you remove the liner and permanently adhere.

If you pull your leg up and take a sideways look at it, you have the ball of your foot that then arches up, at the other end you have your heel that also begins to arch up and they meet in the middle. The idea is to get that bump right underneath where your HEEL starts to arch up (the calcaneus). Thats where you want it. I can't insert any links but google Insolia, fitting tips from the product engineer and you can read more about it there, they do a much better job of explaining, with pictures and everything.

I tried them on a 4inch pair of pumps that had very little cushion and they worked great. They didn't feel like the most comfortable shoes in the world and they mention that it doesn't aim to add comfort. Instead of starting out with a comfort level of 8 and, after all that strain of carrying all your weight on the balls of your feet, 4 hours later you end up with a comfort level of 2, you start out with a comfort level of lets say 5 and end up with a comfort level of 4-5 at the end of the night. It works, I came home after 4 hours, a lot of walking and standing, and my feet hurt a little but they were not throbbing with the pain I usually have even with really cushioned heels.

Since they don't cover the entire length of the shoe, I am going to try adding some cushions to the ball of my foot and see if that works better. I am also going to experiment with adding the Dr. Scholls sole expressions over them to see if that helps any. That might add to much height and make my shoe slip off but I won't know for sure until I try. But I am definitely buying more of these for the rest of my ridiculously high shoes.

My only real gripe was getting that plastic liner off, I don't know if they are all like that or if I got a defective pair. OMG, it took me like 25min to remove separate those liners. I broke two nails and almost threw them away in frustration. You would think that company who thought of this brilliant idea would add a little tab or something to help with that.

reviews.title: Okay but is there something better
reviews.text: In some of my shoes it was a great fit, but in others not so much.  The security is an issue at times.

reviews.title: but to my delight these actually work
reviews.text: where have these been my whole life?!? I rarely have life luck with insoles.. apparently my feet are high maintenance, but to my delight these actually work! they reduce the weight on the balls of my feet and significantly reduce toe squishing/rubbing

reviews.title: Yes, they work and I bought more packages!
reviews.text:
The inserts do somehow redistribute weight to make high heels 2-4" and up more comfortable and you can tell right away when you put your foot in shoes with the inserts. If you have some major foot problems these won't help that much, for the average high heel wearer....order some! Your feet will thank you.

C.

reviews.title: Love this Insolia high heel inserts
reviews.text:
This insolia high heel inserts made my high heel comfortable to wear.
I can walk longer now wearing my high heel. I want to buy some more of this.

reviews.title: Did not work for me
reviews.text: Just didn't feel that these did anything for the pain in my heel or arch.  Best I can offer is that the price is right so they might work for you.  I went with another 3/4 orthotic with higher arch support from a comfort footwear store and I feel much better but those were much more expensive so it might be worth to try something less expensive first.

reviews.title: Finally, foot relief !!
reviews.text: They really do work. I wore these for about 6 hours in a pair of heels that I normally can't wear for more than an hour. It shifts your weight right to your heels. So ladies, if your issue is the balls of your feet. Try these !!

reviews.title: Fantastic!
reviews.text:
I have to admit that I was a bit hesitant about what this insert claimed it could do. After getting over my initial " don't buy snake oil" resistance, I bought a three pack. A few months later, I'm about to buy another 3 pack.

A bit about my shoes; I have a love/hate relationship with my 5+ inch heels. They (used to) cause me quite a bit of pain. I am not a professional high-heel shoe wearer. I'm more of a casual, night-on-the-town sort of wearer.

I really love Insolia. They are fantastic at preventing your foot from sliding forward in heels. Insolia is much different than your drug-store, foam insert. (I can't even begin to tell how many times I've been disappointed with those silly foam things.) Instead of making walking feel squishier, they prevent your foot from slipping forward, thus preventing quite a bit of pain from forming on the balls of your feet. I can definitely tell that my weight is shifted, which is fantastic for walking and standing in your favorite pair of stilettos.

reviews.title: Wow! What a difference!!!
reviews.text: These inserts made a huge difference for me!  My beautiful but previously torturous boots with 4 1/2" heels are now comfortable for all day wear!  Before I bought these inserts, I maybe made it 3 hours in those boots before my feet were killing me (even with nice padded insoles added).  I wore them out last weekend (and put a spare pair of shoes in my car, just in case I needed to change after a couple hours), and found that I was able to wear my boots without pain for 8 hours!  I noticed I was able to stand up straighter, and that I was able to walk with my heels landing before my toes.  I definitely noticed the difference in weight distribution-  I had a lot more weight on my heels than usual.  These inserts did take a bit of fiddling to get them positioned correctly for me.  They ended up working best for me shifted closest to the inside (arch side) starting about 6 mms from the back of the heel, and pointed toward the big toe.  I did put a thin padded insole over the Insolias after I got them positioned.  Warning- Do not peel the whole backing off until you get it positioned correctly!  Bend the backing a little bit back at the heel to make it stay in place temporarily, or even use some double stick tape for the position testing.  The adhesive is super strong on these inserts and is meant to stay put once you place them. On my first attempt at using these I actually tore a bit of the lining on the bottom of my shoe trying to peel the insert off to move it a little!

reviews.title: must have for high heel lovers!
reviews.text: I put these on my shoes and they really make a difference. I was able to wear my 5 inch heels for 8 hours straight. Although my feet hurt when I took off my heels. It does make heels barely, I wouldn't say you are without pain but it does make it easier to wear heels for long periods of time.

reviews.title: She does not recommend this.
reviews.text: Wife wears heels every day at work, constantly on her feet, and these did not help her at all. Her feet still hurt. She does not recommend this.

reviews.title: Not much support
reviews.text: I bought these after reading all of hte hype but must say they didn't work for me.  I didn't notice any difference after putting these in my heels.  I really wanted them to work.

reviews.title: very light, absorbs well, works well alone or over other skin care products
reviews.text:
My skin is dry, totally clear (no acne) save for some redness & visible veins from Type 1 rosacea. This sunscreen feels quite gentle and does not seem to irritate my skin at all. It adds a light but effective level of hydration without feeling heavy. It is a chemical sunscreen, so zero white cast. It is thin, and goes on like a light lotion. Typical viscosity among my other K-beauty brands.

I have a wide assortment of sunscreen, of various formulations and types. It is my #1 skin care product as I use it daily all year long. It is a habit. I avoid the sun for a few reasons, so sunscreen is vital to my well being. I live in a variable climate (from dry, freezing temps to humid hot) and I have sunscreen preference depending on season and situation. I tend to prefer mineral (my fair skin can handle any white cast) but do utilize chemical sunscreen often.

I am going to like this for basic daily summer use. I tend to reduce my morning skin care routine down to a basic wash or splash of water and a moisturizing sunscreen. Since this offers SPF 50 (P++++), I know my skin is going to be pretty well protected. Since it feels like a moisturizer and goes on clear, I can skip my other skin care if I want. I tend to skip morning skin care in the summer because I am likely to just sweat it off anyway (I have a more involved night time routine to make up for it). So a sunscreen such as this is easy to use, makes my skin feel good, and easy to reapply throughout the day. I dont wear make up usually, but for sake of review I used it under a light foundation tint. Seems to play well- I didnt notice any rolling/pilling. I'll use it up. I have also used it over other skin care (hydrating toner, serum, light moisturizer) and it worked well on top of all that too.

Not sure yet if it will be repurchase. I do like Fascy Lab products, so it's a contender. Worth trying anyway
4.5 stars

Active Ingredients:
DIETHYLAMINO HYDROXYBENZOYL HEXYL BENZOATE 2.0%
BIS-ETHYLHEXYLOXYPHENOL METHOXYPHENYL TRIAZINE 1.0%

Inactive Ingredients:
WATER, BUTYLENE GLYCOL, C12-15 ALKYL BENZOATE, METHYL METHACRYLATE CROSSPOLYMER, 1,2-HEXANEDIOL, NIACINAMIDE, BEHENYL ALCOHOL, POTASSIUM CETYL PHOSPHATE, SODIUM ACRYLATE/SODIUM ACRYLOYLDIMETHYL TAURATE COPOLYMER, POLYISOBUTENE, AMMONIUM ACRYLOYLDIMETHYLTAURATE/VP COPOLYMER, SILICA, ADENOSINE, CAPRYLYL/CAPRYL GLUCOSIDE, DISODIUM EDTA, SORBITAN OLEATE, SODIUM HYALURONATE

reviews.title: Great Sunscreen!!!
reviews.text:
Pros:
Did not irritate my sensitive skin
Feels like it moisture well

reviews.title: Gentle and effective 50 SPF
reviews.text: I was late to use sunblock regularly.  The older versions were too whitish and thick, uncomfortable on the skin.  This South Korean version with 50 SPF is quickly absorbed into the skin and does not leave any whitish color or streaks.  It does not feel oily or sticky but instead more like a light lotion.  It does not use parabens - the product list shows the harsh ingredients that the sunscreen does not contain.  Aside from SPF, the sunblock also has moisturizing ingredients.  It is easy to use this everyday without causing a breakout or irritation of the skin.
