This is a nice, lightweight moisturizer that absorbs quickly and doesn't cause skin to look/feel oily or greasy. It softens and smooths skin. Fine lines are thinned. My dry, sensitive skin looks and feels refreshed. And thanks to Vitamin C, my normally dull skin has a dewy glow. I've not found many products that are able to do this to my skin but XXI does!

I have dry , sensitive skin and have no issues with this cream

XXI works. It works VERY well and some results like soft, smooth skin occur immediately after use. Results are long lasting. XXI recommends using this in the morning and at night. I use it once a day in the morning. A very small amount is sufficient to cover my face. Because it works so well and delivers great results and only a small amount is needed per use, I do believe the price is reasonable. However, if you find this isn't right for you, XXI offers a 30 day satisfaction guarantee. I absolutely recommend XXI Bio-Pure lotion.

reviews.title: This Line Continues To Impress Me (I Have Tried Many High End Products)
reviews.text:
I have tried many high end skin products over time and I also make my own skin care which I use a lot as well. Once in a while I like to check out another line to trade off for a while. This line, Wow. I am impressed so far.

HOW THIS COMES
This comes in a box. The bottle inside is glass with a lock pump top. Turn to the side and you can pump and then turn it back and it locks.

TEXTURE OF THE LOTION
This is what I would think of as a lotion texture. Thick, but not too thick.

MY SKIN TYPE
I tend to have normal to dry skin and it has some uneven patches on it which most probably don't notice, but I do.

HOW I USE THIS
I start with the XXI cleanser which is great stuff. Then I apply a Hyaluronic Acid serum on my skin. After that, I use this Vitamin C lotion and I finish with the XXI firming lotion on top of that. I only do this 1 time at night.

RESULTS
The results of this product line have been really impressive. My face has looked younger, more glowy, and my skin looks more awake and less tired. I have been through a lot of deaths this year and my face has looked very tired. I could not believe how much this and the other two XXI products affected how I look. It was quite exciting for me right now.

THE BOTTOM LINE
My skin glows after using this. It is soft, moisturized and when combined with the other XXI cleanser and firming lotion, the results are impressive.

MY RATING
FIVE STARS. I will continue to buy products from this line (well the ones I mentioned, anyway). I really have been thankful to have them right now. They have given me a boost when I really needed one.

reviews.title: Dry
reviews.text: Left my dads feeling very dry

reviews.title: My Mature Skin Loves This One
reviews.text:
I have super dry, sensitive, mature skin and apparently it loves this lotion.

I use only 1 to 1 1/2 pumps for my entire face and neck. This lotion absorbs into my skin almost instantly.

I love that my skin is hydrated all day, even under makeup. My skin feels so soft and so smooth.

I also love how gentle this product is to my skin. I've had no redness and no irritation whatsoever.

I've used this lotion in conjunction with the XXI Daily Cleanser and also their Intensi-Fi Firming cream and my skin looks and feels fantastic.

10+ stars for this cream. I absolutely love it.

I hope this helps you.

reviews.title: The perfect daily go-to moisturizer!
reviews.text: This moisturizer is my daily go-to regardless of whether it's a dress down weekend day or heading to work in the week. My sensitive, dry skin has been loving this moisturizer for months now and I am very happy with the results. I cleanse with the XXI daily cleanser and then follow up with the Vitamin C moisturizer as part of my routine. I will use the XXI firming cream around my eyes and neck for extra hydration and to minimize fine lines and absolutely love my glowing skin! Looking forward to trying more XXI products!

reviews.title: "Skin is Glowing"
reviews.text: I adore this moisturizer - I use it with the Bio-Pure serum. It goes on really nicely, smells great, iand somehow is very light weight while being really moisturizing. I have started getting compliments from my friends that my skin is "glowing". I don't wear make up, the only change I've made is using XXI products (Scrub & the vitamin C serum & moisturizer). Highly recommend!!

reviews.title: LOVE IT!!
reviews.text:
If you are looking for clean products with natural ingredients that work, try this line! The vitamin C lotion Is lightweight and makes my skin feel
Smooth and bright. Don’t hesitate!!

reviews.title: Best face lotion on the market!
reviews.text:
I have so many positive things to say about this incredible lotion, but let me start with the most important- yes it’s that good and you need it in your life immediately!

I’ve been looking for the perfect face lotion since I realized my skin isn’t getting any younger and it’s time to start taking care of it. This. Is. It.

Let’s start with the weight- I don’t know how they do it but it’s the perfect amount of I know that it’s moisturizing my skin but still light weight and comfortable. No more feeling like you are wearing a mask. No more scratching your cheek and finding gunk under your nails. Just beautiful skin.

The next most important thing I consider when applying a product to my face is smell. I can’t handle any artificial scents or perfumes anywhere on my body, but especially my face (it’s so close to my nose!). This has a lovely aroma that completely disappears when I rub it in! No more wanting to wash the stinky lotion off my face, this is perfect!

Why is Vitamin C important in lotion? Well let me tell you a story. Before I bought this I went on vacation with my family. I used sunblock liberally, but after days on the beach I ended up with some color on my face- most of which was concentrated on my top lip! Wow, all these fun things that come with getting older, right? Well when my 5 year old asked me why it looked like I had a mustache I started looking into ways to fight hyperpigmentation. Vitamin C was the answer, and after using this lotion for only a few days the dark spots went away and my son stopped checking my lip to see if I needed to shave.

Next let’s talk about the bottle- first of all just the smallest amount comes out when you pump it so you don’t end up with a handful of lotion that you are trying to reverse pump back into the bottle. Just want a little lotion? One pump. Need more? Two pumps. You are in control! No waste means more value for your money. Also the bottle is made of glass and doesn’t have a cap (I always lose those things!) which makes it super sustainable. The pump locks so no mess when you need to take it with you. It’s basically the perfect bottle.

In closing, everything about this lotion is perfect, and if you have gotten this far congratulations to you! Now stop reading and add to cart, you will not regret it!

reviews.title: I love how it makes my skin look, but not how it feels
reviews.text: This is a thick creamy lotion that moisturizes very well and had my face looking fantastic with all my dry fine lines gone. However, after waiting an hour for it to fully absorb it still felt heavy and kind of greasy on my skin. I'm super picky and need moisture but hate the feeling of stuff on my skin, I prefer lightweight formulas that fully absorb and feel like there is no residue left behind. This one is a pass for me especially at the current list price of $48.

reviews.title: Just what I need
reviews.text: I use all of the XXI skincare items. I like them all, but this one is my favorite! It’s lightweight and makes my skin feel hydrated all day. It is smooth to apply and my skin feels soft after it absorbs, not greasy like other moisturizers I’ve used in the past.

reviews.title: Love this lotion!
reviews.text: I’ve been loving for a Vitamin C lotion and boy did I find one! I already see a different in my skin! I’m so happy I found this product! It’s a must try!

reviews.title: Delightfully light for a moisturizer that lasts all day!
reviews.text: I absolutely love XXI! It lasts all day without making my sensitive, oily skin breakout. I’ve been using it for the last 7 months and I am only on my second bottle! A little goes a very long way. I highly recommend it for anyone looking to brighten their complexion with a natural product without perfumes or chemicals!

reviews.title: Pure Hydration!!!!
reviews.text: I have super sensitive skin and its extremely difficult to find product that I truly enjoy and that go well with my skin type! This lotion is absolute PERFECTION! It is fragrance free, goes on super smooth and is super hydrating!! I Highly recommend this product and all of XXI to anyone and everyone!!

reviews.title: Love this lotion
reviews.text: I love this product line. I use them all. I have very sensitive skin that is easily irritated. I break out or get red splotches on my face. Never with this! It’s so light and my skin feels so much smoother.

reviews.title: Great moisture boost and blemish correction
reviews.text:
First - seller was so great about a package getting stolen. There was verified proof that it was delivered, but left at my front door in an urban area. Seller worked with me and replaced everything which was so nice.
As for the product, I have been loving it. Super sensitive skin and no irritation or reaction which is amazing. I have to be so careful with new products and really happy this worked out.
This has really been keeping my skin moisturized and I have noticed a really nice and bright complexion after just a short time! Much better than other vitamin c products I have used.

reviews.title: Softens, Smooths and Soothes Skin
reviews.text: I ordered LA LA Leaf Guayaquil hemp lotion for my husband . While it is indeed a hands and body lotion, it targets especially dry skin areas-elbows, feet and heels. He has unbelievably dry , rough elbows and does so year round. He's always rubbing creams and lotions into his elbows with very little positive results. Until now. LA LA Leaf is softening his terribly dry, cracked elbows and doing so each time  he applies it. He has pretty much stopped complaining about his dry elbows, instead expressing  (nearly EVERY. SINGLE. DAY!) his excitement now that he has finally found a remedy.  lol.  While I ordered LA LA Leaf for him, I've now started to use it on my hands as well. Its lightweight, absorbs quickly and doesn't cause my skin to look or feel oily or greasy. Let me tell you, the hemp oil, rose flower water white bark extract , aloe (a fave of mine) ingredients do a heck of a job softening and smoothing my hands after I apply LA LA Leaf. I'm already planning on using extra LA LA Leaf in the winter when my hands get the dreaded deep, extremely painful cracks due to cold weather. I noticed in the info provided above, this lotion can be used after spending time in the sun. Because my skin is so terribly sensitive and dry, I do believe I will use LA LA Leaf after spending time outside in the sun when the weather gets warmer. Regardless of where you use LA LA Leaf on your body, its going to leave you with soft, smooth skin and will do so quickly.  I do believe LA LA Leaf is worth the cost. It doesn't take much lotion to cover the skin (in the areas we are using it on) so this tube is going to last us a decent amount of time. I have every intention of continuing to use/buy LA LA Leaf. It's wonderful

reviews.title: light, liquidy, quick absorbing, no added fragrance (but contain E.O.), gentle, great post shower
reviews.text:
Quick absorbing, non greasy watery hand & body lotion. I like my body lotions to be on the thin side as I find it easier/quicker to apply as well as quicker to absorb. When I have extra rough spots, then I don't mind using a thicker emollient. But for just out of the shower lotion to lock in moisture.. I want a thinner lotion. This provides that. Yet, it is moisturizing enough to use any other time of day. Leave at kitchen sink for hand moisturizing as needed is great too. But not at bathroom sink lest someone mistakes it for toothpaste, haha. It is in a toothpaste style container. Just flip cap and squeeze.

It feels nice on skin, has no added fragrance. I like their 1513 face lotion and I like this hand/body lotion too. I tend to have dry skin, so I find both are better for me for summer or days I just a light hydration boost. Easy to use, no frills lotion that is gentle on skin. I appreciate the nourishing ingredients and that they offer a no fragrance added option. Any hint of scent is entirely naturally occurring. However, this may not be for you if you seek to avoid essential oils as is is listed at the end.

Overall it is a nice product. No irritation. No issues thus far.

Key Ingredients:
--Rose Flower Water: Helps maintain the skin's pH balance
--Organic White Willow Bark Extract: A natural exfoliant that helps reveal brighter, radiant skin; Acts as an astringent and anti-inflammatory
--Aloe Leaf Juice: Hydrates and helps to lock in healing ingredients • Hemp Seed Oil: Treats inflammation and redness

Ingredients:
Organic Aloe Leaf Juice, Organic Coconut Oil, Glycerin, Stearic Acid, Emulsifying Wax, Witch Hazel Water, Vitamin E, Sunflower Seed Oil, Phenoxyethanol, Organic Hemp Seed Oil, Rose Flower Water, CBD, Organic White Willow Bark Extract, Organic Rosemary Leaf Extract, Organic Neem Seed Oil, Organic Alcohol, Xanthan Gum, Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate, Essential Oil

reviews.title: Nice and great size
reviews.text: Having health issues that causes very dry skin, I appreciate a lotion with better ingredients and moisturizing. This one isn't as thick as some but it's doing the job hydrating. It's a great size too!

reviews.title: Healthy ingredients
reviews.text:
Coming in from the garden, my hands are always really dried out from working in the soil. This is the best lotion that I have found to quickly deal with that issue. The lotion is silky and smooth. It absorbs quickly and is effective at rehydrating my skin.

The ingredients in this lotion are not harmful to my skin or my body and that is a huge plus.

While this lotion has a hefty price, the tube is very large compared to many other brands.

reviews.title: Gentle On Sensitive Skin, Good Hydration But Not 24 Hours, Natural/Organic Emphasis (4.5 Stars)
reviews.text:
La La Leaf Guayaquil Hemp Lotion feels cooling and calming to my dry, sensitive skin. It feels light, absorbs quickly and moisturizes my skin fairly well. This lotion leaves my hands and skin soft, silky and comfortable. I don’t feel my dry skin gets as much hydration as I get with my favorite premium body butters and creams, but those are heavier products so I don’t expect as much from a lotion.

When applied after my shower, I get about 8 hours of hydration. That’s pretty good, though not the 24 hours claimed on the site. I do have dry skin and find this lotion hydrates better than most lotions. I’ve occasionally found one that gives me longer lasting hydration, but it’s uncommon. Excipial keeps my dry skin comfortable and itch free more like 12 hours but has chemicals such as parabens, while Guayaquil Hemp Lotion emphasizes organic and natural ingredients (but does have Phenoxyethanol).

Guayaquil Hemp Lotion feels gentle and doesn’t irritate my sensitive skin at all. It doesn’t list chemical fragrance and is labeled fragrance free, which is a big plus as some fragrances trigger sensitivities for me. But it still may have fragrance. I smell a faint non offensive scent and don’t know if it’s from added “essential oil” (last ingredient) or just the natural scent of the natural ingredients.

Guayaquil Hemp Lotion has 250 mg of CBD (THC free), which is still being studied but “may” hydrate dry skin and help inflammation. The ingredients look good but this lotion does have moderately risky (per EWG) Phenoxyethanol in the top ten ingredients and drying alcohol in the bottom 5. I like this lotion a lot as it feels pleasing to my sensitive skin, hydrates my dry skin pretty well, is 84% organic and barely has a scent. 4.5 stars. Made in USA. Vegan.

Ingredients: Organic Aloe Leaf Juice, Organic Coconut Oil, Glycerin, Stearic Acid, Emulsifying Wax, Witch Hazel Water, Vitamin E, Sunflower Seed Oil, Phenoxyethanol, Organic Hemp Seed Oil, Rose Flower Water, CBD, Organic White Willow Bark Extract, Organic Rosemary Leaf Extract, Organic Neem Seed Oil, Organic Alcohol, Xanthan Gum, Tetrasodium Glutamate Diacetate, Essential Oil

reviews.title: Amazing lotion
reviews.text: Amazing lotion, probably one of the best I have used.  My skin stays glowing after forever. There is no scent which is nice. Pretty packaging.

reviews.title: Love the ingredients.
reviews.text: This lotion has definitely the best ingredients and all are very clean(100% natural,vegan,no parabens and hemp extract)I’am very satisfied with that and I like this body lotion.I like the consistency it is not greasy is very thick and moisturize my skin very well,I live In Florida and for me is very important to have an easy body lotion which wouldn’t make me feel greasy.The only thing I can’t get over it is the smell,I know it is because all the products are natural and it doesn’t have any fragrances but it is so strong for me.I can recommend this product it is a great moisturizer!

reviews.title: Love this lotion
reviews.text:
LA LA LEAF- Guayaquil Hemp Lotion has really been helping my dry skin. It is not a thick lotion. I use it when I get out of the shower. It does not leave a greasy flim once it dries. It dries and absorbs quickly. It is very moisturizing.

It is made out of all natural ingredients including rose flower water, white willow bark extract, and Aloe leaf juice. It has no parabens or phthalates.

My only complaint is even though it is supposed to be unscented, the smell is quite strong. It doesn't linger long though once it dries.

I definitely recommend this lotion.

reviews.title: On the thinner side, but okay overall
reviews.text: I was really hoping for a thicker lotion or cream, but it is kind of thin.  It is unscented.  The packaging is very nice and the amount you receive is generous.  This lotion is vegan and all natural, which is always a plus.  It does take a couple of minutes for this lotion to absorb, but it does moisturize well.  I am not sure that I would want a second tube, but for someone who is looking for an unscented lotion, this one isn't bad.

reviews.title: A Little Thin, But Still Lovely!
reviews.text:
Hemp lotion is becoming a favorite of mine-there are so many out there to choose from. I tried one from Ulta that I loved and since then I've been trying other brands and this was one of them.

I feel that this really moisturizes my skin, deep down. Putting it on fresh out of the shower seems to keep my skin in lovely condition all day long. There is no scent to it, which kind of bums me out. I was expecting a lotion to be a little bit thicker, but since it's still very moisturizing, I deducted only one star for that. Other than those two things, I have no complaints and am considering buying more in the future.

reviews.title: Took a chance ...missing the mark
reviews.text: It's tough because I know when lotions are natural they are not necessarily that thick but this stuff's pretty watery and I'm getting tired of the whole CBD infused products but they're not using good quality stuff so I don't know not my best lotion purchase

reviews.title: Nice Lotion
reviews.text: Great lotion with no scent. I need at least one bottle of lotion that has no odor and what a great bonus with the hemp ingredient. Clean product. I like it, it's light and soaks in well.

reviews.title: Vegan hand lotion
reviews.text: I'm still on the fence on this one - I think it moisturizes fine but it's so thin that if you aren't paying attention the whole bottle will be in your hand. Best to apply in very small amounts, don't turn it upside down and squeeze because you will get too much! And it seems to be priced accordingly for anything associated with the word vegan. I would probably not recommend based on the price.

reviews.title: just like mousse or shaving cream, but is a soap for body
reviews.text:
The bottle and texture is very much like a hair mousse or shaving cream, but is a wash for the body. Instead of a pump or squeeze bottle, this comes out though as pressure aerosol - also just like a mousse or shaving cream. The ingredients necessary to make this happen are Butane and Propane. They are aerosol propellents. If you have ever used mousse, hair spray, various perfumes/body sprays, air fresheners, aerosol deodorants, or shaving cream,etc.. then you are already familiar with these ingredients. They are generally recognized as safe, and are used in small, diluted quantities in skin care. Fun fact, "These ingredients are used in cosmetics and personal care products as a replacements for chlorofluorocarbons, or CFC propellants, some of which have been shown to have negative effects on the environment". I'm not a big fan of slathering my body with butane, propane, or isobutane, but I understand their use in this type of medium. So it's up to you to decide.

This company does have non aerosol body wash available too..

The resulting foamy wash comes out thick, it is creamy. Because it reminds me so much of a shaving cream, that it what I have been using it for. Then whatever is left over I use on rest of body. Is pleasant smelling enough to me but it isn't quite a pure aromatherapy experience for me. Clearly it uses a variety of essential oils- rosemary, orange, eucalyptus, peppermint, limonene. However, for whatever reason, it contains "parfum (fragrance)" as an added ingredient. As well as Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone, another synthetic fragrance ingredient. Is there not enough naturally occurring scent from the use of essential oils? I think the added synthetic fragrance is redundant and takes away from the olfactory experience.

As an actual wash, it is fine.
3 stars-- this is just a middle of the road rating. I am not in love. The experience is underwhelming. I am much too quickly going through the container. Aromatherapy experience is a little off as I pick up on something other than essential oils. Otherwise the cleansing action is fine. But.. I likely won't repurchase.

I think if I really want a foam wash, I will just use another body wash or make my own wash and pour into a foaming soap dispenser.

CRUELTY FREE | NO PARAFFIN, SILICONE OR MINERAL OILS | PLANT-BASED | PARABEN FREE | VEGAN

Aqua (Water), Sodium Laureth Sulfate, Butane, Glycerin, PEG-40 Hydrogenated Castor Oil, Isopropyl Palmitate, Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Oil, Mentha Aquatica Extract, Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Oil, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Peel Oil, Eucalyptus Globulus Leaf Oil, Mentha Piperita (Peppermint) Oil, Linalool, Limonene, Benzyl Alcohol, Geraniol, Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone, Sodium Levulinate, Sodium Anisate, Parfum (Fragrance), Hydroxyethylcellulose, Sorbitol, Propylene Glycol, Glucose, Propane, Isobutane, Citric Acid, Lactic Acid, Sodium Hydroxide

reviews.title: I like the scent
reviews.text: Just tried this out on my hands so that I could see how it works and smells. It smells good. The smell does dissipate after time. I am not so sure how I feel about a foam for my body, but I will adjust. When I wash, I prefer to use a pouf and this will work with gel or foam, so I won't even notice. I think I am going to be liking this and looking for more

reviews.title: Refreshing Scent, Easy to Use
reviews.text: The only thing I didn't like about this shower "foam" is how quickly it will be used up. It comes in a relatively small caninster and it comes out like shaving foam, only it's for the body and not for the face. It feels great and helps clear my stuffed sinuses. The scent is "mint and rosemary" but I feel like it's mostly mint. It's a step up above your ordinary shower gel but it won't likely last as long, as it's hard to tell how much of the bottle you are actually using since it's not liquid. Except for that caveat, I liked this.

reviews.title: Nice!
reviews.text: I have been a fan of this brand since I discovered it as a teen. The shower wash is nice. It smells lovely, has a great texture, and it leaves my skin soft and not dry. I really, really like this! I just wish it came in a larger package.

reviews.title: Feels and washes lovely - smells like MEN'S COLOGNE, though :(
reviews.text:
This wash is luxurious. Just a couple of pumps was all I needed for my shower. The directions say to use your hand and rub in but I used a pouf and it worked well. The foam really is luxurious and goes a long way.

My problem is the very strong scent. In no way does it smell like mint or rosemary (two scents that I LOVE!). To me, it smells like a men's cologne. In fact it smells just like this hand wash that a male friend keeps in his bathroom. I think guys would probably like this very much - me, not really. The scent is also VERY strong while using - like VERY. The good news is that it leaves just the lightest scent on your cleansed skin - thank goodness. Just very light.

It left my skin clean and soft. Too bad I really do not like the scent as I will likely not be using it again. : ( Sad too as I truly appreciate how it is clearly marked Vegan as that is important to me. If you found my review helpful, why not click the "Helpful" button. Thanks! : )

reviews.title: Soothing
reviews.text: I absolutely love it just a little foam goes a very long way!  Refreshing

reviews.title: Smells Great, Good Foaming
reviews.text: I have been enjoying this body foam. The smell isn't too overwhelming, and honestly I could use a bit more smell. It foams up really well, and glides on the skin. You can use not a lot and still cover your entire body. It washes off nicely, and you don't really smell it afterward. I have been using this for about a week and the container hasn't rusted or anything either, which is something that tends to happen with products in a metal container. I wish there was a way to know how much was left, so I know when to get a new one, but overall I am happy with this body wash.

reviews.title: Great smell and great lather, not so sure about the ingredients.
reviews.text:
This body wash not only smells great but provides a thick silky lather. I generally don't enjoy using foaming body washes because they don't foam up enough to get a good lather going so I have to use more of it than I want to; that is not a problem when using this! Two squirts of foam and I can really work up a respectable lather all while basking in the wonderful scent of rosemary and water mint!

With that being said, I don't think I'll be purchasing this after using my current bottle just because the ingredients are VERY questionable. I'm very conscientious about what I put on and into my body and I try to stay as natural as possible. There are some very questionable ingredients in this body wash you should watch out for including butane, isopropyl palmitate, propane, sodium hydroxide, and benzyl alcohol, all of which I'd rather not have going on my skin and into my body. I understand that isopropyl palmitate is what causes that rich lather I spoke so highly about earlier but be aware it can problematic for some people. I'm also not sure what the purpose of butane and propane is in this product...maybe for aerosolization purposes? If that's the case I'd much rather see a pump used on this product to create and propel the foam like some other foaming washes.

reviews.title: Good and soapy
reviews.text: I like the foamy milky soap but not too crazy about the scent… doesn’t smell bad just not my favorite. I’ll try other scents.

reviews.title: Fun, not strong
reviews.text:
Unlike other Kneipp products, this one didn't have a strong scent. I do like that it doesn't leave a smell on my skin so that I can wear my own perfume after.
Foam comes out a lot and has nice feel.

reviews.title: A wonderful bathing treat -- use it sparingly and it goes a long way!
reviews.text:
Sad to learn that Kneipp is discontinuing this wonderful product. They have had 4 different shower foam soaps in their lineup, my favorite being the Men's version (now discontinued). I think the main problem for most customers is that they use too much and then it runs out quickly. You really don't need much!

My technique is to use other inexpensive soaps first and then hit the "prime spots" with Kneipp (pits, privates, rear), as well as neck and face. The aroma with this one is really relaxing!

reviews.title: Yo! Stress relief isn’t the word. The scent alone calms my body.
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:522558cd2a0f5dd82f1651d9632e07fe]] This is an awesome foam body wash. I loved every minute of bathing with this. I do not use it on my private area only my surrounding parts. I rather use sensitive skin soap for those delicate areas. This foam is actually gentle on my skin and doesn’t dry me out. It smells wonderful and have my body relaxed after a steamy hot shower with this. Whoever created this scent knew what they were doing and should keep doing it for hard working mothers such as myself that needs products like this one to relax❣️

reviews.title: Luxurious
reviews.text: The aroma is very relaxing, and the foam leaves my skin feeling soft and refrehed.

reviews.title: Smells GREAT, quite pricey tho
reviews.text: Lovely scent, not too overpowering like some scented bath products. Quite expensive in my opinion.

reviews.title: The scent is so good, really practical change from gel to foam.
reviews.text: I really enjoyed this soap. The scent is so good, its so practical change from gel to foam. I guess I used it too much for the first time, I only need 1 pump and spread it with loofah and that's enough for the whole body.

reviews.title: Super foamy!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:69e74be6ba26ae9d29daee25655928f7]] It's a nice mousy body wash. The sent is great and super relaxing in the shower. It's hydrating too. It's just that it's not something that will last you months. It's better than my lever body wash but that lever one lasts me forever.  Because of the formula, it would be perfect to shave your legs.  Overall, it's ok.

reviews.title: Lucious Foam
reviews.text: Luscious thick foamy wash. I have another foam wash and this one seems way more moisturizing. I like the way it feels when washing, it don't feel like I have to use so much of it as I do my other wash. The smell is fantastic to me. Very light and not over-powering.

reviews.title: Smells SO good!
reviews.text: I love this stuff! It smells really refreshing. I'm into essential oils and it smells just like that...strong but invigorating. It comes out just like shaving cream foam. Scent lasts after you leave the bath/shower. 10/10 recommend

reviews.title: The way it makes me feel……
reviews.text: I have been using this Shower Foam for a few years and I totally love it.

reviews.title: Take your bath or shower to the next level
reviews.text: If your bath or shower is missing something, this might be it! This scrub is so nice and cozy. It smells amazing, like a cup of hot cocoa. It has great grains for exfoliation, there's different sizes to make sure your skin gets the best exfoliation. The mask in it is great to keep your skin soft and smooth too. For the ultimate treatment put a thick cream on after this and your skin will glow!

reviews.title: Not a fan
reviews.text: The other items in this line I liked a lot. This was unpleasant feeling and snaked more like first than coffee.

reviews.title: Love the smell but....
reviews.text:
This body scrub smells like brownie batter. It is strong, so some may not like it.

The coffee grounds sink to the bottom, which is annoying. Also, there aren't enough grounds to really get any exfoliation.

It also was really difficult to get off my skin, and made a mess of my shower.

reviews.title: Unusual, but Refreshing Scrub
reviews.text: I enjoyed this cocoa coffee scrub. Very rich and luxurious feel on the skin, leaves you feeling refreshed and super silky smooth. Love the feel and smell of this scrub!

reviews.title: Smells delicious!
reviews.text: This is a good scrub. It smells amazing and very chocolate-y! The scrub itself is a good texture. I wish it was a tad less runny but it isn't a big deal. The crushed coffee beans and sea salt give good scrub and the glycerin adds moisture. I will say that it does make a big mess. My shower looked so gross afterward. Thankfully I have a detachable shower head so I was able to easily spray around and clean things up because it was all over the place! Oh well. My skin felt soft and smooth and I'm obsessed with the scent so I'll continue to use it.

reviews.title: Good value but messy!
reviews.text: Glad I found this product but it definitely needs to be mixed before use! Make sure to clean the shower/tub after use because it will leave a mess behind if you're not careful with it. It's packaged really well so no leaks or anything. Smells great and leaves you feeling nice and clean!

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text:
I had high hopes for this body scrub. It’s priced high so I expected it to be high quality. I was disappointed as soon as I opened it, the product was separated. All of the thick gritty stuff was on one side and all of the liquid oily stuff was on the other side. I had to physically stir it up before I could even use it.

The smell was overpowering. It wasn’t necessarily bad, just was too strong. I also thought the scrub was way too rough. I used it on my knees and it was border line painful. There is no way I could use this on sensitive skin areas like my stomach or chest. After rinsing off the grit in the bottom of the tub literally hurt my feet stepping on it.

I also do not like how there is no ingredients list on the jar. I like to see what I’m scrubbing on my skin.

Overall I do not recommend this body scrub.

reviews.title: I'm not sure it's worth the mess.
reviews.text:
I used this body scrub last night and it smelled amazing as soon I opened it, but applying it is very messy, it looked like I dumped a coffee filter out in my shower, it was everywhere!
It did smell good as I said and feel good while applying, it doesn't come off skin very easily but did my skin feel soft.
I'm just not sure it is worth having to spend 5 minutes cleaning my shower curtain and shower each use though.

Thank you for reading my review I hope that it was helpful to you.

reviews.title: Quite sticky (interesting consistency); smells exactly like the ingredients (coffee and salt!)
reviews.text: EDITED: Tried to be descriptive in the title. This scrub is very interesting to me, because it is MUCH stickier than I thought it would be. I previously was using the Terre de Mars coffee/aloe scrub and I got GREAT results (soft, matte skin after washing off with near complete resolution of my frequent forehead breakouts), so I wanted to give this coffee product a try when I saw it for comparison. From a smell standpoint it's fine (like sweet coffee but with a saltiness to it), and texture-wise it does have some grit behind it to feel like you're getting a good scrub when you apply it (can see the sea salt). As I noted earlier though, it is QUITE sticky, and the dark color of the coffee can be a bit difficult to get off your skin (I follow the scrub w/ a cleanser and thorough rinse after which gets it all off without too much mess). I haven't used it long enough to say it's really making a big difference, but the few times I did use it I did feel like my skin was softer than before, so that's a good sign.

reviews.title: Left a brown film on my skin, had to rewash after using
reviews.text: I used this body scrub while in the shower. It smells like chocolate, so I like the scent. The product is fairly thick with lots of gritty stuff in it for exfoliation, though I found I needed to mix it up with my finger before using it since it had separated a bit. It's definitely messy, so I'm glad I chose to use it in the shower. I initially liked using the scrub and how it felt, but then I had issues rinsing it off. No matter how much I tried to rinse, there was a dark film on my skin, which was very noticeable on my fair skin. Even scrubbing with a washcloth didn't help. I ended up needing to use soap to get the product off, which seemed frustrating because I had already washed before using the scrub, since the directions indicated my skin should be freshly cleansed before using it. I think in the future I will use the scrub, then get washed so that I don't end up with the dark film on my skin. It seemed like such an expensive product shouldn't leave me looking dirtier than I was before I got into the shower! In good news, my skin can be sensitive to some products, and this scrub didn't bother me at all.

reviews.title: Stinky!
reviews.text:
I love scrubs! I live on a tropical island where shorts and a tshirt are what I live in. I love to have soft, silky skin (who doesn’t!) when it comes to wearing shorts or dresses. So I was excited to use this scrub. It’s a bit pricey. I usually buy cheaper. Even the popular vegan store has a scrub that’s cheaper than this and it’s one of my favs. Packaging is nice. Typical tub of scrub. I opened it expecting a nice smell of coffee beans but yuck! It reminded me of dirt! So stinky! But whatever, I’ll still try it out. It cost $40 bucks so I didn’t want it to go to waste. A bit runny. Felt very rough and not in the good way. Also! It left a brown residue, like others have stated. I had to use an exfoliating glove to scrub it off. Another thing, when rinsing the scrub, it left a gritty texture on my shower floor.
Overall, this was a thumbs down. I have better scrubs that are cheaper, work great, and smell amazing.

reviews.title: Not sure what to make of it
reviews.text: This product smells so good you want to eat it.  NO, I did not end up eating it.  It felt nice on the skin. But it didn't feel like it was exfoliating on my skin.  Not sure I felt any difference after applying moisturizers on my skin.  It stated that it would help open up pores and open them up so it could absorb better. Gave it a 3 because I could not see of feel the effect of it.  It is hard to tell with these types of products.

reviews.title: luxurious and delicious smelling
reviews.text: I have used a couple of body scrubs from 100% pure in the past and I have really enjoyed them. I think my favorite one have been the vanilla and citrus ones. This coffee scrub smells amazing in the shower, especially in the morning. It really wakes the senses up. It is thick and a little bit goes a long way. The one thing about this coffee scrub that I do not entirely love is that the coffee granules in my opinion are a little bit too big. Their sugar scrubs are more gentle on the skin. If your have sensitive skin these might be a bit toot rough for you but it is worth giving it a try if you love the scent of coffee and if you apply it light handedly. The ingredients in this coffee scrub are pure and non toxic just like everything els that 100% pure makes. I also think this mask would of been very nice in a glass container instead of plastic.

reviews.title: Coarser (salt based) body scrub that is effective
reviews.text: First of all, this is a salt scrub and not a sugar scrub. Salt and sugar have very different qualities. As someone who has tried many body and face scrubs: from sugar and salt to ground almond, from speciality stores to drug stores to homemade, this scrub performs well. It is a coarser scrub due to the salt. I was a little nervous about rinsing it down the drain in my tub, but I was glad to find that the coffee bean and cacao (listed in the ingredients) are incredibly fine and it is only the salt that is the rough material, so it dissolves and rinses away just fine. Because of the natural ingredients, there’s separation, but it can easily be mixed prior to use. I took a photo to show where the ingredients are located on the container. It was a little messy since the scrub is dark brown - it looks exactly like the product pictures. It leaves skin smelling slightly like pure dark chocolate. I scrubbed one arm and not the other to compare and it really gets rid of bumps and uneven skin texture. It was not irritating or too rough, it felt nice. Some sugar scrubs just dissolve quickly and are too greasy, but because these salt crystals are more coarse, they do scrub (hate to use the word scratch) away excess stuff on the skin’s surface. When mixed, it also was not too greasy and did not cause my tub to be left oily after showering off the scrub. I’m interested in seeing how this continues to improve my skin with continued use.

reviews.title: I think it's just dirt and rock salt
reviews.text: This doesn't smell like chocolate or coffee to me, it literally just smells like dirt. The exfoliant in this feels like hard sharp large chunks of rock salt. It's a hard pass for me especially at the current price of $40.

reviews.title: Love the smell but....
reviews.text:
This body scrub smells like brownie batter. It is strong, so some may not like it.

The coffee grounds sink to the bottom, which is annoying. Also, there aren't enough grounds to really get any exfoliation.

It also was really difficult to get off my skin, and made a mess of my shower.

reviews.title: Just okay
reviews.text: I didn’t like how the coffee grounds and the oil separate. The smell is pretty good, and it does feel good going on. It exfoliates well, but it does make a mess in the shower. I don’t think I would buy this again. I have used better body scrubs.

reviews.title: Very pleased with this game!
reviews.text:
I just opened up my copy of this game today and gave it a shot after reading over the rules a few times. Although I played solo (which is not suggested in any way since you control at least 6 factions yourself), I found the game to be very enjoyable. I could see where this could easily become a favorite among friends, whether or not we played as teams. Here are a few things to note:
+The box that the game comes in is very well made (Looks great too)
+Great quality for the game board (It is a 6-fold board, by the way)
+Cards are very good quality
+Tons of cubes (90/color; 720 total)
+Excellent mechanics for determining turns for players (In my opinion)
+Multiple tie-breakers
+Low required playtime with regards to it being a strategy game (especially compared to Risk)
+/- Limited Rounds are implemented instead of any particular "end goals" (I personally think this is genius; some people may not prefer it)

Edit:
This game is a hit amongst friends. It's relatively simple to teach (as long as people are paying attention) and flows pretty smoothly. Almost anytime my wife and I go to an event where board games are present this one has been requested almost every time.

reviews.title: Great game
reviews.text: This game is really fun it is a based loosely on Risk but much better rules and gets rid of the worst of Risk.  The game play is fairly quick (90 minutes which is good for a strategy game) as there is a dice that counts down unlike in Risk which goes on forever.  You really have to be strategic in your turns to win.  You can play multiple people which is the most fun or multiple factions with less people still fun but I find that people tend to focus on one and sacrifice one so its a bit less fun.  Great game overall.  I highly recommend

reviews.title: Even though I don't normally like games like Risk...
reviews.text: I purchased this game through the Kickstarter campaign.  I'll admit, I am friends with the creator but I wouldn't give this game a good rating if I didn't enjoy it.  I play lots of table top games (Pandemic, Ticket to Ride, Dominion) and am a big fan of Wil Wheaton's show TableTop on Youtube, so I would have tried Spheres of Influence even if I didn't know the creator.  I was worried that I wouldn't like it since I normally don't care for global domination-type games (Risk, Axis & Allies) I find them too long and more frustrating than fun.  However, this game was fast paced, and very engaging.  There was significant strategy involved, and there are added elements (regional resources, oil production, and capital cities) that make the strategy a lot more interesting than just battling your opponents.  I played with 5 people--a mix of people who like global domination games and a couple who don't.  Everyone really enjoyed it.  Took about an hour to complete.  Good quality game components.

reviews.title: Got this game on the basis of a DiceTower review ...
reviews.text: Got this game on the basis of a DiceTower review for it.  I agree with the review in that this is a "Risk killer," i.e., probably would never want to play standard Risk given having this game available.  With the variable starting positions, resource concerns, and action card play, this game goes well beyond the move and roll pattern that characterizes standard Risk.

reviews.title: If you love the original style RISK, toss it and buy this game.
reviews.text:
Like other reviewers here, I backed this game on Kickstarter. As someone else said, it was a very well run campaign. One of the best I've seen. It's a good game, good quality components, has a good price, and it shipped on time (which is a rarity on Kickstarter).

As to why I backed the game and why I love it? Again, as others have said, this is a "RISK killer" game. When I find a game that does the same thing as another game, but better, the old game goes (donated typically). This game is obviously like RISK, however it is different in some important ways.

1. Random Starting Locations: You're dealt two location cards and pick one.
2. You don't fill the board, you only start in your beginning location, so much the board is empty and you must rush to/fight for them.
3. One move at a time. You move from one 'territory' to another one territory at a time. No insanely long turns where someone invades multiple other territories at a time.
4. There are turns and there are rounds. Each player takes multiple turns within each round and one of the very cool things is those turns are randomized, so you don't know when during a round you're going to get to take your turn, which adds a lot to the strategy aspect.
5. You do get cards like in RISK, but they are more meaningful and provide far better options than merely more armies.
6. Water movement exists and how troops move in and battle in or from the water is a good balance of strategic and realistic vs simple.
7. These days it seems hard to find a game that allows for more then four or five players. This allows for up to eight
8. The game is very simple, but very strategic. You can learn to play it in a few minutes, but the subtleties of strategy will likely take some time to see, which allows more people to enjoy the game and helps with it's longevity.
9. Lastly the game has a time limit. After so many rounds you see who is ahead. So no 12 hours marathon games like traditional RISK.

reviews.title: Like risk but better. Well made game
reviews.text: Great game. Makes you plan out your moves better than stacking up troops in risk and conquering the world. Plus turn limit helps with the time of the game. Last more than 2 hours depending on the number of players. Very unique artwork. Battle system is cool. Makes it more fair for the defender without making the attacker too overpowered when they have a lot of troops.

reviews.title: My Favorite Game
reviews.text: This is probably my favorite board game ever. Since we got it last summer, we've played about 30 times. We loop in new people all the time and everyone loves it. It's a well thought out, dynamic, better version of Risk. Highly recommended.

reviews.title: This game takes everything annoying about Risk
reviews.text:
This game takes everything annoying about Risk, and makes it way better. There's no reason, beyond nostalgia, to play Risk again. The turn system is great, the combat is better. The objectives like points of interest(earn special ability cards) and oil fields(earn more turns per round) make the game much more interesting than just holding choke points. While I personally like long-game world domination strategy, the round limit is pretty refreshing, and gives you a little more excitement and sense of urgency. Obviously, you could just remove the turn limit if you wanted to, but I'd recommend trying it first for sure. The rules are fairly simple and make a lot of sense. Once you get going it's a breeze, allowing strategy to be your primary concern.

This is absolutely worth buying. The Dice Tower on Youtube(not affiliated, just a fan) has a nice review if you want a better idea, or the creator(Josh Lamont) has a longer video that teaches you step by step how to play.

reviews.title: Great game that rivals the likes of Risk
reviews.text:
I’ve been on the fence about purchasing this game but decided to pull the trigger and try it out.

The game itself is a great strategy game that has a lot more action compared to a standard Risk game. It starts off a bit slow but when the last two to three rounds come, the game ramps up and factions go up and down the score tracker with every turn. The only qualm I have is the tracker board is a bit flimsy.

reviews.title: Lovely game. I've played 4 times so far and ...
reviews.text: Lovely game.  I've played 4 times so far and there is still so much to learn.  Everyone I have played with enjoys it as well.

reviews.title: Risk with no headache!
reviews.text: I really like the combat mechanic in this game.  It prevents someone from just sweeping the board.  It's also interesting to see how empires spread across the globe and how the game will mimic history.  This is easily one of, if not my favorite new game.

reviews.title: This game is deceptively simple in its easy play; however
reviews.text:
I have been playing war games and simulations for over 45 years. I have collected over 300+ war games and have played them all with both friends old and new. This game is deceptively simple in its easy play; however, it requires sound strategy to win. The rules are easy to learn, but that's when the fun starts as there are so many possible strategies to choose from.

The game components and board are high quality and nicely done. This demonstrates real commitment by the designer, to hold out for the best.

I will need more time to thoroughly evaluate this game, but I believe that out of the 300+, it will land firmly in my top ten.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: This game is really fun! The artwork of the board/cards looks great.

reviews.title: Awesome Game! One of my favorite board games of all time!
reviews.text: If you like Risk, you'll LOVE playing this game. Spheres has a lot of options in the game play which makes far more interesting and fun to play. It is fast paced, alway changing and plays typically in about 90 minutes. After you play once, you're ready to play straight away again! One other great thing, unlike Risk, you'll never be left out of the game. My friends and I usually play it several times in a row. If you like playing world domination games, Spheres of Influence is the right game for you!

reviews.title: Great game! Very entertaining
reviews.text: Great game! Very entertaining, can be played equitably with 2 people.The board it's self is nice and thick with great coloring. The score card could be a little bigger and made of nicer paper.

reviews.title: One of my top five games easily. Super deep strategy
reviews.text: One of my top five games easily.  Super deep strategy, quick pacing, and best of all ***it ends!!!**** it does not have monopoly or risk syndrome where you can only win by defeating everyone else. I kickstarter the game and have had it for some months, playing it several times

reviews.title: Spheres Influenced Me!
reviews.text: The componant quality is top notch and the board is gorgeous!  The easiest way to describe it would be to say it plays somewhat like Risk, though it has much more interesting mechanics and depth of gameplay.

reviews.title: Love the randomized turn deck mechanic and its so great ...
reviews.text:
One of my gaming group's favorites! Love the randomized turn deck mechanic and its so great that non-Risk people will sit down, play, and enjoy.

Also - all the backstabbing you want but very few broken hearts!

reviews.title: This is a must have
reviews.text: I believe I have had this long enough to determine it's value. It is well worth the money. This is a must have for any beauty routine. It comes very well packaged and protected. It comes with the charging stand, plug, charging cord, little filters that go inside the product and three Microdermabrasion tips. The tip that comes already on the Diamond Microdermabrasion is the normal one, then there is one for sensitive skin, and the last one is a precision tip. When you use this it gently uses suction and the the diamond tips to pull away the dead skin, black heads and even small whiteheads. Afterwards lightly wash your face. It does leave your face a little red but only for a few hours. By the next it was gone. I did have some areas of slight  bruising from going over the same area to often. It wasn't really bruising it was more like a suction mark on your skin but it was gone by the next day. I have used this several times and I absolutely love it. My skin looks and feels much better. This is one of those things that I won't be able to live without. I love it. Had I not seen it on sell, this is one of those things I would have never tried but since it was discounted I am so glad I got it and I think you would have too. I highly recommend this Microdermabrasion device.

reviews.title: Not happy.
reviews.text: I had high hopes for this product.  But unfortunately I think I received a used product, and therefore I sent it back immediately.  Not happy.

reviews.title: Does what it says...
reviews.text: Good product, time consuming but worth it. Glad I got it.

reviews.title: Facial wonder
reviews.text: This microderm is great for clearing up skin, really exfoliates and helps get the "gunk" out of your pores. Also great for spot acne treatment too, it helps extract zits and they heal up faster.

reviews.title: Great Micro-dermabrasion
reviews.text: First time ever using a Micro-dermabrasion, so I didn't know what to expect. I was delightfully surprised and I loved it. It was easy to operate and the instructions were clear and easy to understand.  The results were as claimed.  I would recommend this product to anyone seeking to revitalize and add youthful look to their skin.  The Micro-dermabrasion is very well made and even though I have only used it once I can tell it will last.

reviews.title: Where has this been all my life?!
reviews.text: I'm 37 years old, and have been hearing older women say they wish they'd started taking care of their skin sooner.  So I'm trying to learn from them.  The problem is, I tried using products that said they would sting once applied, but when I apply them, I feel nothing.  My skin is hard as nails.  Even though I've exfoliated for years, I figured I must have a ton of dead skin begging to be removed.  Then I found this product *enter heavenly choir singing*.  So I used the microdermabrasion machine on the lowest setting on my face and afterwards, I finally felt tingling and stinging when using my liquid products!  lol  My face turned red from the microdermabrasion, but only for about 5 minutes on my face, maybe 10 minutes on my neck, and less than 5 minutes on my hands.  I imagine the reaction time could be different if your skin isn't made of nails.  It doesn't hurt- it feel like a small piece of sandpaper being rubbed against against your skin, and it makes a kissy noise when it comes off.  Afterwards, my skin was super smooth- if you've ever used DHC's Velvet Coat primer on your face, my face felt a lot like that.  It was great!  And then I tried putting on some of my serum and whoo hoo!  I felt the sting!  I was thrilled!  I changed my filter and blech!  So happy to see all that dead skin on the filter.  I couldn't be more pleased with this product and I highly recommend it for tough as nails skin!

reviews.title: Great!
reviews.text: I really like this.  It leaves pores clean and my skin smooth.  Thanks!

reviews.title: Zero Suction Save Your Money
reviews.text: I did a lot of research on microdermabrasion machines I love the results at the spa but I don't want to pay spa prices all the time. I decided to purchase this one because of the great deal. It arrived quickly, packaging was really nice the tool itself is heavy and seemed like it would do a good job. After sufficiently charging it I tried it but there was zero suction. Sent it back immediately.

reviews.title: I really can't say enough good things about this machine!
reviews.text:
*UPDATE* April 18th, 2017 - I was just browsing the net trying to find a light therapy device and I was getting pretty exasperated with too many choices. I happened to look over and see my Dermabraze MD unit on my vanity and thought, I wonder if their company makes other products? So when I Google'd the company one of the first things I saw was a "Fakespot" link, and having only ever seen products with an F rating I thought I'd check this one because I expected it to have rave reviews like mine. I was FLOORED when I saw the grade F! That simply cannot be accurate! I purchased mine in November of 2016, and nearly 6 months later I still regard it as the best money I've ever spent on skincare, PERIOD. I used it on my full face just last night! It gets a regular workout too, between myself, my mother and my daughter. Even my son has used it! I just had to come update my review when I saw that deceitful grade but in a way I'm thankful because I know now that Fakespot can't be trusted. I KNOW THIS DEVICE WORKS AND WORKS FANTASTICALLY!

I'm fortunate to have had a great example when I was a child. My mother always took very good care of her skin. Until I reached my teen years I literally believed that all women used peel off masks, apricot scrub, Noxema and Oil of Olay, lol. Skin care has come a long way, and when I started working in the beauty industry I was introduced to higher quality products and left my mother's favorites in the dust, haha. As far as exfoliating, before trying this machine I'd always stuck with scrubs, chemical exfoliants and microdermabrasion crystal creams. Now I feel like a need a good kick in the rear for not investing in real microdermabrasion years ago! I absolutely love this machine ... it is an investment, but the effectiveness and sheer convenience of it make it more than worth the money. You just can't get results like this with any other method. My favorite thing about it is the suction. Sure, you can use products to manually exfoliate skin, but vacuuming the oil and dead cells that collect inside and clog pores combined with abrasion literally and immediately take years off the skin's appearance. It also allows the high dollar serums and moisturizers we apply to do a much better job. Again, to me this totally justifies the investment. If you're serious about skin care and spend good money on expensive products, you owe it to yourself to prep your skin so your products can do their jobs!

Pros of the Bright Therapy unit:

Rechargeable
Strong, adjustable suction
Different attachments for heavy, light and acute abrasion.
Comfortable to hold, fit's easily in the hand
Has disposable filters allowing you to see exactly what the machine removes every time you use it.

If you're looking for a great quality microdermabrasion system I think you'll be pleased with this one, I can't find a single con to owning it. I absolutely love mine!

reviews.title: quit working after two months
reviews.text:
My new favorite facial tool! I feel like I just rejuvenated my face! Works and feels just like my aesthetician's microdermabrasion tool. The only difference is I did it myself and just saved $85.00 bucks. I usually get my microdermabrasion's every 3 weeks at my Spa. I do rather enjoy them, however I thought I would buy one myself and give it a try. I actually like doing it myself better because I can go over certain oily parts of my skin several times more than my aesthetician does. This is a pretty powerful tool. I like the first setting because I have sensitive skin. My daughter used it but she used it on setting 4 and liked the more powerful suction. It is extremely nice and comes in a very expensive box. I am very very happy with this purchase.
I am re-submitting another review due to the product quit working after only 2 months. Outside of window to return (30 days only). I would not recommend this product.

reviews.title: Very Satisfied.
reviews.text: Works Great!

reviews.title: Pro Diamond Microdermabrasion Handheld...

so far, this product i am using every two days.
I am very concerned about how to clean these diamond heads. They are feeling dull after just 3 uses.
What do I do about this. Please respond if someone knows how to fix the diamond heads from dulling.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Using and it is helping with liver spots

reviews.title: Great!
reviews.text: I am a licensed esthetician, and I have only used this product once and I am pretty impressed. I used it on myself to get a feel for it. I love that it comes with a manual explaining in detail everything that comes with it as well as a chart that shows you each part of your face, the direction you should use, and max strength level that should be used. There are 4 suction levels, I never went above 2 for my first time playing around with it, even then, my skin felt so soft. It doesn't hurt while doing it, and personally I didn't have any discomfort the next day either. Over all great product so far and I am excited to keep practicing with it.

reviews.title: Really smooth skin

Really worked great. Been trying it out for a week and I really like the way it makes your skin feel. Very smooth. My wife uses it too, she loves it.
We haven't had any problems with it so far.

reviews.title: the crystal tips are good but I went on to the website looking to ...
reviews.text: i have not used the product yet, but i did check it out, the crystal tips are good  but I went on to the website looking to see how much are the replaceable tips, but I couldn't find any I wasn't happy about that

reviews.title: Perfect for homemade antibacterial spray. So many uses!
reviews.text: These were just what I needed to make my own alcohol antibacterial sprays. After being unable to find Lysol spray I resorted to mixing 1 part rubbing alcohol to 2 part water and adding to a spray bottle that ended up with a leak. I ended up trying these and it made 10 individual sized sprays that are perfect for on the go or putting one in every room if the house. I use these to spray groceries down, spray on cleaned countertops and faucets, etc.  The plastic is thick and none have leaked. The set even came with two cute little funnels to help add your own liquids. There are so many uses for these and they are 2oz which is perfect for airline carry on bag requirements. I am so glad I saw these. Now, instead of having all of my antiseptic spray in one large bottle, I have 10 with the enclosed labels added, in each car and room of the house.

reviews.title: Thin cheap bottles not too expensive
reviews.text: At about a dollar a piece for something that cost a few cents to make it's not the best deal in the world, but it's not bad. They work for my application - holding hand sanitizer - and hold an adequate amount.

reviews.title: They Work Great
reviews.text:
These perform very well. I use them to make my own essential oil (EO) bug sprays, after sun sprays, room refreshers, etc. They are small, spray well & don't leak.

The small size is great for portability. I take one of my bug spray formulas outside with me for while I work on my farm. Because I only use EO's the effectiveness wears off much faster than synthetic products. I keep the bottle on me to respray as needed. I have a bottle in my purse with a germ fighting blend for when stores are out of the cart wipes. I even made up a bottle of toilet spray. I use peppermint EO for that.

The funnel has a very narrow spout making pouring a slow processes but it keeps me from accidentally over filling the bottles. The tube that goes into the bottle is plenty long. It actually curls up in a C on the very bottom of the bottle. You can cut it if you want, but I leave it as is, I think they function better with the long tube. The stickers are a bit of a joke. I can't really fit "Bug Spray" on one tiny label. I'd have to write "BS" so I write "Bug" instead or "Sun" for example. It just barely fits.

Overall, great little spray bottles that suit my farm girl needs.

reviews.title: I got leaking inside the cap, but it didn't come out.
reviews.text:
This is a nice spray bottle kit, it comes with 2 funnels and a sheet of labels, I found the labels too small for use, each one is approximately 1 inch x 1/2 inch in diameter, and not sure if it can be used in an inkjet printer or not. All 10 spray bottles included all the necessary parts, and the stem that draws up liquid was long in each one, none were short and I really appreciated that. You should be able to spray down to the last drop. I tested mine with rubbing alcohol, for sanitizing my phones. The funnel came in handy for filling the bottle. It made the job so easy! The sprayer does spray a very fine mist. I used it several times to clean some things then left it overnight with the cap on. Next day I tested it again. First off, the cap contained some of the rubbing alcohol inside it, which means the container will leak into the cap standing up. Not necessarily a huge deal breaker, but a negative for me. Holding it in my hand I got the rubbing alcohol on my finger too, so this may waste product just from this. Several drops every 24 hours may not seem like a lot, but if you make your own essential oil room spray then this could be an expensive waste. Lastly, I had an issue with the spray top, the angle is so slight I wasn't sure if I would spray myself in the face of I wasn't wearing eyeglasses. You have to be careful, ensuring you're spraying away from yourself. I did not get any leakage of the alcohol out of the cap, it seemed pretty tight. It did pool in the cap, didn't run down the bottle. I did get a rubbing alcohol ring when I set down the cap on my coffee table tho. Beware of that possibility whatever you use in these bottles.
All in all, a nice kit and I give it 4 stars.

reviews.title: Wash them well before using 💀
reviews.text:
A spray bottle is a spray bottle, right? 🤔 Lol, nope!

So, here we are... shopping on Amazon, presented with a thousand options for something as simple as a bottle that sprays. 😰

My selection criteria was this: (a) The plastic must be able to handle liquid sanitizer made with a base of 60% alcohol (the 91% ISP variety), *without suffering undesirable changes* and (b) Have a good spray nozzle capable of producing a fine, even mist *without getting clogged*.

This set of bottles from MOSTOF checks both of those boxes. ✔ I tested them specifically against 2 different types of liquid (not gel) sanitizer. Initially, I chose this particular set for the larger sized funnels that are also included, and the fact that it comes with (10) travel sized bottles.

None of my bottles leak and they spray a fine, even mist without leaving droplets. After seeing the spray function in action, I decided to use a couple of these for facial mists and essences. I don't know (yet) whether they'll spray every last drop, but the sprayer feed wraps all the way around the bottom of the bottle, which does look promising. 🤞 So far, every liquid I've put in them has been fine; the plastic didn't change and the sprays perform the same.

Ultimately, I had to dock a star from my rating because the bottles came filthy inside, 😬 every one of them is heavily scratched up, and one of the bottles I received doesn't sit evenly. ➡️(see photos 3&4: defect (warped) bottle is on the right)⬅️

PRO'S:
+ Contains everything you need to package liquids immediately
+ Includes larger sized funnels = quick flow in filling
+ Spray nozzles produce fine, even mist without leaving droplets
+ Sprayer feed goes all the way around the bottom of the bottle
+ No leaking from the bottle necks or spray nozzles
+ Spray nozzles have not clogged
+ Caps fit tightly
+ No adverse reactions to the plastic (re: high alcohol content)
+ Airline approved carry-on size
+ Excellent price for ten bottles (and two funnels!)
+ Convenient PRIME shipping
+ Bonus sheet of labels (they're small, but they work)

CONS:
- Bottles came really scratched up and very dirty
- One of the ten bottles I received doesn't sit evenly

SUMMARY:
Ultimately, I think there's some quality control issues that the manufacturer could improve upon. As long as you're not an Etsy seller, or someone that's gonna be gifting these, you may not mind the scratches or the possibility of uneven bottom(s). Provided you wash them out really good, they still do the job! 👍🏼 Should anything happen to change with these as time goes on, I will update this review. Thanks for reading! Stay safe. 😷

reviews.text: This electric drill comes with about 11 different drill bits and sanding pieces for the grinders. This also comes with a brush to remove the powder you get from the drill. reviews.title: I'm not sure this is worth the price asked...
reviews.text: I've always wanted to get into the art of doing my own acrylics so when I saw this drill set, I knew I had to review it.  Firstly, it comes with numerous accessories- a fluffy brush for removing dust from the nail and work area, a set of to separaters for painting, a nail board, and the drill bits themselves.  The entire set is nicely packaged and comes nestled in a velvety foam materials.  The bits are held in their own clear container within the box.  The set comes with a detachable adapter that plugs into the end of the drill and then into the wall outlet.  The drill itself is small enough to handle pretty easily, although I can see where it would be difficult for someone with smaller hands than myself (I have large hands.)  Inserting and removing the bits can be a challenge.  The mechanism isn't well made so I have to fight with it.  The power cord is much too short and I had to use an extension cord to give myself some wiggle room away from the wall outlet.  Some of the bits' textures do a better job than the others, while some don't do anything at all.  The over all look and feel of the set isn't worth the price, in my opinion.  It works a bit too awkwardly to be something I'd use that often.

reviews.title: Surprisingly good for the price
reviews.text:
For the price, I'm genuinely surprised with this nail drill. First of all, it doesn't suffer from the two major issues most cheap nail drills face: lack of torque and internal wobbling. A lack of torque is when the rotation mechanism stalls out if you press hard enough against the nail. You'll know a nail drill has internal wobbling because it will almost tickle or feel uncomfortable when you file your nails.

With that being said, here's what I don't like: I prefer much thinner nail drills. Even if you "choke up" your grip onto the thinner part of the drill it's still thick enough to make my hands tired if I'm using it for longer than a few minutes. I can't imagine this being comfortable to use all day in a nail salon. Next, the instructions. It took me way too long to figure out how to change the drill bit, partly due to the vague instructions and partly because I had to use so much force I genuinely thought I was going to break it. The twist mechanism is misleading. You turn it and it stops so you think you're done turning it but in actuality you have to keep turning it until you hear a click.
It also would be nice for the manufacturer to explain the differences between all the drill bits and what the intended use case is for each one.

FOR THOSE OF YOU THAT CAN'T CHANGE THE DRILL BIT:

Turn the top portion of the drill toward the side labeled "R" until you hear a click (this is important) use pliers and pull out the drill bit. You'll need to use more force than you think.
Once you've removed the drill bit, stop turning the top portion of the drill toward "R". The mechanism will go back to the center by itself. Pick your desired drill bit and put it where the old drill bit was. Turn the top portion of the drill towards the labeled "S" until you hear a click to secure the drill bit (The drill bit WILL fall out if you don't twist it towards "S" until you hear a click).

Still, this kit could be used in a salon or for personal use at home. You can manicure and pedicure as well as shape, polish, and remove rough skin, including calluses.

It reminds me a bit of a dremel, so you could probably get creative and use it for hobbies and crafts. I like the fact that it can be charged via a USB cable, which makes it portable.

reviews.title: Strong little device.  I think this might be good for diabetics too.
reviews.text:
It is a great little grinder. And runs by USB power so its very low wattage.
My mother is diabetic and has to be very careful about the shape of her toenails. Its very important to avoid infection. Regular clippers are hard to position correctly sometimes. And there is a risk of 'over cutting' the nail and causing issues that way.
With these you are shaping the nail slowly so you can control it a whole lot better. Less chances of clipper accidents.
**I'm not a doctor nor do I play one on TV. Its just my opinion that these are a good choice for diabetics.
I was given these as a vine promotion for the review. But that aside I like it and see it as a durable useful tool. Mostly because I end up being the one that has to take care of the toenail business And these make it easier to take care of.
