reviews.title: awesome
reviews.text: i am super fair complected (the lightest shade or second lightest shade available of any foundation or concealer ive worn, just to help illustrate). i have gotten several compliments on my complection since i started using this. I was worried any self tanner at all could make me look really weird but its super natural looking,  It makes my dull pale skin look more alive. I love it.

reviews.title: Why is this  no longer in stores?
reviews.text: This is far and away the best sunless tanning product on yhe market--why is it not in leading retailers any more?!!!!

reviews.title: Gives a light tan to skin
reviews.text: I like this lotion because it gives a subtle tan to skin, but I do not the shimmer it leaves behind on my skin.

reviews.title: Didn't work for me.
reviews.text: I've been using this for about two weeks and haven't noticed any change in my skin tone. I like the glow and the smell isn't bad, but my boyfriend even said my legs looked whiter after a few days use. I'll be trying another brand instead.

reviews.title: best product for redheads that I have found
reviews.text:
I have tried them ALL and have a closet filled with half used bottles of tanner ranging in price from $5 to $30 and I have to say, this gives me the best color and the best coverage. Most people have yellow undertones to their skin which works well with self tanners, but I have very fair skin with pink undertones and most of them come out orange and uneven which is very obvious on my fair skin. I am sooooo impressed with the light brown color - it looks very natural, sunkissed, and glowy. I just stocked up!
Also, I think it smelled nice - kind of like a light grapefruit scent. I'm not sure what offended the other reviewer unless she dislikes the smell of citrus or the way the product reacts to her body chemistry. Most of them smell bad when they react to the bacteria on your skin's surface, but that washes off in the shower in the morning when you wash with a good antibacterial soap (I use Dial).

reviews.title: I like this product though
reviews.text: This has made a visible difference in the “11” that was forming between my eyebrows. I can also tell a difference in crow’s feet. The only issue is it’s not in a functional bottle; the squirt cap doesn’t work for me with a glass bottle. So I just have to open it and use a q-tip each time to dip it out. I like this product though.

reviews.title: No.
reviews.text: Please don’t waste your money. The bottles are impossible to open, have tried to contact the company with no result, and have no idea what is really in the bottles.

reviews.title: Digg’n it!
reviews.text: Well... I am By all accounts a Product Junkie.. I try just about anything new or different... looking for the little black pearl... I think I found one here. I only gave 4 Stars because I’ve only had it two days (so used it 4x) so I’ll update later. I worked in the medical field for 17 years and STILL I’m one of those “Instant Gratification Hunters” knowing very well they don’t exist. “Time” is key and it’s a Fact everyone has a completely different biology , Chemistry —- etc. but I used to do Botox 2x a year. ONLY between brows (compliments of being Italian 😬) but I’m so fascinated by our chemical make-up and what we eat vs. how it affects our bodies. Anyone with common sense KNOWS there is no “miracle pill” or “miracle Serum”.. it’s s cluster of Nutrition, DNA, Genetics, (you get the gist) and is why we have such varying experiences. Doesn’t mean the products are good or bad, it just might not be the right products for some. All said I actually (so far) am loving this product. I am Confused why so many reviews complain about opening or getting the serum out of the bottle. (Tip upside down and pinch the rubber extended dropper - Viola! Perfect dropper snd cant get any more simple than that. You don’t squeeze glass. This is why the dropper is rubber do you can get (pinch) a perfect amount got your skin. Now that’s out of the way. :) Moving on.. for those who know Holistic Medicine, Essential Oils or Serums/ we know all of these keep better in glass and that’s why pretty much every serum we buy (of quality) unless it’s a cream (but creams aren’t Serums, so not sure why I went there. But anyway...) I found it quit brilliant the way they gave us 2 smaller jars opposed to 1 larger jar. Another tip- every time we open a bottle a serum etc. it deoxygenates (in English= air dilutes it’s quality and in turn that breaks down the vital ingredients. So it was well executed to have. Smaller jar. (PS:  I keep My 2nd one in the fridge/ same with the 24k eye patches, but I’m going off subject again) You CAN keep your “open bottle” in the refrigerator- it’s a preference. Just don’t ever keep products in HEATED areas. So the glass is a must. And other than a “dropper” thE type of rubber tip here tells me its up to us how much to pinch off. It doesn’t need to go all over the face (does anyone get Botox injections all over🤔) so for me I pinched off (again turn upside down with a light shake while from finger tip holds the cap, soooooo easy!) but I use About the size of pea or just a bit bigger..I spot treat the obvious areas and focus most on my forhead and the remaining amount I ever don lightly, in upward strokes on my face, temple, and jaw .. if you find that amount isn’t enough for you then test a few days till you get it right and any excess (of ANY TREATMENT CREAMS OR SERUMS, always wipe the back oh your hands. “Win-Win”.... and for the opening, again I have No idea the issue (likely because I worked In the industry. Any of our injectable meds came vacuum sealed with a metal lip lock we’d call it. But there are trail tracks on the top of the metal. I use Pair of flat tweezers, find the dotted line. Insert twezers there going on the top of the lid pushing outwards (holding jar tight) until it breaks the seal. Super simple. Once broken it easily peels off. And as I mentIroned earlier, I’m. Product junkie and never ever had any work done but all for it when the time comes, but our best complexion does NOT come in s bottle but rather in food. I’m 46 and these are recent pics of my youngest daughter & I. I have 3 kids (15, 21 & 28) and I’m a grandmother :). If you want good skin, won’t bore you with my full routine. But  few “Must haves” for me are Obviously a great Face wash. ALWAYS Toner, followed by an Essence. Essence is so important/ it’s like the sponge to really pull all our products in. (Exfoliate 2-3 times a week) and I lighty dry brush every morning before I wash-  I could Talk for an hour on that subject! But you need dry clean skin so doing it at night. You have makeup buildup. Grime, gunk... so you don’t dry brush that. If you don’t wear any makeup.. then it’s up to you. I love My little face & neck dry brush. Up and outward. 8/10’light strokes. Then go on to your routine. Say bye bye to dead skin cells and your face received much more of the products you’re applying.  So Dry brush, Wash, Tone & Essence. And then I spot apply “Botulinum”... Once dry (if in. Hurry. Use a  blowdryer on cool. Dries each layer in a zap!) and I use a few more but my staples are Always the Vitamin C. There is a great inexpensive one I found. Literally changed my skin for the better. And a Good HA & Peptiide. You can find many products that have peptides in HA or HA in peptides. I prefer layering. So it’s super easy once you understand. The dropper will not pour out, nor would you want to keep opening the lid) hope this helps. and don’t forget your sunscreen ☺️

reviews.title: No Results Unfortunately
reviews.text: The bottle is not user friendly and the instructions, oh yeah, what instruction?  If there are instructions they are written in Chinese.  I don’t see one small change in my skin.  It didn’t break me out nor did I get a rash or great feel.  Literally I have no clue what I put on my face Morning and Night for two months.  I do know my make didn’t want to stay and I use a very good foundation.

reviews.title: Don’t know how to use it.
reviews.text: No instructions. Only some writing in Japanese.

reviews.title: Don’t waste your money
reviews.text:
I don’t see any results from this produc!
And you have to find a way to get the cream out of the bottle! Awful! Don’t waste your money,since March 2018 until today August 2..don’t see any difference!

reviews.title: no more smile lines
reviews.text: I have spent a lot of money for products that say they plump skin, and do this or that and I was always disappointed.  I gave this a try because of the ingredients,  Opening this was no problem at all. I have been using it faithfully 2 x's a day.  It has to be about 3 to four weeks.  After the first week I saw results.  I was amazed.  I have really deep nasolabial folds and I spent hundreds of dollars for shots to fill them. After using this in the first week I saw a huge  improvement. I have one side deeper than the other and I saw improvement in both sides but a little more on the side that was  less deep.  Now that side is completely filled in.  The other side is catching up.  I would recommend it HIGHLY.  I love the fact I can save tons of money and still have the effects of the injections.  This product is a God send.

reviews.title: It works
reviews.text:
It really does work!!

I thought for sure it wouldn't as it was really cheap however this is not the case.

I could see results in one week.

reviews.title: It works
reviews.text:
This product definitelly has something in it that works similar to Botox. I have had Dysport (Botox) injected in my forehead for migraines and this product gives similar effects. After applying the gel, I can’t move my forehead as easily as before. The effects are temporary and it has helped reduce the pain from the headaches. I’m currently using more of it because the effects of Dysport are wearing off (injected 2 months ago).
As far as the complaint about the bottle it comes in, you have to pry off the top (typical injection lid found on injectables), remove it and then put the replacement top on it. It wasn’t difficult. I have prescription for injectable b12 shots and every injectable I’ve seen has this same lid. They are meant for syringes with needles. I’ve also seen this in bottles for medicines for pets in which a syringe (w/o a needle) is used for the dosage.
In essence, I think it works, temporarily at least. I haven’t been using it much longer than 2 months so I can’t say what the long term effects are. However, even the injectables are predicted to last about 3 months. My last one lasted a good 1 month before the effects began to lessen.

reviews.title: PRODUCT IS Amazing lady, i have spend lotS OF money on ...
reviews.text: PRODUCT IS Amazing lady, i have spend lotS OF money on productS. This product seem scary but it works...purchasing more. ENJOY CHICAS

reviews.title: Good Product
reviews.text: I believe if you've maintained your face well throughout the years and you just want to continue the maintenance this product will work for you. However, if you have hardly ever or never taken care of your face and you want miracles to happen, this product will most likely not work for you. Remember living healthy plays a big role.

reviews.text: i didnt notice any difference and had a few people try it too with no resuts

reviews.title: Like somebody says if you like to try different products ...
reviews.text: Actually the bottle does open without any problem whatsoever, the only thing is there is no way to find out what ingredients. Like somebody says if you like to try different products go for it loool.... Let's see how it works and keep you post it so far I applied twice

reviews.title: this works
reviews.text: leaves no residue ..perfect..packaging no problem at all..lovely, thank you

reviews.title: Really worked well!
reviews.text: Erased the deep lines between my eyes and erased little wrinkles from chest

reviews.title: This stuff is AMAZING.
reviews.text: Wow!!! This stuff is AMAZING.

reviews.title: Accurate description and price
reviews.text: Nice product

reviews.title: Smells Great and Works Great on your Face!
reviews.text: This product is the first cleansing oil I've used on my face. I'm really enjoying it. It easily removes makeup and leaves me with a soft face and no break-outs after removing my make-up. My nineteen year old son, JUST came up to me and gave me a hug good-bye and commented on how soft my face was. True story! This hasn't happened before, and the only difference to my cleansing regimen has been this cleansing oil. So far I'm loving it. It easily and smoothly goes on my face and removes my make-up. The cleansing oil smells nice, it's using light, nice smelling oils and feels good on my face. I highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: I have been using a cleansing oil as step one in a double cleanse routine for 20 years now. Back then there was only one company from Japan that made such a thing that was available in the US. So for years, that is what I used. I decided to try this product simply to try something new. It has the same consistency as my normal cleansing oil, but didn't seem to lather or clean as well. It also left my skin feeling oily despite how much a rinsed it away.  It has a fresh, citrus scent and the bottle size it a tad bit bigger than my normal. That being said, I liked this product but probably would not purchase to use again. The cost is higher and I can't get past the fact it doesn't clean off makeup that well.

reviews.title: A little goes a long way.
reviews.text: This cleansing oil has an almost citrus smell so I thought it might be too much for my sensitive skin.  I cannot use most products.  I have not had a problem with this one  One squirt is more than enough to remove even waterproof mascara along with cleansing your entire face.  I am 64 and this leaves my skin silky smooth and clean.  I massage the oil into my skin, add a bit of water and continue to massage it into my skin until becomes white and begins to break down.  You will feel it change as you go, then rinse it off with warm water.  I am very happy to find this product.  It seems that cleansing oils are fading away after being the rage for awhile.  I wish I had known about cleansing oil all my adult life.  I will continue using A24 Cleansing Oil cleansing oil.

reviews.title: Doesn't leave my skin feeling oily
reviews.text:
I have another brand like this and love it. I've used this one a couple of times and it's just like my other one. I use a jade gua sha stone with it. Doesn't leave my skin feeling oily. Nice sunflower scent. Lathers up with warm water. Will definitely keep using it.
I like that the pump can be closed so there are no accidental spills.

reviews.title: This Makeup Remover Really Works!
reviews.text:
This A24 Cleansing Oil really works! I usually use baby oil to remove makeup, but this A24 Cleansing Oil
was even more effective! Like the baby oil, it is hypoallergenic. However, this cleansing oil does not contain mineral oil, a petroleum product. Instead, it contains USDA-approved organic Aloe Vera leaf juice. Camellia Japonica Seed Oil, Shea, Safflower oil, jojoba oil, avocado oil, Argan oil, Macadamia seed oil, olive oil, marigold, green tea, chamomile, horsetail, acai, lavender, ginseng, rosemary, sage, comfrey, nettle, quinoa, orange peel oil and grapefruit peel oil. All good things! And no water is added.

This wonderfully effective oil is also great because of the ingredients it does not contain! There are no parabens, silicones, phthalates, alcohol or artificial colors or fragrances.

This awesome makeup remover is made in the United States.

reviews.title: Great cleanser
reviews.text: This is a gentle cleanser for all face types. I have oily skin and I can use this product without it making my face oiler. It feels like it really cleans my skin as though it’s really getting into the pores. I don’t have to wash my face twice to remove all dirt from it.

reviews.title: Softens skin, it's not greasy.
reviews.text: I am using this because of the deep cleaning claim and not to remove makeup. I live in the mountains where it is windy and dry, and I have mild eczema so I am always battling dry skin. I really had my doubts that an oil based product could clean my skin... well, I just discovered that yes it can! I am probably not using it as directed, as I like to wet wash my face while I am in the shower. Standing over my bathroom sink just makes too much of a mess. When it first lathers up it sort of has a smell like citronella, which I am not fond of, but I found that smell to quickly dissipate, and end up smelling quite nice and fresh, and not greasy. My skin feels soft and clean after using this product. It it was a larger bottle i might use it up on the rest of my dry skin body, if it wasn't so costly! (Sorry, TMI!)

reviews.title: Mild yet powerful cleansing oil
reviews.text:
This is what I use as the first step to my night time skin care routine. It is a gentle yet powerful cleansing oil that effectively removes layers of sunscreen, waterproof mascara, liquid eyeliner, liquid glitter eyeshadow, and eyebrow pomade at once. I haven’t tried this with liquid foundation yet, so I cannot say much about it’s ability to remove a full-face make-up.

What I love about it is the texture. It is not a watery nor a heavy type of cleansing oil. It is rather lightweight that easily glides onto the skin for a gentle massage. Two pumps of this cleansing oil is enough to cleanse my entire face thoroughly without leaving any traces of waterproof make-up and sunscreen. It also doesn’t leave any greasy or sticky residue.

On its own, it is a hydrating and refreshing cleanser. I have an oily skin, so I love how I feel very refreshed after using this; it helps remove excess oil and unclog my pores. I noticed that my nose where I have the most blackheads is clearer than before.

Rather than using cleansing wipes, I have always used cleansing oils and balms because these truly remove make-up and sunscreen. I also love that cleansing oils and balms are environmentally-friendly without leaving any wastes. However, not all cleansing oils and balms are created equal. I feel that this A24 cleansing oil is very luxurious due to its premium ingredients. It is the only cleansing oil that I have tried that can be used on its own without a second or follow-up foam/gel cleanser.

I strongly recommend this product to anyone in need of a cleansing oil for hydration and thorough removal of make-up and sunscreen.

reviews.title: Highly effective
reviews.text: My wife says this makeup remover is one of the most effective she's used. Her initial impression was that it might be something that she wouldn't want to continue using because it's extremely oily. She was worried it would leave an oily residue on her skin after using it. However, despite going on very oily, it leaves no nasty residue and washes away quickly and easily, taking makeup off with it. She says her skin feels pleasant and cleansed after using this, and it's far less harsh than many chemical makeup removers.

reviews.title: Super oily
reviews.text: This makeup remover is really oily. Leaves my face feeling really oily. Also, it does not remove waterproof eye makeup well at all.

reviews.title: Effective but Messy
reviews.text: I currently use another oil-based cleanser for my face so I was excited to try this one.  It sounds funny to say an oil cleans without leaving your skin greasy but it does just that.  In comparison to the other, this one cleans just as well, however, I don't like that I have to lather the oil to make it work.  It causes too much of a mess when cleaning my face at the sink.  I will have to use this cleanser exclusively in the shower.  I will say, there's a bit of confusion in the instructions.  It says to clean makeup around eyes but then it says to keep away from eyes?  Other reviewers said they used it to remove eye makeup so I gave it a try.  I did not experience any stinging or irritation.

reviews.title: Very mild and worked out great for my oily sensitive skin.
reviews.text:
I requested this cleansing oil due to its claim on natural ingredients (99.75%).
First of all, I have an oily combination skin type that gets sensitive depending on my hormonal cycle. So naturally I have been hesitant to use any oil type skin care products on my face.
But I have learned that using an oily skin cleanser will not produce more oil on your skin, instead, a good oil cleanser is designed to balance and remove everything but skin's moisture.
So I have been searching for one to try out. Since this one is my first cleansing oil, I don’t have any experience with other cleansing oils to compare this with. So my impression about this is as a general cleansing product whether oil or otherwise.
The bottom line is that I liked how it worked on removing make-ups and how my skin felt afterwards. It didn’t feel heavy on my oily skin and felt soft and clean after washing my face following this.

reviews.title: Great two-in-one makeup remover & cleanser
reviews.text: I love this cleanser. It’s a great one-stop-shop to remove makeup and wash my face. The oil removes liquid eyeliner and mascara, along with foundation. It leaves my skin feeling clean and fresh—and the best part is I don’t need a second cleanser to remove that oily makeup remover feeling I get with other products. The smell is mild, and though I have sensitive skin, this oil to foam cleanser is safe and gentle.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: I bought this to replace a different oil cleanser that went out of stock and I’m really happy with the product. It removes eye makeup, including mascara, and my foundation. It has a refreshing citrus scent that’s mild and not overwhelming. I like that it only uses clean, natural ingredients. I have sensitive skin and this felt nice on my skin—and didn’t cause a reaction like so many products out there do.

reviews.title: Too Expensive? Get the Refill Pack!
reviews.text:
I'm a sucker for beautiful packaging.
Here we have a gorgeous tea canister that is well made and beautiful. It also closes tightly, keeping my tea fresh. A good tea canister is not cheap, so I didn't have sticker shock with this product.

Thankfully, the company thoughtfully puts out refill packs of this delicious and calming tea.
I'll definitely be ordering a refill because not only do I like it myself, but after going through so many different calming and sleep inducing teas, this is my spouse's absolute favorite. Not just because of the taste, but it helps her to totally relax and even sleep throughout the night. Other teas and sleep formulations such as melatonin and valerian supplements just don't do the trick.
For me, it's more about the delightful taste. Slightly floral, slightly sweet. Yes, it does relax me and is helpful as part of a nighttime ritual, but not as much as my spouse. We both love it but for different reasons.

And I just adore the look of the canister. A lovely gloss ceramic, with an Art Deco nod, that fits in any decor. No hiding it, it's proudly on the counter, ready for anytime you need a little tea comfort.

reviews.title: Mild effectiveness
reviews.text:
This made me slightly more sleepy (using 2 bags per). Not a huge difference. My melatonin pills are still my go-to when it is time to get some shuteye. I probably won't get more when I'm done because these did not taste good at all.

The jar they came in, however, is quite awesome!! I totally intend to reuse it somehow when I'm done with this. Sadly, it is probably the best part of this.

reviews.title: The best thing about this is the jar it comes in
reviews.text:
If you look at the photo attached you’ll see that the package has a spot that reads “Don’t be put off by the pungent odour! It’s just the strength of the Valerian Root. (It disappears when in hot water).”

I believed that message. I should not have believed that message. It smelled like old gym socks left in a locker for a year and, while the taste was not as bad as the odor (or odour, if you’re Australian) it wasn’t very pleasant either.

As a food scientist/chef I always look back at my mentor telling me “It ain’t nutrition until it passes the lips”. It doesn’t matter how healthy something is, if someone won’t eat it or drink it, it can’t do them any good. Taste matters.

Taste is subjective and you may not be offended by the taste, I was.
As for its effectiveness, I can’t really say. There is literature that backs up to some extent, the claims made and it very well may help calm you or help you get to sleep. Then again, the same can be said for a cup of warm decaffeinated tea. Perhaps this is better than that but I’m not drinking it.

The packaging is very nice, it would make a nice gift for someone who is into this type of product. The painted glass jar is nice as well (the cap is plastic).

For what this costs, they should have at least used better tea bags; asking that the bags have strings w/ tabs on them, or that they are made of a higher-grade paper, shouldn’t be too much to ask when a teabag costs more than $1.00 per.

Again, the taste is subjective, you may like the flavor or, at least, not find it offensive as I did.

Review of: WelleCo - Sleep Welle Natural Calming Tea Caddy

reviews.title: Beautiful (unnecessary?) packaging, ok taste
reviews.text: I was surprised at the packaging when this tea arrived.  It's pretty elegant, especially for tea.  I would expect a high-end candle to look like this, not a canister of tea!  It's beautiful, but again, for tea.  The box is nice with a soft, thick elastic band to keep the top on.  The jar is a light purple glass with a plastic lid.  They did slap a warning about the horrific smell when you open the cap.  It's pretty bad and strong.  However, I bravely pushed on and made a cup.  It doesn't really smell that bad once it's steeped.  I steep it in 195 degree water for 7 minutes.  It does seem to take the edge off, but the flavor is really just okay.  At $68 (currently) for 50 tea bags, I think the packaging must be really expensive.  I usually drink Yogi Bedtime Tea because it's readily available at the grocery store and I love the way it smells and tastes.  While I think this WelleCo tea seems to be more effective, I really would prefer to just drink a cup of nice-tasting tea before bed.

reviews.title: Sleepy tea
reviews.text: I like the taste of the tea and it does make you feel very calm.

reviews.title: Most Of The Effort Was Put Into The Packaging
reviews.text:
The Sleep Welle Natural Calming Tea by WelleCo put a lot of effort into the presentation to reflect a high end product. The price also reflects that at $68.50 for 50 tea bags. The packaging was nice, but I didn’t think it was THAT nice. The canister is unique, but I expected something heavier and more substantial for the price.

The flavor of the tea is nice, but doesn’t seem anything particularly special. I’m having to use two tea bags for an 8 oz. cup for it to be strong enough to really taste. I had some sweetener and it is a pleasant cup of tea. I don’t feel like it helps me sleep, but I make it the afternoons for a relaxing break. But having to use two tea bags makes the pricing even more ridiculous, since I will only get 25 cups of tea from it (not even a whole month’s worth at 1 cup/day).

A word of advice: don’t give up on it when you open the container and smell it. It smelled not so pleasant to me. But I was game, and still gave it a try. It definitely smells a whole lot better than it smells! It also smells much better once it’s brewed.

Honestly, I can’t find anything to justify the cost. It does taste nice brewing it with two bags. I’m just not living the lifestyle of spending over $100 for a month’s worth of tea.

reviews.title: Don’t smell before you brew it!
reviews.text:
I’m a huge tea drinker and was looking for something different to drink before bed instead of my usual chamomile blend. I had heard of this Welle co brand before, but had never personally tried it prior to this. So I was really excited to give it a go.

First impressions, the box it comes in is super luxe looking and very giftable. And the container that holds the tea bags is a very pretty purple color and is glass, giving you that premium look. However once I opened the container I was highly disappointed. They didn’t even splurge on the pyramid tea bags, they went with those cheap pouches. For this price point I expected much better if I’m being honest!

Scent wise this tea smells like sweaty socks, it is not a pleasant smell and I highly recommend not even smelling it before brewing because it can easily put you off to drinking it. But once brewing I was surprised that the scent was almost fruity, so much better than in the container! The directions say to steep one to two bags for 2-10 minutes, in very hot but not boiling water. I usually like stronger tea, but with this I just can’t justify using 2 tea bags at that price point!

Taste wise it actually has a very mild taste, and none of that sticky socks smell or taste at all. It’s very mild tasting with a slightly fruity/berry taste to it. Is it amazing? No, but it’s not bad. It’s definitely an acquired taste! I will say though that I have been sleeping like a baby the last few nights I have drank this, so even though it’s not my favorite tea it does seam to work, at least for me.

reviews.title: Ridiculously overpriced but tastes good
reviews.text: The caddy is heavy and gorgeous(I have a thing for packaging and will definitely reuse this). The tea itself is good, has a very fruity with a slight bitter flavor, but more of a daytime tea for me to relax and enjoy rather than a nighttime tea before bed(Anima Mundi Herbals has a Lucid Dream Tea that is way more effective to help me sleep). This didn’t help me sleep and it’s ridiculously overpriced when you can get tea with basically the same ingredients for less than $10 elsewhere. I’m giving three stars just because the caddy is gorgeous,reusable, and the tea is yummy if you like tropical fruity teas.

reviews.title: So Calming & Soothing
reviews.text: I really love drinking this tea to calm down after a day of hard work or just before bed. This tea comes in a very nice, very well made, reusable glass container. I love how the container really holds in each tea bags flavor compared to each individually wrapped in paper packets or in a paper box. I will definitely continue to use this glass container to store all of my future teas in as well as it is so pretty! I am really in love with this calming, soothing and subtle tasting tea. I do not prefer to add sugar, honey, milk, etc to my teas. I think this tea is perfect as is and will definitely continue to drink this to calm my nerves at the end of the night.

reviews.title: So good
reviews.text: Really helps me relax and sleep peacefully. Better than most on the market I’ve tried!

reviews.title: Great product
reviews.text: Definitely has helped in calming at the end of the night allowing for an easier night sleep.

reviews.title: Tasty tea but needs improvement
reviews.text: The tea is calming and very tasty if you can get over the pungent smell that comes from the tea bags before infusion. The smell is not a problem now for me but it took time to get used to it. The bottle is pricey. I would recommend just buying the tea bags and storing in a tea canister. For the price of the tea bags I would real natural ingredients instead of added flavors. I have tried a few calming teas but I find the taste of this tea to surpass all the other teas.

reviews.title: My favorite roller shape
reviews.text: I have a lot of facial rollers. The Y shape is my favorite. The gentle suction massage is very relaxing. This one has a nice weight and feels well made. It rolls smoothly and silently. It came in a velvet pouch in a sealed bubble bag. It's competitively priced right now at $13.99 plus an extra 40% off.

reviews.title: Great Skincare Tool
reviews.text: This is definitely good quality! The roller itself has a cooling effect when applied on the skin, even without any skin care product. Instructions are easy to read/follow.

reviews.title: Face massager
reviews.text: This is a relaxing tool. It feels nice going across the cheeks. It glides easily. It is also pretty.

reviews.title: Wow, I am impressed!
reviews.text: I have a bigger version of this tool for the body that is also silver/chrome but it is not well made and looks shoddy compared to this because it is probably not metal and made of plastic.  I didn't have any expectations for this but was so pleasantly surprised by how nice it is.  It looks very fancy and feels really good in my hand and on my face.  It's easy to roll around on my face and jawline without having to put face oil.  I have Gua Sha tool with a roller at the end that costs 3x this and they're both in the same caliber imo.  Get this, you will love it! And at this low price of $14, get one for your friend!

reviews.title: Feels good. Heavy duty construction. Not sure of any health claims.
reviews.text: I don't know about the validity of any of the health claims. The Massager is solidly constructed and does feel good when run on my face and arms. You need to be careful and let the massages roll without putting excessive downward pressure. If you push too hard, it can leave your face feeling uncomfortable and even in some pain. Lite pressure and my face and arms feel great. I will use this to give a massage on my face and arms.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text: This is a beautiful face roller. It feels good in the hands and is definitely a high quality beauty tool.

reviews.title: Nice, but what kind of metal?
reviews.text: The quality is clearly visible in the construction and the extremely smooth rolling mechanism. There’s a nice weighty feel to this as well that adds a little something extra to the whole massage or lymphatic draining experience. Honestly I’m really happy with this and have been enjoying using it. My only complaint is the lack of transparency regarding the material, which is only described as “Metal” both online and in the provided pamphlet. For anyone who has metal sensitivities or a nickel allergy - I’d suggest being cautious about using this and maybe starting with a patch test on a small portion of your body before diving into using it on your face.

reviews.title: Well engineered face massager
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:c94a2c2720b868dbd87475026003c182]] This is a review of " YUNRUO Face Roller Face Massager, Metal Facial Roller, Face Lift Beauty Roller for Neck Eye Body Skin Care”
[As a vine reviewer, I am pledging not to be compensated or influenced by the vendor; I state my opinions based on my personal experience without any bias or pressure]
[I am not a doctor and therefore the review below should be viewed against this disclaimer]

In the interest of full disclosure, I should start by stating that I had never tried any face roller until a few weeks back . So clearly it was with a bit of skepticism that I tried this product, primarily because the looks appealed to me .

This face massager is made with a steel Y- shaped stem and two little oval plastic balls with geometrical patterns inside. The idea is to run this massager on your face, a bit like a razor, and the moving movement is expected to shape your face. For someone who is often teased for a double chin I think this product may work well. The most interesting thing about this product is how well made it really is. The two balls do I have a grabbing kind of effect and if you run it along your jaw lines, it does kill the impression off compressing the facial muscle. Like all such products, the efficacy cannot be ascertained until you use it for some time. There is even a well-illustrated pamphlet that comes with a massager in a nice stain carrying pouch.
But just going by this ergonomically designed massager, I am willing to give it four stars for now. A couple of words of caution: the steel stem is probably rust proof, but I would avoid moisture. Similarly, the balls are made of plastic, although they seem reasonably study, I would still urge a bit of care. But on the whole, this has been a very fun and satisfying experience so far using this product.

reviews.title: relaxing and smooth
reviews.text: very relaxing face massager , the metal roller is smooth and provide nice massage to face skin, also very nice on the neck, it come is small bag for carrying

reviews.title: works well
reviews.text: This product is well made and designed, made from the solid steel, and the surface is well polished, the handle is comfortable to hold. The rollers are small and have no issue to roll over my face and neck, and around my hands and arms as well. I can use it while I am doing my job. Comparing with other rollers, this rollers have a central toward pushing/pulling force depending the rolling direction, which provides an extra comfort when moving it over the skin. It works well on the small muscles. I like the feeling while the rollers move over my skins, and my muscles had a nice relaxation after the operation. Definitely, like it.

reviews.title: Feels Sooooo Good!
reviews.text:
I think I am in love with this face massager. It feels so good and slightly pulls the skin in between the two rolling balls, but in a good way. The rolling balls look like little disco balls and the entire roller is made of sturdy materials.

I have been wanting a facial roller because I heard that it's good to help drain your lymphatic ducts and stimulate blood flow in your face. But the added sensation of your face being gently sucked on (I know, that doesn't sound good but trust me!) is AMAZING.

For the price, this thing is a steal.

reviews.title: Good 3D rollers!
reviews.text: These metal rollers have a 3-D design and this give a good kneading affect. These are of course, waterproof as they are made of good quality metal. A very easy and effective option to feel my face firmer.

reviews.title: Perfect.
reviews.text: I like how it feels on my face. It well give you the best message for your face while sitting in your home watching TV. Easy to use and easy to clean . If you want it a little colder, just toss it in the freezer for couple minutes. Love it.

reviews.title: My favorite facial tool
reviews.text: I love how relaxing this is for your face. Easy to use around the jaw line & eye area. The quality is great for the price.

reviews.title: Great face massager
reviews.text: You can use this face massager with the lotion or facial serum of your choice. It does a great job distributing the serum on your face and massaging it in, the rollers are cool and soothing. Even my boyfriend likes to use it!

reviews.title: Title and description are a little misleading...
reviews.text: The wife and the MIL are huge fans of these face rollers, so we've tried just about every version and model out there. Although this does have a nice heavy, metal handle, the balls are PLASTIC. Stating in the title that it's a "Metal Facial Roller" is misleading because there are models where everything is indeed metal. In the description, it also says "our facial roller tool is High quality metal material". Ummmm... that's JUST THE HANDLE. The models we've tried with plastic balls had the fake metal paint water off, and some of them even crack after a while, so this is important in determining quality of the tool and the price that it should command. Most of the ones with plastic are priced around $8-10, while the all metal ones are $24-30. That being said, the price of this one might seem like a good deal if you think it's an all metal model - but it is actually overpriced for the plastic-ball-model.

reviews.title: Nice weight to it!
reviews.text: I’d ordered a cheaper roller in the past and was disappointed by how light and flimsy it was, so I was pleased with the weight of this model. It feels substantial and well-made. There’s a bit of a learning curve to using it that has to do with the angle at which you hold the handle as you roll in each direction. The included instructions were a bit sparse but gave me an idea, at least. I haven’t seen significant changes in my skin from using this device, but it feels good, at least!

reviews.title: Terrific Set!
reviews.text:
I am very happy with my new makeup brushes! I have had a few different brands in the past, and normally get the Eco Tools Brand when I need to replace them, which are awesome, but I wanted to try something different and I wanted a case for it like the one with this set. These ones are super soft and I can already tell that they won't do what cheap ones do, which is fall apart fast. I've used them and no stray hairs come out like cheap ones do. The blender sponge feels like a great quality! They are all marked for what they are meant for which is super helpful. My one complaint is that it doesn't come with a blush brush. You can use the powder brush for blush obviously, but unless you have a spare or don't use powder at all, then you have a problem. Other than that, "they're great!" (Spoken like Tony the Tiger). ;-)

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

reviews.title: Super Nice Set
reviews.text:
I’m a sucker for a good brush set. It’s even better when it comes with case and a sponge!

So the case is very sturdy and feels well made. You can use it as a brush holder and you can use it store brushes. I am using the shorter half as a brush holder and find that it blends in nicely with my other holders.

Now I’ll do a brief breakdown of the brushes and sponge.

The lip brush is great and it blends in my lipsticks really nice.

There are three brushes for eyes- eyeshadow brush, blending brush and angled liner. I have tried these and they are very soft and work very well.

The powder brush is a bit smaller than I would personally like for powder. It’s not quite dense but in a brush that is used for powder products that’s okay. When I’m buffing in the powder I don’t want the brush to be too dense or it will move the foundation around. I’ve been using this brush for blush because of it’s smaller shape and it’s working great.

Now the final two- the foundation brush and the sponge. I decided to do half of my foundation with the brush on one side, and the other half with the sponge on the other side.

The foundation brush is a typical brush of it’s kind. It did not make the foundation look streaky and applied very smooth.

The sponge is the most interesting piece in this set. It doesn’t feel super soft like a Beauty Blender or a Real Techniques sponge. It feels like a softer stress ball and feels a bit rubbery. I’ve never had a sponge like this. I soaked the sponge in water and drained the excess as I would do with any sponge. It felt heavier but did seem to soften a bit and did expand in size. I dabbed the foundation on my face and pressed the sponge around to press the product in and I got a really smooth finish. The sponge did not seem to absorb much product.

Overall I think it’s a very nice set!

I purchased this product at a discount in exchange for an honest review. I did not get paid to do this review and my opinions are 100% honest.

reviews.title: Absolutely love this kit
reviews.text: I can not tell you just how wonderful of brushes these are! This set is absolutely magnificent! The leather carrying case on it, is a thick beautiful leather. The brushes are all very nice quality, and the blending sponge is just perfect. So far I have not found ONE thing that I dislike about this kit. The quality of this kit took me completely by surprise. 15$ is well worth this. I love how soft the brushes are! They do not lose strands like cheap brushes you buy from department stores. As long as you keep them clean regularly, I can see these brushes outlasting almost all of my expensive brushes. id definitely recommend these! They would be perfect presents for such a good price!

reviews.title: Very soft
reviews.text: Purchased for my 18 year old niece.  She loved them.  Said they are very soft.

reviews.title: This is other of my favorite brushes I've ever bought
reviews.text:
This is other of my favorite brushes I've ever bought. It is well made. Yes it has a chemical odor (it actually smells like new car and it is because of the PU leather) but it is a manageable odor that fades away fast once you air it. The brushes are well made, and their flatness and puffiness are perfect. The bristles feel well attached and well glued and they are really soft, even when they are synthetic ones, which is good because it is cruelty free. The sponge is just perfect!! Excellent quality and covers up perfectly. The case is absolutely nice, pretty, and handy, so you can have it just to hold your brushes or to hang them around. I definitely love this case, brushes, and sponge in an awesome price.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest review.

reviews.title: Fantastic, high quality set of bushes...
reviews.text:
These are at the top of my list of favorite set of makeup brushes! This set includes 6 brushes, a black beauty blender sponge, and a black holder/travel case. The brush are synthetic, meaning they are great for either liquid, creams, or powders.
Brushes:
Powder/Blush brush - rounded
Foundation brush - best for concealers and under eyes
Eye Shadow brush - for eyelids
Blending brush - blending darker colors in crease/outer
Angled Liner brush - great for eyebrows and lining eyes
Lip brush - also great for inner eye highlighting
* I love that they are labeled as well on the brush handles. The metal ferrules are securely and firmly attached to the handles. During washing each one prior to using (***highly recommended for any brushes before using - wash first***), but not one brisel has shed yet. Since they are synthetic brushes, they are in fact 100% vegan and 100% cruelty-free. Any animal hair type brushes made are primarily used for powders only. Majority of brushes being made now are mostly made with synthetic brisels. If you ask me, they really look a lot like the Morphe brushes that are actually made by the Crown company! ;)
Beauty sponge:
Latex free, but has a more dense, smooth (like suede) texture and feel.
Little tip:
I have found that it works really great for setting the powder under my eyes. Dampen it and squeeze out with a paper towel, and dap into an illuminating powder and dab/press under eyes to set. It will reduce crinkles and cakiness under eyes.
The holder is PU leather, very sturdy and great for holding brushes and/or traveling.
I have to mention that I did receive this set at a discounted price from the seller for me to try out and leave my own honest review. With that said, I would definitely recommend this brush set to anyone, especially at the sell price it is at while writing this review. As soon as I can I will update with a video review for you. I hope my review helps you, please feel free to contact me if you have any questions and if my review did help you, would you let me know by clicking YES below for me, I would greatly appreciate it!

Also inspected the collar to see how well it was mounted and if it would hold up to wear and tear. Only time will tell but they seem to be really well made.

reviews.text: I like the foundation brush. Before I am just using my finger when applying foundation, and now since I like this product this is my everyday partner for my foundation.

reviews.title: I'm so happy to have it in my collection
reviews.text:
High quality, and so soft!!

reviews.title: Buy this now!!!!
reviews.text: For years i was the typical guy that had some hair on my back. Every couple days i would twist and turn in an attempt to rid my back of what hair i could with a razor and my arm. Needless to say i got pretty good at it but often left spots that i had missed and took a long time in doing so. Along came this little beauty and my world lit up. The construction as others have said seems very flimsy but it holds up well. the angle with a razor attached is perfect and what took me 30 minutes now takes me less then 10. Its a great tool indeed. I would highly recommend this to anyone with some back hair they need to get rid of.

reviews.title: Ok...but could be better
reviews.text: The Razorba works alright.  I have a few issues though:  1.  If you are shaving your back with a full crop of hair, it isnt going to work very well, just like trying to shave your face or wherever without trimming things down first. If you are really hairy you will need to either nair anyway or be very very very patient with this thing as it will only shave about a square inch of long hair on its own.  Seems like it will be good to use if your hair is very short though.  2. The plastic slot that you put the razor in could be designed better. It is basically a softer plastic tube that grips the handle of a normal disposable razor inside the harder plastic body of the Razorba.  This could be improved by putting some sort of screw clamp on the side so your disposable couldnt move.  I think this could be done without increasing the cost much since I suspect they make a pretty huge profit margin on these things.  The problem with the razor not being held securely is that it can turn slightly in the plastic sleave and cut you, this doesnt really hurt but if you are a bleeder its hard to get it to stop.  Another thing that could be improved is to make a hole in the end of the head of the thing opposite where you put the razor for water to flow out of.  After you rinse off the razorblade water collects in the head which when you go to do another stroke flies all over your bathroom, but more seriously makes the razor fit less tightly in the soft plastic tube.  All in all this product does work but their are a few minor annoyance type issues that coule be fixed easily to make it better.

reviews.title: Steve
reviews.text: This product works well if used correctly. I used a mirror and it helps. I found that if you wet your back and add some good shaving cream in small areas works well. When shaving the upper back, push down and then shave upward, dont slide the razor sidways,,,it will cut you. I found that pushing down and then up helps unload the hair as you shave. I also shaved my lower back going from the center of the back to the side worked well. It does take time but it does the job. Back hair grows in different directions. So finding the right direction helps. Trader Joe's sell an excellent shave cream under their Brand name.

reviews.title: It has made my life a lot easier
reviews.text:
I don't like hair on my back, yet I have lots of it. Over the years, I tried shaving, Nair, even had waxing once (never again because of the pain). With the Razorba, it contours well to my back and I usually begin in the shower, and "touch up" in front of the bathroom mirror.

Regarding its quality, I have dropped it many times and it split, but I was able to snap it right back together, and it has held up very well.

I recommend that for back hair, first use electric clippers to remove as much of the "length" of the hairs that you can, as that way, you'll simply have "stubble" to deal with. Shave against the grain, as it lasts a little longer.

reviews.title: very happy with this

reviews.title: Pleasantly Surprised and Pleased
reviews.text:
Okay, I have back hair. Not real thick or matted, but very visible. Black and straight and fairly long, in patches shaped like upside-down Ls across each shoulder and down my outer back.

I've never heard a woman say, "Wow, back hair is hot," so a few years ago I started to do something about it.

Initially I tried duct-taping a razor to a kitchen spatula in order to extend my reach. It was still very difficult to get reach all areas of my back and get GOOD contact and get the hair off. Eventually I would get a good shave, but it would usually take me well over an hour of stretching and straining, and by the time I was done my bathroom looked like it had been the site of a hairy, bloody war.

I first saw an ad for the Mangroomer (also available from Amazon) and tried that, but was disappointed. I thought it would be less brutal on my back than sliding a razor around there, but it didn't cut very close at all. Maybe it would work fine for some people; maybe those with fine light-colored back hair. It gets plenty of positive reviews on Amazon. But it didn't work well for me.

The Razorba worked much better for me. I used it with a Gillette Atra Plus razor blade (two blades plus a lubricating strip, and a pivoting head), aligning the razor as shown on the Razorba website. I got a good shave in MUCH less time than my old spatula, razor and duct tape contraption -- maybe about 15 minutes. It might take you a little longer to get the hang of it -- the fact that I'd had previous "practice" using a much more difficult tool surely helped me. Bottom line, it was a much easier and faster task than I'd expected.

Tips:

- I first wetted myself down in the shower, and applied shaving gel. The gel helps a LOT. Hint: If you can't reach all parts of your back to apply the gel, apply it to the wall of your shower and rub your back against it to get it on.

- For the first pass, I shaved in the shower with the shower off, mostly going over my back by feel. I ran the tub water, and rinsed the razor off frequently.

- Rather than trying to move the razor in one direction only -- stroke, lift, replace, stroke, lift, replace -- I recommend just moving the razor blade up and down without lifting it from your skin. Kinda back and forth, as if you were using it to RUB the hair off. That maintains good contact with your skin the whole time. This is the technique that worked best for ME, anyway. If you keep lifting the blade from the skin and repositioning it, it's harder to keep the angle right.

- After the first pass, I rinsed off in the shower, then took a look in my batroom mirror. I still had big patches about the size of my hand, below each shoulder blade, that had to be reshaved. I applied more shaving gel as described before, and used the "rub up and down" technique described above to get the rest of that hair off while checking myself in the mirror. It was also easier to shave my shoulders with the aid of a bathroom mirror.

- If you are having difficulty getting the hair off with your strokes, you MIGHT try this: Take the razor blade out of the razor, turn it around so that it faces the other way, and put it back in the razor that way. Now you're saving action will be a "pushing" rather than a pulling action. That might help you get the hair, by cutting in a different direction with regard to the direction of your hair growth.

- Be careful and don't push so hard that the razor digs into your skin. This wasn't a real problem for me, as I believe the design of a modern razor blade makes this unlikely. What requires more care: Be careful not to move the razor blade SIDEWAYS across your skin in a slicing motion; you'll cut yourself that way. Do that a lot, and you'll look like you just had sex with Catwoman or something. I got careless and did give myself two raw scrapes that way, the first time. (They were more like scratches than deep cuts.) With more practice, it should be easier to avoid this.

Two main "passes" as I've just described, plus some minor spot clean up in the mirror, were enough to get all the back hair off with a smooth shave. Again, I was pleased to find that the Razorba did a good job rather quickly and was pretty easy to use -- much easier than any other self-shaving technique I'd tried.

How often do you need to reshave? This will vary for you, based on the coarseness of your hair, how fast it grows, how "smooth" you want to keep yourself. I think once a month or two should suffice for me.

Its a lot of steps and not for the faint of heart.  It works for me.  It kept my hair straight for at least 2-3 months and then my curls reverted back slowly.  Will buy again.

reviews.title: Formaldehyde?! Not safe. 2 Stars ⭐
reviews.text:
After going over the ingredients in these products, the only two I would feel even remotely safe using would be this shampoo and conditioner. As a hair stylist, you do not want to use a product that contains formaldehyde and apply heat to it. You certainly do not want to use it every 6 weeks, as it's harmful to your health. It can cause breathing issues among other things. It's very hard to believe that I even saw actual formaldehyde on the back of this product, and so high up in the ingredients list.
Even hair stylist must wear protective gear, such as gloves, aprons, masks, and be trained how to safely work with this hazardous chemical. Salons must have adequate ventilation and follow strict protocols regarding blow dryer temperatures and monitor formaldehyde levels at all times when they provide Keratin Treatments containing formaldehyde because of the immediate and long term health dangers.This is something you cannot possibly do at home.
Also, the pretreatment that you add to the shampoo is extremely strong and stripping to your hair. When they say add just a little, no one knows what that means. That particular product, or the two main ingredients, should only be a small percentage of the product you're adding it to. I don't think you can mix this properly at home without causing damage to your hair and/or your skin.
I "might" use the shampoo and conditioner, but I will not touch the pre-treatment nor will I touch the DIY home keratin treatment. Over the course of a few treatments, I can see this product going really awry for some people. They will end up with damaged hair and possibly other problems. It is not safe. I absolutely would not recommend it!

reviews.title: OK
reviews.text: I tried this on thick curly 3c hair. The results were quite decent. You first wash your hair with the pre-treatment shampoo to clarify the hair and remove dirt and oils. At this point, make sure to detangle the hair well and section it into smaller parts so that you can apply the keratin treatment. The hair should be 80% towel dried. You then comb in the keratin treatment to each section of hair. I recommend wearing gloves. Allow it time to process and then blow dry and finish with a flat iron. The hair came out quite silky and smooth, but it began to revert into fluffiness as the day went on. Adding a bit of hair oil helped to restrain the humidity. For an at home kit, it is decent, but it is not as strong as salon treatments.

reviews.title: Decent Set!
reviews.text:
This set of different Keratin based bottles is pretty nice for the price. It arrived safely and is packaged well. They even included a Carbon Pick Comb that’s great for some thick hair.
I can’t speak on the ingredients that I saw were a concern in another view but, it’s helping us and it makes the hair look good.

reviews.title: Smooth long curls
reviews.text: Keratin’s make the hair smoother (NOT STRAIGHT), shinier, and frizzy free for up to 8 months depending on the strength of the product. Keratin treatments rebound the keratin in your hair so that they are all put back into place. There are a lot of steps but don’t get discouraged because they’re quiet simple. To start of give 2 good shampoos with a clarifying shampoo (specifically the keratin complex one, then towel dry and blow dry the unconditioned hair. Once fully dry, make small sections and apply the treatment from roots to ends and comb through with a fine tooth comb to make sure hair is fully saturated. Let that sit for 30 minutes then blow dry again without washing out the product. Once fully dry the final step will be to straighten the hair with a flatiron so the keratin will set in your hair. After you’re completely done you won’t be able to wash your hair for up to 3 days depending on the strength in the solution.

reviews.title: use in a ventilated space!
reviews.text:
first the elephant in the room - read the ingredients and you will see there is formaldehyde. technically this is terrible but it is somewhat manageable. be in a ventilated space. personally i turn the bathroom fan on while i am using it for the whole process - especially during the flat iron part. i also turn on a regular floor fan pointed into the bathroom, whatever i can do to keep the air moving.

ok - now that we have covered that - the actual product. it's sooooo easy to use. the process is so simple and easy. the directions are super easy to understand and follow and there are pictures included. it's pretty quick - you can get through it all within an hour once you get started on the actual product. just follow the directions.

then for maintenance between treatments there is a shampoo and conditioner provided. these are EXTREMELY thick! like the conditioner is so crazy thick it's almost too difficult to get it out of the bottle. it's a workout. but it's all good to help maintain though.

now - the results. i'm not disappointed at all. i know it's working. it's not a crazy perma-straight situation though. this is NOT salon results. let me repeat that ... NOT salon results. i have super natural curly hair - i can still wash my hair curly. the same amount of curls as i have always had. when i do wear it curly though, i also still have the same amount of frizz. it's sad and true. that said though - it's when wear it straight that the product shows it's worth! the picture i have posted it right after coming after getting caught in the rain. in the past my hair would just revert back to the full on curl, no waves just the game of 'how big is my hair gonna grow?' watch party. this time though ... i ended up with a style that i actually like! something i could easily still get away with wearing for the rest of the day/evening. as rainy and muggy as it has been lately - i can dig this and appreciate it through and through.

so all that said - do i wish it did a bit more for the curly hair frizz ... yes. but for the price and the fact that i will probably get 4 treatments out of this set - i think the system is worth for the investment - cost and time.

reviews.title: Silky Smooth
reviews.text:
I haven't had a hair cut in over 2 years. Its kind of crazy, but between going through IVF treatments, a pregnancy that we were on pins and needles about, and then COVID hitting, I haven't had my hair colored or been inside of a salon since January 2019. Now that I have a 13 month old, finding time by myself to go is nearly impossible.

With that said, my hair is crazy long and healthy. But going through postpartum, I ended up with some hair loss that is now in the process of growing back. I used to love getting Keratin treatments in the salon because it always made my hair feel so good, look super shiny and healthy, and just overall made me feel good. I was anxious to try this at home Keratin system to see if I could get similar results, and much to my surprise, I did! Not only is it easy to do, but my hair looks, and feels so much healthier! It really did wonders on smoothing out some of the frizzy dead ends that need trimmed, and created a sort of gloss look overtop my hair. It brightened up my naturally dark ashy hair color, and was just what I was looking for.

Definitely recommended for anyone with long hair looking to revitalize your long locks!

reviews.title: I love it...
reviews.text: This keratin set really impressed me,I never thought I can see such results with a home keratin treatment.I did it just ones and it’s been almost a month and my hair still looks great.It definitely makes my hair shinier,healthier and smoother,I love this treatment and definitely recommend it to every woman who is wondering to have a good looking hair .

reviews.title: Great travel set
reviews.text:
Very cute brush set! Would be great for a travel set or something to put in your purse for on the go because of its variety and small size. I love the purple "ombré" effect on the brushes and the case is a great match.

The brushes themselves are relatively sturdy, nothing felt loose and I wasn't able to pull them apart. The bristles were soft, and they did a decent job of picking up and delivering powder which is one thing I really look for.

I really liked the case a lot too, not only is it very cute but it also holds all the brushes very well. The only issue you may run into would be a heavy plastic scent which is common with this type of case (it may smell like wet paint or just like chemicals). To fix this all you need to do is open up the case all the way and let it air out for a day or two (this is easy to do when you have the brushes out to wash).

Note I did receive this item at a discounted rate in exchange for my fair and honest review. All opinions expressed are solely my own and are completely honest.

reviews.title: For the price it's such a pretty case. The brushes are meh and not a complete set.
reviews.text:
I have 2 brush kits from this same seller and I'm going to be comparing them while I review them. I'll also be comparing to some other brushes I own. (I’m so sorry I don't have pictures for now, I’m having issues uploading them).

The other brush kit is:

[[ASIN:B011NX9QXE Aisxle 5 Piece Eye Makeup Brush Kit - Beauty and Travel Set - All In One - Soft Bristles and Easy to Carry - Easy to Use - Professional Eyeshadow Brush Makeup Kit - Full Set of Eye Brushes Whenever You Need Them - Perfect for Smokey Eye and More - Satisfaction Guaranteed]]

And to compare I have:

[[ASIN:B0030HMQTS EcoTools 6 Piece Essential Eye Brush Set]]
[[ASIN:B00GNA9K8O EcoTools Mini Essentials Brush Set]]
[[ASIN:B002CL84FI e.l.f. Cosmetics Eye Shadow Brush]]

The 5 piece brush kit (black) comes with the following:

Crease brush
Large flat eyeshadow brush
Small domed eyeshadow brush
Eyeliner brush (or for filling in eyebrows)
Pencil brush / smudger brush (could work as a lip brush too)
Metal tube case

The 7 piece brush kit (purple) comes with the following:
Blush brush
Large flat eyeshadow or concealer brush. Not sure
Small flat eyeshadow brush
Smudger or lip brush
Eyeliner brush
Sponge tip applicator
Eyebrow comb duo
Faux leather soft case

My opinion is that despite having less pieces, the brush set with the tube is the better one. The reason being that each piece has an intended distinct function yet they all work together to create a full eye look. Even compared to my trusty Ecotools I must admit this set is more complete (Ecotools eye set doesn't have an eyeliner brush). The other 7 piece kit has a mix of eye and face brushes but it’s not complete for either. Also, the black brushes from the smaller kit are softer and denser, and overall different than they purple counterparts. I would recommend the small kit as a complete basic eye set. The 7 piece set I would only recommend for the pretty case it comes in. Here’s some individual comparisons (sorry no pictures for now, I will update if I can fix the issue).

Both kits have a larger flat eyeshadow brush, which is good for applying color to the eyelid. The black one from the smaller kit is a little less wide, fluffier and denser. The purple one is very stiff and feels really similar to a small concealer brush instead of an eyeshadow brush. Both these brushes are smaller than the large eyeshadow brush from my Ecotools Eye kit and I appreciate that since the Ecotools covers almost all of my eyelid. They are both larger than the ELF $1 eyeshadow brush. I like that the black one is different from what I already own and will keep using it but the purple is just not good for eyes.

Both kits have a smaller eyeshadow brush. The purple one is flat and stiff just like the larger one, but the black one is really nice. It’s short and rounded top but dense and fluffy, like a mini version of the ELF brush. I like it for adding color to the inner part of the eyelid or creating detailed shapes. It’s also good for the lower lash line. Comparing it to my Ecotools kit it’s closest to the petite eye shading brush but it’s bigger and denser. I like both they are just different. This black brush will give more concentrated color than the less dense Ecotools.

Both kits have a pencil, smudger or lip type brush. The black one is rounder and better at being and eye brush, but the purple is again flatter and stiffer and better at being a lip brush. Comparing the black one to the smudger from Ecotools this one is smaller. I like both.

Both kits have an eyeliner brush. This brush I didn’t like in either set. It’s not as precise as Ecotools (from the mini essentials set) and it has less bristles so it’s a little scratchy.

As for difference the tube kit has a crease brush and the larger set doesn’t. It has a sponge tip, a blush brush and a brow comb instead. I feel like that crease brush is essential for a complete eye look. It’s big and fluffy and good for blending. It’s a little smaller and less bristles but comparable to the Ecotools angled crease brush. On the 7 piece kit you get nothing to blend your eyeshadow, all the eye brushes are stiff. The sponge tip is like a throwaway since you get those for free with any eyeshadow. I would be so happy to see it replaced with the angled brush. The blush brush is just ok. It sheds a little. I’m not a huge blush wearer so I don’t have another blush brush to compare but it’s similar in shape and size to the mini powder brush from the Ecotools mini set.

The case is the only thing that I really like about the 7 piece set. It’s very pretty. I feel like when you get this it’s because of the prettiness factor of the case and not so much the brush quality. Purple is my favorite color. I’m just going to take out the brushes I don’t like and replace them with the ones I do use that fit in it. The black tube case is ok and convenient, just not as pretty looking.

I hope this review was helpful.

Disclaimer: I purchased these 2 kits at a discount in exchange for an honest review.

This particular set is listed at just under $35. I think that might be a bit high for this. But if you are looking for a convenient set that you don’t have to put together yourself, this is a nice set.

I write honest reviews to help others make informed purchases because I read reviews myself and appreciate how helpful they can be. I hope that my review helped you!

reviews.title: Decent enough box of goodies - was expecting more from it, though
reviews.text:
This is cute enough and I am sure if you gave it as a gift a recipient would be appreciative. I have a problem in that NONE of the contents have ingredients listed, though. So, although the soap looks beautiful and fancy – I do not know what is in it or what it is made of. Same goes for the bath bombs – no ingredients whatsoever. (One I like the smell of – the other not so much.) Because of this these items definitely come off dollar store-like. The wine tumbler seems well made and comes with a straw and straw brush. The candle says it is soy wax which is nice but to me it smells terrible. The weird part is that the gift box comes with a plastic wine topper. Now the wine bottle topper “looks” nice but it is 100% plastic – worse than dollar store... It also comes with what looks like a random decorative metal key that serves no function and does not go with the contents at all. Yet it is a bottle top opener – for beer… That is so weird to me and I will be taking this out before gifting this gift set as no wine I know of has a pop top – twist off sure – but beer cap? For the price, they need to truly step it up in regards to the contents - include ingredients and include a better wine stopper.
If you found my review helpful, why not click the “helpful” button. Thanks! Enjoy your day! : )
