reviews.text: This was a very easy to use product. We just followed the directions with damp hair and had beautiful curls. My daughter slept in it for school and the curls lasted through the entire day.

reviews.title: Not as expected
reviews.text:
Too thick add stiff.
The foam is not a good quality and too thick to make the waves last the whole day. I got better results using a pair on knee high socks.

reviews.title: Skip the "rapid" solution.
reviews.text:
Claire's is great for ear piercing!

But they aren't a great source for medical advice, especially when they're trying to up-sell you on magic healing liquid!

You can search "American Academy or Dermatology" or a similar site for proper follow-up advice.

reviews.title: Watered down bleach water!
reviews.text: I did not buy this via amazon but was sold this at Claire’s  as the only option. When using it on my kiddo I could smell the “bleach” smell. I googled the ingredients and sure enough it is watered down bleach. This seems wrong for so many reasons. But basically they are selling you an outrageous bottle of bleach water because it makes them money.

reviews.title: Wish I knew it was bleach
reviews.text: Wish I would have known, but after looking into it we decided to make a solution with distilled water and tea tree oil and pink Himalayan salt. No need to be using bleach on my daughters skin. Returned at Claire's. $12 for bleach. Ridiculous.

reviews.title: Does not work as advertised
reviews.text:
I had my ears pierced at Icing at the local mall. I was told that this solution would have my ears healed in 3 - 4 weeks.
I used the solution faithfully three times each day.
After 4 weeks, I removed the piercing studs and found it very difficult to place new earrings in the holes. They bled and swelled so badly, that I had to wait two weeks for the holes heal and grow closed and have the ears pierced again.
Now I will wait 6 - 8 weeks before removing the new piercing studs.
At $70 to pierce each time (cost of solutions and stainless steel studs) I have lost a lot of money because of a product that does not live up to expectations.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Love this stuff.  My ear was not healing and used it and my ear healed right up.

reviews.title: Bad idea
reviews.text: Claire's upcharged me for this stuff without asking when I had my daughter's ears pierced. After 3 weeks, stupidly, we changed her earrings. Her piercings were NOT healed. I wasn't sure we weren't going to need to repierce them. They bled and scabbed. And she'd been treating them 3 times daily. I didn't realize that the usual wait time is 6 weeks or I would have waited longer. After a traumatic and bloody re-insertion of earrings, we waited another six weeks and then were able to change them without trouble.

reviews.title: Really works, no problem with bleaching clothes
reviews.text: I obtained the product from Claire's after getting my ears pierced.  Love it.  I have found that it also helps other cuts and injuries heal faster as well.  I get skin burns from an allergic reaction and I found that those healed quicker too. I liked it so much I am trying to find a way to purchase it outside of getting ears pierced.  As far as the comments go about it bleaching out clothes.  I have not found this to be the case (at least not where I am concerned).  I was not careful and let it get on my clothes and so far I have had no issues.

reviews.title: Yeah, nah.
reviews.text: I've had many piercings over the years, several AT Claire's. I've never had a problem until this last set of earlobe piercings where the new 3 week after care was all they sold. Immediately, it smells of bleach. I figured this was just a coincidence and used it twice a day just like I did with saline solution for ALL my other body piercings that I've never had a problem with. A week after I had my ears pierced, they still hurt just as bad as the first day. I called the store and they were super helpful and friendly, so this is definitely not their fault. Finally, today, I looked up the product and realized it had bleach in it. I cannot fathom how this is okay to use. Even if it's a SMALL amount, I can't even begin to understand how this is okay to put on a brand new piercing and also somehow heal it in three weeks. I don't necessarily recommend not getting your ears pierced at Claire's because I've never had a problem with it, but don't use this solution. Just buy saline solution, honestly. Using this product on your ears will not heal them in three weeks. I don't know how other people haven't had problems.

reviews.title: It’s not bleach, so stop saying it is
reviews.text: Hey guys, this isn’t bleach. It’s not watered down bleach. It does share ingredients with bleach, but bleach also has many other ingredients. Saying that this product is bleach is like saying that bread is a cake because they share ingredients, it’s just not true. For the record, the main ingredient in both Claire’s ear care solution and bleach (sodium hypochlorite) is also commonly used in swimming pools to clean them. Using this ear care solution is not pouring bleach on an open wound unless you also consider jumping in a pool as bathing in bleach. Also, any piercing specialist would recommend using salt water to clean a new piercing. Sodium hypochlorite (NaClo) is a salt, a salt quite similar to table salt actually (NaCl), so this is basically a salt water solution. Finally, the bleach smell people complain about really isn’t there, this product smells of chlorine, which is in bleach as well as pools as well as many salts. Don’t be fooled by simple chemistry, this product is not bleach.

reviews.title: This is bleach!
reviews.text: Google the ingredients.  This is watered down bleach, people!  It smells like bleach.  The bottle says it can "discolor clothing".  THAT IS BECAUSE IT IS BLEACH!!!!! Saline water is all that is needed to clean a piercing.  So scary that this is sold to us as aftercare.

reviews.title: Whats with the phosphoric acid? Ouch.
reviews.text:
Smells like bleach. Once I found the ingredients I found that it wasn't. My first mistake was getting her ears pierced somewhere other than a piercer that uses needles and not guns. However, I forgot that this was an option since everything closed after covid struck. In any case, I used this once and my daughter said it burned/stung so I did what ive done for my own piercings and used bottled/distilled water and a little bit of salt. I rinsed this stuff out of her ears with my mixture and she immediately expressed that she felt relieved. She also woke up the next morning saying she felt virtually no pain from the new piercings.

Maybe this works for other people but it smells like bleach and I fail to see why there is phosphoric acid in it.

reviews.title: Solution does contain MAIN bleach ingredients
reviews.text: This product does in fact contain the the main ingredient found in bleach- Sodium Hypochlorite. Sodium Hypochlorite is sometimes called ‘liquid bleach’. Claire’s gave this solution to me after I got my ears pierced for the 4th time and while it did not irritate my skin, I chose to just use a saline solution (like the previous times before). It also discolored my shirt but does warn on the bottle this could happen (because it’s bleach)

reviews.title: Claire's Lied
reviews.text: I was specifically told the Rapid After Care Cleanser had Neosporin and Saline Solution, which it is not. It is Bleach and Phosphoric Acid. It has worked really well. We started using it again because after the 3 weeks my daughter bumped her ear pretty good and it got infected so I started using it again. The infection is leaving. The product is working. But it's still bleach! That's what I am so shocked at. They lied. And obviously by all this battling there is a problem with their approach (and for my circumstance dishonesty).

reviews.title: Its different for everyone
reviews.text: Reading others review id say everyones body just heals differently. For me this product really worked well, i got my cartlidge pierced and i just could not get rid of the infection after 2-3 months of it still being grossly infected and trying everything i could find on google, i went to the doctors and they even told me my body is probably rejecting it and to just take it out. This was my last resort before just closing it up and it worked within a month or less. My piercing is completely healed now and i got my second one done and using this consistently has prevented it from the horrible infection bump the first time

reviews.title: No, it's Not "bleach". Sodium Hypochlorite is commonly used in wound care
reviews.text: No,Sodium Hypochlorite is not bleach! It can be used as a bleaching agent and is COMMON in wound care. Even if it shares ingredients. It's basically  a saline solution with phosphoric acid added. It's totally safe. If you go to a tattoo shop and get a piercing, you are told to clean with salt water. That's basically what this is, with added Phosphoric acid. And the only thing the phosphoric acid does is balance your Ph. People calling this bleach are highly overreacting. I have had very many peircings. (10 total) and have used this on several of them. I got a conch peircing at a tattoo shop and cleaned exactly how they told me. I got a keloid. Couldn't get it to heal. I used this Claire's rapid solution and it healed in less than a week. I love this stuff I think it's amazing. While yes, everyone heals differently, it may not be perfect for everyone, my point is simply that it is safe for most people, and I've had a great experience with it personally.

reviews.title: It works great cut my healing time in half the time
reviews.text: Great

reviews.title: Definitely works
reviews.text: It absolutely works on every piercing I added to my ear including cartilage

reviews.title: The scent was strong.
reviews.text:
I’m not a huge fan of face masks. It’s messy and takes too much time. As a mother of three kids, I don’t have a lot of time for myself. The exfoliating scrub and hydrating mask's selling point for me was you only need to keep it on for 2 minutes. I use it during my bath time making it convenient and easy to wash off.

The mask is nice and cooling. It was easy to apply during my bath and rinsing off was easy peasy. The scent was meh! Not a fan of the strong smell. My face felt smooth, soft, and hydrated after application.

reviews.title: Fine quality
reviews.text: I am a senior, and my skin is oily - a combination which makes it quite difficult to find masks and exfoliants. Though this product is extremely expensive, it did give my skin a silky texture. I did not find that it had any  effect on removing blackheads. I believe it is a perfect gift, albeit very pricey, for us older ladies, but more for those looking to have a youthful smoothness to skin than those who have perpetual oily plugs.

reviews.title: Smells wonderful and feels great!
reviews.text: I'll start by saying this exfoliating scrub and mask smells wonderful. It's made of natural ingredients and has a natural smell to it which I love! The exfoliating aspect of this mask is great and leaves my skin feeling extremely smooth. Definitely can tell it is removing dead skin cells. It is extremely hydrating but does leave a bit of oily feeling on the skin after rinsing off. I personally will use it 1-2 times a week because of this reason. I was surprised to see the jar is larger than how much cream you actually get.

reviews.title: Lovely
reviews.text: I like this little mask a lot. Definitely exfoliates so I use it rarely, but it gives great results.

reviews.title: Works well but gritty texture
reviews.text:
This scrub works well as a gentle exfoliant. It leaves my skin feeling soft and not too dry. However I’m not a fan of the large chunks of rice in it. I was expecting a smoother scrub texture, but it feels grainy and scratchy and not very delicate.

I’ve read that large grains of natural martial in an exfoliant can subtly scratch the skin due to their uneven texture, so I worry about that with this.

It also has a stronger scent than I was expecting. It’s not too overpowering but I was expecting something milder based on the product description.

Overall this works well, but note that it’s definitely a very scrubby texture. I am also unsure about the value for the price. The packaging has some awkward sentences and the ink on the jar isn’t perfect. Tiny little things like that keep it from feeling truly luxurious.

reviews.title: Feels so soothing & moisturizing
reviews.text: I’ve been using this for nearly 2 weeks now and I’ve started to get comments on how much brighter and soft my face looks! This cream is very easy on sensitive skin, like myself. The texture is creamy but with the occasional rice bits, it’s still simple to apply. The scent is pleasant, but subtle and doesn’t linger which I like! I love how it comes with a little spoon, makes it easier & less messy to apply to your face. The whole process of putting this on and then washing it off, makes my face feel like I just got a professional facial done at a spa! The scrub leaves my skin feeling so moisturized and refreshed. Very please with the scrub!

reviews.title: Absolutely amazing mask!!
reviews.text: A lot of products boast their benefits and this one delivers! It is a great cleaners and exfoliator. It is a terrific balance of both, the scent is not overwhelming, I actually really like it, and your face feels soft and replenished afterward! Great ingredients, very gentle mask, leaves sling nice fresh moisturized! I highly recommend it!!

reviews.title: Coco’s Conclusion: Natural Ingredients, Vegan, No Animal Testing
reviews.text: This product comes in a paste. It has dual qualities as it is an exfoliating scrub and facemask. You can use it for any skin, whether that be oily, combo or normal. It has natural active ingredients, which is my favorite part. It goes on like a paste and has a great scent. I felt like my skin was very hydrated and glowed after use. There are only about 2 1/2 ounces of product in the container, which makes this fairly pricey, but sometimes that is the case with skincare products that work.

reviews.title: Leaves Your Skin Feeling Wonderful
reviews.text: My only issue with this product is that the packaging is deceiving. The way the jar is made was definitely intentional to cut down on the product you receive. So a more accurate amount for the size of the jar would be great.  I have used this for 11 days and my face has never felt or looked better. Even my husband noticed and he never notices anything. It is a paste consistency with little beads to help exfoliate. I covered my whole face, left on for 2 minutes and it easily washed off with warm water. My skin felt amazing afterwards and not tight or sticky like other products I have used before. All in all, I am happy with this product. Just fix the jar issue that gives you less than you are expecting of product and this stuff would be great.

reviews.title: Lovely scent, but probably not for sensitive skin
reviews.text: I don't have sensitive skin, but after my first use, I noticed that my face was red from rubbing in the exfoliation. It didn't take long for the redness to go away, so I gave it another try, but this time, I massaged the mask on very gently, and I mostly left it on for a couple minutes in the shower before rinsing it off. My skin felt moisturized, and it felt a little oily, but not in a bad way. I personally prefer masks that hydrate without the oily feeling, but this one was quite nice, especially during this dry winter weather. Another thing I really loved is the lavender/rose petal smell. The floral note isn't pungent, but it's definitely strong, which might not be a good fit for those who don't like floral scents. The mask is thick, and it spreads evenly without melting in the shower or being so cakey that it's hard to wash off. This is definitely a high quality mask, in my opinion, but I just wish the exfoliation had gentler beads.

reviews.title: Ingredients on packaging not listed on sellings description.
reviews.text: I ordered this based on the sellers listing of ingredients, unfortunately what I received was different and it has ingredients that I am allergic to, so I can not properly review this product. The smell is nice, and the jar is a thick plastic. The box had a lot of fading and didn't match the expensive price tag for the item. I'm sorry I can't review the actual product.

reviews.title: Amazing Mask
reviews.text: I was very pleased with this Dewy Glowy Scrub/Mask. You simply apply the scrub to your face, rubbing it in gently. Then you can leave it on for anywhere between 2 minutes and an hour depending on how long you want. I typically use it for 20-30 minutes but sometimes will do just a quick session. Even after a few minutes, it really made my skin feel amazing. It has proven, pure ingredients and did not irritate my skin at all. Unlike many scrubs, this isn't drying at all - in fact, it is very moisturizing.

reviews.title: Review of scrub cream
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:2cd31f84a2d632c0579bc282c5b8acd9]] After reading pure Australian rice on the ingredient list, I had a high expectation about this cream because my mother and aunt used to tell me about the benefits of rice water on the skin.  I am 68 years old with dark patches on my cheek, forehead, and eye areas. After using the cream for two days, I can say it exceeded my expectations.  I like how  it felt so smooth on my skin, and many dead skin cells were pushed out between my fingers without any irritation.  The benefits to my skin are astounding as it feels amazingly soft and brighter.  I am so happy to have found this product.  I would recommend it to many of my friends.

reviews.title: Amazing!

Washing it off was pretty easy too. The whole 2-minute experience made me feel like I had a  complete facial at home with the scrub and mask. Afterwards my skin felt so smooth, refreshing and moist, it almost did not need for me to apply lotion after. This scrub cream gives the most moistness. Very satisfied.

reviews.text: for better skin

reviews.title: An excellent shave cream...
reviews.text: Went to T&H on St James St this year and had a straight razor shave for the first time. Fantastic! Loved the T&H shave cream and decided to buy some. I decided on the Lavender for something different. Thsi product when used with a brush and mug produces an exceptional amount of  thick lather that lubricates my face very well. It cleans up nicely and moisturizes but does not leave any residue. I have bought sampler packs of Musgo real, TOBS, Geo Trumper etc and although each one of these are good (expecially the TOBS avocado and Eton College for the price) IMO the T&H is the best. I bought a jar of Dreanought due to reviews but it dies not lubricate as well for me at the T&H. Also, the Dreadnoght tub was not filled with cream to the brim (a sizeable void was in the tub) whereas the T&H tub is jammed packed and only requires an almond sized amount for a 3 pass shave. Fantastic stuff. Will try the other scents at some point and expect hem to be as good.

reviews.title: Great Product
reviews.text: This product meet my expectations. I highly recommend this product. It has a very pleasant fragrance and one gets a smooth shave.

reviews.title: Thought $130.00 would hve been for an entire year.

reviews.title: Five Stars

reviews.title: The best shaving cream I've used!!!
reviews.text: I was a big fan of Taylor of Old Bond St due to it's great smell, reasonable price, and comfortable shave.  I began to have issues with irritation after a few months use, and that's when I decided to switch to a more notable brand.  I am completely shocked at how little shave cream you need and how smooth a shave I get.  I use a DE razor with (Feather Hi-stainless blades) and a badger hair brush for every shave.  You will not be disappointed with how silky smooth this shaving cream is. I am speaking conservatively when I say this is the the Rolls Royce of shaving cream.  I highly recommend this shaving cream, and if you are contemplating the price....since when have you purchased a new Rolls Royce for Chevrolet money?

reviews.title: Best out there
reviews.text: One to my second jar now. I've been using it over year now. It lasts more than I expected. Leaves your skin smooth after a close shave

reviews.title: Fantastic smell on this superior shaving product!
reviews.text: Truefitt & Hill make great shaving products.  Their products are a little on the expensive side - at least at first glance, but a little of the shaving products go a long way.  I have been sampling shaving balms, and shaving creams & soaps for the past several years, and so far, the Trufitt & Hill products are superior to the rest of the field.  So far as I can tell, the Lavender Shaving Cream is not sold in a series of products (balms, soaps, shaving cream etc) but just the shaving cream itself.  That is a shame, because I REALLY like the smell of this cream, and the lather it produces is magnificent!  I use a high quality badger shaving brush and apply the cream directly to my (wet) face for lathering.  It takes just a dab to produce a luxurious lather that easily lasts the four passes I normally take with my double edged razor.  The lather that is produced keeps my face very moist during the process of shaving; the razor glides effortlessly and a very comfortable shave is obtained. I highly recommend this product!

reviews.title: Nice cream
reviews.text: I like this cream.  If you have a good brush, you need only traces of this cream to put onto the brush - very economic.  Smell is nice and not too strong.  Very good feeling after shaving. I also use after-shaving balm from the same company.

reviews.title: Smoothest shave
reviews.text: I have been using this cream for years.  It's given me the smoothest shave ever.  I don't have heavy facial hair so one tub will last me at least a year.

reviews.title: A must have
reviews.text:
I try on occasion to find something else
but without fail I keep coming back.
I give up, just going to not wander again

reviews.title: Brilliant Shaving Cream
reviews.text:
Truefitt & Hill Lavender is such a joy to use, it is downright therapeutic. A little bit goes a long way, and it's easy to create a nice lather. It feels very smooth and soothing. I get a close shave with little irritation. My skin feels better hydrated after using this than with most shaving creams.

Fragrance: The primary note is lavender, as you might have guessed. This lavender is masculine, and there is a bit of a "barbershop" smell. The effect is very relaxing and calming. This is not the sharp, pungent, synthetic lavender that you get with many cheap men's fragrances and grooming products. It is cool, natural smelling, and "round".

So far, this is the best cream I've used. It is also the most expensive, but well worth it. Other good choices are Proraso (not as good, but great price) and Taylor of Old Bond Street (close to the same shave quality, cheaper, smells decent). I have not tried Geo F. Trumper yet, so can't offer a comparison to that.

reviews.title: Excellent Truefitt & Hill Product
reviews.text: Being a huge fan of Truefitt & Hill shaving cream jars but new to the lavender scent, this product smells and shaves great.  Delivery via Amazon was also prompt.

reviews.title: Good stuff
reviews.text: This is some of the best cream for ensuring a very close shave allowing the face to withstand the rigors of daily shaving.

reviews.title: Close shave
reviews.text: I originally received a tub of this as a gift. After using it realized it gave the best, smoothest shave I've ever had. I have tried other Truefitt & Hill shave creams but none worked as well. Besides, it smells pretty good too.

reviews.title: One of my standards.
reviews.text:
T&H, Trumper, Castle & Forbes, Taylor...line a few jars up for a shaving rotation...they're all good. I have my personal preferences, you will, too, but they're all good. A good shaving cream, a fine brush, a good blade...very subjective on which one an individual will prefer...and even preferences will undergo changes.
Make shaving enjoyable. Buy a "real" razor, learn to make scraping hair fun.

reviews.title: How much should the best cost?
reviews.text:
In short, this is THE best shaving cream/soap/gel/foam I have ever had the joy of using. Period.

Here is the situation: I have a really thick, course, wiry beard combined with ultra sensitive skin. Quite possibly the worst combination of factors when it comes time to shave. The result has been a life-long (25+ years of shaving) quest for the best combination of products, hoping to produce a smooth shave that doesn't leave leave my face resembling a slab of meat. I have tried practically every method and product known to man, short of ritual sacrifice. Some products worked well, some were abysmal, but I always wondered if there was "something better" and would eventually move on from the latest greatest. Then I found this product about a year ago. I am finished looking for a better cream/soap/gel/foam.

Metrics: I shave in the shower, after I have washed my face. I use a safety razor with Feather blades. I sometimes use shaving oil, but only if I have more than 4 days of growth. The cream is applied using a super badger brush.

First, the scent of this product only lasts about 10 minutes after shaving, so it isn't too perfumicatious. The lather this produces is unbelievable. My previous benchmarks were Proraso and the Art of Shaving, both great products that did the job. This stuff is in a completely different realm. You don't so much work the cream into the brush as much as just wave the shaving brush over the top of the tub once or twice, gathering up shaving cream fumes. That is all you need to produce more than enough lather. I suspect backward engineered alien technology is in play here.

The quality of the resulting shave is beyond belief. Nothing more to say about that.

Yeah, but what about the cost? My first round of Trufitt & Hill lasted about a year. No kidding. A can of average foamy at your local store comes to about $3.50. If that can lasts you 6 weeks, you will use about 8 of those in a year, equalling $28. $28 for a year of horrible shaves, or spend about that same amount for a year of baby bottom skin with no irritation. The choice is yours.

reviews.title: Makes shaving a joy
reviews.text: A must have!  Smells great, makes tons of lather, makes skin soft, prevents nicks due to drag.  It's perfect !

reviews.title: Shaving Cream for True Aficionados
reviews.text:
I love my morning shave - it's a ritual for me. Dunking my razor and brush in warm water every morning, working up a good lather, and shaving is such a blissful experience. If you are serious about your skin, but a good shaving cream. Trufitt and Hill is one of the best. I did a lot of research before I bought it. It actually gives you a very close shave. The aroma is deep and masculine.
PS: It lasted me 6 months and I shave everyday.

This is a mediocre set. It isnt especially bad nor good. The scissor blades don't meet in the middle so they dont cut very well. One set of nail clippers has a weird angle and leaves ridges on the surface of the nail. The other does a better job but for some reason launches the pieces of nail far away - not great for keeping the house tidy. The case doesnt stay shut.

reviews.title: Perfect mani&pedi and facial set! Has everything you would need.
reviews.text: Great product, the tools used to extract blackheads are amazing. Also the tools are very sharp.

reviews.title: Great Set!
reviews.text: Trimming my nails is an important part of my hygiene, and this set of tools are really nice!  Super sharp, and easy to use.  I was really impressed with the variety of tools available.  Able to knock out any issue you may have.  From ingrown nails to eagle claws.  One thing I found extra impressive were the trimmers.  Usually, you need to soak your nails prior to trimming.  If not, they have a tendency to fly off into space, risking eye damage to anyone nearby.  These are so sharp, they just melt through my nail like butter.  I was a little surprised because I've never seen that happen before.  I suspect that'll change as I use them more and more, but it was still impressive to run across that.  This will be my one-stop-shop for my nail care needs.  Would work great for men or women!

reviews.title: OK
reviews.text:
There are a couple complaints about the case, but it's actually one of the better features of this kit, attractive, nicely padded, secure, with a place for everything, though it's a test of dexterity to get some of the tools out of their place. Just keep everything where it belongs and it closes easily with a snap.

The tools are mostly fine. They're adequately sturdy. The scissors are a little thin and flexible for tough cuts, but they're supposed to be for trimming facial hair, for which they're fine.

The fit and finish of a few of the tools isn't the best. A couple of the nail clippers have a little overbite, where one blade doesn't perfectly align with the other, which can make for messier cuts. And a couple of the blades on the callus removers are pitted a little.

On the whole, it's an adequate kit for nail and foot care and grooming.

Made in China.

reviews.title: Best callus removal set
reviews.text: This callus set is one of the best I’ve had it’s tools are sturdy n sting for when I need a little extra power it’s grinder file cuts deep in the callus without breaking skin or getting dull

reviews.title: Great product!

reviews.title: Implements galore
reviews.text: Fairly comprehensive 18 piece home manicure set with implements for finger and toenails, eye brow grooming, callus treatment, nose hair scissors and an ear wax pick (common for use in Asia). The pieces are all constructed from black painted stainless steel and are mostly machined nicely. The pieces are all set in place in an included, snap shut, carry case.

reviews.title: Has the tools needed to handle our face/hands/feet/skin issues.
reviews.text:
Short summary: I like a number of the instruments available in this set; they work well for my needs, and for the needs of our family members.
The case securely holds each instrument in its slot; but some instruments are tough to remove from their slots.

More details:

a) Case is soft to the touch and comfortable to hold. There is a tab that I press in to open the case; to close the case, I press both parts of the case together.

b) One compartment (the side with the tab) holds:
--- Nail File
Works well to smooth out my nails after I trimmed them.
--- Fingernail Clipper
The curved blades cut my nails well. The lever has square-shaped divots that give good
grip. It is a bit hard to rotate the lever around the pin; but, with continued use, it should
be easier to open and close the lever. There is no nail file attached to the clipper
--- Nose trimmer
I did not find this to be as good at cutting my nose hairs.
---.Cuticle nipper
This trims my cuticles well.
--- Eyebrow trimmer
This does a decent job in trimming eyebrow
--- Cuspidal/Obtuse push-type broach
I didn't have a need to use this; but several family members used this, and they say it
does an adequate job with their nail polish routine
--- Ear pick
I tried it, but I didn't like the sensation of it scraping against the inside of my ear.

c) The other compartment holds:
--- Toenail clipper
Curved blades cut my toenails well; however, it does not cut my big toenail because
that nail has too much fungus and is too thick. I used a different toenail clipper
recommended by my foot doctor.
--- Oblique nail clipper
I don't use this often for cutting my nail; but I like how easily it cuts the nails. Other
family members love it, and are more familiar in using it
--- Nail cleaning knife
I don't use this; others in my family say it works well to remove the debris under their nails.
--- Tweezers
My family members do not like this, because it doesn't open enough for them to use; so they went
back to using their preferred tweezers
--- Zig Zag callus remover
--- Oblique callus remover
--- Flat callus remover
note: so far, those of us with calluses (including me) do not have them so thick that
they need removal using these instruments.
--- Dead skin fork
a family member used it; she says it is quite useful in removing the loose skin right
around her cuticle area
--- Ear pick (has something like a serrated shape, perhaps)
I much prefer this ear pick; it felt more natural and is like what I used in the past.
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a pick)
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a scoop)
None of our family members currently have acne, so we haven't had a chance to try it;
but, we have similar instruments just like these, and those worked well when we've
needed to deal with our skin issues.

Lastly: I would have liked the case to be slightly larger, so that it is easier to remove all these instruments. When I removed all the instruments, some of which I used, others which my family members used, and then put the instruments back into the case, we couldn't get it to close. Finally, we removed the toenail clippers, and that easily closed the case.

Overall, this is a good manicure set, with instruments that I and/or my family members find to be very useful for our particular needs.

reviews.title: Way better than expected
reviews.text: Half of this stuff looks cool, but I don't even know what its used for...but I love it!

reviews.title: Multi-purpose tools
reviews.text: Totally worth it for the price

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Excellent

reviews.title: Smells great, very intense scrub
reviews.text:
When you open the lid of this lava scrub, you'll think you just stepped off the plane landing in Hawaii. It smells so tropical, so dewy, and so tasty I almost tried it. And it's no wonder, with ingredients of coconut oil, papaya extract, and macadamia oil.
I tried it a few times on my face (mixed with soap, of course), and it was a little too strong for me. The sugar crystals are large and sturdy, requiring a vigorous scrub to dissolve. It didn't cause me to break out, but I did notice that the rest of the day and the following day, it looked like half my skin got taken off. Dips, discolorations, and small zits that were hiding beneath the surface were all visible - but after a couple of days everything went back to normal. Those of you who have tried chemical or acid peels on your face know what I mean.
I get it, if there are that many impurities on my skin, it's a good idea to scrub them away. But I don't really like looking at my face like that in the mirror. So... now I've switched it to a body scrub and use it in the tub. Perfect! Arms, legs, and chest are now totally smooth and smell great.

reviews.title: A little too abrasive for my face but WONDERFUL as a body scrub
reviews.text: I have pretty sensitive skin, so I tried a bit on my face before fully committing and it was a little too rough for me.  However, I absolutely love it as an exfoliant for my hands, chest and arms.  It smells awesome, not too perfumey.  The charcoal is dark and will definitely show up on your sink or bathtub, make sure you wipe it all down when you're finished or it'll look pretty dirty.

reviews.title: Pina Colada-ish smell, Very Gritty, Good Scrub
reviews.text:
This isn't that scrub that you use every single day, its more for those occasional deep cleanings, and exfoliating. Or just for a good at home spa treatment. The size of the jar, and the amount of product is fairly small but it doesn't take much to get a good facial cleansing. To me, it smells very much like a Pina Coloda, but not over powering just a nice fresh scent.

The scrub itself is has a medium sized grit, and its feels fairly "sharp" against the skin. Also, this isn't great for rubbing on and then taking a long soak in a tub, it turns the water black. I was worried it would stain my towels, the floor, tub something, but it didn't.

This scrub is fairly specialized and is one of those love it or hate it things. I gave it 5 stars because the quality is excellent, the ingredients are top notch, and it has a pleasant smell. But it has to be tried before you'll know if its right for you.

reviews.title: Moderate scrubbiness. Left my skin soft, although irritated at first. The scent is overwhelming.
reviews.text:
I love that this scrub contains such good ingredients. It's also a bit abrasive, which is something I appreciate. It goes on thick, but thins down while retaining a moderate amount of scrubbiness. The scrub in the container looks black at first, but as I spread it across my face and scrub it in, the areas that are scrubbed turn gray. It lets me see where I've already worked the product, and lets me focus on areas I want to put more pressure. I use a spatula to apply it so I am not constantly putting my hands into the container.

It says to use gentle circles, but I find that I am probably a little more aggressive. It does make it abrasive against my fingertips, so I have started using a little facial scrubber for applications. I have some dry patches, as well as light acne scaring and this scrub seems to really take the outer layer off of those areas for better cell turn over.

The scrub is potent, and the only negatives for me are that it leaves me feeling oily once I rinse off and the scent is overwhelmingly tropical. I am sure the scent is great for some, but I am not overly into the pineapple, papaya, and coconut like scent. Those may not be the exact fragrance ingredients, but they are what I am reminded of. I can get through the scent to scrub my face, but it does leave behind some residual smell afterwards along with a fair amount of slickness and oiliness. I actually go ahead and use a toner on my face afterwards to pull off as much leftover scent and oils as I can because of personal preference.

The price at $20 is fair for me. A little bit of this will go a long way, it has solid ingredients, and it's an abrasive scrub that actually works to pull imperfections to the surface and turn over the outer layer of skin cells and damage. If the scent were more mild, and the formula less oily, this would be 5 stars for me. I'd still get it again.

The full ingredient list includes: "Sucrose Glycerin Polyglyceryl-10 Dipalmitate Butylene Glycol Propanediol Water Fragrance Charcoal Powder Phenoxyethanol Sodium Methyl Cocoyl Taurate Iron Oxide Caprylyl Glycol Chlorphenesin Sodium Chloride Vanillyl Butyl Ether Coconut Fatty Acid Silica Volcanic Ash Avocado Oil Macadamia Integrifolia Seed Oil Aleurites Moluccanus Seed Oil Coconut Oil Euterpe Oleracea Fruit Extract Papaya Fruit Extract Aminomethyl Propanol Saccharomyces/Zinc Ferment Saccharomyces/Magnesium Ferment Saccharomyces/Silicon Ferment Saccharomyces/Copper Ferment Saccharomyces/Iron Ferment Leuconostoc/Radish Root Ferment Filtrate Gardenia Taitensis Flower Extract Tocopherol Ascorbyl Palmitat"

reviews.title: OMG IM IN LOVE!!!!!  but beware....
reviews.text: I am in love with this lava product!  I love a great exfoliator!  when I leave my job at night, I am soooo greasy, oily, and floury that I just want to rip off the dirty day in the shower with a great cleanser.  I always felt that a scrub couldn't have enough exfoliating power for me, but I may have met my match with this 1.  its super gritty and its DEFINITELY NOT FOR PEOPLE WITH SENSITIVE SKIN!  at 1 point I thought that it might be to strong even for my iron face.  the product is black and very thick, so u only need a little bit.  I cleansed my face with it, and now my forehead feels baby soft.  the rest of my face also feels AMAZING, but my silky smooth forehead is what is really standing out for me.  I would be sure to be extra gentile when u r applying this to ur skin.  I will definitely be adding this product to my top 3 super take the day off products and I will be sure to reorder it when it eventually runs out.  I hope this was helpful.

reviews.title: Charcoal gets rid of the nasties and coconut oil moisturizes
reviews.text: I REALLY like this it works great

reviews.title: Delightful Tropical Scrub
reviews.text:
Upon opening the jar, I could smell the papaya, flower extract, and sugar. It smells fruity, sweet, and yet floral all at once. I personally love the smell and I can still smell it even hours after using it in the shower. It works much like any other scrub you would get in a jar. Scoop some out and rub your skin in circles. I used it after I washed myself with soap to open up my pores more. My husband also used it as well. We both used it all over the entirety of our bodies.

I love the blackness of the scrub so you can really see how much skin you are scrubbing. Also, my shower is white, but there was no staining. Just simple rinse your shower if some lingers on the bathtub edge. After a thorough rinse, my skin felt silky smooth. I mostly smell like papaya now although I can smell the floral notes on my husband.

We both really love this sugar scrub. Do note this is not as harsh as a salt scrub. I would say this is more relaxing to get your skin silky smooth and a more clean feeling. I have to say it takes me make to Thailand when I have gotten scrubs there. My skin feels just as good.

For the price of $20 dollars, the quality is there, even though you can get more quantity with other brands. I will definitely be purchasing this whenever I run out of this jar. Personally, you cannot beat quality and I think it is worth the price. My husband and I were absolutely delighted with this tropical scrub and cannot wait to use it again!

reviews.title: Far too course for my face
reviews.text: This scrub is far to course for me to use on my sensitive skin on my face. It is even a little rough as a scrub on my body. It does work great as a foot scrub though! It is very messy though so I have to spend extra time making sure to clean down my shower and tub whenever I use it as it splashes and leaves black streaks absolutely everywhere.

reviews.title: The best scented strong exfoliator I have had
reviews.text: I don't like wimpy, smooth can't-tell-it-is-an-exfoliator so many are.  So realize that is a preference.  But this has great granules that soften then dissolve over time.  The scent though is just stunning.  I can't even describe it but wow!  If you want a real exfoliator and love a good smell... go for it this is your product!

reviews.title: Love the smell!
reviews.text: I really like the smell, not crazy about the black it washes off pretty good but have to make sure it gets rinsed from all parts of the shower or makes shower look bad.

reviews.title: It was opened.
reviews.text: It was opened and leaked when the package arrived.

reviews.title: Soft & soothed skin
reviews.text: I used this later in a day, after derma rollering, and I felt it helped soothe my skin. No lingering irritation or redness from the derma roller. I'm not sure if it was this serum alone or how it worked in conjunction with my other skin care products, but my skin felt velvety soft afterwards. I enjoyed the lavender-scented milky texture of this serum. It came in a sealed box. It's affordably priced at $9.99. I would get this again.

reviews.title: Super Serum!
reviews.text:
My wife has sensitive skin. There's a lot of products out there she really doesn't like. She loves this brand and loves this product. So does her skin!

No redness afterwards from irritation, feels so refreshing while being applied, isn't greasy, no tackiness after and smells wonderful. You can't go wrong with this brand

Also, super-fun-to-open-boxes seem to be a thing with Celleanser. Love that little detail when it comes to their presentation. We're thinking anything from their line would make an awesome gift because of it and you can feel good knowing the product itself is also going to work

reviews.title: It's good
reviews.text: This is a nice pump serum, which I like better than those that use a dropper. Has a thick, milky texture and subtle medicinal scent that doesn't last long. Absorbs quickly and doesn't leave your skin feeling sticky, however, not much in the way of hydration until you add a cream! Like the other Celleanser products, ingredients listed in English on the box (if you open box by using the perforation, you'll see the ingredients and brand story inside, all written in Korean. It's reasonably priced, which is nice for those of us on a budget!

reviews.title: Loving this serum
reviews.text: My skin is feeling hydrated, plumper and more radiant with this serum. It’s just giving soft beautiful skin.

reviews.title: Sensitive skin approved
reviews.text: Simple, clean, scentless serum. This is sensitive skin approved.

reviews.title: It's a good serum
reviews.text:
- Glass skin finish
- Hydrating, mildly calming and cooling
- No scent
- The serum is a milky color and spreads out clear.
- No breakouts
- It's a little tacky and dries slowly because of the hydrating glass skin properties. If you're after this kind of product it could work for you--especially because this is a clean and vegan formula.

reviews.title: Starting to react, tiny itch on my face by left nostril.
reviews.text:
I have been using this for about 5-days. I replaced the current moisturizer to try this serum. I have dry and mature skin.

Results;
1)Started to get a small reaction on my left side nostril. Starting to itch. Tiny spot.
2)Face felt smooth after application
3)used only a larger size dime, for my face
4)not oily
5)faint scent of alcohol
6)light feeling. Not heavy on my face
7)feels clean
8)Vegan
9)Not tested on animals.

Negative:
1)pricey for the amount
2)Tiny itch starting to form, near left nostril on face

There are a lot of ingredients! Nevertheless, It is pricey but the top-5 ingredients besides water, are listed here:
1)Glycerin-good hydrator by pulling water to surface of skin; helping to “plump “ your skin
2)Propanediol- one of it’s uses is an emollient to help retain hydration and moisturize in skin.
3)Cetyl Ethylhexanoate - one uses is to help texture; protects skin and traps moisture. Bad is derived from palm oil (sustainability issues)
4)Sorbitol - a type of sugar that supposedly makes a formula less cloudy; attracts and hold moisture; stabilizer (helps hold formula together); Used to add sweetness to lip products.
5)Dipropolyene Glycol - a type of alcohol. A chemical that helps stabilize a formula with its texture and helps dissolve other substances; supposedly, enhances penetration into skin.

The list of ingredients go on forever. There is a warning: Do not apply it on a part where you have a scar, eczema or dermatitis. So, since I’m starting to form a tiny itch, I will stop using it. I will be going back to my moisturizer. 3-stars, for me.

reviews.title: A Very Nice Hydrating, Soothing, Barrier-Repairing Antioxidant Serum
reviews.text:
(Ingredients list is at the bottom of this review.)

I have really enjoyed using the Celleanser Vegan Clean Serum, and my skin seems to like it too. I also tried the Celleanser Clean Toner Mist, and the Celleanser Clean Cream. They all worked nicely for my dry, sensitive skin, and they worked nicely together.

This serum is creamy with a viscous gel-like texture that provides an extra layer of hydration, moisture, and antioxidants on the skin. It is applied before moisturizer. The serum is smooth and cooling when applied. It soaks in well, but it does leave a nice layer of extra hydration on the skin. It is not sticky, and it layers perfectly with other products. The scent is sort of lightly herbal or earthy, similar to many Korean skincare brands.

I will say that the finish isn't my go-to. It isn't really dewy or glowy. But I wouldn't call it extremely matte either. Maybe somewhere in-between, but more toward the matte side. I typically like to use dewy, glowy skincare, so I would probably use this product at night for that reason.

The packaging is very nice with a unique-looking, tall, slim plastic bottle with a very functional pump. A little goes a long way, and I imagine this bottle will last me a while. At $19.99 for 30ml / 1.01 fl. oz., it's a pretty good deal. Especially because the ingredient list is nice.

I haven't been using it long enough to speak on the long-term results, but I do like the serum so far, and I will definitely continue to use it. I would recommend this to anyone who likes the ingredient list and wants to give it a try.

A NOTE TO CELLEANSER: It would be wonderful to add accurate, full ingredient lists to all of your products listed on Amazon. It would go a long way toward helping your customers save time and make informed decisions without any hesitations or uncertainties. Thank you!

INGREDIENTS:

Water, Glycerin, Propanediol, Cetyl Ethylhexanoate, Sorbitol, Dipropylene Glycol, Camellia Sinensis Leaf Extract, Panthenol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Centella Asiatica Leaf Extract, Asiaticoside, Madecassic Acid, Asiatic Acid, Boerhavia Diffusa Root Extract, Dipotassium Glycyrrhizate (Lavender) Extract, Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Extract, Thymus Vulgaris (Thyme) Extract, Origanum Vulgare Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract, Rehmannia Chinensis Root Extract, Angelica Gigas Extract, Paeonia Lactiflora Extract, Astragalus Membranhuba Fruit Extract, Zizip Solanum Lycopersicum (Tomato) Fruit Extract, Potentilla Chinensis Extract, Taraxacum Officinale (Dandelion) Leaf Extract, Cornus Officinalis Fruit Extract, Narcissus Tazetta Bulb Extract, Lecithin, Cetearyl Olivate, Sorbitan Olivate, Butylene Glycol, Xanthan Gum,Sodium Acrylates Copolymer, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Adenosine, Carbomer, Tromethamine, Citric Acid, Disodium EDTA, Caprylyl Glycol, Hydroxyacetophenone, Ethylhexylglycerin, Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Oil, Linalool.

Also, my skin feels so hydrated and feels so smooth. I can't give this product enough stars love it. I recommend this for any skin type!

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: This product makes your skin smooth, really good!

reviews.title: So cool.
reviews.text:
The cat eye look is so cool!
The glittery blue covers so well!
These polishes dry under a UV Lamp in under 90 seconds!
After using these nail polishes I feel like I have just went to a nail salon!
I’m very happy with this set of nail polishes!
The magnet they include does a good job at creating a cat eye!

reviews.title: One coat coverage, buttery smooth formulas & special FX finishes! 😍 Amazing set of polishes!
reviews.text:
This set of gel polishes from Pine Gel really blew me away! These cover in one easy coat and have a nice glossy finish. The glitter polish was a little bit thick, but it's packed with glitter so I guess that's to be expected. This set of six shades contains (1) Glitter, (3) Crèmes, (2) Magnetics, and a cats eye magnet wand needed to work the magnetic polishes. All of the colors cure for 60 secs in an LED lamp or 90 secs in a UV.

The crèmes are all buttery smooth in application, and self level beautifully. Each dry down to a nice glossy finish. The magnetics are easy to manipulate with the enclosed magnet stick. I was able to do a double cat eye line on my first try! (These are gonna be a lot of fun to play with using different shaped magnets for different effects.) Both magnetic polishes covered completely in one easy coat. The glitter shade is jam-packed with small royal blue glitters and is accented with a sprinkling of medium sized blue hexagons. The depth and sparkle to this polish is incredible. The glitter load makes this shade a little bit thick - but it's still workable. None of these gel polishes have a bad odor. None of them gave me any issues during application or the curing process. I cannot speak to the wear time, as I always use a peel-off base coat or full coverage tips, with gel polish products.

Because these are so rich in pigment, (and a lot of them are dark colors), it is best to work in thin coats. They do cure with a sticky layer. All of these (except the glitter) cured for 60 sec in my LED lamp. With the thickness and opacity of the glitter polish, I did a double cure on that one to be safe. All swatches in this review are ONE coat and are shown WITHOUT top coat.

I think the coverage, formula, and finishes of these polishes is amazing and absolutely worth the price. I highly recommend these! Thanks for reading. :) If my review and photos were helpful to you in any way, please click the button below to let me know. Thanks!

reviews.title: Cat eye gel nails are amazing!
reviews.text: Pine gel nail polish smells great, looks amazing, and lasts a long time.  The dark blue is our favorite color and we love that you can get the deep color with great glitter and shine using just one coat, on top of the base coat.  There are no base or top coats included, but as long as you have them, the set, along with a nail clipper and file is all you need to have salon quality manicure and pedicure.  The magnet included with the kit is rectangular, as shown, and is effective at making long streaks on the nails.  With some practice, you’ll be able to create very defined cat eyes, but make sure you keep the cure lights right nearby, or you’ll lose the streaking.  Additionally, make sure that you buff the nails lightly, clean the nails thoroughly, and run full cure times if you want to have the nail polish last more than two weeks.  Following this simple procedure will hopefully give you a high quality and long lasting finish like I have experienced.  I recommend Pine Gel Nail Polish and am sure you’ll be super happy with the results.

reviews.title: Very pretty
reviews.text: Very pretty gel polish colors, they are very thin but is very opaque

reviews.title: Thick polish but not too thick
reviews.text: These polishes are thick and only truly require one coat to cure and be done. I love that the colors are on the top of the bottles for you to know which one you're using. I forgot to use the magnet to get the cat eye effect but will try it later before curing the polish. I like the various shades of blues and purples in the set. Despite how thick it is, it only took one time to cure and dry which is a huge plus since you have to wait a while for a thick regular polish to dry.

reviews.title: Nice colors
reviews.text:
This is a cool set. If you don't care for the cat eye design you can still use this polish like anything else. The polish is pretty thick so if you apply a thick coat you can pretty much get away with 1 coat. The magnets aren't super strong, but they don't seem any weaker then other brands that offer a cats eye effect nail product.

Set times are the same as any other gel polish under the UV/LED light. I do find that even after letting this polish cure for 90 sec, if I don't apply a top coat then it always had a slightly tacky feel. Not really uncommon but I have used some brands that don't do that so wanted to point it out.

Overall, really nice set.

reviews.title: Labeled nicely and thick gels
reviews.text: Really like this gel set. They’re such beautiful colors and the glitter one is very stunning. Looks exactly as pictured. One coat will do but I like my gels thicker so I did two coats. Very high gloss on its own. Recommend.

reviews.title: Fantastic Gel Nail Polish Kit
reviews.text: This was my first at-home manicure using gel nail polish with cat-eye colors and magnet. The instructions were easy to follow. The polish went on easily (be careful not to apply too thick). The color is true to the product photos. Will purchase again from Pine. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: beautiful colors
reviews.text: I used the darker cat's eye recently on a family member's hands, she loved it. A base, 2 thin coats, and a topcoat. UV light and was perfect. The bottles are small and come in 6 colors.  I think it would be good for the girl that has a kit already started or a shop. Great stocking stuffer. Oh, I didn't pick up on the "light" pine scent.  I'd order again.

reviews.title: Very nice gel polishes
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:64010d8a82d58e7f01ce22f266a26aa0]] Love all of the colors from this set. They cure very quickly. I do add a top coat and a base coat just to add shine. But they are pretty shiny which is nice. I use a primer with any gel polish brand and these lasted over two weeks on my nails. I really love the colors and the longevity of these gel nail polishes. I used my UVLED lamp from the sensationail brand and it worked perfectly fine.

reviews.title: Nearly one coaters
reviews.text: With the exception of the glitter in this set, they're pretty much one coaters! All cured in 60 seconds; the creme colors are nice, but I got this set for the magnetic ones and I'm not disappointed! Even without the magnetic effect, those two polishes are shimmery and beautiful. With the magnetic affect, they're very different, but still stunning. I actually feel that even with the magnetic affect, one coat was just fine. I added a second just to see what that looked like, and it made the edges darker, and the magnetic effect less profound, in my opinion. Still, this set is absolutely worth it! The tops of the bottles are labeled with a colored sticker for convenience, which is great.

reviews.title: Doesnt quite work
reviews.text:
I love the colors.
They are plastic feel- which I don't particularly love.
My issue is the magnet doesn't quite work with the polish. After using the colors on all 10 fingers, I only got one finger to have the cat-eye effect.

reviews.title: Cool colors and easy to use
reviews.text: This is an affordable set of gel polish. It comes with 6 colors and 2 of those are the cat eye polish which I am a little obsessed with at the moment. You get a magnet stick in this set to activate the cat eye look. It's simple to do and looks awesome. The glitter polish in this set was ok too. The bottles are quite small but these sets seem to me like they are almost sample packs and when you find some you really like then you can look for it in bigger bottles.

reviews.title: Love!
reviews.text: I've got nothing bad to say about this brand of gel polish nor the colors this set comes in. I apply a base coat, 2 layers of color and a shiny top coat. Between each layer I would cure the gel in my lamp. I had very minimal chipping throughout the entire month I wore this color. I mainly took off this gel coat because I needed to trim my toenails again, not because the color looked bad. Will continue to use this set of gel polish and would recommend!

reviews.title: Loving this cat eye set!
reviews.text: The gel polish set works great! I haven’t had any problems with this set if I prep my nails right. The cat eye effect works without a problem. Regular colored polishes work just as well and look really cute.

I recently underwent chemo for breast cancer & my nails turned brown & brittle from medications. I wanted & needed someway to cover my nails.
I saw this cateye polish so I wanted to try it. I did my own nails & they came out ok, you will need practice to get them perfect. I loved the cateye effect it's different & eye catching!!! Will order again.
