I like the buttons designated for each separate drying time and the LED display is easy to read and understand.  It works like a champ and does a great job!  I truly think this lamp is worth the price, and I think you'll like it as much as I do.

reviews.title: Love it
reviews.text: I love this! Now I can set my nails in no time! No more nicks in my polish because I can't ever seem to let them fully dry before I have to do something! This thing is wonderful! I have only used it with regular nail polish so far, but my daughter has some gel polish I will try next. If anything changes I will update my review. I have a feeling my daughters and I will get plenty of use from this. Nice little machine!

reviews.title: Very Nice
reviews.text: I just used this product for the first time just now.  I had been using one that I bought 8 years ago, that one was hot on my hands to the point of burning.  This one didn't do that at all.  Warm not hot.  I believe this light is newer technology with LED/UV lights, my old one was UV only.  I prefer this one very much so,  It is compact and very easy to use. Just simply push the button the corresponds with the time you wish, your hand insert turns it on., turns off automatically with the time selected has run out.  Super Easy! I recommend this product for doing any gel polish.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: This is the first UV/LED gel nail polish lamp I've ever tried, but it's been amazing so far! It seems pretty standard, it has options for timing from 10 seconds to 120. The best thing about it is that you can set the setting, and it will remember the setting and turn on when you put your hand in via motion sensors! That's super convenient when you have one or both hands covered in wet polish! It plugs into the wall and has a cord that I've found to definitely be long enough, and it's great that you don't have to rely on batteries for it. 5 stars!

reviews.title: I like it
reviews.text: Works great!

reviews.title: Works well- pretty design
reviews.text:
I love the look of this lamp. It almost looks like it came straight out of a salon from the 50s, but also manages to look very modern. It's relatively small and doesn't take up too much space among my other bathroom appliances beneath the sink. The plug isn't very long, maybe 4 feet, so wherever you do your nails you will need to be sitting near an outlet.

My daughter has sensitive skin, and when she stuck her hand under the lamp she said it bothered her hands a little because of the heat. I don't think the heat was too intense. I have a fresh burn on my finger that didn't feel bothered by the light so I'm attributing that to her being younger and more sensitive to heat, but I do think it's something to be aware of.

My fingernails weren't completely dry when I pulled them out, and I had to run them through twice as many cycles as the nail polish recommended, but honestly I think it was due more to the nail polish I used than it was a fault of the lamps (for reference, it was China Glaze brand Gelaze). The nail polish was really super runny and had a watery consistency with very thin coverage.

Two days later though (a record for two people who are hard on their nails) my daughter and I both have zero chips in our polish. I'm pretty happy to have received this and think it would make a great gift or be a good purchase for those who want to save a little money from getting a professional manicure.

Just one last note: I had to go to a beauty supply store to find a gel polish that needed a lamp. It seems like many gels these days advertise no lamp needed. Personally I feel like those aren't any better than regular polish, but it's something to consider.

reviews.title: Great for gel nail polish!
reviews.text: This is just like the ones at the salon! You have the option of setting the timer for 30, 60, or 90 seconds to dry gel polish. I love it and am so glad I got it!

reviews.title: Good Quality Nail Lamp
reviews.text: This is a really nice, good looking nail lamp. It's kind of on the luxurious side, and seems nicer than ones that have been used on me at the nail salon. You set a time based on the buttons on the side, and apply pressure to the inner plate. My mother tried it out, and though she had to use it for several minutes per side for her normal drug store nail polish, it did the job. Maybe with gel polish, one minute countdown would do the trick, but not for the normal stuff. Still, cool product, happy to have it. Could probably use it on feet too, if you wanted to.

reviews.title: BURNED OUT IN ONE MONTH
reviews.text: So disappointed.  Loved it when it worked. I used it twice no problem. Today is my third time and it just QUIT. Now I see it is unavailable for purchase. I guess that means they won't replace it so I am out the money. "Let the buyer beware".  Maybe this type of item is better to buy in a store when you can return it then buy another at the same time.

reviews.title: Will recommend! Will buy again!
reviews.text: I have had my lamp for so long and I will buy this brand again whenever I need a new lamp! I feel like I have had my lamp for about a year and use it about every two weeks, and it is still in perfect condition. There are no lights burnt out and this thing works wonders!

reviews.title: 54w nail curing lamp
reviews.text: Nail curing lamp 54w. I am happy with this nail lamp. I can use it for my hands and my toes. It is quite large and roomy inside. It has multiple settings and the cord is a good length. I like how you can add time with the push of a button on the back. The lamp is made of hard plastic and appears to be durable. I  like the rose gold accents it makes it feel just like the ones I use at the salon.

reviews.title: Must have!
reviews.text: Product does as it should salon quality!

reviews.title: It’s decent
reviews.text: I was led astray by the great reviews. When my product came, it had purple ink all over it and it works but it takes over 5 minutes to cure my gel polish. The “54 W” title I think is a bit exaggerated because I’ve seen a 42 W work better and faster than this one. It’s worth $20 I guess but I would rather invest in a good one. The reviews had me fooled thinking I would get a great product for a cheap price.

reviews.title: Very impressed
reviews.text:
I am very impressed with this nail lamp.
Looks very high quality and works very good.
It's super fast and easy to work on and  dries my nails perfectly.

reviews.title: Fabuloso 😍
reviews.text: Estoy muy emocionada con este producto, es una maravilla

reviews.title: Best value for shellac/gel polish lights
reviews.text: I was concerned these lights would not be as powerful as the professional brand I had previously been using, but I have been VERY pleased and satisfied with the results!!! Definitely a great purchase!

reviews.title: The best curling/setting cream I have ever tried to date
reviews.text:
This product is so good that I actually purchased a second jar. I rarely do that since I have product ADHD. Initially I purchased this because I thought it would elongate my curls. Nope it did not do that. My hair shrank as close to my scalp as possible. After I had a good laugh, I tried this cream to do twist outs. When I was air drying my hair, my twist shrank up to two inches. I guess it is the nature of my 4a/b/c and porous hair. But when I unraveled my twist, the wave pattern was just gorgeous. My hair didn't frizz until day three but I was playing in my way too much and I didn't do the pineapple method to maintain the style.

This creme leaves a soft hold to my hair and as shocking as it was, I didn't need to use a lot. I made the first mistake of saturating my hair with this cream and it left it a bit greasy but my hair still looked cute. It left my hair moisturized for a week, which impressed me by miles because usually my hair has trouble holding on to moisture. I also used this for coil outs, wet rollersets (with an aid of a setting lotion to make it last), etc and it has worked beautifully. Also I don't if anyone has experienced this but this product also helps to detangle my hair while I am sectioning my hair. Some folks might not experienced this but for me this is huge because my hair likes to wrap around itself.

I was so close in purchasing Miss Jessie's (the Butter Creme and the Meringue) line but with the results I am getting with this product, I don't have to bother. What it lacks in amount, it comes out cheaper in terms of price per ounce. The only con is I wished that it came in a bigger jar but then again I don't need a lot of product. I usually catch this on sale at my local drugstore. When it goes on sale again, I will buy two jars.

reviews.title: Great styler
reviews.text: I bought this a couple of years ago just to try it out and I really liked it. I recently purchased this again to replace my favorite styler that was discontinued. This has the same feel as my old product when I apply it and it leaves my hair feeling the same way too. The scent is fabulous and my hair feels silky soft.

reviews.title: Would use on relaxed hair not natural hair
reviews.text: Gave 3 stars since This product is geared towards natural hair. It was too heavy for my natural hair. I have kinky tight curl natural hair pattern. Smells good but works better on my hair now that i am relaxed. Shows curl pattern and also allows my to stretch my relaxers by keeping my hair moisturizer and allows me to go weeks without using heat.

reviews.title: Oh yeah
reviews.text:
I love this stuff. I'm a natural 4a/3c. My hair is fine and can get buildup easily from heavy oils and silicones. Not a problem with this. My twist outs turn out gorgeous. I put it in over my leave in and my hair is soft and beautiful.

note- I've used the featherwhipped too. It's okay but I think this is much better.

reviews.title: I loved it....but
reviews.text:
I loved the way this cream felt on my hair. I have very thick mixed black hair. It is not kinky, but not straight. This butter gave it a wonderful feel....however, after a few days with my hair twisted with this butter, it was very hard.
I just don't think it was the right product for my hair.

reviews.title: Love this stuff.
reviews.text: It really is a wonderful product, it came speedily but I ordered 2 and only got 1. Maybe I need to check into that. But overall, I love this product.I put it on my hair before I braid it and I have wavy hair once I do. Just the look I am looking for.

reviews.title: Great curls, staying power 4 b c hair
reviews.text: This item works well, giving curls and staying power. My hair is 4b/c.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Thanks

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Roots of nature made my hair feel very soft and my coils more defined. It also made it more manageable

reviews.title: Not!
reviews.text: At least Qhemet's is natural, this product is not. This product is cheap because it's full of chemicals but if you don't care about that then go for it.

reviews.title: Want dry, undefined curls? Try this product
reviews.text: I bought this product on a Sunday because I couldn't find the original product that I was looking for and decided to try something new. I read the directions and decided that I was going to use it as a wash and go. I shampooed, conditioned my hair, and then had my hair soaking wet so I could apply the product. I separated my hair and applied it in sections as I do when I use the lovely Kinky Curly Curling Custard. I knew something was wrong when I didn't see my hair become curlier like it does when I use KC, but I decided to continue anyway. I should add  that my hair is a mix of 4b/c and when it's wet it's very curly.  So I dry my hair, and the results are a hot goddang mess. My curls look ugly, they're all over the place, and just ugh! Despite this I do a braid out on my hair and decide to see what it looks like in the morning. The morning comes and my hair is ugly and rough. I have never had a product leave my hair feeling so coarse and disgusting. I was so ashamed by the way this product left my hair. I was so upset that I returned it the next day at Walgreens. I'll stick to my expensive products that leave my hair feeling soft and defined, thanks.

reviews.title: One of my staple products!!
reviews.text:
I LOVE this stuff!!! During my Big Chop intense days of trying to find the right product I came across this and loved it. in the past 11 months since my BC I have learned to make my own hair care products but I use some store bought ones like this one. I love the smell of it, the feel of it and I add coconut oil to it, rewhip and is in business!!

I use this on my wet hair to define my curls. It doesn't dry hard and keeps my hair soft. There is no residue left behind on this one at all. I have long ago stopped being a product junkie and have my set staple products and this is one of them.

It is not for everyone but I'd say give it a try. If you buy from Sally's and give it a weeks try and do not like it, I know they have a return policy that is great, you can return it and get something else or your money back. I suggest whipping in your favorite carrier oils, doesn't change the consistency at all.

reviews.title: The only product I need
reviews.text: This works great for my twa.  The texture is a bit thick and creamy and it absorbs very easily without leaving the white residue. I wet my hair and apply the butter. I finger comb my hair to create the coils or I lightly brush my hair (with a soft brush) in circles to create the coils. When my hair dries it has a nice hold and shine. It even last to the next day. I don't have to use the eco styler gel or any type of holding spray. I would have never tried this product had I not receive a sample from the local beauty mart while purchasing some hair for a sew in months before i did my bc. How ironic!!

reviews.title: Great product, but watch the price!!!
reviews.text:
I use this product regularly. Keeps my curls soft and springy, but...

I think the current price +$10 is really expensive. I was only looking for it on amazon because my usual supplier--the dollar tree-- was out.
If I can get this item for a dollar, there is no reason Amaozon should be selling it for more than ten times that.

reviews.title: This stuff is A.M.A.Z.I.N.G!!
reviews.text: I have type 4a AA hair and have searched high and low for a curl cream that I can find in stores.  I am a Qhemet Biologics addict, now reformed.  This cream is the perfect in-between comparable to their Olive and Amla Heavy Cream and the Burdock Root Buttercream.  It is smoother and more absorbing than both. It also has a very soft hold. I have not had a softer, more defined twist or braid out- ever! After one application my super dry ends were moisturized for three days! Give it a try, you will NOT regret this inexpensive purchase.

reviews.title: Works great for twist outs and braid outs, just like the packaging says.
reviews.text: I purchased this product two years ago, and it gave me the best braid out on earth! I also purchased the Feather Whipped, but the Butter Whip gave me more moisture. It's consistency is thick (exactly like store bought cake frosting) but it goes on like a hair lotion or hair milk. I have low porosity hair and live in a climate where it's humid all year round, so there is a bit of a "poof" factor no matter what products are used in an out style. However, when visiting a cold climate I did a braid out on hair that had in braids for 3 days. My hair was so moisturized that it felt DAMP, but it wasn't! So yeah, moisturized hair for a week with this product! The only thing added was oil on top during the application process. For all you wash and go ladies, this product excels in twist outs and the like! Hope this helps!

reviews.title: Nice color match
reviews.text: Color matched well with my daughters hair. We haven't tried curling it but looks like these will work well.

reviews.title: Pretty nice quality hair for the price.
reviews.text:
I've been using extensions since 2006, due to health issues causing extreme hair thinning. I used to get the fused extensions at the salon, 1500 dollars back in the day. They broke off from the weight of the extensions because my hair was so fine and thin. The fused glob of hair was just too much and so many fell out. I started buying the clip in hair, taking the clips off and using them as weft hair. I use salon pro white glue, much like eyelash glue but used for weft hair. It is a wonderful solution and I still alternate between tape in hair and the clip in wefts. I also buy closures and custom color, to make my own toppers. What a job! But, I'm happy with the results. I have more control over the color and style, and I feel 'normal'.

This hair is a medium to coarse texture, thick, and has a really nice body wave. The is soft and smooth and doesn't feel damaged. It will blend with fine hair. You can use a straightening iron, just be sure that you can control the temperature. Once you burn this hair, it will stink and it will be ruined. However, if you control the temperature and use care, the hair looks great straight and shiny. You can also curl the hair. I have used felted hot rollers, and those work really well. They are safer, and not as damaging, and the curls last so long.

These 16" extensions are very nice. Although the ad isn't quite accurate. The color is a darker caramel blond, and a reddish brown. This is not piano keys, but close. The wefts are not in the ombre style, they are bold and varied high lights. If you look at the ad images, the photos showing the people aren't shown with this product in their hair. The wefts laying on a surface showing the color differences between the offered colors is the most accurate. I've offered some images that show the color more accurately. You can custom color most of the wefted hair, but adding color is easier. Typically the hair has so much red in it that it is impossible to cover or remove, and the dye can be hard to remove. You can add a longer root to eliminate the chunky roots. My hair is so fine, the sewn edge can be obvious, so have a good match to my natural blonde ash tone roots helps. Any coloring will add to the damage and potentially shorten the life of the hair, but for me it makes me happier to have an exact match to what I want, and I am hard to match for color.

The weight of the hair is shown in a photo in ounces. In grams, we get a little over 104 grams, far less than the advertised 120grams, and I would complain about this and have, just not for this brand. That being said, the fullness and the weight is still good, and the wefts seem to feel average when compared to what I usually order. If you order the longer options, the hair will be thinner, as most hair is sold by weight. 120 would be better, of course. This weight is significantly lower than 120grams.

Shedding. Over the last 7 years or so, shedding has become a real problem. It never used to be this bad. There is a weft glue that is meant to slow or prevent shedding and I always use it. It makes the weft more stiff, but if you use the weft the way I use them, you need to do something to protect your weft hair. You can find it online. If you are using this hair for special occasions and clipping to thick strong hair and are simply making your hair more full, wild and glamorous, then you may not need to worry about shedding. I did finger comb this hair once out of the packaging, and there was significant shedding. Again, I say, this is a common issue now, and unique to this particular brand. I have no idea what changed but the wefts should not shed this much, but they all do this with most brands. I have to say that if the wefts didn't shed so much, they would last for a couple of years or longer. Also, keep in mind I use the wefts differently, so clipping, as intended, may offer longer life and far less shedding.

The length is 16" and the hair hangs nicely, with a gentle, natural taper. There is a very nice body wave. It's not a blunt edge finish, which is nice.

The clips are the same type that I see on most clip in kits. They are small, and hold nicely even on fine hair.

Over all, I think this is a great value and will require the extra effort to keep them looking great over a year or more. To keep human hair looking good and tangle free, I use the sleep and shine shampoo and conditioner. It keeps the hair fresher and mat free longer. There are a lot of youtube videos that can help you with application and care, and give you tips and tricks.

4 stars.

reviews.title: Wow, beautiful hair!
reviews.text: These hair clips are gorgeous. They look and feel like the real human hair they are. They are soft and silky, the quality is amazing.  The color is beautifully blended.  You get 7 pieces.  One large enough for the largest part of the back of your head.  They fit so well around the head.  They clip in easily and remove without pulling hair out.  I put one in and it clipped in nicely.  I then thought, how will I ever get this out?  Well, by putting a little pressure on each end of the clip they come right out and did not pull one hair out of my head even when using all 7 pieces.  The color is a bit off for my hair but I am in the process of  transitioning my color due to graying.  This is fantastic hair, you won't be disappointed.

reviews.title: Natural looking hair, good value for 7 pieces
reviews.text: Any review of hair extensions by a guy in his fifties is going to be short (and mainly guesswork!) but luckily I found willing volunteers in my wife and daughter, who report that these hair extensions are very easy to put in, even for inexperienced users. It looks like good quality hair - very soft and straight, and has varied shades of brown and blonde running through it. The clips are easy to open and shut, but there was a little trouble with the clips slipping - that may be due to lack of experience using extensions. Once in, they provide a noticeable boost to volume and really pretty highlights. The extensions did come with extra fastening clips, care instructions, and a decorative hair clip. For reference the images show the 16 inch extensions, in Light Blonde/ Highlighted Golden Blonde color.

This is my first experience with extensions and I have been SO impressed. First, the highlighted blonde matches my hair 100%. I know this won't be the case for everyone but I was shocked at how close it was to my "natural" color. They arrived neatly packaged and it was really easy to seperate and keep them tangle free. The clips are also a great feature as they pop in and out with ease. I was easily able to work them in without the clips showing through my hair. I washed and air dried each piece after wearing and they was so easy to restyle. The price is a bit steep but considerably less than what I would expect to pay for good quality.
If you're a newbie and considering highlights, these are definitely a great place to start. I highly recommend!!

reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:26b251054fc6c6ec321edf330079ffc6]] This thing shaved my head as close as a razor blade does. It is pretty much the closest shave you can get with an electric shaver.

I have had 5 and 6 head shavers but this one goes to seven! And that many rotating heads make your shave go quicker.

It fits in my hand and is comfortable to hold. It's USB-C rechargeable which is super convenient.

Update: it had a glitch and stopped working, I took some tweezers and rotated the gear inside the main unit manually and it started working again.

Update: it suddenly stopped working again and this time it will not start. I really liked this shaver but it just abruptly died. It's charged, and the lights and display work but the motor doea not run. I can not use this.

reviews.title: 7 head shaver. Waterproof. The on/off button is easy to accidentally toggle when using
reviews.text:
First off, I'm going to review this for face/beard shaving. I don't have a bald head (yet, anyway), so that isn't my focus.

I have used rotary floating blade shavers for decades (usually Norelco). They work best on my face, especially for the neck region. These shavers have always featured three heads.

Well, here we have seven heads. Seven heads!! I have used very similar shavers to this one with five and six-head shaver attachments. This is one more. The question is: Why do the high-quality venerable brands stick to three heads, and these shavers use so many more?

The answer isn't quality of shave. In fact, I was impressed at how well this shaver cleared the stubble on my face at the end of the day. It was comfortable, and managed to navigate the nooks and crannies of my face and neck as well as my low-end Norelco (if not better). But it's no match--not even remotely close--to my top-of-the-line Norelco (though that costs about 4x as much, so that's probably not a fair comparison). But--and this is huge--the ergonomics of the shaving heads leave something to desire. First off, there's very little reservoir under the cutting heads for shaved hair (see photos). This means you'll need/want to wash the head (it's water immersible) after each shave. Second, the middle head of the seven doesn't pop up. To remove it, you have to twist it off. It's a bit of a pain--and there's even less room under this head for hair than any of the others.

The third issue I have with the cutting heads is that there's no obvious way to replace them without simply replacing the entire shaving head assembly. Based on my experience with other shavers quite similar to this, expect the blades to get dull after a bit of use, and factor in the cost of an entire replacement head unit when they do.

In terms of seven vs six vs five heads? Well, the motors in these shavers are effectively the same. So if you have seven heads, the amount of power you provide to each head is less than it is in a five head design. That's going to affect the maximum speed and also the ability to easily tackle heavy stubble. Given that I don't sense a huge amount of improvement with seven vs five (or six) heads, I'd be inclined to veer towards fewer heads.

In general, I'd suggest that you can interchange the 7/6/5 head assemblies if you want to get a replacement.

In the kit you get:

Seven head cordless shaver with LCD readout of charge remaining
Waterproof (IPX level not indicated)
Nose hair trimmer attachment
Hair clipper attachment
Two face cleaning attachments
Three length guides for the hair clipper attachment
Charging cable (requires USB-A power source). The charge port on the shaver is a standard USB-C port.
Small cleaning brush

Shave is controlled via a simple on/off button on the back. Given how you would normally hold this--palmed (see photo)--I found it annoyingly easy to accidentally push the power button when I was using it.

The non-shaver attachments are a mixed bag. On the plus side, the nose hair trimmer works very well. This is in contrast with the nose hair trimmers that come with clippers, which are worthless. The nose hair clipper here works as well as the dedicated nose hair clippers I've used. On the minus side, the hair clipper is quite mediocre--it operates at a low speed and struggles with any amount of hair. It's about as good/useful as the clipper that's integrated into some shaver handles. Those can clip a bit of trim hair, but they are an exercise in frustration for any serious hair removal. Same here. The face cleaner attachments are probably OK, but I can't imagine using them.

On the whole, I'd consider this a decent little kit useful for shaving your face and for nose hair removal. The remaining attachments are, in my opinion, not very useful. While it does a good job of shaving out of the box, my experience is that the heads get dull somewhat quickly, and you need to buy a not-inexpensive replacement assembly. Also, cleaning the assembly is a nuisance.

Pros:
** 7 head shaver does a good job on face
** Waterproof (IPX level not indicated)
** Nose hair clipper works well
** Li-Ion battery: 2 hours charge for 90 minutes use
** Charge port on shaver is standard USB-C, so you can use charge cables you have at home
** Can lock the power button for travel (by holding for 3 seconds)

Cons:
** Too easy to push the power button when palming the shaver in the hand during use
** Hair trimmer has limited usefulness
** Face cleaning attachments not very significant
** Shaver head assembly doesn't provide much room for hair and will need to be cleaned after each shave
** Middle head of shaver attachment difficult to remove for cleaning
** Must replace entire shaving head unit when one/more blades get dull
** 7th head in shaver assembly does not float like outer heads

Note that there is a dizzying number of similar 5/6/7 head shavers sold on Amazon. As far as I can tell, having reviewed many of them, they are all made by a handful of companies and work pretty much equivalently well. The snap-in attachments even work across different models and brands. There are variances in features like level of waterproof and how the power switch works (and the body shape), but the function is pretty similar. So shop around for price.

reviews.title: Cheap and unreliable.
reviews.text: I purchased the 7d head shaver and after about 2 months of semi-regular use and cleaning as specified by the manufacturer, the device can hardly get a grip on my hair to shave and has developed an incredibly irritating high pitched whine/screech to it. It was loud and annoying when I first turned it on but I could deal with it as the cost of having an electric shaver, I never expected it to be silent. This new shrieking noise is unacceptable. It always took multiple passes to get a decent clean shave on my head, but now it takes an exponentially greater amount of passes with the shaver to get anywhere close to a shave with this device. The accessories, when used with a light touch as described in the manual, have also developed structural issues and are also unusable. One can expect this device to last about 2 months before becoming garbage.

reviews.title: My New Back Scratcher
reviews.text:
I don't know anyone who can really reach their back without a special tool, but few roll up like these do. I like how my complimentary set is so easy to store, either flat or rolled up.

Inside the package you find 3 separately sealed scrubber sponges.

As someone with sensitive skin I was concerned about them hurting to use but one side is much softer than the other and it really felt great. This makes a great back scratcher. I did notice some fraying even from the first use, not much but enough to give me the heads up that these will not last a very long time but maybe 2-3 weeks of daily use each if your careful perhaps longer.

reviews.title: Gentle yet exfoliates nicely.
reviews.text: The back is the hardest spot to reach for everything from cleaning, to exfoliating, to moisturizing that I tend to slack on that area as much as I hate to admit it. The loofah back and body scrubber sponge has changed all that for me from the second I used it. They are so easy to use and comfortable to hold. The belt-like portion is long enough where I can hold both handles and not feel strained or awkward but relaxed as I loofah away. The handles are very soft and braided. The belt is about 28 inches long and 3 1/4 inches wide. The loofah makes up about 11 inches in the middle. It's soft and not paper thin but kind of squishy and padded if that makes sense. It's put together very well, feels and looks of high quality. I've used other loofahs in the past that were very hard and actually hurt to use. I've been pleasantly surprised by these in the fact that they truly do give a gentle exfoliation. They feel nice and never too rough or scratchy. I absolutely love that these don't hurt because I honestly used to be scared of most loofah products. I also love that there are three of them in this pack. Not that I want to share cause I like them a lot although you could share with a spouse if need be. I suggest keeping them all to your self though because, well, they are very nice quality. reviews.title: Well-Made, Effective Loofahs
reviews.text:
These loofah back and body scrubbers are wonderful. I received the pack very quickly and love that it came in a pack of 3. My husband, my son, and myself each got one for ourselves. I have used one before in the past so I am going to compare to that and these are way longer. The length really helps with my shorter arms and being able to reach without strain is great. The scrubbers are a good width as well, it makes scrubbing in the shower quicker and more efficient to give a good clean with more ease.

The material is a very soft and natural terry cloth. The feeling against my back when using is amazing. It does not feel scratchy at all and just seems to remove the grime and dirt well. It definitely cleanse my skin wonderfully. Also, the terry cloth seems to give an exfoliation that just an average loofah does not. It is usually hard for people to exfoliate their back because of the area and reaching problems so once I used this I could seriously feel a difference from just the first use with removing dead skin cells. I feel softer and smooth every time.

The scrubbers have really convenient handles, large enough for even my husbands big hands and easy to hold the soft fabric. The handles seem to be attached well and the seams on the scrubber itself have stayed tight even through using almost every day.
After using I just hang the loofah scrubber up by one of the handles and it dries fast enough for the next use.

Such a sweet deal for the pack of 3 and even better is the great quality. I would recommend this to others and actually think I am going to grab a pack for my mother. Its perfect for those who want ease of use when cleaning areas that are hard to reach as well as just having a luxurious feeling when in the shower. I received these loofahs for a slightly reduced price in exchange for my honest and unbiased review. If for any reason I find that I need to change my review, I will update this.

reviews.title: Anti Aging Serum - The Best Hyaluronic Acid Face Cream Moisturizer
reviews.text:
[[ASIN:B01699WMHO Anti Aging Serum - The Best Hyaluronic Acid Face Cream Moisturizer for Anti Wrinkle & Skin Tightening Night Cream. Tempt by Cazbe Skin Care Products Rejuvenate your youthful glow]]

I am always looking for something that will help me turn back the hands of time. I want to look good for as long as I can and because of that I've often used Vitamin C serums. I know they are effective and help with aging skin a lot, My skin has always been a challenge. I have oily skin with large pores and sometimes have uneven and dull skin. I also have some expression lines on my forehead. I wanted to start using this serum as soon as I got it home. The serum comes in a very nice bottle which I like a lot because not only is it pretty but the serum is delivered in an airless pump which distributes exactly the right amount of serum to my fingertips for easy application to my face. Since Vitamin C products are easily oxidized I appreciate the product being dispensed this way so that it protects the serum and keeps it effective. I have been using this serum the morning on cleansed skin after toner, and then again at night before moisturizer on clean skin in the same way. The serum has a nice scent that is not overpowering and it only takes a pump or two of the serum to cover my entire face. The serum doesn't feel greasy or oily and it's just the right consistency to spread easily. There was no burning or irritation with this product, and it absorbs well into my skin with no left over residue. My skin feels smooth, silky and refreshed after I apply this serum as if my face has a refreshing blast of moisture. It looks supple and moisturized for hours and I've noticed my makeup stays on better. I have been using the serum for about two weeks now. Since then I have noticed a lot of amount of improvement in the tone and smoothness of my skin. It doesn't look as dull and my skin also looks firmer. My skin is more radiant and soft and the expression lines in my forehead appear a little softer and the improved clarity of my skin is noticeable. My skin also looks better when I'm not wearing makeup. My skin has improved a little and I've been asked what had I been doing to make my skin look better? That makes me feel more confident that the serum is working. I am going to continue to use this serum and consider it a good value worth every penny of the asking price here since a little goes a long way.

reviews.title: enjoying this - great in tandem with a thick skin cream
reviews.text:
My mother asked me to find her a moisturizer with matrixxyl and peptides. Very specific and she didn’t want anything that did not have at least those two ingredients.

The reason is a conversation with her dermatologist. Matrixxyl is made of 2 peptides, palmitoyl-tripeptide and palmitoyl-oligopeptide, which stimulate collagen and elastin production in the skin. And peptides form a long chain of amino acids to become proteins, penetrating the top layer of skin cells and signaling the cells including collagen.

The Cazbe serum works really nicely in tandem with my mom’s Ponds cream (she has used Ponds as long as I remember). The serum absorbs pretty rapidly and unlike other serums, doesn’t seem to leave her skin feeling sort of ‘tight’ or dry. After a few minutes (she will put the serum on before brushing her teeth, and then follow with moisturizer after teeth), she will apply the Ponds cream to completely moisturize her face. I’ve tried this a few times and find I can only really use it around my eyes – it dries out my cheeks and chin. If used just around my eyes, it feels a lot better.

Her skin is looking beautiful. I think in combination with a really solid cream to sort of ‘seal in’ the benefits, after washing your face, and just before bed, it’s a great part of your beauty routine.

* we received the serum for evaluation and review

reviews.title: Not so sure.
reviews.text: This product leaves the skin soft enough and feels nice but my concern is more about ingredients. I like to see my skin care's first ingredients be more like the heavy hitters like glycolic or lactic or even natural like aloe so I was disappointed regarding this one's contents.  First is water and second is witch hazel. Which I likr, but three and four are glycerol stearate and peg-100 stearate. Peg 100 is a mixed bag of reviews and some even question the safety of it regarding get use in skin care.  It's disappointing because it leaves my skin feeling amazing but I do like more natural ingredients for the first couple ingredients than this product has.

reviews.title: Very pleased with this anti aging serum! Recommended!
reviews.text:
This product review is for Anti Aging Serum - The Best Hyaluronic Acid Face Cream Moisturizer for Anti Wrinkle & Skin Tightening Night Cream. Tempt by Cazbe Skin Care Products Rejuvenate your youthful glow.
My wife bought this and this is her review: This is a great serum and I really love it. It smells nice and clean and isn't to thick. I use two pumps of this in the morning and two pumps at night. This amount covers my entire face and my neck area with no problem. I have been using this for two weeks now and have noticed results. Now my skin looks even toned and feels so much softer than before. My skin has no patches of dryness now, which it did before and I notice I have no red spots either. So I know this serum is working for me, therefore I highly recommend this. Most serums I have used before are clear and this one is white, so it kind of feels like a mix between a serum and a moisturizer. It spreads a long way so I expect this bottle to last me two months or so. For the price this is great if it does last two months so that makes me happy. I incorporated this into my daily routine with no problems and no issues of skin irritation or anything for that matter. In the morning I apply this right under my makeup and it works really well as a primer this way. My makeup looks better and last longer throughout the day. At night I put a moisturizer over this and again it works well for me. When I wake up my skin feels so soft and I am pleased with that.
Please note that I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

reviews.title: TOTALLY works!
reviews.text:
I'm a low-maintenance girl and really not that into hair accessories, but I saw these for $3 at the store and decided to give them a try. I am BLOWN AWAY by how well these work!

I have long, thick hair. I pulled my hair up like I was going to create a high ponytail, but I did NOT put in an elastic. Instead, I just started coiling it into a bun. Once done coiling it, I spun the first pin through the base of the bun to hold the end of my hair down. Then I spun the other pin across through the body of the bun to hold its shape. I let go and IT DIDN'T BUDGE A BIT. It stayed exactly in place and did not try to uncoil. It stayed neat and in place even when I bumped the bun pretty hard getting into my car.

It took less than a minute from start to finish. SO COOL!

reviews.title: freakin awesome!
reviews.text:
I love this thing!!
I used to put my hair up in buns with hair ties, but I'd always end up redoing them in the middle of the day. This spin pin is SO GREAT--it doesn't get loose (especially if you put them in while your hair is damp/wet after a shower)! I'm sitting here with them in right now shaking my head around and they're not moving or getting loose at all! What's more is that they're really easy to use and I have mine in my hair in about 10 seconds. No joke.
Really great product!!!

reviews.title: False advertising by "NotJustWigs"
reviews.text:
The item I received is clearly not the Goody Simple Styles product advertised by "NotJustWigs". I did receive functional spin pins, but they are black instead of the brown color shown on the product image as well as [...]

More importantly, the spin pins I received have sharp, pointed ends without the rubberized protectors shown on the Goody Simple Styles product. These unprotected ends are sharp enough to cut skin if used without caution. I have a feeling this merchant is buying a cheaper, lower-quality product and marketing it under a higher quality brand name at a higher price. Bad form, NotJustWigs, bad form.

reviews.title: A few of these will take the place of a half dozen bobby pins

I purchased two boxes, and it usually takes 3 of these in my heavy, full hair to keep it all up and secure, but once I get them in they stay all day.  It takes a few times to get used to using them, but once you get the hang of it, they are so easy to use.  One of the best hair accessory inventions I've ever seen.

reviews.title: Not authentic Goody product.
reviews.text:
Beware: if the item isn't packaged with the Goody label, it's not the real thing!

The item received wasn't the Goody brand. It was a poor imitation of the original, which is unfortunate, because I really wanted to replace the pair that I lost. The pins were packaged in a thin, clear plastic bag encased in an ivory mesh bag - clearly not Goody. I don't automatically have a problem with buying imitation products, but these simply weren't well made. I could see and feel that the end points were jagged and not cut smoothly, but attempted to use them anyway. I immediately noticed the difference in quality. They're supposed to glide through your hair when you spin them in, but since the points weren't buffed down properly, you could feel it ripping through instead. I had to return the item, which sounds easy enough. However, one has to still spend time packaging it, going to the post office, and waiting for/following up on the email confirmation that the item was returned successfully to the warehouse and refunded. I have Amazon Prime and this is one of those rare occasions when an order goes wrong. But it's still annoying enough to take the time to write about and to let the company know to not replace the genuine item with generic products. Needless to say, I still need a pair of *Goody* spin pins.

reviews.title: I love these spin pins.  Such a great concept.
reviews.text: Whoever created these pins are genius. :) Seriously what a brilliant concept!  I absolutely love these pins.  They're by far my favorite accessories.  So light and so invisible yet does a great job holding my hair all day.  Buy 2 or more.  You won't regret it.

reviews.text:
I have to keep my hair up for work but tight buns and heavy hair pieces give me a headache. I can use these two pieces with nothing else and keep a secure bun all day with no headache. I usually put my hair into a lose french braid (only about 4 cross overs) twist the braided tail into a bun and put one pin in the top and one in the side. I do think crossing the pins works better than one in the top and one in the bottom.

I have used it with wet or dry hair, it is a little more stable with wet hair.

My hair is fairly thin, I think thicker hair would need more pins. My hair is only about 8" past my shoulders. I have run with it up and it stays without a problem, then the nice thing is I can nap in them too, they ae not too heavy or bulky that they hurt your head.

reviews.title: False description
reviews.text: I purchased these from NotJustWigs and am extremely disappointed.  The picture and description lead you to believe that you will receive a brand new product.  The product I received did not come in a box as pictured in the description (they came in little bags tied with a ribbon), and the original ends appear to have been cut off, so they are sharp.  I have bought many of these pins in a regular store so I know what these are supposed to look like.  The picture is not at all an accurate representation of what I received. reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text:
This thing has blown me away!

Mostly got it because I thought a straightening brush would be faster than my traditional flat iron. I wasn't wrong, I was able to go from my before pic to my after in less than 5 minutes which is impressive enough but that's not the best part!

It doesn't fry your ends!

I was very skeptical when the description said that the steam made it healthier for your hair and caused less damage and kept it moisturized.
After using it I'm a believer.
Usually when I straighten my hair my ends end up looking and feeling like straw. Literally. It looks horrible no matter what type of conditioning product, heat protector, or oil I use. I was blown away when I saw my poor over bleached ends were as smooth as the rest of my hair! It's like night and day.

It's super easy to use and it comes with a little bottle to fill the steam chamber with water. It took less than a minute to heat up and it didn't pull hair or burn my scalp.

It is hard to get some of the baby hairs well with it but that's really not a big deal.

It has clips to help section your hair into layers and I don't think I've ever been able to straighten my hair so thoroughly so quickly!

I was surprised because when I opened it I felt like there was no way it would work on my hair but it was so easy to use!

If your hair is damaged I would 1000% reccomend this to help keep your ends from looking fried.

It's as affordable as most traditional straighteners and works so much better!

Get it.

reviews.title: A+ Great item. Used daily not always as a steamer but works great for stick straight hair.
reviews.text: A+ Recommended. My family member uses it, not personally. She uses it daily for mainly combing and straightening her hair. Gets hot quick, less than 30 secs. Used on long around 12-18 inch black, thick, full set of hair. Used after showering with damp hair mostly and works fine. Uses it as a brush/comb. Steam is used when her hair is dry. We had a hard time finding distilled water with all the shortages. So this review took us a month to complete. Normal everyday use is great on her thick rebellious hair. We don't use the steam as often but works great when hair is dry and needs moisture to brush. Great accessory or upgrade to your regular combing/brushing. Some parts are hard to get too, without a mirror or hard reach areas, like behind your head. Hope this helps. If you buy a gallon of distilled water its plenty for occasional use.

reviews.title: Works great on very long hair
reviews.text:
If you are looking for a hair straightener brush that really works great on long hair (past the middle of the back) this might just be for you.
I have very long hair that has terrible ends. I'm in need of a good hair cut! However, this straightener brush really made them look so much better, almost healthy! I used it with the steam feature and I don't think I've liked a straightener this much in a very long time.
I should probably mention something more about this device. This has a 30 second (to a minute) fast heating time, three different heat settings, a steam feature, an anti-scalding feature, and constant temperature protection. It also comes with a glove to protect your hand from the heat, a little water bottle thingy for the steam feature (I think), and a hair clip to use while straitening.
I really like this. Usually this type of thing takes me at minimum fifteen minutes or so. This took me about 7 to get a decent effect.
I recommend this if you are looking for a hair straightener that will give you a near salon look.

reviews.text: I am a habitual flat iron user and recently purchased a wet to dry heating tool/comb, but decided to take a chance on this straightening brush steamer: I must say:  I am quite impressed with this apparatus.  It took me a quick second to get in a groove of running it through my hair, but after a few times, it was pretty easy.  The tension/pull is really good and I liked how it left my hair.  My hair feels so smooth and I am pretty satisfied with how my hair turned out (after my first try)...
