It comes with a glove to protect the hand holding your hair while in use. I also like the addition of the steam function. This will be my go to hair straightener from now on.

reviews.title: Deceptive Advertising In My Opinion.
reviews.text: I'm not up on beauty products, I'm 51yrs old.  I have a hard time using a hair straightner and remembered when my daughter was a teen I bought a hair straightener brush for her, so I was on the lookout for one for myself.  I saw this and the ad heading said "hair straightner brush".  I thought the picture looked kind of funny, not like a "brush" but thought maybe it was a cover or something on it.  When it came, it looked just like the photo and that wasn't a cover.  That narrow skinny black thing running down it is what they call a brush.  But that is NOT a brush!!  It looks nothing like the one I had purchased for my daughter.  It really does have 30 second heating and it's pretty. I do love the color lol. But it isn't what I was looking for nor what the title heading on the product page says it is. So just make sure that you're aware before buying this, that it isn't a true straightner brush!!

reviews.title: Hair Tool Done Right!
reviews.text: This steam straightener is the absolute truth! I gave my hair a break for about a month from fully straightening it. I'm also overdue for a relaxer touch-up. So this was the perfect time to give this straightener a try. I'm the queen of flat irons, but I've never used a steam iron. There is a real difference! The steam silks each strand of hair. It's priced at $39 & that's a real bargain, for a tool that works so well. It's not quite meant for your edges. Get too close to your scalp & you'll surely feel the heat. It comes with a heat resistant glove, clips, water dropper, cleaning brush & protective sleeve! #BeautyMustHave

reviews.title: I am impressed!
reviews.text: I was very nervous to try this steaming straightener brush. The directions are very thorough so I felt confident. There are a lot of warnings about this product so make sure you read the instructions carefully. Once I did a few hair sections it felt like I had been doing it for years and all my fears were gone. I did not burn myself and my hair came out super smooth and pretty. It’s easy to do a little curl at the end if you rotate your wrist outwards which I like. I would recommend this product! Quick shipping and great packaging

reviews.title: Hair straightener
reviews.text: First time trying a straightener with steam. So far i like it left my hair manageable, not to flat maybe i need more heat,and it was a little puffy next day. Not perfect but worked fine for me.

reviews.title: My new favorite hair tool!
reviews.text: This straightener is amazing, I was looking for something to make my hair look great and fast for the busy days and this work is minutes. I'm so impressed! There are 4 heat settings I use the 3rd setting and my hair is naturally wavy and it gets it pin straight . I can even use it on my short bangs! I'm never going back to a regular straightener. It comes with a heat protection glove also which is great!

reviews.title: Works great, looks great
reviews.text: My wife has long, medium thick hair and this worked awesome for her. Left her hair super soft and straight.

reviews.title: Steam straight!
reviews.text:
This steam straightening iron really works great on hard to straighten hair or thick wavy hair. It comes with a heat glove and two clips. You will definitely need the heat glove because the steam gets hot!
This had my hair straight in no time and kept it straight. It left my hair shiney and healthier looking than what a normal heat iron would. It was super easy to use and works amazing! I love it!

reviews.title: Excelente la mejor compra que hago.
reviews.text: Llego a tiempo Excelente servicio. Lo recomiendo,voy a mandar a buscar otro para mi nena.

reviews.title: Refreshing Soap for Your Face as Well as Your Body
reviews.text: This black soap bar feels as thick as a brick but feels great on my skin. It's made with activated charcoal and has the coloring of charcoal, but thankfully not the scent! Under a hot shower it releases a nice minty scent and the hardy soap appears to less vulnerable to melting away in the soap dish unlike other softer soaps I've used. It's made for men but I don't see why women can't use it as well, as it's refreshing. It's best used for the face, as it cleans out your pores, but as I wash my face and body in the shower at the same time, it's a perfect face body combination soap.

reviews.title: A high-quality soap with great aroma, foaming and cleansing that I plan to use quite often
reviews.text:
Review of: TCM Men's Cleansing Activated Charcoal Bar Soap - For all skin types - 5oz (1 Pack)

In the handful of times that I’ve used the TCM Charcoal Bar Soap I’ve noticed a number of items that I’ve been really pleased to experience.

The first is the size of the bar; no wimpy sliver of soap here. The bar right around 3 inches in length, 1 ¾ inches wide and 1 ½ inches tall. A size big enough that, until I used the soap enough times to round off the edges, the size was almost too large to hold easily using just one hand.

A second, immediately noticeable, item was the smell. Not a sweet smell but more of a slight, dusky, flower-like aroma that, for me at least, was super nice. Perhaps, in it’s own way, as nice an aroma as I’ve found with any bar of soap.

The amazing amount of lather this bar gives off was another pleasant surprise, far more than I had expected from something that doesn’t fit into the ‘body-wash’ category. When washing my face, I only need to rub the bar in my hands a couple of times and then when the soap is rubbed onto my face the suds really start forming. The lather is impressive, especially compared to other ‘cleansing’ bars I’ve used before.

The charcoal, or maybe it’s some other ingredient, does seem to do a nice job of cleaning my face, especially after I’ve applied sun screen earlier in the day. Sun screen, in my experience, soaks into my skin really deeply and is almost like a magnet in attracting dirt, and many ‘regular’ soaps simply don’t seem to cut through the grim. The TCM soap, however, removes any embedded dirt and also does away with the slick feeling the sun screen leaves on my face. noticed that many cleaning soaps especially after washing my face.

On the other-side-of-the-coin, however, I’ve noticed that the TCM Charcoal Bar Soap leaves my skin drier than I expected; for me there was no real sense of having any moisturizing left on my face after washing. This dryness was apparent to me as, after washing, my face was almost too dry to be able to comfortably use my electric razor. Still, while that was a surprise to me I don’t consider the dryness to be a major concern compared to the cleaning benefits the soap gives.

All-in-all, the TCM Charcoal Bar is a high-quality soap that I plan to use quite often.

If this review was helpful, please click the Helpful button

reviews.title: my boyfriend knows his bar soap
reviews.text: my boyfriend LOVES all kinds of bar soap!!!  he gets soooo excited when a new 1 arrives.  he really liked the fact that this 1 was charcoal... he's had many over the years and has really liked them... this 1... not so much.  he said it feels more like a lotion rather than a soap... that being said, it does lather up very well.  he said it is more of a grey then a black and he didnt like the size of the bar.  he said it was awkward in his hand, but it dirtied the floor less than other charcoal soap.  he didnt love this bar.  I hope this was helpful.

reviews.title: Cleansing Bar
reviews.text: This has been a great bar of soap in the shower to use as a body scrub. With the charcoal in it, my skin feels smoother and cleaner. It has a great scent to it as well. This is a nice thick bar of soap, though for 10 dollars, it is on the expensive side.

reviews.title: Smells nice but uncomfortable to hold
reviews.text: I like the smell of this soap and I like how my skin feels after ri take a shower using this soap, bit I find the shape of it very uncomfortable to hold and use.  There is a reason why mainstream soap bars have a certain shape rather than being cut as rectangular prism.

reviews.title: Good soap, big bar, and nice smell!
reviews.text:
I'm enjoying this soap!

The bar is a bit bigger than your typical bar, so that makes the ~$10 price tag somewhat OK, though I think it should be a bit less expensive.

Still, the soap works well and hasn't left me with any dry skin issues. My skin is particularly sensitive so I try not to change up my bath products frequently, but this has had no harmful effects of any kind after a few weeks of use.

The bar itself is holding up well, I'd say it wears down like any typical bar soap; no faster or slower.

There's no residue or anything like that from the bar. It lathers up like typical bar soap and washes off easily.

I do really enjoy the scent. It is very, very subtle, and not something you're going to pick up on post-shower, and I think a lot of the smell comes from the shea butter. Reminds me of that a bit with a "clean" scent to it. It isn't particularly masculine or feminine so I think everyone would like it.

If you need a good bar soap for sensitive skin, I think this one is worth a shot. Plus you'll replace it less frequently thanks to the large size. Note that I only use this in the shower, and I wouldn't recommend it for using as counter soap. For one, its too big and awkward for that, and it is probably not quite sensitive enough for daily face cleaning, which is the only reason I dropped a star (since its supposed to be). Instead, I use this strictly as body soap, and for that, it works very well!

reviews.title: Smells like spices and cleans great
reviews.text: My dad is not a big fan of soaps but he loves this one. It smells like spices and is very nourishing on his skin. It cleans great as well. Suds up nicely and is easy to grip. Definitely recommend!

reviews.title: Deja la piel muy limpia
reviews.text: Tiene una fragancia suave. Deja la piel muy limpia y suave. Hace espuma rápido. Mi esposo lo usa para cara y cuerpo.

reviews.title: Big and sharply cut, but cleans great!
reviews.text: Got this to use on my face. My face tends to get oily through out the day, probably more then most. This charcoal soap is great. A little more expense for typical bar soap, but the same as buying a bottle of facewash. This soap is a rather large bar and didn't fit into my soap box. After using for a week, my soap container will shut with the edges worn down a little. This soap is my go to for face washing and even my beard. The scent is very light and doesn't linger. It lathers good and after you rinse with warm water my face feels smooth and clean. By the end of the day my face has a little oil again, but doesn't seem to be as bad. I believe my redness and couple acne spots has improved and more face washing hasn't dried out my skin.

reviews.title: Full size bar soap with natural plant oils and activated charcoal
reviews.text: This bar soap is a nice full size and includes cleansing natural ingredients. It is free of any harsh chemicals and it has a fairly mild added scent. I would personally leave out the added fragrance but otherwise this is a very good soap formula that nourishes the skin and cleanses with the power of activated charcoal.

reviews.title: GIANT bar that smells incredible!  Buy the 3 pack for better value
reviews.text:
This is a nice, fat bar of soap. I can NOT get over how fresh and CLEAN this smells! I could smell it before I even unwrapped the bar. This soap lathers up great and leaves my skin feeling soft, clean and smelling like the fresh air. I love a giant bar of soap and this fits the bill-its easy to hold on to.
Its feels nice and firm, so it doesnt dissolve too quickly, which is a good thing because it IS a bit pricey at 10 bucks for a single bar. I make sure to keep it up on a tray to make sure it dries out between uses. So far, I have used it about 3 weeks and its not even half way gone- so overall, it seems to be worth it, since I am usually replacing my bars by now.
**BONUS** My bathroom smells like i just cleaned the place ( I have a teen boy in here, so to say my bathroom smells good is a win)! I wonder if it would stain, because im seriously thinking of buying some just to put in my drawers and closets- thats how great it smells! If i buy more, i will make sure to buy the 3 pack- its a better value at just over 8 bucks a bar.

reviews.title: Works well on dirty paws...if it fits
reviews.text:
These types of paw washing cups are an excellent way to clean muddy paws. I’m so glad the days of lugging a bucket of water are behind me. I’ve tried a couple different versions and found this one to be good, if it fits your dog’s paws.
I have a golden retriever. The box I received said “medium” and it works for her, though it’s a little tight. There are a lot of silicone bristles inside and while that’s good for cleaning, if you’re on the borderline of it fitting your dog, too many bristles make it difficult to get your dog’s paws in and out and to agitate the cup enough to clean. I didn’t see a size noted when I ordered, so I’m not quite sure if it’s possible to order different sizes since I don’t see that info on the Amazon page, just on the box I received.
Cleaning the cup is easy - just rinse if the water isn’t too dirty or unscrew the top and remove the silicone bristle insert for a deeper clean.
The included towel is nice and fluffy, but only the size of a standard washcloth. My dogs feet come out pretty wet, so the cloth is soaked by the time I’m done wiping her feet. I use a towel instead.

reviews.title: Works good
reviews.text:
I tested this out on my pugs the day it arrived. I first tested to make sure they didn't fight me too much when I stuck their feet inside of the cup. They didn't. So, I filled with warm water, stuck their foot inside, turned the cup, then pulled their feet out to dry.
Seemed to work good. I just want to be able to rinse their feet of any dirt, sand, pebbles, or anything else that gets stuck in their pads.
I like that it has a small handle so I can hook it up near the back door.

reviews.title: Easy to use, gentle, and effective
reviews.text:
Beautiful bright color makes it easy to locate in the puppy’s storage bin. I also like that its simply designed, easy to use, works well, its gentle on their paws, and it comes with a super soft drying cloth.

The silicon bristles are removable and it can be disassembled and thoroughly cleaned in minutes. We get a lot of use out of it and at first, our puppies found it to be a nuisance to pop each paw in the cup, now our pups have come to expect it.

We have been able to keep our light tan carpet free of dirty paw prints and have peace of mind that there aren’t any harmful residual chemicals left on their paws from our walks. We are happy with this product and would recommend it to others.

reviews.title: Easy to use
reviews.text: Works! Looks just like the picture. My dogs were not afraid of it at all! Would recommend.

reviews.title: Good for cleaning paws
reviews.text: The product is very easy to use and easy to clean your dogs feet.  However make sure it will fit your dog feet.  Our 65lb pitbull it worked on however our 92 lbs Doberman feet were barely too big.  However it kinda worked.  Does come with a little towel so they can get clean feet during a bath or after a hike.  Was very useful

reviews.title: Wish my dogs loved it as much as I do!
reviews.text: The concept is great and the design is effective. None of my 3 dogs are as much of a fan of it  as I am though. They are good about letting me trim their nails, but wanted no part of dunking their feet into the cleaner. They like to swim...but this is different! 😂 I'm hoping they tolerate it better with more frequent use. As a result, I'm finding it necessary to have an assistant in order to use it. With one person holding the dog and one operating the washer, the cleaning goes much smoother. I love being able to get the sand off of their feet so they don'tdrag it into the car.

reviews.title: Double check size you need in my picture/review
reviews.text:
I was under the impression that the medium size would work for my chihuahua mixed dog based on the chihuahua they have pictured in their product picture. Boy, was I wrong because this thing is huge!

The box has a clear list of breeds each size is for and it would've been so helpful if it was included in the description. I've included a picture of it and typed it out below:

-Large: bulldog, German shepherd, golden retriever, husky, Labrador

-Medium: Australian shepherd, beagle, border collie, corgi, English bulldog

-Petite: chihuahua, Maltese, pomeranian, shih tzu, yorkie

Although I can't test it out on my dog, I tried it with my fingers. The silicone is soft and feels comfortable and the entire thing is easy to take apart and clean. I took off one star as the silicone has a pretty strong chemical smell. I would wash and air it out before using on your dog, especially if they have sensitive skin. The microfiber cloth is seriously one of the softest things I own haha.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text: A little messy so so outside if you can. Washes great. A little hard for larger dogs but once you get it, it’s magical for muddy paws!

reviews.title: Convenient
reviews.text: The package came a little busted but the product was intact! It looks durable and of high quality. It works as intended cleaning their muddy paws --or at least most of it. The rubber inside is soft but sturdy enough to remove the mud. The cloth that came with it is extremely soft and useful for making sure my dogs' paws are dry. I just leave this product in my car since my dogs love to get muddy paws in the dog park. I can quickly clean them before letting them in the car. Perfect for messy animals! I'd recommend it.

reviews.title: Nice idea... if it works for your dogs...
reviews.text: I ordered this for my schnauzer who I consider a medium sized dog in height if not weight. While it seems suitable, her paws are so skinny that there is not much contact with the rubber bristles. Needless to say, it does not get her any cleaner than soaking her paws then wiping them... so the grass really does not come off and neither does all of the stuff in her paw pads. Ironically, it makes my small dog want to join it the paw cleaning... he gets jealous at anything he is not allowed to do as well. It then becomes a mess and he is much too small... and still I have to do it. Also, there is the problem of where to put the cup that everything is accessible when we first come in... it really needs a dedicated shelf and something to make sure that it does not tip over. It is easy to clean the device parts. I assume that it may work for some that do not have small or skinny legged dogs, but not for us. It is easy to hold and use, but it is just too much work to get there with my small dogs and lack of a place to hold it. I am rating it three stars. As always, your mileage and preferences may vary...

reviews.title: HANDy for Dirty Paws
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:a9d4366e9768dd47df33f15a9df4853d]] It has been a wet Spring in Iowa. And puppies love the to play in the mud. This paw cleaner worked pretty well to get the garden mud off a Phoebe's paws. She is only an 8 pound Scorky (Scotty - Yorky mix). The cleaner would probably work better on medium or larger dogs but it did the job pretty well for her too. And she did not fight it at all so it was certainly not a painful experience. The included towel also worked well for drying her paws after the scrub. It was easy to use over the sink. You probably would not want to use it over the white carpet in your den.

The ringed lid screws off and allows the silicone fingered pad to slide out for easy clean up. There's also a wrist strap to guard against accidental drops. All in all, a well made, good quality product.

reviews.title: Works Good. Not For Large Dogs.
reviews.text:
I ordered this for my two dogs and it worked good, but it's not for large dogs.

I have a flat coated retriever mix and a bloodhound/chocolate lab mix and both of them have big paws. This is a medium and their paws barely got halfway in. It did work, but I couldn't get their paws in very far.

This is built really well and comes apart easily for cleaning.

Overall, a pretty good cleaning tool for those muddy paws after a nice walk. Just make sure you have the right size.

reviews.title: Cleans paws well, difficult to use with older dog
reviews.text: Dog Paw Cleaner Size Medium - First, let me start off by saying this paw cleaner is a great invention! It does clean the paws well and is fast and easy to use. I was excited to get this and try with my three little dogs because I'm constantly washing feet in a bowl of water. It worked well with

reviews.title: Great for my baby
reviews.text: I love it to keep her yeast infections down. I also keep the allergens outside. I have COPD and she brings a lot of pollen, grass, bugs ect. Wonderful

reviews.title: easy to use
reviews.text: i use it for all my dogs and they are perfectly calm with it and washes great and washes out easily

reviews.title: works great for haircuts
reviews.text: I really needed this for my boys, I cut their hair and they always have hair all through their clothes and even though I tell them to shake there shirts right there when they stand up, there is always a trail of hair going down the hall to the bathroom.  This is so nice, the neck is stretchy and fits different sizes of necks, easy to clasp shut and open.  I only wish the back was longer like the front but I had no problem with keeping hair off them. It was really nice to only have to clean up in one small area.

reviews.title: Does the job
reviews.text: Works well. Full coverage and easy to use closure- hook and eye on stretchy elastic. I don't know about it being "ultra soft", it feels like most hair cutting capes...kind of plastic like. Works for my family's needs.

reviews.title: Strange cut
reviews.text: The front of this cape is super long (almost too long) however the back is cut in a way such that no matter how you adjust the neck, only about the top 12" of the back are covered, leaving the lower back exposed. It is like a long rectangle with the corners of the short end joined behind the neck at a diagonal. It is better than nothing but the cut is just off. Maybe if you have an actual barbers chair it works better. I was using a towel as a cape when I cut my husband and kids hair, and this on top of a towel for the lower back (or two of these capes alternating directions so the long part can go in the front and the back) should do a better job than just the towel. The fabric is cheap thin polyester, which gets the job done but is definitely not "ultra soft silk fiber" as stated in the description. The neck is easy to adjust and fasten, so at least it has that going for it.

reviews.title: Nice lightweight cape
reviews.text:
Worked great... adjustable clips make it perfect for any size person. The lightweight fabric gave me the protection I needed with the ability to move my arms around while weaving my hair. Also needed to let the hair product dry so sat outside in the sun for 30 minutes for the developer to dry. Was very glad that it wasn’t heavy material.

:) Recommend if you need to “Do It Yourself Hair Coloring” during the Covid crisis.

reviews.title: Safe For Dry Cutting
reviews.text:
It's nice size and good for our purpose, cutting hair.
And since it called a 'Barber's Cape' and thusly one would expect dry cutting
I'm not sure how relevant this is.

HOWEVER - it's also stated it's resistant to chemicals.
But I don't see how it could be protective to ones clothing when it's not water proof,
liquid pours right through, so it's not good for various processes using chemicals etc.
And the reason for 3 stars

The material around the neck is pleated / drawn together with elastic.
It's adjustable with metal hooks, but feels poky and uncomfortable.

reviews.title: Wish it was longer on the back
reviews.text:
My first impression is that it is very similar to what my barber has. But on a closer look, I found that the material is very thin. Also I really would like to have it longer on the back. When I cut hair on the back of the head, I wish the cape would be longer enough to cover the clothes. Otherwise, it defeats the purpose.

It has elastic ring around the neck with a metal clip. It works well. Price is very good. But you get what you pay for.

reviews.title: Good only for home use
reviews.text:
Since COVID-19 I have been cutting my mother's hair. I had been hoping to find a cape that I could protect her clothing while doing the cutting. I was very happy to be offered the opportunity to review this one.

The cape, in general, is okay. This is definitely not a professional quality cape that you find at salons. It's very thin and not waterproof. For dry cutting, it's perfect. This should not be considered for anything involving hair coloring.

The cape is quite large. It definitely serves the purpose of covering the entire body. It also has a nice, silky feel to it.

The fastening clasp is ultra-cheap and I found this to be the most unlikable aspect of this product. The clasps take considerable time to fiddle around with and fasten. It would have been a much better idea to offer velcro or button straps.

But here's the thing: This doesn't cost a lot of money. You definitely get what you pay for. So, if you are looking for something inexpensive for home cutting during the pandemic then this will fit the bill perfectly.

reviews.title: A Necessity for Home Hair Cutting
reviews.text:
Back in the golden oldie days B.C. (Before COVID-19), I wouldn’t have known the difference between a barber’s cape and a bullfighter’s cape. But today, home hair cutting is now the norm, and a barber’s cape is practically a necessity.

This SmoTecQ one does the job with little drama. Measuring an adult-sized 57 by 63 inches, it has an elasticized neckband with two thin but sturdy metal clasps that can adjust to fit tightly around any size neck. The fabric in the back tended to gape open a little when I tested it, and it’s much shorter in the back than in the front, but overall it does a good job at what it’s intended to do. Hair doesn’t stick to it too badly, so it’s relatively easy to wipe clean after use. Folding it up to try to put it neatly back into its plastic storage bag was a challenge.

That’s not to say that hair still doesn’t go all over the floor, though, and that’s why my amateur haircuts, whether I’m getting them or giving them, take place on one of my outdoor patios.

Based on my zero knowledge of what makes a good one or a bad one, I have to rate this barber’s cape at a middle-of-the-road three stars. I wish I didn’t need one at all, but in the current reality, it’s a must-have item, and this one seems to do the job.

reviews.title: Made of quality fabric. Must have for hair cutting.
reviews.text:
This barber’s haircutting cape is sufficiently large for any individual. It is made of good quality fabric. The hairs do not get stuck in it. I feel that the mechanism to tie it around the neck could be improved.

Other than that, it is a handy cape.

reviews.title: Nice barbers cape
reviews.text: Nice long barbers cape.  Feels like those at the barber’s/salon.  No complaints

reviews.title: Important tool to have for haircuts
reviews.text: Works as advertised. As someone who cut another person’s hair without this and got hair everywhere including the fabric of the chair the person was sitting in... I can tell you this is worth having. If you’re a newly minted quarantine hairstylist, get yourself one of these so you don’t get hair all over the person’s neck and back, clothes, etc.

reviews.title: great cape for hair cutting!
reviews.text: perfect to cover your client while doing their hair. the closure is super nice too.

reviews.title: Let's Hair In
reviews.text: I am not a professional, but I do give my boys haircuts at home. They hate getting hair all over them (and since there are 4 of them and I don't plan on stopping the home haircuts anytime soon) I thought it would be worth ordering a cape. I got an adult cape because my 10 yr old is almost as big as me and I know my other boys won't be far behind. This cape is terrible! Hair gets down the top of it and they're just as itchy as if they weren't wearing anything. The quality of the cape itself seems fine, but that doesn't really matter if it doesn't work for the purpose it was made for.

reviews.title: Works great for at-home haircuts
reviews.text: I think many of us are attempting to stay groomed at home during the Covid 19 pandemic including myself. Using a plastic bag around my body was no longer cutting it. So I got this barber cape to use for my 5 feet 10 inch body and it's been working well so far. It's much more comfortable around my neck. There's no edges that tickle me like crazy. The sheer material used doesn't attract much hair and can be blown off easily. It also covers my body way better than my old plastic garbage bag did.

reviews.title: Thin and functional. Batman would be jealous.
reviews.text:
This cape works equally as well as a hair cutting cape as it does as a costume! I (jokingly) ran across the house with this as a cape when it arrived, and my son immediately commandeered it as his own.

In all seriousness, this works quite well as a hair cutting cape and keeps the clippings from landing all over you when you are getting your hair cut. My wife typically cuts my hair wet, and I did notice a bit of moisture coming through this, but not enough to bother me. If you are looking for a salon caliber cape, plan on spending twice as much. At this price, this works great for my needs.

reviews.title: Works well for its intended use!
reviews.text: Love the feel of this item very soft!

reviews.title: Not the best quality, would be great for a younger child or on the go
reviews.text:
A little disappointed in this package. I'll start with the good, the gloves work nicely and fit comfortably. The gloves are soft and easy to adjust on the hand and they're lightweight too so they don't get hot and make your hands sweaty.

The standing lamp is a micro USB cable and receiver for power. There is flex when I gently push the top of the lamp and the base. It feels really fragile. The light is a nice soft blue and doesn't get hot when it's on, the power button is fairly easy to press.

The individual handheld lamp isn't chargeable. It runs off three AAA batteries. The hand lamp is a deep blue light but it seems feint. The pink grips also slide on and off the casing fairly easily. It feels really lightweight and cheaper rather than a quality lightweight material.

I think this would be a great kit for travel, you wouldn't be devastated if you lost or damaged and had to replace these tools or maybe for a younger child who is starting to express interest in gel manicures. You wouldn't mind if there's some wear on these and you would replace them with nice quality items when they get used to handling them. This is the nail kit equivalent of giving your cousin your MadCatz controller instead of the official Nintendo one. It's good to have but I think it's worth it to throw a little more into the savings bank and buying a higher quality set or individual set of lamps.

reviews.title: Nail lamp
reviews.text: Very nice nail lamp and flash light. I like the pink color of the flash light lol but they work well and are portable. Perfect for flash curing too.

reviews.title: Nail Lamp set
reviews.text:
Very pleased with this versatile set. These are not as powerful as a full hand, high wattage nail lamp is, but it has its uses.

2 UV LED nail lamps, charging cable, and a pair of UV protective gloves. 3 AAA batteries are not included to power the flashlight lamp.

The white lamp comes with a USB charging cord & must be plugged in for use. Quick on and off button easily accessed with the fingertip being cured. While this is for one finger at a time, if you have small fingertips, two to three nails fit comfortably. Keep in mind, this is lower wattage than the full hand lamps, so curing time may be longer. This is great for the end stages of nail art, quickly curing rhinestones or painted accents.

The pink flashlight style nail lamp is not only great for nail art but also for crafts. Portable, battery-operated. Works well with nail supplies but also with epoxy, curing craft glues, and other craft needs. It's especially fabulous when fixing a nail on-the-go. The silicone sleeve allows for accuracy when holding and operating the flashlight.

The gloves are great at protecting your hands when doing a manicure, especially when using the full hand nail lamps where your entire hand is exposed to the UV & LED lights at a high wattage. They're not the best quality, but they're exactly the same quality of all the pairs I already have. A few stray strings that are easily clipped, a thin satin material that protects the skin to being exposed to direct UV light. The finger portions can be rolled down for those with shorter fingers.

reviews.title: Cheap, but it works. Gloves are awful.
reviews.text:
I ordered the three-piece set with lamp, flashlight, and gloves. The gloves are super, god-awful cheap. The material is so thin it is see-through... and if you can see through something -- that means light is passing through it. I wouldn't trust the gloves to protect me at all and you shouldn't either. Thankfully, I already own much better nail lamp gloves and I can just throw these away. The LED lamp and flashlight both work, with the LED lamp powered by USB (it must be plugged in) and the LED flashlight powered by three AAA batteries. The flashlight is super cheap and I don't expect that to last either; it's made of Aluminum -- I think -- and it has these silicone covers on them that slide around and I don't like that as it's annoying when they slide around.

Despite all my complaints -- the lights work. Range of light is 365 to 405 nm, which is near-UV to visible (violet), with near-UV meaning UV wavelengths are near the visible border closer to the violet side. These UV LED lights are cheap, common, and they work, so no complaints there. I'll update this review if this product breaks down, but for now, I give it three stars for "It's OK. It works but the quality is seriously lacking."

reviews.title: Cheap but gets it done
reviews.text: OK so this is def a cheap uv light set. The flashlight is a blue light flashlight that is actually pretty bright and you can use it to cure your nails on the go I guess. The mini uv lamp is very tiny. One finger at a time tiny. I like that I don't have to put my whole hand under a lamp but it does make the process take longer. The gloves are pretty flimsy. You can see thru them so if they are meant to protect your skin from the uv then it's not gonna work.

reviews.title: Just received a light…it’s okay.
reviews.text: Only received the light so I can’t really comment on the other things meant to be in the packet. But the light seems to work fine so far… it’s not automatic but the on and off finger pressure is easy enough to control

reviews.title: Has to be plugged in
reviews.text: The photos on the description make it seem like this nail lamp does not have to be plugged in making it truly portable to use anywhere. That is not the case, it has dmto be plugged in to work not to charge as it says in the description. If that is not a deal breaker for you, then I will say that this is a decent lamp if you want to do each nail 1 at a time. In my daughter's case it works good to make nail charms although there are often some that come out squishy and not completely dried. I have not tried the flashlight yet because I keep forgetting to buy the required batteries

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text: The size of the LED light is so convienent! I also love the little blue light! Both I use when doing my nails and designing them!

reviews.title: Great if you are just starting out doing your own gel nails.
reviews.text:
A few things to note with this little device: batteries are not included in the flashlight so you will need 3 AAA batteries ready. The desk unit must be plugged into use, the cord is about 30” long, and the desk unit stays on for 1 minute before it shuts off (you can turn it back on but it does shut off at 1 minute). The unit is small and will fit one finger at a time (maybe two if you have thin fingers) and the gloves are one size fits all which means they were too big for me, in the picture I had to scrunch down each finger and they kept sliding into my nail.

The weren’t really any instructions included, but using this product isn’t rocket science - plug it into a usb brick or outlet, plug it into the outlet and push the power button.

This would be a cheap and easy way to see if doing your own nails is something you want to do, but for the more experienced, one nail at a time might get frustrating.

reviews.title: Mini set
reviews.text: This mini set is good. Easy to travel with and work on the go. Tiny but does big work.

reviews.title: Convenient Push Button - Gloves Nice and Long
reviews.text:
Syle: White Lamp, Black Gloves, UV Flashlight
This mini Lamp works well and I like the position of. The push/power button being right on top of the base where your finger sits. This way I can hold my tip in place with my free hand while pushing the power button with my finger being cured. Not quite the same as hands-free touch sensor, but nearly close to it. I really loved the Gloves and surprised they not only fit my hand (long fingers) but that the ends reach all the way to the base of my nails, affording maximum skin protection. I really hasn't expected them to fit so well. If you have much shorter fingers you may have to 'cuff' them to fit shorter. The Flashlight works fine and I can use it for quick flash cure to set my Gel on each Nail and then full-cure under Lamp. Overall a nice combo set.

reviews.title: Great Mini UV Lights!
reviews.text:
These 2PCS Mini UV LED Lights are Excellent!
Both are easy and very convenient to use! I especially love the hand held flashlight design of this mini UV light. It's small enough to throw in my purse or even sit on the couch and do nail art to my nails. This set of mini lights comes with a pair of UV protection gloves.
I love this set!
Great Value!

reviews.title: Help those poor abused hands
reviews.text:
This is a very nice hand care bundle for very hands on people. First you get a very nice hand cleaner that contains activated charcoal. This detoxes and cleans very nicely of the toughest dirt, eliminating strong odor but doesn't damage your hands like some strong cleaners might do.

Next you get a moisturizing and healing cream. This is great for those who have done some damage to their hands in some physical hand jobs where they hand up with dry and cracked hands. This gives back some moisture and heals those hands.

Last but not least is a nice protect and moisturizer. This adds a nice barrier to your hands but also adds much needed hydration to much abused hands that are dry, cracked and scaly. When you have this on your hands it is much easy to wash and wipe away stubborn dirt.

This is a nice little set that can also be gifted, especially for people who work with their hands like mechanics or wood workers but many others. This works really well and will help restore some of those much abused hands.

reviews.title: I wash my hands alot
reviews.text: This is a perfect bundle for me. The barrier cream I have to apply several times a day, because I wash my hands all the time. I like the hand cleaner. Dirt and grime get cleaned off wonderfully and my hands get exfoliated. Then, I finish up with the salve. That helps relieve the cracks from dry skin.

reviews.title: Workman's Friend Ultimate Hand Care Bundle
reviews.text:
For me, I like the Hand Cleaner and Barrier Cream the most in this bundle. I found that the Superior Healing Hand Cream was just average. These products might seem dude oriented, but they work for anyone.

Superior Hand Cleaner with Activated Charcoal (individually 4/5 stars):
--Works great to remove grease and odors. I like the pumice in the cleanser, as it gives me a real clean, scrubby feeling.

Superior Barrier Skin Cream (individually 4/5 stars):
--Actually does help prevent grease or oil from staining my hands. Also helps keep my fingernails from becoming a dirt mess when I weed without gloves. But doesn’t provide much of a moisturizing effect.

Superior Healing Hand Cream (individually 3/5 stars):
--Truly unscented hand lotion. But it’s just average as a lotion -- it took a few days of 2-3x per day applications to help heal my cracked hands.

reviews.title: Great for working hands
reviews.text: This is a great set if your hands regularly get dirty or dry. There's a barrier cream that can be used as a hand lotion, charcoal exfoliating hand soap, and a thick hand cream. There is a faint scent, but it's clean smelling if anything. The soap says you can use it with or without water. I wouldn't eat anything with my hands without using water with it though. The heavy cream is really nice to out on just before bed, just the tiniest amount will take care of your hands. Both creams soak in quickly and aren't greasy at all. I wash my hands it seems like a thousand times a day and these keep them from being tight and dry.

reviews.title: Awesome products
reviews.text:
This is a great, complete hand hair set for anyone with dry, cracked hands. It would make a good gift.

*The charcoal scrub is very gritty so it's good for getting dirt out of the cracks on your hands.
*The barrier cream is helpful for prevention of dry hands (stop it before it even starts).
*Both the barrier cream and the hand cream have the nice side effect of reducing the appearance of wrinkles/aging on your hands.
*The products are gentle and excellent quality.
*A little goes a long way.

reviews.title: Helps dirty hands
reviews.text: We do a lot of house work, art, construction work, and gardening. We use a lot of soap. That orange goop with pumice in it is our normal heavy duty hand cleaner, this soap is equivalent to that soap without the orange scent. You definitely do need water for it to be effective even though the bottle claims you don't. The hand cream is a great heavy duty healing lotion, similar to eucerin. I haven't noticed an appreciable difference in the ease of cleanymy hands after using the barrier cream however.

reviews.title: Love it
reviews.text: This is great STUFF

reviews.title: Fantastic care for tough, cracked skin
reviews.text:
I work with my hands a lot and have fairly tough skin. Normal lotions and creams don't really work. This kit is the full package, starting with hand cleaner that works hard to get dirt out of every area of your hand and fingernails and works well when used with a brush. You also get the healing hand cream which was softer than I was expecting but it's really an amazing product that helps soften tough skin and provides a lot of moisture to help heal cracks.

The final product is the barrier skin cream. This is more of a protective cream you apply once you have your skin where you want it. It protects your skin and helps prevent cracking and dryness. All three of these products are fantastic and have been working so much better than I expected. I had heard of this brand before but never had a chance to try it until now and I'm a big fan. I think my favorite product is the healing hand cream. It's the most useful for me and really works fantastically to heal my dry skin, especially in the winter when it's especially dry where I live. The other products are also good but I'm positive once the healing hand cream is used up I'll be purchasing more. Great product, made in the USA and has a real quality look to both the packaging and product. I should also mention there isn't a lot of scent at all to these products, they just do their job and don't try to make you smell a certain way which I really like.

reviews.title: Love these products
reviews.text: My hands are dry and cracked. These products haven’t made my hands feel softer and moisturized.  I was extremely pleased they have a skin cream too. I use the cream on my elbows and other dry patches of skin. I have seen some improvement on my elbows where it isn’t as rough.

reviews.title: Ingredients
reviews.text:
These have very little odor. They include a lot of currently popular natural ingredients along with a few ingredients not favored by those most into that kind of thing.

Barrier

It isn't quite like a protective glove. Probably does help with protection for a limited time, not a whole day of work with your hands, during which it gets absorbed, wiped away, worked out by other substances and so on. Takes a couple minutes to soak in, but it's not very greasy once it does.

Active ingredient: dimethicone.
Inactive ingredients: deionized water, stearic acid, propylene glycol, glyceryl stearate, isopropyl palmitate, Ricinus communis (castor) seed oil, cyclopentasiloxane, phenoxyethanol, ethylhexyglycerin, sodium hydroxide, mineral oil, Simmondsia chinensis (jojoba) seed oil, xanthan gum, disodium EDTA, acrylates copolymer, Aloe barbadensis leaf extract.

Hand cleaner

Looks like it has plenty of charcoal, it's so dark, but more of the grit--and it's very gritty--is from pumice than charcoal. Does't foam, cleans surprisingly well anyway, moisturizes a little.

Ingredients: aloe leaf juice, pumice, glycerin, sunflower seed oil, sorbitol, carbon [that's the charcoal], grape seed oil, lanolin oil, vitamin E acetate, xanthan gum, carbomer, 2-phenoxyethanol, ethylhexylglycerin, sodium hydroxide.

Hand cream

Not a heavy-duty cream but has some nice stuff in it that's thought to help with skin health, moisturizes for a while.

Ingredients: water, glycerin, stearic acid, dimethicone, sunflower seed oil, cetearyl alcohol, ceteareth-20, allantoin, petrolatum, hydroxyethylcellulose, aloe vera, Symphytum officinale (comfrey) rhizome/root extract, Calendula officinalis oil, tocopherol, diazolidinyl urea, iodopropynyl butylcarbamate, acrylates/C10-30 alkyl acrylate crosspolymer, sodium hydroxide.

Workman's Friend is a brand about two years old based in Texas. The hand cream and soap are made in the US, the barrier is made in Canada.

reviews.title: Perfect for people that work with soap
reviews.text:
Excellent product my wife clean dishes and the soap put her hands itching, with this product itching is gone I would recommend to everyone
Mi esposa lava trastos y el jabón le da picazón extrema, pero con este producto la picazón se fue sus manos está muy hermosas
